bltpar1: Tiny parrotbill, warm rufous crown and nape, grey face with a black throat patch and white moustachial streak, olive-brown back and orange-buff flanks; very short stubby orange-yellow bill and pinkish legs.
bltpar2: Small green parakeet, mostly green with a brownish-maroon breast, blue forehead and crown, red ear patch and red bend of wing, and a maroon-red lower belly; pale horn bill and grey legs.
bltsal1: Medium saltator, dull brown above with a black face, chin and throat, a pale buffy-grey chest grading to warm buff belly; bright orange bill with a dark culmen and light brown legs.
bltspi1: Small bright rufous-brown spinetail, uniformly rufescent with a paler chin and a contrasting solid black throat patch; blackish bill and blue-grey legs.
bltsun1: Small sunbird; male is iridescent metallic violet-blue on crown and tail with crimson-red back and breast, a black throat and belly, and yellow rump; long decurved black bill and dark legs.
bltthi1: Small streaky furnariid, rusty-brown above and greyish below with a long pointed black-tipped tail, a rufous wing panel, and a small black throat patch; thin dark bill and dark legs.
blttit1: Stocky black-and-white cotinga-like bird; male is mostly white with a black crown, black wings and a broadly black-banded white tail; bare red facial skin and a thick red-and-black bill, grey legs.
blttit2: Tiny long-tailed tit, grey-brown back, black mask and black throat patch, a rufous crown and breast band, and white underparts; very small black bill and orange legs.
blttra1: Slender hummingbird with a very long forked tail; male is glittering golden-green above and below with green-blue gorget and a long blackish forked tail; short straight black bill.
blttro1: Large trogon; male has a metallic green head, back and breast with a bright red belly separated by a white breast band, blackish face, and a wholly black underside to the tail; stout pale yellow bill and grey legs.
bltwre1: Medium wren, warm rufous-brown above with a grey face finely streaked white, a black throat and breast, and buffy belly; long slightly decurved dark bill and dark legs.
blubun: Small finch; male is deep blackish-blue overall with brighter cobalt-blue highlights on the crown, cheeks, shoulders and rump; short stubby black bill and dark legs.
blubus1: Medium bustard, soft blue-grey overall with a finely vermiculated brownish back and a paler grey neck and underparts; longish pale legs, slim build, and a short straight pale-based bill.
blucou1: Large slender cuckoo-relative, glossy deep blue-violet overall on head, body and long tail, with bare bright blue skin around the eye; black bill and blackish legs.
blucra2: Tall elegant crane, pale ashy blue-grey overall with a broad pale crown, and long trailing dark inner-wing plumes sweeping down past the tail; pinkish bill and dark legs.
bludac1: Small tanager; male is brilliant turquoise-blue with a black throat patch, black back, wings and tail with blue edging; short conical black bill and pinkish legs.
bluebo1: Medium grass-parrot, pale olive-grey above and on the breast with a blue forehead and face, a red belly patch and red under-tail, and yellow flank scalloping; greyish bill and dark legs.
blufin1: Small finch; male is uniform slaty blue-grey overall, slightly paler below; short conical yellow to horn-coloured bill and dark legs.
bluflo1: Small flowerpiercer with a hooked upturned bill; male is dusky blue-grey overall, slightly bluer on the head and brighter blue on the shoulders; black hook-tipped bill and dark legs.
blugrb1: Stocky finch; male is rich deep blue overall with two rusty-brown wing bars and blackish lores; very heavy silvery-grey conical bill and dark legs.
blujay: Crested jay, bright blue above with a black necklace across the breast, white face and underparts, and black-and-white barred blue wings and tail; black bill and legs.
blujeb1: Plump terrestrial babbler, deep blue crown, face and breast with a white throat patch and white belly, and rich brown upperparts; black bill and pinkish legs.
blumoc: Slim long-tailed mimid, entirely deep blue overall with a black face mask through the red eye; slightly decurved black bill and black legs.
blupit1: Plump short-tailed pitta; male has a vivid blue crown, nape and underparts, a black face and throat band, a rufous-brown back, and black-barred blue flanks; stout pale bill and pinkish legs.
blusee1: Small finch, male uniform dull slaty blue-grey overall, slightly paler on the belly; thick conical dark bill and dark legs.
bluvan3: Small striking vanga, bright azure-blue above on crown, back, wings and tail with clean white underparts and a black mask through the eye; blue bill and dark legs.
blwbis1: Small weaver-finch; breeding male is mostly black with a flame-orange to scarlet crown, nape, back and breast band, and black wings edged buff; conical black bill and pinkish legs.
blwkoo1: Large kingfisher with pale buff streaked head, whitish underparts, dark brown back and bright sky-blue wing patches and rump; massive blackish-and-pale bill, pale eye. Male shows blue tail, female rufous-barred tail.
blwlap1: Black-and-white lapwing with brown back and breast, black crown and breast band, white face and belly, and broad black flight feathers. Red eye, red-and-black bill, long red legs; white forecrown patch.
blwlau1: Olive-brown laughingthrush with heavy black scaling on crown and breast, rufous-tinged face, and blue-grey panel on the folded wing. Short black bill, dull legs; rounded body with long graduated tail.
blwlea1: Bright leaf-green leafbird with blue-washed wings and tail; male has a large black throat and face mask edged with violet-blue malar streak and turquoise shoulder. Stout black bill, greyish legs.
blwlov1: Small stocky green parrot, male with red forehead and forecrown, black underwing coverts and flight feathers; bright green body, red-and-yellow bill. Female all green with horn-coloured bill, grey feet.
blwmin1: Small slim babbler with grey-blue crown and nape, rufous-brown back, pale buff underparts and blue-edged wings and tail. Fine dark bill, pale eye, pinkish legs; long graduated tail.
blwmon1: Clean black-and-white monarch with black head, throat, back, wings and tail, contrasting white breast and belly; blue-grey bill, dark legs. Medium flycatcher shape with upright posture.
blwori1: Bright golden-yellow oriole with all-black wings and a black tail tipped yellow; black eye-line through the eye. Stout pinkish-red bill, grey legs; robust thrush-sized songbird.
blwpit1: Plump short-tailed pitta with buff underparts, fawn breast, black crown stripes and broad black eye-line, buff supercilium, green back, brilliant blue wing-coverts and rump, white wing flash and red vent. Stout dark bill, pinkish legs.
blwsal1: Stocky finch-like bird with olive-green upperparts, grey crown and nape, white throat bordered by black malar, grey breast and buffy belly, and blackish wings and tail. Heavy black conical bill, dark legs.
blwthr1: Large deep-blue thrush, blackish-blue overall with fine pale-blue spangling on head and breast and glossy blue shoulder patches. Bright yellow bill, blackish legs; long-bodied with a fairly long tail.
blwwar1: Plain warm-brown warbler with unstreaked rufous-tinged back, buffy-white underparts and warm flanks, short pale supercilium and dark eye-line. Fine dark bill with pale base, pinkish legs; rounded short wings.
blyhor1: Huge black hornbill with white tail and a large pale-yellow casque atop a heavy creamy bill; male has rufous head and neck, female all black-headed. Bare blue throat skin, blackish legs.
blylew1: Small greenish leaf warbler with olive-green upperparts, yellowish-white underparts, long yellow supercilium and dark eye-stripe, dark crown sides with paler median crown-stripe, and two pale yellow wingbars. Dark-and-pale bill, pinkish legs.
blypaf2: Rufous paradise-flycatcher with glossy blue-black head and crest, blue eye-ring and bill, rich chestnut back, wings and long tail, and greyish-white underparts. Dark legs; slim with very long central tail streamers in male.
blypip1: Streaky brown pipit with buff-edged dark back, bold dark malar, well-streaked buff breast and plain belly, broad pale buff supercilium and median-covert edges. Pale-based dark bill, pinkish legs; large-headed, fairly short-tailed.
bnbkin1: Large kingfisher with chestnut head, neck and underparts, white throat and collar, blue back, wings and tail; very heavy bright coral-red bill, dark eye, red legs. Robust upright build.
bnbpyt1: Tiny dull olive tyrant with greyish-olive upperparts, pale grey throat and breast washed olive, faint pale belly, and dark wings with weak buffy edges. Short blackish bill, pale eye, dull legs; stubby tail.
bncbab1: Plain ground babbler with rich rufous-chestnut crown and nape, olive-brown back, whitish throat and breast streaked dusky, and buff-brown flanks. Slender pale bill, pinkish legs; rounded short-tailed shape.
bncbrt1: Large dark megapode, sooty-black overall with bare reddish head and neck skin and yellowish throat wattle; heavy blackish bill, bright red legs and large feet. Bulky body with broad arched tail.
bncfly: Olive-brown Myiarchus flycatcher with grey throat and breast, pale lemon-yellow belly, rufous edges in wings and tail, and a slight bushy crest. Long blackish bill, dark legs; upright posture.
bncrai1: Slim grey-faced rail with olive-brown black-streaked upperparts, blue-grey face and breast, and black-and-white barred flanks and undertail. Long slightly decurved reddish bill, brownish legs.
bncwoo2: Tiny pied woodpecker with brown-and-white barred back and wings, pale buff face with brown ear-patch and moustache, buffy streaked underparts; male has a small red patch behind the eye. Short grey bill, grey legs.
bncwoo3: Tiny grey-brown woodpecker with white-barred brown back and wings, brownish-white face with dark cheek-stripe, and pale streaked underparts; male shows a tiny red spot on the rear crown. Short dark bill, greyish legs.
bnhcow: Stocky blackbird; male glossy greenish-black body with a contrasting matte chocolate-brown head; female plain greyish-brown overall, slightly paler throat. Short thick conical dark bill, dark legs; finch-like build.
bnhgul1: Pale grey-backed gull with a pale brownish hood in breeding plumage, white underparts and neck, and black wingtips with white mirrors. Red bill with dark tip, red legs, pale staring eye.
bnhnut: Tiny short-tailed nuthatch with blue-grey back and wings, dull brown cap reaching the eye, whitish cheeks and underparts, and a small whitish nape spot. Thin pointed grey bill, greyish legs.
bnnpar2: Large stocky parrot with a huge pale horn-coloured bill, olive-brown head, green body and wings, and red-orange thighs and shoulder bend; bare grey eye-ring. Greyish legs; female may show orange forecrown.
bnrcot1: Male brilliant scarlet-red on crown, breast and underparts with contrasting black throat, neck, back, wings and tail; stout dark bill, dark legs. Compact red-and-black cotinga; female duller olive-red.
bnsfly1: Small drab flycatcher with greyish-brown upperparts, whitish underparts diffusely streaked brown across breast and flanks, pale lores and faint eye-ring. Dark bill with pale-based lower mandible, dark legs.
bnttre1: Slim treecreeper with dark brown white-streaked upperparts, plain rufous-buff rump, and warm buff-brown underparts lacking white. Long decurved bill, pale below; stiff brown tail, brownish legs.
bnttre2: Streaky brown treecreeper with dark brown upperparts spotted and streaked pale, rufous rump, and rich buff-brown underparts. Long slender decurved bill with pale lower mandible, pinkish legs; stiff pointed tail.
bnwkin1: Large kingfisher with buff-orange head, neck and underparts, dark brown back and wings, and a paler blue rump and tail. Massive bright coral-red bill, dark eye, red legs; heavy upright build.
bobfly1: Large flycatcher with olive-brown back, dark head with white supercilium meeting around the nape, concealed yellow crown patch, white throat, and yellow underparts. Very thick heavy black bill, dark legs.
bobfly2: Male deep blue flycatcher, rich ultramarine above with a glistening forehead, blackish face and throat, and orange-rufous breast fading to whitish belly. Black bill, pale legs; compact upright build.
bobher1: Stocky pale heron with a massive broad flat black bill, black crown and long black crest, grey back and wings, white face and underparts, and rufous flanks. Large dark eye, greyish legs; thickset build.
boboli: Breeding male is mostly black with a creamy-buff nape patch, white scapulars and white rump and lower back. Stout dark conical bill, dark legs; female streaky buff-brown like a large sparrow.
bogcuc1: Large long-tailed ground-cuckoo with greenish-black glossed upperparts and breast, rufous-buff belly, scaly bluish throat, and bare colourful facial skin around the eye. Heavy greenish bill, stout legs; broad long tail.
bograi1: Slim rail with olive-brown black-streaked upperparts, slate-grey face, breast and belly, and faintly barred flanks. Long slightly decurved red bill, reddish legs; compact short-tailed marsh rail.
bokmak1: Stout bush-shrike with bright olive-green back and wings, yellow underparts, a bold black breast band and necklace, and yellow throat and supercilium; grey crown. Heavy black hooked bill, pale eye, greyish legs.
bolbla1: Glossy black blackbird with a slight metallic sheen, contrasting browner wings visible in flight, and dark eyes. Slender pointed dark bill, dark legs; medium-sized with a short squared tail.
bolear1: Small earthcreeper, grizzled grey-brown above with a broad buff supercilium, grey-tinged forehead, and pale buffy-grey underparts. Long slightly decurved bill, brownish legs; slim ground-dwelling shape.
bolrec1: Largish rufous-brown ovenbird, rich reddish-brown crown and back with buff streaks, buff supercilium, and rufous-streaked throat and breast. Heavy wedge-shaped slightly upturned pale bill, dull legs; chestnut tail.
bolsla1: Antshrike with grey upperparts and underparts; male has a black crown, blackish wings boldly spotted white, and white wing-coverts tips. Stout hooked black bill, grey legs; female browner with rufous crown.
boltap1: Small dark tapaculo, uniform slaty-grey overall with slightly browner flanks and faint barring on rear underparts and undertail. Short black bill, pale legs; rounded body with a short cocked tail.
boltyr1: Tiny olive tyrannulet with greenish upperparts, pale grey throat grading to yellowish belly, faint pale supercilium, and yellow-edged wing feathers without bold wingbars. Short dark bill, dark legs; compact.
bongul: Small delicate gull; breeding adult has a slaty-black hood, pale grey back, white underparts, and white leading edge to the outer wing with black trailing tips. Thin black bill, pinkish-orange legs.
bonpet: Small gadfly petrel, grey-brown above with a dark M across the upperwings, white forehead and face, dark crown and eye-patch, and clean white underparts and underwing with a dark diagonal bar. Black bill, pinkish legs.
boorat1: Tiny green hummingbird with glittering green upperparts and throat, white belly, fluffy white leg-puffs, and a long racket-tipped blue-black tail with bare shafts. Short straight black bill; male spectacular.
boptyr1: Tiny pygmy-tyrant with bronzy olive-brown upperparts, greyish face and throat, pale yellowish belly, and a bold pale-yellow eye-ring. Short dark bill, dull legs; rounded big-headed shape with short tail.
borbar1: Small chunky barbet, mostly bright green with a red forehead and crown patch, blue throat and cheeks, a black band across the breast, and a yellow patch below it. Stout pale bill; greenish legs.
borbri1: Bizarre crow-sized songbird, sooty black overall with a bare bright yellow-orange head bearing short yellow bristly feathers on the crown, red on the nape and thighs. Heavy hooked black bill; black legs.
borchi2: Small grayish-brown chickadee with a dull brown cap, black bib, white cheeks, warm rusty-brown flanks and grayish back and wings. Short black bill; dark legs.
borgrm1: Bright pea-green corvid with a thick black mask through the eye, chestnut-red wings, and a long green tail tipped white. Heavy coral-red bill; red legs.
borstu1: Tiny round dark brown warbler-like bird with an almost invisible tail, pale buff supercilium, dark eyestripe, and scaly buff-brown underparts. Fine pointed bill; pinkish legs.
bortre1: Medium treepie, ashy gray overall with a darker grayish head, paler gray underparts, blackish wings, and a long graduated black tail. Stout black bill; dark legs.
borwhi1: Plump whistler, olive-green above with bright yellow underparts and a yellowish face and throat, lacking strong markings. Stout dark bill; grayish legs.
bostib1: Small warm rufous-brown babbler with a chestnut crown, pale yellowish-white throat and breast finely streaked with black, and buffy underparts. Pale yellow eye; small bill; pinkish legs.
botgra: Large glossy iridescent blue-black grackle with a very long, deeply keeled V-shaped tail and a long pointed black bill. Pale yellowish to brown eye; long black legs. Male much larger than the tawny-brown female.
botspa: Plain pale brown sparrow, gray-brown above streaked rufous and black, with an unstreaked grayish-buff breast and a flat-headed look. Pale lower mandible; pinkish legs; long rounded tail.
bouwre1: Boldly marked wren with a chocolate crown, reddish nape, white supercilium and blackish eyestripe, reddish-brown back spotted black and white, white underparts heavily spotted black, and a barred gray-brown tail. Long slightly decurved bill.
bowbab1: Dark brown wren-babbler, rich brown above with bold white streaking on the head, mantle and underparts, and a blackish face. Stout pale-based bill; sturdy pinkish legs; very short tail.
bowsht1: Plump rufous-brown shrikethrush, warm reddish-brown above and on the breast, with grayer underparts, a faint pale eye-ring and lores. Stout dark bill; grayish legs.
brakit1: Medium raptor with a white head, neck and breast contrasting with bright chestnut back, wings and belly, and rounded chestnut tail tipped paler. Pale yellowish hooked bill; dull yellow legs.
brarub1: Glittering green hummingbird with a sparkling green throat and breast, bronzy-green upperparts, and a rich coppery-red tail. Straight black bill; the female duller and spotted below.
brasta1: Pale gray-and-buff starling with a long shaggy black crest on the crown and nape, creamy buff face and underparts, and gray wings and back with a black-and-white tail. Bluish bill with yellow tip; yellow legs.
bratan1: Brilliant scarlet-red tanager with contrasting black wings and tail, the whole head and body vivid crimson. Silvery-blue lower mandible; dark legs. Female dull brownish-red.
bratap1: Small dark slate-gray tapaculo, uniformly blackish-gray with faint brownish barring on the flanks and lower belly. Short black bill; pinkish legs; short cocked tail.
bratea1: Small brown duck with a grayish head, warm cinnamon-brown body, a glossy green speculum bordered white, and a pale buff wing patch. Bright red bill and red-orange legs.
bratin1: Plump rich brown tinamou, dark reddish-brown above finely vermiculated and barred, with a grayer head and paler buffy throat and underparts. Slender pale-based bill; dull legs; small head, no tail.
brbant1: Small round antpitta, warm brown above with a grayish face and crown, a pale eye-ring, and pale buffy-brown underparts with an indistinct rufous breast band. Stubby dark bill; long pinkish legs; nearly tailless.
brbant2: Small olive-brown antwren with a black throat finely spotted white, gray face, breast and upper belly, and brownish posterior underparts. Dark wing-coverts tipped white; pale eye; thin bill; gray legs.
brbbul1: Olive-brown bulbul with a darker brownish-black cap, white cheeks and throat, a warm yellowish-buff breast band and yellow vent. Slender black bill; dark legs.
brbfly1: Small flycatcher, gray-brown above with a slightly darker head, a broad flat bill, pale orange-buff throat and breast, and whitish belly. Dark bill; grayish legs; upright posture.
brbfly2: Plain brown flycatcher, warm olive-brown above with a bold whitish eye-ring, pale lores, whitish throat and buffy-brown breast and flanks. Pale-based broad bill; pinkish legs.
brbhon1: Small dull honeyeater, brown above and whitish below with fine brown streaking on the breast, a pale face and a slight pale eye area. Slender slightly decurved dark bill; pinkish legs.
brbhum: Slim hummingbird with glittering green upperparts and throat, a grayish belly, and a notched blue-black tail. Bright red bill with a black tip; the female grayer below with a white eyebrow.
brbmoc1: Mockingbird with chestnut-brown crown and upperparts brightest on the rump, a whitish supercilium and black eyeline, dusky cheeks, and whitish-gray underparts. Bold white wing stripes and white outer tail feathers; dark bill and legs.
brbmot1: Green motmot with a rufous crown bordered black, a black mask, blue-green forehead, green back and breast with a small black breast spot, and a long racket-tipped tail. Broad flat black bill; dark legs.
brbnee1: Large powerful swift, glossy blackish-brown above with a brown throat and grayer body, a contrasting white horseshoe patch on the vent and undertail, and a pale brownish saddle on the back. Short black bill; very short stiff tail.
brbpar1: Tiny stocky parrotlet, bright green overall with a black back, and red on the outer tail visible in flight. Pale horn bill; pinkish-gray legs; short tail.
brbpar2: Small long-tailed parakeet, green overall with a brownish-maroon breast, blue in the wings, a dull brownish ear patch, and reddish on the bend of the wing. Pale bill; grayish legs.
brbpuf1: Large black-and-white puffbird, glossy black crown and upperparts, white forehead, face, throat and upper breast, a black band below, and an olive-brown lower breast; central belly white spotted brown. Lacks a white eyebrow. Heavy black bill.
brbrol1: Stocky roller, deep cinnamon-rufous on the head, back and breast with a contrasting purplish-blue belly, wings and tail. Bright yellow bill; short legs.
brbscy1: Slender brown woodcreeper, warm reddish-brown overall with faint pale shaft-streaks, and an extremely long, thin, strongly decurved brownish bill. Grayish legs; stiff propping tail.
brbsol1: Plump gray solitaire, uniformly slate-gray overall with a slightly paler belly and a thin white eye-ring. Pale orange bill; orange legs; upright posture.
brbswa1: Small swallow, dark glossy blue-black above with sooty brown underparts and a slightly forked tail, lacking strong markings. Tiny black bill; dark legs.
brbtan1: Vivid tanager, brassy yellow-green head and underparts shading to greener flanks, a deep golden-orange throat and breast, and blue-black back and wings. Short dark bill; grayish legs.
brbtod1: Tiny plump tody, bright green above with a vivid red throat patch, whitish-gray underparts washed pale yellow on the flanks, and pink sides. Long flat bill, red below and dark above; pinkish legs.
brbwar1: Small bright warbler, green above with a rufous-chestnut crown, a gray face and throat, a broad flat bill, and yellow underparts. Pinkish legs; short tail.
brbwar2: Plain warm brown bush warbler, rufous-brown above and buffy-brown below with a paler throat and faint pale supercilium, lacking streaks. Fine dark bill; pinkish legs; rounded tail.
brcale1: Robin-like ground bird, olive-brown above with a gray head and nape, a white throat, a brown breast band, and white belly. Slender dark bill; long pale pinkish legs.
brcdov1: Long-tailed pigeon, warm rufous-brown overall, richer cinnamon on the underparts, with a grayish wash on the head and a long graduated tail. Dark bill; reddish legs.
brcfly1: Small flycatcher, dull brown above with two buffy wing bars, a faint pale eye-ring, and whitish underparts heavily streaked brown across the breast. Dark bill; dark legs.
brcful1: Plain small babbler, warm olive-brown above with a grayish crown and nape, pale grayish face, and buffy-white underparts. Fine dark bill; pinkish legs; rounded tail.
brcmar1: Large brown martin, grayish-brown above and white below with a dusky brownish breast band and a small dark central spot on the lower breast. Short black bill; dark legs; forked tail.
brcred1: Active warbler, slate-gray above with a brown crown, bright yellow underparts, blackish wings and a black tail with white outer feathers. White eye-arcs; thin dark bill; dark legs.
brctch1: Bush-shrike, brown above with a chestnut-rufous crown, a long whitish supercilium, blackish eyestripe, and pale buffy-white underparts. Heavy black hooked bill; long graduated tail; pinkish legs.
brctyr: Tiny tyrannulet, olive-green above with a brown crown, a short whitish supercilium, plain wings, and pale yellowish underparts. Short stubby dark bill; dark legs.
brcvir1: Small vireo, olive-green above with a brown crown, a bold white supercilium and dark eyeline, white throat, and yellowish-washed underparts. Short pale-based bill; gray legs.
brdowl: Large round-headed grey-brown owl with no ear tufts and dark brown eyes; pale facial disc, horizontal brown barring across the throat and upper breast giving way to bold vertical brown streaks below. Yellow bill, feathered legs.
brebla: Slim glossy blackbird; male iridescent black with green sheen on body, purple gloss on head, and striking pale yellow eyes. Female plain greyish-brown with dark eyes. Slender pointed black bill, long black legs.
brebul1: Large grey-brown bulbul with shaggy grey head streaked white, a rufous-brown ear patch, and brownish-grey body shading to whitish belly. Long slim grey-black bill, slender build, longish tail; dark legs.
brespa: Small pale sandy-brown sparrow finely streaked above with a plain grey-brown unstreaked breast, pale eye-ring and indistinct face pattern lacking strong markings. Pinkish bill, pink legs; long notched tail.
bretai1: Small dark warm-brown skulker with rich rufous-brown upperparts and paler greyish-brown underparts; very long, graduated, frayed wedge-shaped tail. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs; plain face lacking strong marks.
brfgle1: Plump rufous-brown ovenbird with bright rufous-tawny upperparts, contrasting paler buff-orange throat, and a more olive-brown back and rump; warm cinnamon tail. Stout pale-based bill, pinkish legs; large dark eye.
brfowl1: Large heavy owl with prominent sideways ear tufts, grey-brown upperparts boldly streaked black, and pale buff underparts finely barred and streaked dark brown. Yellow eyes, pale bill, bare grey legs.
brfwoo1: Small woodpecker with black-and-white barred upperparts, pale buff face with a brown forehead and crown, and whitish underparts streaked black with yellowish flanks. Male shows red nape; greyish bill, grey legs.
brgeup1: Small stocky euphonia; male glossy bronze-green above and on the head with a bright golden-yellow belly and lower breast. Female greenish overall, yellower below. Short stubby bill, dark legs.
brhapa1: Small slim warbler with olive-grey upperparts, a contrasting brown crown and nape, whitish throat and breast and pale buff belly. Pale eye, long graduated tail; thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
brhbar1: Large green barbet with a dull brown streaked head and breast, bright green body, and a large heavy yellow-orange bill surrounded by bare orange-yellow skin around the eye. Greenish legs, chunky build.
brhgre1: Tiny plain greenlet with olive-green upperparts, a contrasting warm brown crown, and pale yellowish-white throat and underparts. Pale eye, short stubby pale bill, greyish legs; rounded compact body.
brhgul2: Medium gull with a dark brown hood, white crescents above and below the eye, pale grey back and wings showing white outer primaries, and white underparts. Dark red bill and legs; pale eye.
brhhon1: Small olive-green honeyeater with a black head, a narrow white nape crescent, and pale buff-grey underparts. Bare blue-green skin patch above the eye; short pointed dark bill, greyish legs.
brhkin1: Medium kingfisher with a brown-streaked head and neck, blue-and-black back and wings with brilliant blue rump, and white throat shading to buff underparts. Large bright red bill, red legs.
brhpar1: Stocky green parrot with a dull brown head, a bright red ear patch and shoulder, and a bluish wash on the face and breast; green body with red bend of wing. Pale horn bill, grey legs.
brhpar2: Stocky green parrot with a grey-brown head and back, bright green wings and underparts, and a yellow-green to bluish belly. Dark grey upper bill paler below, grey legs; pale eye-ring.
brhthr: Medium thrush; male grey-brown above with a greyish head, bright rufous-orange flanks and breast, and a white central belly and undertail. Yellow bill, yellow legs. Female duller, browner.
brican1: Small finch (canary); male bright yellow-green below with olive-streaked greenish upperparts, a yellow rump and yellow eyebrow, and a greyish-green crown. Stout pale conical bill, pinkish legs.
brigra2: Plain warm brown grassbird streaked darker on the back, with paler buff-brown underparts and a long broad graduated tail tipped paler. Stout dark bill, pinkish legs; rounded wings, stocky build.
brihon1: Medium olive-brown honeyeater with a darker grey-brown head, a pale yellow ear patch and a 'bridle' of yellow facial lines, and a yellow-green nape patch. Pale gape, dark bill; greyish legs.
brispa1: Boldly marked sparrow with a grey head striped with black, a white throat bordered by a black malar stripe, rufous upperparts, and grey underparts. Black-tipped pale bill, pinkish legs.
briter1: Slender dark tern with sooty grey-brown upperparts, a black cap separated from the dark back by a white collar, a white forehead and supercilium, and white underparts. Long forked tail, black bill and legs.
britit: Small grey crested titmouse with a black-bordered grey crest, pale grey upperparts, whitish face outlined by a black eyestripe and chin, and pale grey-white underparts. Short black bill, grey legs.
brlwar1: Tiny olive-grey leaf warbler with a pale yellowish-white supercilium, dark eyestripe, two faint pale wingbars, and whitish underparts with a yellowish wash. Thin dark bill paler below, pale legs.
brnbar2: Plump dull barbet with sooty brown upperparts, a paler buff-brown head and underparts, and a dark eye with bristly facial feathers. Heavy pale-pinkish bill, dark legs; stocky big-headed shape.
brnboo: Large slender seabird with uniform dark chocolate-brown head, upperparts and upper breast contrasting sharply with clean white belly and underwing coverts. Long pointed yellowish bill, yellow facial skin and feet.
brncac1: Large shaggy rufous-brown ovenbird with a spiky erectile crest, warm rufous-brown plumage darker above, faint pale streaking, and a long rufous tail. Stout dark bill, greyish legs; whitish eye.
brncre: Tiny slender treecreeper, mottled brown-and-buff streaked upperparts blending with bark, white underparts, a thin whitish supercilium, and a long stiff pointed brown tail. Slender decurved bill, pale legs.
brnhao1: Medium hawk-owl with a rounded head lacking ear tufts, plain grey-brown upperparts, and white underparts boldly streaked and blotched rufous-brown. Bright yellow eyes, dark bill, yellowish feet.
brnhao3: Medium hawk-owl resembling Brown Boobook but darker; rounded head with no ear tufts, dark sooty-brown upperparts and dense dark brown streaking on whitish underparts. Yellow eyes, dark bill, yellow feet.
brnjay: Large dark jay with sooty brown to blackish head, breast and upperparts contrasting with a white belly and white-tipped tail. Heavy black bill (yellow in young), black legs; long tail, big-headed.
brnnod: Dark sooty-brown tern with a contrasting pale greyish-white cap and forehead fading into the dark nape, a long wedge-shaped tail, and slightly paler brown underwing. Long slender black bill, black legs.
brnpar1: Stocky grey-brown parrot with a brown head and neck shading to a paler grey-edged 'scaly' look, green wings and underparts, and a yellow underwing. Pale horn bill, dark grey legs.
brnpel: Very large grey-brown pelican with a long pouched bill, a whitish head and neck with chestnut hindneck in breeding plumage, and silvery-grey wings and back. Greyish bill and pouch, dark legs.
brnpig1: Medium dark pigeon; male slaty-grey with a glossy green-and-bronze iridescent nape and neck, blackish wings, and dark vinous-grey underparts. Dark bill with red base, reddish legs.
brnshr: Slim warm brown shrike with rufous-brown upperparts, a black mask through the eye bordered above by a white supercilium, and buffy-white underparts. Stout hooked dark bill, grey legs; long tail.
brnsku3: Large bulky dark brown skua with broad wings showing bold white flashes at the base of the primaries; mottled rufous-and-brown body, heavy build, short tail. Heavy black hooked bill, black legs.
brnthr: Large long-tailed thrasher with bright rufous-brown upperparts, whitish underparts heavily streaked dark brown, and two pale wingbars. Yellow eyes, long slightly decurved bill, pinkish legs; long rufous tail.
broacc1: Small streaked accentor with grey-brown upperparts streaked darker, a greyish head, a pale buff supercilium, and warm buff-pink underparts. Thin dark bill, pinkish-brown legs; rounded build.
brobab1: Medium drab grey-brown babbler with a paler greyish head and throat, faint streaking, and buff-brown underparts. Pale eye, slightly decurved dark bill, yellowish legs; long graduated tail, untidy plumage.
brobul1: Stocky brown bullfinch with greyish-brown upperparts, a darker grey crown, a whitish throat and pale grey-brown underparts, and a white rump and black tail. Stubby dark bill, pinkish legs.
brocow1: Stocky blackbird; male glossy bronzy-black with a slight greenish-purple iridescence on the wings, a thick neck ruff, and striking red eyes. Heavy pointed black bill, black legs. Female duller blackish.
brodip1: Large stocky all dark slate-brown dipper with uniform sooty-brown plumage, a short cocked tail, and a plump rounded body; sometimes a thin pale eye-crescent. Straight dark bill, dark legs.
brodro1: Glossy black drongo with a strong blue-green metallic sheen, a deeply forked tail, and a slender build. Stout slightly hooked black bill, black legs; red eye.
brofal1: Large variable brown falcon with dark brown upperparts, a pale buff face with dark malar streak and dark crown, and buff underparts streaked and barred brown. Bluish-grey bill, long legs; long tail.
brofly1: Plump rufous-brown flycatcher with warm brown upperparts, two cinnamon wingbars, a pale buff throat and breast, and rufous-edged wings and tail. Broad flat dark bill, pale legs; large head.
broful1: Small plain warm-brown fulvetta with uniform olive-brown upperparts, a slightly greyer head, and paler buff-grey underparts. Pale eye, short pinkish bill, pinkish legs; rounded compact shape.
broger1: Tiny warbler with grey-brown upperparts, a pale grey face and underparts washed buff, and a faint pale eyebrow; white tail tips with a black subterminal band. Red eye, fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
brogos1: Medium slender hawk with grey-brown upperparts, a paler face, and whitish underparts finely barred rufous-brown; rounded wings and a long barred grey tail. Yellow eyes, dark-tipped bill, long yellow legs.
brohon1: Small, plain olive-brown honeyeater with paler grey-buff underparts and faintly streaked breast. Pale yellow gape and a small yellow-buff patch behind the eye; slightly downcurved black bill and dark legs. Olive-yellow edging shows in the wings.
broill1: Medium ground-babbler, warm olive-brown above and on wings and tail, with paler greyish-buff breast and whitish throat and belly. Plain face with subdued greyish ear-coverts, dark eye, slender dark bill, and pinkish-grey legs.
broinc1: Large hummingbird, glossy bronzy-green overall with coppery iridescent sheen on head, back and underparts. Straight medium-long black bill; dark slightly forked tail. Sexes similar, dusky and metallic without bold markings.
broinc2: Large dark hummingbird, sooty brown overall with a small white spot behind the eye and a buff or cinnamon throat patch. Long straight black bill, dark coppery-green gloss on rump and tail. Drab and uniform.
brojac1: Slender, long-billed jacamar with glossy blackish-bronze upperparts and dark iridescent breast; clean white belly and undertail. Long thin straight black bill, very short legs, long graduated dark tail.
brojac2: Small dark jacamar, sooty blackish-brown overall with a whitish throat patch and white lower belly. Long, straight, slender black bill; short black legs; rounded short tail. Drab compared to glittery jacamars.
brolga1: Very tall grey crane with a featherless red head and nape and grey crown, plus a black dewlap below the chin. Pale grey body, drooping wing plumes, long dark legs and a long straight grey bill.
broman1: Tiny finch with a dark glossy-brown head and throat, brown back, and white underparts boldly scaled with dark barring on flanks. Iridescent green-bronze shoulder patch; stout silvery-grey conical bill and dark tail.
bromes1: Ground-dwelling, rail-like bird, uniformly warm reddish-brown above and slightly paler rufous-brown below. Plain face with a faint buff supercilium, dark eye, short slightly decurved bill, sturdy legs and a long rounded tail.
bronun1: Small puffbird-like bird, plain brown crown and upperparts with a buff-rufous loral band and narrow buff eyering set in red orbital skin. Pale rufous chin and breast, rufous flanks; large stout black bill with basal bristles.
broori1: Oriole-like bird, medium-brown above with a black-streaked crown and black, white-streaked face. Buff underparts heavily streaked black on throat and chest. Deep red iris, deep pinkish-red bill, greyish legs.
bropar1: Plump parrotbill, uniformly warm reddish-brown above with a slightly greyer face and paler buffy-brown underparts. Short, deep, yellowish hooked bill; dark eye, long brown tail. Plain, lacking strong markings.
bropri1: Slim long-tailed warbler, plain greyish-brown above with a faint pale supercilium and warmer brown wings. Buffy-white underparts, paler throat. Fine blackish bill, pinkish legs, graduated tail with dark subterminal spots beneath.
broqua1: Small rotund quail, mottled grey-brown and rufous above intricately marked with fine cream streaks and black bars. Greyish-buff underparts finely barred and spotted; short dark-grey bill, yellowish legs, very short tail.
brosic1: Large brown bird-of-paradise; male blackish-brown above with iridescent blue-green scaling on breast forming two glossy fan-shaped breast shields, and a very long pointed sabre-shaped graduated tail. Long decurved black bill, pale eye.
broson1: Large, slim, dark songlark; male rich dark brown above with paler scaly edging and a dusky-brown breast and belly contrasting with a paler throat. Slender pointed bill, long blackish legs, longish tail. Streaky upperparts.
brosun1: Long-tailed sunbird; breeding male glossy metallic coppery-bronze all over with green and gold sheen, darkening to blackish wings and tail with greatly elongated central tail feathers. Long decurved black bill, dark legs.
brotan1: Plain, drab tanager, brown overall with cinnamon-tinged face, wings and tail and a dusky mask through the eye. Underparts dull pale brown. Relatively thick blackish bill, dark legs; lacks bold patterning.
brotho1: Tiny dull-brown thornbill, olive-brown above with a rufous-tinged forehead faintly scalloped, and a pale reddish rump. Whitish underparts with fine dark streaking on throat and breast; pale or cream eye, fine dark bill.
brotin1: Plump ground bird, uniform warm reddish-brown to olive-brown above and on the breast, with a paler grey throat and buff belly. Small dull-brown head, slender slightly decurved grey bill, greenish-grey legs; tailless look.
brotre2: Bark-creeping bird, plain greyish-brown above with a pale supercilium and dark eyeline. Underparts buff with dark streaking on belly; pale buff wing-stripe shows in flight. Slender decurved dark bill, strong feet, stiff tail.
brratt1: Stocky flycatcher, variable olive-brown to rufous above with a bright rufous rump and tail. Pale yellow throat streaked dusky, yellow belly; staring pale whitish or yellow eye, stout slightly hooked black bill, pale legs.
brrmin1: Small minivet; male grey above with a black crown and nape, white forehead and face, and a contrasting brown rump. Buffy-white underparts faintly washed brown; slender black bill, dark legs, blackish wings and tail.
brrtap1: Small dark tapaculo, slaty-grey overall with a contrasting rufous-brown rump and flanks finely barred dusky. Short cocked tail, blackish slender bill, pinkish-brown legs; skulking dark bird with subtle barring on lower belly.
brrwhe8: Small olive-yellow white-eye with bright yellow-green upperparts and yellowish underparts, brighter yellow throat. Distinguished by very broad bold white eye-ring; fine pointed dark bill, greyish legs, dark eye.
brsrob1: Robin-like chat, warm brown above with a rufous-edged tail. Whitish underparts boldly spotted and streaked dark brown on breast; pale supercilium and whitish throat. Slender blackish bill, pinkish legs, upright stance.
brtbab1: Small babbler with rufous-brown back, wings and tail, a black crown streaked white, and a creamy throat blending to pale rufous underparts with pale streaking. Pale eye, short stout dark bill, sturdy pinkish legs.
brtbar1: Stocky barbet, mostly sooty blackish-brown overall with a faintly streaked head and a brown throat. Heavy pale horn-coloured bill with bristles at the base; dark eye, greyish legs. Drab for a barbet, lacking bright colours.
brtcha1: Slim wheatear-like chat, plain greyish-brown above with paler buffy underparts and a warmer rufous-buff vent. Dark brown tail (not the typical white wheatear pattern); fine black bill, long blackish legs, upright posture.
brtcur: Large long-legged shorebird, mottled buff and brown above with dark streaking, a buff crown stripe between dark lateral crown-stripes, and a bright cinnamon-buff rump and tail. Long decurved dark bill, blue-grey legs.
brtgra2: Plain warbler-like grassbird, warm olive-brown above and on wings, with paler buffy underparts and a whitish throat. Notably broad, rounded graduated tail with pale tips beneath; fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
brthum: Small hummingbird; male metallic green above and on crown with a glittering rose-magenta gorget and white breast band. Broad rounded green tail; straight black bill, dark eye. Female green above, buffy below, spotted throat.
brtpar1: Small green parakeet with a dull green body, paler yellowish-green underparts, and an orange-yellow face and throat washed orange around the eye and forehead. Pale horn bill, grey legs, bluish flight feathers, pointed tail.
brtplu1: Robust hummingbird, glittering green overall with a bronzy-coppery iridescent rump and uppertail-coverts and a dark blue-black forked tail. Straight black bill with reddish base on lower mandible; bright red feet.
brubro1: Plump pigeon, rich brown above with chestnut and grey tones, a rufous-buff forehead and grey crown, and an iridescent bronze-green wing patch. Underparts greyish-pink; short reddish legs, dark bill, rounded tail.
brubru1: Small boldly patterned bush-shrike; male black above with a broad white supercilium and bold white wing-stripe, white underparts with a broad chestnut-rufous stripe down the flanks. Stout black hooked bill, dark legs.
brucuc1: Slim grey cuckoo, brownish-grey above with a grey head and breast and pale rufous-buff lower underparts. Yellow eye-ring, dark eye, slightly decurved bill yellow at base; long graduated tail barred and white-tipped beneath.
brutin1: Ground bird, grey-brown above finely vermiculated and spotted black and buff, with a streaked crown and pale throat. Greyish breast grading to buff belly; slender decurved bill, pale legs, plump tailless shape.
bruwat1: Medium honeyeater, greyish-brown overall heavily streaked white below and on the face, with rufous-chestnut patches in the wing. Slim slightly decurved black bill, dark eye, longish tail with white tips; pinkish-grey legs.
brvear1: Stocky hummingbird, dull greyish-brown overall with a buffy throat and a violet-blue ear patch (often held flat). Short straight black bill, dark eye; plainest of the violetears, lacking bright iridescent gorget.
brwhaw: Compact buteo, dark brown above with a brown-streaked head and a barred rufous breast on whitish underparts. Broad wings; tail black with bold broad white bands. Yellow cere and legs, hooked dark-tipped bill.
brwjac1: Striking jacana, glossy greenish-bronze back and wings, blackish head, neck and underparts, with a bold broad white supercilium running down the neck side. Red-and-blue frontal shield, yellow-tipped bill, greenish legs and very long toes.
brwowl1: Large dark wood-owl, rich brown above with fine buff barring, a chestnut-brown facial disc with dark concentric rings, and dark eyes. Buff underparts finely barred brown; no ear tufts, pale bill, feathered legs.
brwpar1: Dusky parrot, dark blue head and breast, dull bronze-green body and wings with bronze sheen, and red undertail-coverts. Pale eye-ring of bare whitish skin, pale horn bill, dark legs; short squarish tail.
brwrai1: Large rail, rich rufous-chestnut head, neck and breast with a contrasting grey nape and olive-brown back and wings. Blackish lower belly and tail; greenish-yellow bill, bright red legs and eye.
brwsta1: Large glossy starling, blackish overall with a strong bronzy-green iridescent sheen on the body and a slight brownish wash on the wings. Bold staring red or orange-red eye; stout black bill, dark legs.
brwwar1: Small leaf-warbler, dull olive-brown to greyish above with a faint pale supercilium and dark eyeline. Yellowish-buff underparts; fine dark bill with pale base, pale legs. Plain, lacking wing bars.
bryfin1: Small bright finch, yellow head and underparts with greyish back streaked dusky, and a contrasting bright yellow rump. Greyish wings and tail; short conical greyish bill, dark legs. Male brighter than the streakier female.
bsbeye1: Antbird with extensive bright red bare skin around the eye; black head and breast spotted white, olive-brown back boldly spotted black, and a black tail. Underparts blackish-scaled; stout black bill, dark legs.
bsrcha1: Robin-chat with a slate-blue crown and shoulders, blackish face mask, and a white supercilium. Orange-rufous throat, breast and belly, olive-brown back, and a rufous tail with a dark central stripe. Black bill, dark legs.
bssowl: Medium screech-owl with no ear tufts and bare, unfeathered greyish legs. Greyish-brown plumage finely vermiculated above, paler buff below with dark streaks; rounded head, pale buff facial disc rimmed dark, and large dark eyes. Bill greenish-yellow.
bswdov1: Small dove with grey-blue head, pinkish-buff breast and rufous wings marked with glossy metallic blue-green wing spots. Two dark bars across the lower back; black bill, purplish-red base, and reddish legs.
btbeat1: Slender green bee-eater with rufous-washed throat, blue-green underparts and a long blue tail with elongated central streamers. Black mask through eye, yellow-and-chestnut throat; long decurved black bill and dark legs.
btbeat2: Slender bee-eater with chestnut crown and upper back, green wings and body, and a sky-blue rump and long blue tail with central streamers. Black eye-mask and blue throat; long decurved black bill.
btbsun2: Large dark sunbird; male blackish overall with iridescent metallic blue-green crown, shoulders and throat patch contrasting with sooty body. Long decurved black bill and dark legs; females duller olive with yellowish belly.
btbwar: Small warbler; male deep blue above, with black face, throat and flanks and clean white underparts, plus a small white wing patch (handkerchief). Thin pointed black bill; dark legs. Female plain olive-brown with white wing spot.
btfgle1: Plump rufous-brown ovenbird, warm brown above and on wings with a contrasting ochre-buff throat and pale buff eye-ring and lores. Underparts dull brownish; stout straight pale-based bill and grey legs; longish rufous tail.
bthhue1: Large blackish-slate tapaculo-like bird with a rich rufous lower belly, vent and flanks. Glossy dark head and breast, stout body, short cocked tail; strong black bill and pinkish-grey legs and large feet.
btmjay: Large striking jay with very long graduated blue-and-white tail. Blue above, white below, black face, throat and breast band, and a tall recurved black crest. Stout black bill and black legs.
btnhen1: Dark stocky rail-like crake; slate-grey head and breast, brownish-olive back, with a black tail and white flank flecks. Bright pea-green-and-red bill, red eye, and pinkish-red legs.
btnwar: Bright warbler with olive-green crown and back, yellow face framing a dark eye-line and ear patch, black throat and breast, and white belly with black streaks on flanks. Two white wing bars; thin black bill.
btpphe1: Medium dull-brown peacock-pheasant lacking the ocelli of relatives; finely vermiculated greyish-brown plumage with a distinctive bronzy-coppery long tail. Short crest, bare reddish facial skin; dark bill and greyish legs.
btsfin1: Streaky sierra-finch; male grey-brown above heavily dark-streaked, with whitish underparts and a broad black tail band edged white. Conical yellowish bill and pale legs; female browner and more streaked.
btstan1: Striking tanager; male glossy black overall with a bright orange-yellow rump and lower back patch. Heavy hooked black bill and dark legs. Female olive-yellow with greyish head, brighter yellow rump.
btther1: Large heron with a heavily barred blackish-and-buff body and finely vermiculated neck. Bare yellow throat skin, black crown, slaty face; stout yellowish dagger bill and greenish-yellow legs. Juveniles boldly barred tawny.
btttyr1: Tiny tody-tyrant; olive-green above with a grey crown, a bold white eye-ring on a dusky face, and a black throat and breast streaked white. Yellowish belly; short flat black bill and pale legs.
btttyr2: Tiny tody-tyrant; olive-green above, greyer over the crown, with a cinnamon-buff face, throat and upper breast faintly grey-streaked, and an indistinct buffy eye-ring. Pale yellowish belly; short flat bill.
btwbab1: Skulking dark wren-babbler; rich brown above, with a black throat and breast and white-scaled or spotted face and underparts. Short tail, rounded wings; longish dark bill and sturdy pinkish legs.
btweye1: Small black-and-white flycatcher-like bird; glossy black above and on head, with white underparts and a black breast band. Bold scarlet wattle (eye-ring) and red eye; black bill and legs. Female greyer-throated.
btweye2: Small wattle-eye; glossy blue-black above and on crown with a brown throat patch on white underparts and a black breast band. Bright scarlet fleshy eye-wattle and red eye; black bill and legs.
btywar: Crisp grey warbler; grey above with black streaks, white below with bold black streaking on flanks, and a black-and-white face with a tiny yellow spot before the eye. Black crown and throat; two white wing bars; thin black bill.
bubbab1: Plain buff-brown babbler; warm olive-brown above with rufous-tinged wings and tail, and pale buff underparts with a whitish throat. Faint buff supercilium; slender pale bill and pinkish legs.
bubcor1: Small finch; male soft fawn-brown above with sky-blue face, throat, breast and flanks and a buffy belly. Lacks cheek spot of relatives; reddish bill and pinkish-grey legs. Female duller brown with bluish wash.
bubcur1: Large black curassow; male glossy blue-black with a curly black crest and white belly and vent. Prominent swollen sky-blue bill knob and cere; dark legs. Female similar with barred crest.
bubear2: Slender earthcreeper; dull greyish-brown above, paler buff below with a buff throat. Faint pale supercilium; long slightly decurved dark bill, longish tail, and greyish legs; rufous wing patch in flight.
bubfly: Small Empidonax flycatcher; greyish-olive above with bold buffy-cinnamon wash on the breast and flanks. Conspicuous white eye-ring and two buff wing bars; small bill with dark upper and pale orange lower mandible.
bubgro1: Stocky grosbeak; male entirely deep blue-black, slightly brighter blue on the forehead, brow and shoulders. Very heavy blackish conical bill and dark legs. Female plain rich brown.
bubgro2: Stocky grosbeak; male deep blue-black with brighter cobalt-blue highlights on forehead, eyebrow, shoulders and rump. Massive blackish conical bill; dark legs. Female warm uniform brown.
bubhum: Medium hummingbird; metallic golden-green crown, back and throat, with a buff-cinnamon belly and rufous-edged tail. Slightly decurved bill, pinkish-red with dark tip; dark legs.
bubmot3: Striking mountain tanager; blue back, wings and tail, black head and a yellow belly, separated by a buffy breast band. Distinctive spangled metallic-blue eyebrow stripe; stout black bill and dark legs.
bubpak1: Spectacular kingfisher; blue crown, back and wings, black mask, and rich buff underparts, with a white back stripe and very long white central tail streamers. Bright red bill and red legs.
bubpuf1: Chunky puffbird; blackish-brown above and on the broad head with a white collar and white throat, and a buffy-cinnamon belly with a dark breast band. Heavy black hooked bill; dark legs.
bubrai1: Boldly marked rail; brown back streaked black, grey breast finely barred black and white, a rufous breast band and chestnut crown with a grey eyebrow and rufous eye-stripe. Pinkish bill with reddish base; pinkish legs.
bubsan: Elegant slim shorebird; scaly buff-brown back, plain buffy face and underparts, and a small rounded head with a dark beady eye. Short straight black bill; bright yellow-ochre legs; lacks wing bar.
bubtan2: Colourful tanager; silvery turquoise-green body with a rufous-orange crown and nape, a black mask and a black band across the back and breast. Yellowish-buff belly wash; black bill and grey legs.
bubtyr1: Small tyrannulet; greenish-olive above with grey crown, thin white supercilium and dark eye-line, and an ochraceous rump. Two pale buff wing bars and buff flight-feather edges; greyish-white underparts; small bill.
bubwar1: Tiny leaf-warbler; bright olive-green above with a yellowish rump and a bold yellow-orange wing bar (and a fainter second bar). Yellow supercilium and dark eye-line, pale yellowish underparts; thin bill and pale legs.
bubwar2: Plain tiny warbler; greyish-brown above and pale buff below, with white outer tail feathers and no distinct eyebrow. Nondescript and dull-coloured; pinkish bill and pink legs.
bubwre1: Warm-toned wren; rufous-brown above, white throat and breast with a bold black-and-white streaked face and white eyebrow, and buffy flanks. Slightly decurved dark bill; pinkish-grey legs; barred tail.
bucgre1: Small greenlet; olive-green above with a greyish crown, a buff-cheeked face and buff-washed throat and breast. Pale eye-ring, whitish belly; short stubby pale bill and grey legs.
buchor1: Dark hornbill; sooty blackish-brown body with a white-tipped tail and a shaggy bushy crest. Pale yellowish-horn bill with a low ridge, blue bare eye-skin and pinkish throat skin; dark legs.
bucifr1: Small babbler-like bird; warm buffy-brown body with a bold blue cap (crown) bordered black. Black streaks behind eye; short tail, sturdy build; dark bill and legs.
bucjac1: Slender jacamar; glittering metallic golden-green above and across the breast with a blue-green cheek patch, white throat and rufous belly. Long needle-like black bill; tiny dark feet.
bucjay1: Dark jay; deep blackish-blue body with a black face, throat and breast and a slightly bushy crest. Pale blue eye in some; stout black bill and black legs.
bucmot1: Turquoise-crowned motmot; green back and wings, rufous-brown underparts and a blue crown bordered black, with a black mask and a black breast spot. Long tail with bare-shafted blue racquet tips; black bill.
bucmot2: Motmot with a bright turquoise-blue brow above a black mask, green upperparts, cinnamon-rufous underparts and a black breast spot. Blue racquet-tipped pendulum tail; stout serrated black bill.
bucmot3: Motmot with green upperparts, warm rufous-buff underparts and a blue-bordered black crown and black mask, with a black breast spot. Long blue racquet-tipped tail; heavy black bill.
bucmot4: Motmot with green back and wings, rufous-buff underparts and a turquoise-and-black crown with a black mask, plus a black breast spot. Long pendulum tail ending in bare-shafted blue racquets; stout black bill.
bucnig: Cryptic nightjar; mottled greyish-brown plumage finely vermiculated, with a buff collar across the hindneck. Buff-spotted scapulars and white throat patch; small dark bill, large dark eyes, short legs.
bucpar: Medium green parakeet; mostly bright green with a dull blue wash across the forehead, crown and face. Long pointed tail, reddish underside; horn-coloured bill and pale eye-ring; greyish legs.
bucsap1: Small glittering hummingbird with shining bronze-green upperparts and underparts; male shows an iridescent blue chin and throat patch. Tail is bluish-black, slightly forked. Straight bill is mostly red with a black tip. Compact body.
buebar1: Plump green barbet with a green body and a striking blue face, blue throat and blue ear patch bordered by a small red spot behind the eye and red marks on the throat sides. Stout pale bill ringed with bristles; short tail. Greenish legs.
buebar2: Small stocky green barbet with a black head and throat, a blue ear patch and red spots on the neck sides. Body green; heavy pale ivory bill with bristles at the base. Short tail; greyish legs.
buffal1: Slim long-legged forest falcon with slaty-black upperparts and a narrow white half-collar on the hindneck. Throat and underparts clean white and unmarked. Long black tail with three to four narrow white bars. Dull greenish facial skin and cere; yellowish legs.
buffle: Small compact diving duck; male has a large rounded white wedge wrapping behind the eye across an iridescent green-and-purple black head, bright white breast and flanks, black back. Short grey bill; pinkish legs. Female grey-brown with a small oval white cheek spot.
bufhum1: Plain medium hummingbird with bronzy-green upperparts and dingy buffy-white underparts, lacking bright iridescence. Tail greenish to dusky. Straight blackish bill with pinkish base to the lower mandible. Dull legs; rather drab overall.
buflau1: Warm buffy-brown laughingthrush with rich rufous-buff body, greyer crown and nape, and a plain face. Slightly olive-tinged wings and tail. Stout dark bill; sturdy pinkish-brown legs. Medium-sized, rounded babbler.
bufowl1: Small owl with rich rufous-brown upperparts, a plain rounded head without ear tufts, and a buff-orange facial disc. Forehead and eyebrows buffy; underparts buff with faint streaking. Large dark eyes; pale bill; feathered legs. Compact.
bufowl2: Large owl with shaggy buff-brown plumage finely streaked dark below, rufous-buff underparts with fine dark shaft streaks, and prominent outward-angled ear tufts. Facial disc poorly defined. Bare yellowish legs; dark eyes; dark bill.
bufpar: Large green Amazon parrot with a turquoise-blue forehead and forecrown grading to yellow on the crown and face, often with red and yellow in the wing. Body bright green; red wing speculum and bend of wing. Horn-coloured bill; grey legs.
bufpar1: Small stocky short-tailed parrotlet, mostly green with a blue forehead and forecrown, and a contrasting yellow underwing and red-and-black tail patch visible in flight. Pale horn bill; pinkish-grey legs. Chunky body.
bufsee1: Sturdy large-billed seedeater, olive-brown above with a buffy-ochre forehead, throat and breast contrasting with paler belly; faint dusky streaking on flanks. Heavy conical pale bill; dark legs. Robust, big-headed finch.
buftuf1: Plump rufous-brown ovenbird with warm rufous wings and tail, a streaked dark cap, and a bushy buff-white tufted cheek patch and throat contrasting with streaked brown breast. Slightly decurved dark bill; grey legs.
buggna: Tiny slim songbird, blue-grey above and whitish below, with a long black tail edged white that is often cocked. Bold white eye-ring; black forehead line in breeding male. Thin black bill; black legs. Very small and delicate.
bugnod: Small pale blue-grey tern (noddy) with overall soft pearly blue-grey plumage, a whitish face and forehead, and a dark mark before the eye. Short slightly forked tail. Black bill; black legs. Compact, pale seabird.
bugrob1: Medium robin (Australasian) with slaty blue-grey upperparts, head and breast, and a clean white belly. Plain unmarked face; subtle white wing markings. Short black bill; dark legs. Upright, rounded posture.
bugsta1: Large long-tailed glossy starling, uniformly deep iridescent violet-blue with a very long graduated tail. Plumage gleams blue to purple in good light. Black bill; black legs. Striking large, slender starling.
bugtan: Medium tanager, pale blue-grey overall with brighter sky-blue wings and tail; head and underparts soft grey-blue, paler on belly. Short stout dark bill; dark legs. Even-toned, smooth blue-grey appearance.
buhcou1: Large heavy coucal with glossy blue-black body and wings, and a contrasting buffy-cream head, neck and breast. Long broad blackish tail. Stout black decurved bill; red eyes; dark legs. Big, long-tailed.
buhmac1: Medium macaw, mostly green with a blue head, neck and breast, blue wings and tail, and bright golden-yellow underwing and undertail. Bare greyish facial skin; pale to dark grey bill; dusky legs. Slender, long-tailed.
buhshr1: Stocky shrike with a rich rufous-chestnut crown and nape, grey back, and a bold black mask through the eye. Underparts whitish washed buff with fine barring on flanks; white wing patch. Hooked black bill; dark legs.
buhvir: Compact vireo with a blue-grey head set off by bold white spectacles (eye-ring plus loral line), olive-green back, and two white wing bars. Whitish underparts with yellow-olive flanks. Stout dark bill; grey legs.
bukwoo1: Large stocky woodcock, rich reddish-brown above intricately barred and vermiculated black with buff mottling; bold blackish bars across the crown and a dark eye-stripe. Underparts paler buff with broad dusky barring. Very long straight pinkish bill; short legs.
bulori: Bright orange oriole with a black crown, black eyeline and throat patch, orange face and underparts, and a large white wing patch on black wings. Black back markings; orange rump. Pointed grey bill; grey legs.
bulshe: Slender grey-and-white shearwater with pale grey upperparts crossed by a bold blackish open-M pattern across the wings and back, white underparts, and a dark cap. Long wedge-shaped dark tail. Slim dark bill; pinkish legs.
bumhum: Tiny hummingbird, bronze-green above with cinnamon-rufous flanks and tail; male has a glittering rose-magenta gorget. Underparts whitish centrally. Short straight black bill; minute body, one of the smallest hummingbirds.
bunibi1: Large ibis with grey upperparts and breast, a buffy-cinnamon neck and crown, a white face, and a black belly and flight feathers. Bushy buff-white throat. Long decurved reddish bill; reddish legs. Bare reddish facial skin.
bunwoo1: Small barred woodpecker, blackish above heavily cross-barred with buff, with a buffy-white neck patch and a buff-streaked face and breast; black malar stripe. Underparts barred buff and dark. Slightly decurved dark bill; grey legs.
burowl: Small long-legged ground owl, sandy brown spotted and barred white above, whitish below barred brown; rounded head with no ear tufts, bold white eyebrows and bright yellow eyes. Pale bill; conspicuously long bare greyish legs.
burpar: Large long-tailed parakeet, mostly dull olive-brown to grey-brown with a bronzy-green wash, a yellow belly patch with a red center, and blue in the wings and tail. Bare whitish eye-ring; pale horn bill; pink legs.
burshr1: Slim shrike with a grey crown and nape, rufous-chestnut back, rump and tail, and a broad black mask. Underparts whitish washed pinkish-buff on flanks. Small white wing patch. Hooked black bill; dark legs.
burtho1: Tiny dull olive-grey thornbill with a finely scaled forehead, pale buff-white underparts with faint streaking on the throat and breast, and a contrasting buff-cream rump. Pale eye; short dark bill; dark legs.
burwar1: Small olive warbler with olive-brown upperparts and a contrasting bright buff-cinnamon rump and tail base; underparts whitish with buff wash. Pale supercilium and dark eyeline. Slim dark bill; pinkish legs; often pumps tail.
burwoo1: Small barred woodpecker, blackish above densely barred buff with a buffy nape band, a streaked buff face and breast and a black malar stripe; underparts barred. Distinctive buff-cinnamon rump. Slightly decurved dark bill; grey legs.
busbla1: Plump warbler-like bird, plain olive-grey above with a contrasting glossy black cap covering the crown and ear-coverts. Underparts pale grey, whiter on throat and belly. Dark eye; thin black bill; dark legs.
busflu1: Small secretive rail with a rich chestnut-rufous head, neck and breast, and dark blackish-brown upperparts boldly spotted and barred with white and buff. Short cocked tail; whitish belly. Short dark bill; pinkish-brown legs.
bushti: Tiny long-tailed grey songbird, plain brownish-grey above and paler grey below with a brown-tinged cap or cheeks. Very long narrow tail; large head, stubby tiny bill. Dark legs; pale eye in females. Diminutive and round.
buspet1: Small sparrow, streaked grey-brown above and pale grey-buff below, with a plain face and a small yellow throat spot. Pale wing bars; finch-like build. Stout pale horn bill; pinkish legs. Sandy, sparrow-like overall.
butapa1: Small slender warbler-like apalis with grey to olive upperparts, a buffy-orange throat and breast, and a long graduated tail often cocked. Pale belly; dark face. Fine black bill; pinkish legs. Long-tailed, dainty.
butcor1: Glittering green hummingbird (coronet) with shining green head and back, green throat, and a contrasting rich buff-cinnamon belly and undertail. Tail bronzy-green. Short straight black bill; dark legs; chunky body.
butfly1: Small flycatcher; male has bright blue upperparts, head and tail, a rufous-orange throat and breast sharply set off, and a white belly. Slim black bill; dark legs. Female duller brownish with buff throat.
butfly2: Small flycatcher closely resembling Blue-throated; male deep blue above with a blue (not orange) throat blending to an orange breast and white belly; blue forehead brighter. Black bill; dark legs. Compact, upright.
butfog4: Plump rufescent-brown foliage-gleaner, warm brown above with rufous wings and tail, a buff throat and finely streaked buffy breast grading to plain brown underparts. Pale eyering; stout straight dark bill; greyish legs.
buthum: Large hummingbird, bronzy-green above with grey underparts; male has a brilliant blue throat patch and a bold white stripe behind the eye and another below the cheek, framing a dark face. Blackish tail with white corners. Straight black bill.
butkne1: Large long-legged ground bird, mottled and streaked grey-brown above and pale below with bold dark streaking; a plain face with a large staring yellow eye and a white brow. Stout blackish bill; long greenish-grey legs.
butpig1: Large dark guan, glossy blackish overall with white-streaked neck and breast, a small bushy crest, and bare bright blue facial skin with a blue-and-red dewlap on the throat. Bluish-grey bill; red legs. Long-tailed.
butpur1: Tiny stocky cotinga-like bird, soft grey above with a contrasting buffy-white throat and white tufts on the flanks; pale grey underparts. Big-headed, short-tailed. Short broad black bill; dark legs. Diminutive.
butsal1: Large heavy-billed saltator, olive-green above with a grey crown, a white throat bordered black, and a rich buff-cinnamon center to the belly. Greyish breast; white supraloral line. Thick black bill; dark legs.
buttro1: Trogon with a glossy green back, blue-green head and breast, a bright red belly, and a steely blue tail. Dark face; faint barring on wings. Yellow eye-ring; stout pale bill; dark legs. Stocky, square-tailed.
butwar1: Small drab leaf warbler, plain olive-brown above and buffy-yellow below with no wing bars; a dull buff supercilium and dark eyeline. Rounded head; thin dark bill with pale base; pinkish legs. Plain and uniform.
butwoo1: Large, sturdy woodcreeper with warm rufous-brown upperparts and bright rufous wings and tail. Crown, nape, neck and breast olive-brown boldly streaked with buff teardrop spots; throat plain buff. Long, slightly decurved pale grayish bill; gray legs.
buvbul1: Plain olive-brown bulbul with grayish-brown crown, dull olive-green upperparts and pale grayish-white underparts faintly washed buff on the belly and undertail. Reddish-brown eye, dark slender bill and dark legs; nondescript and unmarked.
buwcin1: Slim brown furnariid with grayish-brown back, pale buff supercilium and whitish throat. Underparts pale grayish with faint streaking; flight feathers show a broad cinnamon-buff wing band visible in flight; dark tail. Thin dark bill, dark legs.
buwlea2: Vivid leaf-green leafbird; male has a black face mask and throat bordered by a turquoise-blue moustachial streak, with bright blue shoulder patch on wings. Yellowish-green crown tinge; female plainer green with bluish throat. Black bill, gray legs.
buwmac1: Medium green macaw with blue forecrown and flight feathers, dull red patch on lower back and belly, and orange-red lower-back/rump shading. Large bare whitish-yellow facial skin; horn-colored upper bill, dark lower; gray legs; long blue-and-green tail.
buwpar3: Tiny stub-tailed parrotlet, bright green overall and paler yellow-green below; male shows brilliant cobalt-blue rump, lower back and underwing coverts. Female all green lacking blue. Small pale horn bill; pinkish-gray legs.
buwsta1: Large highland hummingbird, mostly shining green; male has a brilliant blue crown, glittering green forehead and violet throat gorget, with a buff-cinnamon wing patch. Female greener with buffy underparts. Long straight black bill; white postocular spot.
buwtea: Small dabbling duck; breeding male slate-gray head with bold white vertical crescent before the eye, spotted brown body and pale buff-and-black rear. Both sexes show powder-blue forewing patch and green speculum in flight. Dark bill, yellowish legs.
buwwar: Small warbler with bright yellow underparts and crown, yellow-green back, and two whitish wing bars on blue-gray wings. Thin black eyeline through the eye on an otherwise yellow face. Pointed black bill; dark legs; slim, active.
bwfshr1: Small pied flycatcher-shrike; male glossy black crown, nape and upperparts contrasting with white underparts and a white wing patch forming a broad bar. Brownish-tinged back; white rump. Black bill and legs; upright shrike-like posture.
bwfshr2: Small flycatcher-shrike; male entirely glossy black above including crown, back and tail, with clean white underparts and a bold white wing bar. Differs from Bar-winged by black (not brown) back. Black bill and legs.
bwgdov1: Small dove with plain grayish-brown plumage, pinkish-gray head and underparts, and slate-gray upperparts. Distinctive blackish flight feathers show in flight; pale bluish-gray bare eyering. Short dark bill; pinkish legs.
bwmtan1: Striking mountain tanager with deep blue back, wings and tail, contrasting bright golden-yellow head, nape and underparts, and a black mask and bib around the face. Sky-blue shoulder patch on wing. Stout black bill; dark legs.
bwwbab1: Small rufous-brown wren-babbler with finely white-spotted throat and breast, warm rufous-brown upperparts and flanks, and pale buff-and-black barring on the wings and tail. Cocked tail. Slender dark bill; brownish legs.
bwwwre1: Small dark wood-wren; rich brown above with a blackish crown, bold white supercilium, and black-and-white streaked cheeks. Throat and breast white, flanks brownish; conspicuous white wing bars. Short cocked tail; thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
caaant1: Tiny gray antwren; male has a black crown and nape, long white supercilium, white lores and a short blackish streak behind the eye on a gray face. Gray upperparts with white scapular patch; black wings and tail edged white; whitish-gray underparts.
caacac1: Large rufous ovenbird with uniform bright cinnamon-rufous plumage, a shaggy bushy crest, heavy dark bill and a fairly long tail. Striking pale yellow eyes; legs grayish. Bold and unmistakable in its range.
caawoo1: Medium woodpecker with rufous-chestnut head, crest and flight feathers; buff back and underparts with black-and-buff barred wing coverts and a uniform black chest and tail. Male shows a red malar stripe. Pale bill; gray legs.
cabbun1: Boldly marked bunting; male has a black-and-white striped head, grayish nape, olive-brown streaked back, white throat and breast, and yellow lower underparts. Two faint wing bars. Conical dark bill; pinkish legs.
cabgre1: Plain greenbul with warm olive-brown upperparts, rufous-tinged wings and tail, and pale grayish throat grading to dull yellowish-olive underparts. Reddish-brown eye, slender dark bill and pinkish legs; unmarked and nondescript.
cabspi1: Small rufous-and-gray spinetail; warm rufous crown and wings, gray-brown back, and a contrasting black throat patch finely flecked white. Grayish face and underparts. Slender dark bill; longish graduated tail; pinkish-gray legs.
caclar1: Stocky lark with rich rufous-and-buff plumage heavily blotched and streaked black above; whitish underparts with bold dark streaking on the breast. Short crest, rufous wing panel. Stout pale bill; pale legs.
cacpar1: Medium parakeet with green back, nape and tail, pale brown forecrown, cheeks, throat and breast, and a yellow-orange belly and vent. Bluish flight feathers; bare whitish eyering. Horn-colored bill; gray legs.
cacwre: Large boldly marked wren; brown above heavily spotted white and black, with a broad white supercilium, dark crown, and white-spotted black-streaked breast forming a dense gorget. Buff flanks; barred wings and tail. Long slightly decurved bill.
caipar2: Stocky green parrot with a brownish-black head, fulvous-brown collar across the nape and breast, and a bare gray eyering. Body green with blue and black on the wings; yellowish-green belly. Pale horn bill; gray legs.
calgna: Tiny slate-gray gnatcatcher with darker crown; breeding male shows a black cap. Long black tail with mostly black undertail (limited white) flicked frequently. White eyering; thin black bill; black legs; underparts pale gray.
calgul: Medium-large gull; adult has medium-gray back and wings, white head and underparts, and black wingtips with white spots. Yellow bill with both a red and a black spot near the tip; dark eye; yellow legs.
calhum: Tiny short-tailed hummingbird; male has glittering green back and crown with a striking magenta-streaked gorget radiating over white throat. Female green above, buffy below with green-spotted throat. Short straight black bill; white below eye.
calqua: Plump gray-and-brown quail with a forward-curling black topknot plume; male has black face bordered white, gray breast, scaly golden belly patch, and brown crown. Brown back, scaled flanks streaked white. Short dark bill; gray legs.
calthr: Large plain grayish-brown thrasher with paler buff belly and rufous undertail coverts; faint dark malar stripe. Long, strongly decurved black bill and a long tail. Dark eye; sturdy dark legs; unstreaked underparts.
caltow: Plain warm grayish-brown towhee with a slightly rufous-tinged crown, buffy throat bordered by fine dark spots, and rich cinnamon-buff undertail coverts. Pale buff lores and eyering. Conical dark bill; pinkish legs.
camfli1: Large flicker with boldly black-barred buff back and wings, plain buff face and underparts, black crown, and a white-spotted black breast. Yellow throat/cheeks (varies); male has red malar. Black rump bar; long bill; gray legs.
camscw1: Skulking dark warbler, uniform dull rufous-brown above and below, slightly paler and grayer on the throat. Faint barring on undertail; broad rounded tail. Slender dark bill; pinkish-brown legs; plain and nondescript.
camtai1: Small slate-gray tailorbird with a rufous-orange crown extending from forehead to nape; off-white belly and slightly olive-tinged wings. Male shows a patchy black throat. Long often-cocked tail; slender dark bill; pinkish legs.
camtro1: Bold orange-and-black oriole; deep orange head, breast and underparts contrasting with a black throat, back, wings and tail. Orange shoulder patch and white wing edging. Pointed black bill; pale blue bare eyering; gray legs.
camwar: Small warbler; breeding male yellow below with heavy black streaking, chestnut cheek patch bordered yellow, dark crown, yellow-green back and a large white wing patch. Yellow rump and neck-side. Thin slightly decurved bill; dark legs.
cancan1: Slim brown canastero with grayish-brown streaked upperparts, a rufous wing patch, and pale grayish underparts with a small rufous-orange chin spot. Long graduated rufous-edged tail. Thin dark bill; pinkish legs.
cangro1: Large long-tailed ovenbird, rich rusty-brown above and grayish below, with a buff supercilium and a row of black spots along the sides of the white throat. Slightly decurved pale bill; sturdy grayish legs.
cantow: Plain pale grayish-brown towhee with a rufous crown, a small dark central breast spot, buffy throat, and cinnamon undertail coverts. Pale buff lores and eyering. Stout conical dark bill; pinkish legs; longish tail.
canvas: Large diving duck with a long sloping black bill and forehead profile; breeding male has a chestnut-red head and neck, black breast, and pale whitish-gray back and flanks. Black rear. Red eye; dark legs. Female brown.
canwar: Gray-backed warbler with bright yellow underparts crossed by a necklace of black streaks across the breast; male shows a black-streaked forecrown. Bold yellow spectacles (eyering plus loral line). White undertail; thin dark bill; pale legs.
canwre: Compact wren with rich rufous-brown upperparts finely spotted black and white, a contrasting bright white throat and upper breast, and rusty-cinnamon belly. Barred wings and tail. Long slender slightly decurved bill; brownish legs.
capbat10: Small stocky batis; male has a gray crown and back, black mask through the eye, white throat, and a broad black breast band on white underparts, with rufous flanks and a rufous wing panel. Bold yellow eye; black bill and legs.
capbul1: Dark sooty-brown bulbul with a slightly crested blackish head, conspicuous bold white eyering, and dull brown body grading to yellow undertail coverts. Slender black bill; dark legs; lacks the red eye-wattle.
capbun1: Bunting with a boldly black-and-white striped head, grayish nape and breast, rufous-brown streaked back, and chestnut wings. Whitish throat; pale gray belly washed buff. Conical bill (dark above, pale below); pinkish legs.
capcan1: Small canary with bright yellow face and underparts, a distinctive gray nape and hindcrown, and grayish-green streaked back. Grayish ear coverts; greenish-yellow forehead. Short conical pale bill; pinkish legs.
capcon1: Small conebill; male deep slaty-blue overall with a contrasting white forecrown and crown patch, blue-black mask, and buffy-cinnamon lower belly. Fine pointed black bill; dark legs; active and warbler-like.
capcro1: Slim long-billed warbler-like crombec, plain grayish-brown above and warm buffy-rufous below, with a faint pale supercilium. Very short tail giving stub-tailed look; long slender slightly decurved bill; pale legs.
