crekin1: Large pied kingfisher; black-and-white spangled upperparts and bushy crest, white underparts with dark breast band, barred flanks. Heavy black dagger bill, dark legs.
cremyn: Glossy black myna; all-black plumage with bushy frontal crest over the bill, white wing patch and white-tipped tail. Pale yellowish-ivory bill, orange-yellow legs.
creoro1: Large blackish oropendola; deep black body with chestnut rump and vent, bright yellow outer tail feathers, pale blue eye. Long ivory-yellow spike bill, dark legs.
creowl1: Medium owl; greyish-brown mottled plumage, dark facial disc, very long white-tipped ear tufts forming whitish brows over a dark face. Pale bill, feathered legs.
crepar1: Plump dark-green partridge; male glossy blue-black with a tall fan-shaped bright red crest, bare red face skin, black bill, red legs. Female green with grey head.
crepig1: Slim grey-brown pigeon with a thin black pointed crest; pinkish-grey body, iridescent bronze-green wing patches, black-barred wings. Dark bill, pinkish-red legs.
creque1: Brilliant trogon; male iridescent green head, back and breast with full rounded crest, vivid red belly, white undertail. Yellow bill, dark legs.
cresht1: Boldly marked bird; olive-green back, yellow underparts, black-and-white striped head with pointed black crest, black throat (male). Heavy hooked black bill, grey legs.
crespi1: Slim ovenbird; rufous wings and tail, brown back, greyish crown with slight crest, pale supercilium, buffy-whitish underparts. Thin dark bill, greyish legs.
cretre1: Slender swift; blue-grey above, paler below, long deeply forked tail, narrow pointed crest, chestnut ear-coverts. Tiny black bill, dark legs.
crfant1: Small round antpitta; olive-brown above, white face crescent below eye, dark-streaked whitish underparts, scalloped breast. Short dark bill, long pinkish legs.
crfbar1: Small green barbet; bright grass-green body, red forehead and throat, blue-grey ear patch and chin, yellow eye-ring. Stout dark bill, greenish-grey legs.
crfbar3: Small green barbet; green body, crimson-red forecrown, yellowish-orange face and throat, pale yellow eye-ring. Stout pale-based dark bill, greenish legs.
crfgle1: Rich rufous ovenbird; bright cinnamon-rufous head, underparts and rump, contrasting blackish-brown back and wings. Slender straight dark bill, yellowish legs.
crfpar: Green parakeet; mostly bright green with a red forecrown patch and scattered red marks on bend of wing and thighs, blue-tinged flight feathers. Pale horn bill, grey legs.
crgdov1: Small ground dove; sandy grey-brown body, pinkish breast, black-spotted wing coverts, chestnut primaries in flight. Distinctive yellow-and-black bill, pinkish legs.
crheag1: Very large, powerful eagle with dark grey-brown upperparts and a short bushy black-tipped crest. Underparts cream washed rufous, heavily blotched and barred black; thighs barred. Boldly barred black-and-white tail and broad rounded wings show rufous underwing-coverts. Heavy hooked grey bill, large yellow eyes, stout yellow feet with huge talons.
crhman1: Tiny compact manakin; male glossy black with a brilliant scarlet hood covering crown, nape and breast, an orange forehead, and bright yellow on the lower belly and flanks. Wings and tail black. Short black bill, dark legs, with bright orange-red eyes.
crhpar1: Small dark partridge with sooty blackish body and a vivid crimson-red head, throat and upper breast extending onto the nape. Belly blackish. Short stout black bill, red orbital skin, and reddish to dusky legs; rotund game-bird shape.
cricha1: Small slim chat; male with brilliant scarlet crown, breast band and rump, white throat and lower belly, and a black mask/lores. Back and wings grey-brown. Slender pointed dark bill, dark legs, longish tail.
crifin1: Slender long-tailed finch; male glowing crimson-red on face, throat, breast, flanks, rump and tail, with brown crown and back and a black belly speckled with small white spots. Stout reddish to pale bill, pale legs.
crilon1: Tiny pale prinia-like warbler, sandy grey-buff above with fine dark streaking on crown and back, whitish below. Very long graduated black-and-white spotted tail held cocked. Short fine bill, pale legs.
criros2: Medium-large parrot, mostly deep crimson-red with black-scalloped back feathers, bright blue cheeks, and blue shoulders, wings and tail. Pale horn bill, dark grey legs; long blue-edged tail.
crithr: Large slim grey-brown thrasher, plain greyish-brown overall lacking spots, with a deep rusty-chestnut undertail (crissum) and pale buff belly. Long strongly decurved black bill, yellow-orange eyes, dark legs, long graduated tail.
critop1: Large spectacular hummingbird; male iridescent crimson-and-gold body with a glittering golden-green throat, black head, and very long curved central tail streamers crossing beyond the green outer tail. Short straight blackish bill, dark feet.
crmwoo2: Medium woodpecker with black underparts boldly spotted and barred white, deep crimson back and mantle, and a crimson crown (male) or partly black crown. Yellowish to buff face, dark bill, grey legs.
crobab1: Small ground-dwelling vanga; olive-brown above, greyish below, with a striking face pattern of a black mask bordered by white supercilium and white moustachial streak, and a blue-grey throat (male). Slender straight blackish bill, pinkish legs.
crocht2: Small chat-tyrant, dark olive-brown above with a tawny-rufous to orange crown patch, blackish face, and pale buffy-yellow underparts. Two pale buff wing bars. Small black bill, dark legs.
crocis1: Plain large cisticola, rufous-brown above with diffuse darker streaking, plain buff to greyish below. Rufous wing panel and a long broad rufous tail tipped pale. Short bill, pinkish legs.
croeag1: Large dark eagle, uniformly slaty-grey to brownish-grey with a short bushy crest. Tail black with a single broad white band and white tip; underwing dark. Heavy hooked grey-black bill with yellow cere, yellow legs and feet.
crohor1: Medium hornbill, blackish-brown above and white below, with a brown head, white-tipped outer tail feathers, and red eyes. Long downcurved red-orange casqued bill; blackish legs.
crolap1: Medium plover-like lapwing, grey-brown above and on the breast, with a black crown encircled by a white ring (halo). White belly, black-tipped white tail. Red bill with black tip, long red legs.
croslf1: Medium dusky flycatcher, greyish-brown above and paler grey below, with a blackish crown concealing a golden-orange crest patch. Dusky face. Black bill, dark legs.
crowoo1: Small hummingbird; male with glittering violet-blue crown, throat and belly contrasting with brilliant green back and breast; deeply forked blue-black tail. Short straight black bill, dark feet.
crpowl: Tiny owl, rufous-brown to greyish above finely spotted, with a streaked breast and whitish belly with bold brown streaks. Pale-spotted crown and two black 'false eye' nape spots. Yellow eyes, yellow bill, feathered yellow feet.
crrtou1: Medium green toucanet, mostly bright green with a blue throat, chestnut undertail, and a vivid crimson-red rump. Large bill mostly maroon-red with a paler ridge; bluish facial skin, grey legs.
crseag1: Medium-large dark raptor; dark brown above, brown below finely spotted white, with a bushy black-and-white nuchal crest. Broad rounded wings; tail and flight feathers black with a broad white band. Bare yellow face and feet, hooked grey bill, yellow eyes.
crtwhe1: Small white-eye with olive-green upperparts, a contrasting blackish crown and face, white eye-ring, and a creamy-yellow throat and underparts. Fine pointed dark bill, pale legs.
crvbul1: Plain drab bulbul, olive-brown above and pale buffy-whitish below, lacking strong markings; some races show whitish or red eyes. Slim dark bill, dark legs.
crwcin1: Medium furnariid, dark brown above and whitish below, with a bold white supercilium, white throat, and a pale cream-buff wing panel visible in flight. Slightly decurved black bill, dark legs.
crwwoo1: Medium green woodpecker, olive-green above with crimson-red wings and a red crown and crest (male). Buffy face, scalloped or barred underparts. Pale grey bill, greenish-grey legs.
cryfof1: Medium forest-falcon, slaty-black above and clean white below, with a short rounded blackish tail crossed by a single narrow white band and white tip. Bare yellow-orange face and legs, hooked black bill.
crywar1: Small slender warbler, plain olive-green above and pale yellowish below, with a faint pale supercilium and indistinct face pattern. Fine pointed bill, pale legs; nondescript.
cssvir1: Stocky vireo with green upperparts, a bold yellow supercilium and yellow throat bordered by a broad chestnut breast band, and white lower underparts. Heavy hooked grey bill, blue-grey legs, red eyes.
cstdro1: Small glossy black drongo with blue-green sheen and a notably square-cut (not deeply forked) tail. Red eyes, stout black bill with rictal bristles, black legs.
cthhue1: Large dark tapaculo, blackish-grey overall with a rich chestnut throat and upper breast and chestnut on the forehead/face. Heavy build, longish cocked tail, blackish bill, strong pinkish legs.
cubbla: Medium all-black blackbird with a faint bluish-violet gloss, appearing plain dull black overall. Slender pointed black bill, black legs, dark eyes.
cubcro1: Large all-black crow with a slight purplish gloss; heavy black bill with a high arched culmen. Black legs; dark eyes, bristled nasal feathers.
cubgna1: Tiny gnatcatcher, blue-grey above and whitish below, with a distinctive bold black crescent curving behind the eye onto the ear-coverts. Long black-and-white tail held cocked; fine black bill, dark legs.
cubpar1: Medium green parrot scaled with black, a white forehead and crown, rosy-red throat and cheeks, and dull red belly patch (variable). Pale horn bill, pinkish legs; bluish in wings.
cubpew1: Small grey-brown flycatcher, olive-grey above and pale grey below, with a small whitish crescent behind the eye and an indistinct crest. Dark upper bill, orange lower mandible, dark legs.
cubree1: Small rufous-brown furnariid, warm brown above and buffy-whitish below, with a whitish throat and pale supercilium. Long slender strongly upcurved bill, pinkish legs.
cubscy1: Slim brown woodcreeper, rufous-brown overall with fine pale streaking on head and breast. Distinctive very long, slender, strongly decurved (sickle-shaped) pale bill; dark legs.
cubsol1: Slim grey-brown thrush-like bird, olive-grey above and pale grey below, with a thin white eye-ring and dark malar streak. Straight blackish bill, dark legs; white outer tail feathers.
cubthr: Large grey-brown thrasher, plain greyish-brown with diffuse rounded spotting on the breast, pale wing bars, and orange eyes. Long strongly decurved black bill, dark legs, long tail.
cubtod1: Tiny chunky tody, brilliant iridescent green above with a red throat patch, pink flanks, pale grey breast, and pale yellow-white belly with blue ear-spot. Long flat bill red below, black above; reddish legs.
cubtro1: Colourful trogon; metallic green back and crown, blue-and-green head, white breast band below a grey breast, and bright red belly. Distinctive blue-and-white scalloped notched tail. Red bill, dark eyes.
cubvir1: Plain vireo, greyish-olive above and pale yellowish-white below, with conspicuous pale lores and a whitish ring around a pale eye. Stout hooked grey bill, blue-grey legs.
cucara1: Medium toucan-like aracari, black above and yellow below with a red rump and red-and-black breast bands. Crown uniquely covered in curled, glossy black plastic-like feathers. Pale ivory-and-black bill, bare blue-green face.
cucjay1: Large jay with a tall curly recurved black crest, blackish-blue upperparts and breast, and a contrasting white belly, rump and tail tip. Stout black bill, black legs, pale eyes.
cuckoo1: Medium stocky bird; male slate-grey below with a glossy iridescent green-and-purple back and wings, large head, and big dark eyes. Short broad black bill, short feet; large rounded head and short legs.
cugwoo1: Medium green woodpecker, olive-green above with black-streaked yellowish underparts, a white face with black streaks, a red crown (male) and a red breast patch. Grey bill, greenish legs.
cunant1: Plump terrestrial antpitta, warm brown above and dull olive-brown below with faint paler streaking, plain face. Short blackish bill, long pinkish legs, very short tail; rotund upright posture.
cupowl1: Tiny owl, greyish-brown to rufous above with fine white spotting, whitish below with brown streaks. Crown finely spotted, two dark 'false eyes' on nape. Yellow eyes, yellow bill, feathered legs.
cutia1: Stocky colourful babbler; rich chestnut-rufous crown and upperparts, blue-grey face, white underparts boldly barred black on the flanks, and black-and-white barred wings. Short stout black bill, yellow legs, red eyes.
cwbros1: Medium rosefinch; male deep crimson-red on head, breast and rump with a whitish-pink supercilium, and dark brown streaked back and wings. Stubby conical pale bill, dark legs.
dabcin1: Medium-sized dark brown ovenbird with paler streaked throat, a long whitish supercilium, and dull rufous wing patches visible in flight. Tail dark with rufous outer feathers; bill slender and slightly decurved, blackish; legs dark.
dabcuc1: Slim cuckoo with grayish-brown upperparts, warm buffy-cinnamon underparts, and a black mask through the eye bordered by gray crown. Long graduated tail dark with bold white spots below; bill slightly decurved, dark above, yellow below.
dabhon1: Drab gray honeyeater with olive-gray upperparts, paler gray underparts, and a diffuse grayish ear patch. Slender, slightly decurved blackish bill; dark legs; faint yellowish wash on wing edges; overall plain with no strong markings.
dabros1: Plump finch; male deep crimson-pink overall with a darker brownish back and dusky-maroon breast, paler pink on forehead and rump. Stubby conical dark bill; brownish wings and tail; legs pinkish-brown. Female dull brown and streaked.
dabspi1: Small slender spinetail; grayish-brown above with a rufous crown and rufous wing panel, plain grayish-brown face. Whitish throat with a dark grayish breast; long pointed graduated tail. Slender dark bill; grayish legs.
daejun: Small sparrow with slate-gray hood, back, and breast contrasting sharply with a clean white belly, plus white outer tail feathers flashing in flight. Short pale pinkish conical bill; pinkish legs. (Slate-colored form; other races vary.)
dafbab1: Small babbler with a glossy black cap and forehead contrasting with warm rufous-brown upperparts and tail. Throat and underparts rufous-buff to whitish; staring pale cream or whitish eye. Short stout dark bill; legs pinkish-brown.
dahori1: Bright golden-yellow oriole with an all-black head, throat, and upper breast, yellow body, and black-and-yellow wings and tail. Stout pinkish-red bill; deep red eye; dark legs. Striking black hood sharply set off against yellow.
damroc1: Long-tailed terrestrial warbler-like bird with rufous-brown upperparts, a whitish supercilium, and heavily black-spotted white throat and breast. Underparts buff with rufous flanks and vent; longish graduated tail. Slender dark bill; pinkish legs.
dantai1: Small tailorbird with a rufous-chestnut crown, greenish-olive upperparts, and pale grayish-white underparts. Distinctive black-streaked throat and side of neck; long cocked tail. Slender slightly decurved bill, dark above, pale below; pinkish legs.
darbar1: Boldly patterned barbet with a black-and-white speckled face, yellow throat and breast crossed by a black breast band, and a black crown. Back spotted black-and-white; reddish vent; thick pale horn bill; reddish-yellow eye; grayish legs.
darnew1: Small plain flycatcher-like bird, dark sooty-brown to blackish overall, slightly paler grayer below, with a faint indistinct eye-ring. Short fine dark bill; dark legs; no wing bars or strong markings; uniform dusky appearance.
darnot1: Small ground-dwelling tinamou-like bird, buffy-brown overall heavily marked with dark blackish bars and streaks on the back and flanks. Pale buff throat and finely barred breast; short slightly decurved bill; yellowish legs; rounded plump body.
darpew1: Medium pewee, uniformly dark sooty-gray overall with a slightly darker head and crest, paler grayish throat and belly center. Upright posture; broad flat bill dark above, dull orange below; dark legs; faint pale wing edges.
darter2: Slender cormorant-like waterbird with a very long thin snake-like neck, dagger-shaped yellowish bill, and a glossy black body. A whitish stripe runs down the neck; rufous-brown crown and neck; silvery-streaked wing coverts; dark webbed feet.
darter4: Slim long-necked darter with a thin pointed pale bill and a long snake-like neck. Glossy blackish body with silvery-streaked wing coverts; a pale whitish stripe edged dark runs down the rufous-tinged neck. Dark webbed feet; long stiff tail.
darwoo1: Boldly marked woodpecker with black upperparts spotted white, a black-streaked yellowish-buff face and breast, and a crimson red lower belly and vent. Male has a red crown; black-and-white barred wings; dark chisel bill; grayish legs.
dasfly: Small dark flycatcher, sooty gray-brown above and on the head, with diffuse dark gray streaking on white underparts and dusky flanks. Short pale eye-ring; long primary projection; small flat dark bill with pale base; dark legs.
datori1: Slim oriole with a bright yellow body and an olive-yellow back, and a black throat (male). Black wings with yellow markings; yellow undertail; pinkish bill; deep red eye; gray legs. Crown olive, contrasting black bib distinctive.
datsee1: Small finch; male has a gray head and upperparts, a deep chestnut-rufous throat and breast, and a whitish belly. Short stubby conical pale gray bill; dark legs. Female plain buffy-brown above, paler buff below, unstreaked.
datthr1: Gray-brown thrush; male has plain gray-brown upperparts, a black face, throat, and breast forming a solid black bib, and a whitish belly. Bill yellow with dark tip; legs dull pinkish; pale eye-ring; grayish-buff underwing.
daujac1: Small jackdaw, glossy black with a pale silvery-gray nape, ear coverts, and breast, and a black face, throat, and wings. Creamy-white eye; black crown and forehead; short stout black bill; black legs. Pied appearance distinctive.
daured1: Striking redstart; male has a frosty gray crown and nape, a black face, back, and wings with a bold white wing patch, and bright orange-rufous breast, belly, and rump. Thin black bill; black legs; rufous tail with dark center.
dausta1: Small starling; male glossy with a pale grayish head and underparts, a dark glossy purple-black back, nape patch, and wings, and a buffy wing bar. Short pointed dark bill; dark legs; clean pale-and-dark contrasting plumage.
davlew1: Small bright leaf warbler, yellowish-green above and pale yellow-washed below, with a bright yellow supercilium, a pale crown stripe, and two yellowish wing bars (upper often faint). Bill dark above, orange below; legs yellowish.
dawtru1: Large rounded ground bird with a velvety-black head, neck, and breast, a dull dark gray-green back and wings, and a short tail. Soft glossy plumage; pale grayish bill; stout grayish legs; hunched upright posture.
dbipig1: Large pigeon with a pale gray head, neck, and breast, contrasting dark slate-gray to blackish back, wings, and tail. Chestnut-maroon belly and vent; reddish eye and orbital ring; dark bill with pale tip; reddish legs.
dbptyr1: Tiny tyrant with olive-green upperparts, a grayish head, and pale yellowish underparts faintly streaked on the breast. Two bold whitish-buff wing bars and double dark band across the wing; pale eye; tiny dark bill; pale legs.
dbwqua1: Plump dark wood-quail with a sooty-black back, head, and breast, contrasting a chestnut-rufous throat and a short crest. Bare red orbital skin around the eye; underparts dark; short stout dark bill; dull legs; rounded compact body.
debflo1: Small flowerpiercer; male brilliant deep glossy blue overall with contrasting jet-black wings, mask, and tail. Short upturned hook-tipped black bill; black legs; intense violet-blue body distinctive. Female duller blue-gray with greenish tones.
delpig1: Stocky dark pigeon; male glossy blackish with an iridescent bronze-green nape patch and two dark bands across the back/wing. Grayish head; reddish eye; dark bill; reddish-purple legs. Female browner with buff wing markings.
demcra1: Elegant slate-gray crane with a black head, neck, and long drooping black breast plumes, and a white tuft of feathers streaming back from behind the red eye. Long black tertials drape over the tail; pale gray-tipped bill; black legs.
demwir1: Tiny dark brown bird with a remarkably long, thin, wiry tail of just a few stiff filamentous feathers. Rufous-brown upperparts, a small rufous forecrown patch, and grayish-buff underparts; fine dark bill; pinkish legs.
descis1: Small pale sandy cisticola, buffy-gray above with bold black streaking on the crown and back, and plain pale buff-white underparts. Faint pale supercilium; short tail tipped buff with dark subterminal spots; fine bill; pinkish legs.
deslar1: Plump sandy-brown lark, plain pale grayish-buff above matching desert sand, with paler unstreaked buff-white underparts and a faintly streaked breast. Short stout pale horn-colored bill; rufous in wing and tail corners; pale legs.
desspa3: Pale desert sparrow; male sandy gray-buff overall with a clean black throat bib and black around the eye and bill, plus black-and-white wing markings. Pale gray crown; stout pale gray bill; pale pinkish legs. Female plain pale sandy.
dhbfin1: Brushfinch with a dusky-olive head and grayish nape, olive-green upperparts, and bright yellow underparts. Faint paler throat; lacks bold head stripes, appearing rather plain-headed; stout conical dark bill; grayish legs.
diaplo1: Small plover-like wader with a dark slate-gray head and breast, a bold white forehead band curving down behind the eye, and fine black-and-white barring across the breast and flanks. Rufous-chestnut nape; short reddish-pink bill; pink legs.
diatan1: Striking deep violet-blue tanager with a velvety-black face and throat and a brilliant pale blue to white crown patch (diadem) bordered by a black band. Stout dark conical bill; dark legs; rich blue body distinctive.
diatap1: Tiny dark slate-gray tapaculo with a short cocked tail, a silvery-gray forehead and supercilium set off by a darker mask, and variable rufous on the flanks and rump. Small dark bill; dark legs; rounded compact body.
diatap2: Tiny gray tapaculo with a short stubby cocked tail, dark gray upperparts, paler gray underparts, and buff flanks and vent barred with black. Browner above in females; small dark bill; dark legs; rounded plump body.
diatro1: Colorful trogon; male has a slate-gray head and breast, a violet-pink to red lower breast and belly, and a brownish-cinnamon back. Finely vermiculated whitish wing panel; pale blue orbital skin; pale bill; squared dark-and-white tail.
dickci: Stocky sparrow-like bird; male has a gray head with a yellow eyebrow and breast, a black V-shaped bib on the throat, and a chestnut shoulder patch. Streaked brown back; whitish belly; large pale conical bill; dull legs.
didcuc1: Small glossy cuckoo; male iridescent emerald-green above and white below with green barring on the flanks, a green eyestripe, and bold white spots and a white stripe through the green wing. Red eye; greenish bill; gray legs.
dimfan1: Small fantail with rufous-brown to grayish upperparts, a paler throat, and rufous-cinnamon underparts; some birds darker/grayer (dimorphic). Broad fanned tail edged paler; short fine dark bill; dark legs; rounded restless shape.
dindor2: Tiny tyrant flycatcher, yellowish-olive above with a rufous-tinged brownish cap and brownish ear coverts, and bright yellow throat and underparts. Two faint buff wing bars; slender black warbler-like bill; dark iris; blackish legs.
dltcuc1: Slender long-tailed cuckoo, dark slaty-gray to blackish above with a paler gray throat and breast and a finely barred grayish belly. Very long graduated dark tail tipped white; yellow eye-ring; dark bill; yellowish legs.
dobant2: Small antbird with a slate-gray head and breast, brownish back, and a white throat. Bold rows of black-bordered white spots across the wings and back (dot-backed); reddish eye; short black bill; pinkish-gray legs. Female browner.
dobfin1: Tiny grayish-brown finch with a white face bordered by two black bands across the throat and breast, and a black-and-white spotted black wing. Pale buff belly; white rump; stubby blue-gray conical bill; pinkish legs.
dobplo1: Small plover, sandy gray-brown above and white below, with two breast bands in breeding plumage: a narrow black upper band and a broader chestnut-rufous lower band. White forehead; short black bill; dull greenish-gray legs.
doccor: Large blackish waterbird with glossy bronze-black plumage, a long hook-tipped grey bill, and bright orange-yellow facial skin and throat pouch. Breeding adults show two small wispy black or white crests on the head. Turquoise eyes; black legs and fully webbed feet; long neck held in a kink.
docsee1: Small finch-like seedeater; male has grey head, back and breast, a white throat and submoustachial patch, a black breast band, and a white belly. Stubby conical grey bill, dark legs. Females are plain buffy-brown overall, paler below.
dofwoo1: Small olive-green woodpecker barred yellowish below; back and wings bronzy olive-green. Male has a red crown and nape over an olive forehead finely spotted with white dots; female lacks red on crown. Greyish bill, greenish-grey legs.
dohbus1: Striking stocky bushshrike, mostly bright green above with a vivid scarlet forehead and throat, a broad black breast band, and a lemon-yellow belly. Black tail; stout hooked black bill; dark legs. A rare morph replaces the red with yellow.
dolgul2: Stocky grey gull with a slaty-grey head, neck and underparts, darker grey back, and black wingtips. White rump and tail with a black subterminal tail band. Thick bright red bill and red legs; pale eye. Robust, short-legged build.
dollar1: Stocky, large-headed roller; dark blue-green overall with a blackish head, brighter turquoise-blue throat patch, and a bright coral-red bill. In flight shows large pale blue coin-shaped patches on the dark wings. Short red legs.
dorcht1: Small drab chat-tyrant, greyish-brown above with a dull rufous-tinged crown and a pale buffy supercilium. Underparts pale greyish-buff, often with cinnamon flanks; rufous edging on the wing feathers. Thin black bill; blackish legs.
dosfra2: Medium-large brown francolin finely streaked and vermiculated with chestnut, buff and black; pale buffy underparts streaked dark. Bare reddish skin around the eye, a pale supercilium, reddish bill, and red legs bearing two spurs on the male.
dotbar1: Bold barbet, glossy black above with a brilliant scarlet breast and rump, a black throat and belly, and a broad white wing patch. Massive pale ivory bill with two notches near the tip; bare red skin around the eye. Black legs.
dotkit1: Small slim raptor, slate-grey above and on the head with a whitish throat divided by a dark central stripe, and rufous-barred white underparts. Distinctive white fluffy undertail coverts; dark grey tail with pale bands. Yellow cere and legs.
dottan1: Tiny tanager; male is deep glossy blue-green spangled with small black dots over the head, breast and back, becoming plainer blue on the belly. Short black conical bill, dark legs. Female is duller greenish with faint spotting.
dowant1: Small slim antwren; male is black with a large white patch on the wing coverts and white-tipped wing feathers, plus a partly concealed white back patch. Female is grey above with rufous-buff underparts and the same white wing markings. Thin black bill.
dowcra1: Tiny crake with brown upperparts boldly streaked blackish and fine white spotting on the wing coverts. Underparts mottled grey and black with black-and-white barring on the belly and vent. Short greenish bill; dull legs.
dowwoo: Small woodpecker, black above with a white back stripe, black-and-white barred wings, and clean white underparts. Head boldly striped black and white; male has a small red nape patch. Short stubby black bill; outer tail feathers white with black bars.
drapri1: Small slim warbler-like prinia, olive-brown above with a faint pale supercilium and a long graduated tail often cocked. Underparts washed yellow, becoming brighter yellow on the belly. Fine blackish bill; pinkish legs.
drasee1: Plain small seedeater, uniformly grey-brown above and pale buffy-grey below with little patterning; faint pale wing bars. Stubby pale conical bill, dusky legs. Sexes alike and very nondescript.
drawhi1: Plain thick-billed whistler; greyish-brown above with a whitish throat, a soft grey chest band, and a whitish-to-buff belly. Stout dark bill, dark legs. Drab and unmarked overall, sexes similar.
drbpyt1: Tiny pygmy-tyrant, olive-green above with a greyish head and a striking pale or whitish iris giving a staring look. Throat and breast dull greyish, belly pale yellowish. Short thin dark bill; pale legs. Rounded, big-headed shape.
drwtyr1: Plain pale sandy-brown tyrant with slightly darker wings and tail, a whitish supercilium and pale lores, and a diffuse pale eye-ring. Underparts pale sandy-whitish. Broad black bill; black legs. Drab and unstreaked, sexes alike.
dstkne: Large long-legged plover-like bird, sandy grey-brown streaked dark above and on the breast, with whitish underparts. Bold double stripe over the eye: a black line and a white supercilium. Large yellow eye; black-tipped yellow bill; long yellowish legs.
dubpar1: Tiny stub-tailed green parrotlet; male is mostly green, slightly paler below, with a blue patch in the wing and a blue rump. Female lacks blue and is plainer green, yellowish-green on the face. Pale horn bill; pinkish legs.
dubtan1: Small olive bird, dusky greyish-olive overall with a darker face and a pale staring eye; underparts paler greyish-olive. Short pointed dark bill; dark legs. Dull and uniform, lacking bright markings.
ducatt1: Stout-billed attila, dull greyish-olive above with a plain greyish head, a whitish throat, and pale ochre-buff underparts. Rufous-tinged rump; hooked pale-based bill. Pale eye; greyish legs. Big-headed, upright posture.
duccoc1: Medium white cockatoo with all-white plumage and a short rounded white crest. Faint pinkish wash at the feather bases of the face; bluish bare eye-ring. Grey bill and grey legs. Stocky compact build.
ducfly: Small Myiarchus flycatcher, greyish-brown above with a slightly darker head, pale grey throat and breast, and a pale lemon-yellow belly. Faint pale wing bars; rufous edges in the wing and tail. Slim blackish bill; dark legs.
ducfly2: Small flycatcher, olive-brown above with a dusky head, a faint pale supercilium, an olive-washed breast, and a bright yellow belly. Short dark bill; dark legs. Plain-faced and compact.
ducfog1: Medium foliage-gleaner, warm brown above with a dusky cheek, a buffy supercilium and throat, and pale buff-streaked underparts on a brown wash. Rufous wings and tail; fairly long pale-based bill; dull legs.
ducgra2: Small dull grassquit, plain olive-brown above and slightly paler greyish-buff below with no obvious markings. Short conical dark bill; dusky legs. Drab and nondescript, sexes similar.
ducgre1: Tiny greenlet, olive-green above with a dusky greyish crown and face contrasting with a yellow throat and yellowish underparts. Short pale stubby bill; pale legs. Plain-faced, vireo-like.
ducwoo1: Slim woodcreeper, rufous-brown with a dark dusky-brown cap and nape contrasting with buff streaking on the head, neck and breast. Rufous wings and tail; fairly long slightly decurved pale bill; greyish legs.
duegih1: Very large robust honeyeater, entirely drab greyish-olive with an olive cast to the wings and tail. Long slender slightly decurved blackish-brown bill, with a small yellow moustachial wattle near the gape. Dark legs.
dueowl1: Large pale greyish-brown eagle-owl, finely vermiculated and faintly streaked, with prominent ear-tufts and a whitish facial disc. Dark eyes; pale grey-brown overall with whitish throat. Feathered legs; pale grey bill.
duftan1: Medium tanager, lead-grey above with an olive-yellow crown and belly and a blackish face; pale grey throat and olive-yellow breast and flanks. Striking pale staring eye; short dark bill; greyish legs.
dugant1: Small antwren; male is grey above with a black crown, a white supercilium, blackish-and-white streaked back, white-spotted wing coverts, and whitish underparts. Female is buffier on the head. Thin black bill; pale legs.
dugoro1: Large oropendola, dull dark olive-green overall shading to blackish on the wings, with chestnut-tinged lower back and undertail. Tail mostly yellow with a dark central pair. Long pale greenish-ivory bill; dark eye; dark legs.
duhpar: Small green parakeet with a dusky brownish-grey head and a pale bare white eye-ring. Body green, paler yellowish-green below, with bluish flight feathers. Dark horn-coloured bill; greyish legs.
dulgua1: Large dark guan, brownish-olive to blackish overall with fine white streaking on the neck and breast and a slight bushy crest. Bare reddish facial skin and a red dewlap; bluish-grey bill base. Dull reddish to dark legs.
dumant1: Medium short-tailed antbird; male is dark bluish-slate grey with a black throat patchily extending onto the chest, white-spotted wing coverts, and chestnut-tinged mantle, wings and tail. Bright red eye; female has a white-barred throat. Dark bill and legs.
dumant3: Medium short-tailed antbird; male has a slate-grey head and breast, black throat, brown belly and olive-brown upperparts, with bold white wing spots. Bright red eye. Female has a white-barred throat and buffy wing dots. Dark bill and legs.
dusant1: Slim dark antbird; male is uniform sooty slate-grey with thin white tips to the wing coverts and white-cornered tail. Female is plain rufous-brown above and rich ochre-buff below. Slender black bill; greyish legs.
dusbro1: Large bulky broadbill, sooty black overall with a small white patch on the upper back and fine pale barring on the throat. Pinkish-flesh eye; massive broad pale dull-pink bill. Stout-headed, big-billed silhouette.
duscrm1: Small stocky swallow, plain sooty grey-brown above and slightly paler greyish below with a dusky throat. Short squarish tail with small whitish spots near the tips. Tiny black bill; dark legs.
dusfly: Small Empidonax flycatcher, greyish-olive above with a thin whitish eye-ring, two pale wing bars, and pale greyish underparts washed olive on the breast and yellowish on the belly. Small dark bill with a paler base; dark legs.
dusful1: Small skulking babbler, warm brown above with a greyish-brown head, a faint pale eye-ring, and dull buffy-white underparts faintly washed brown on the flanks. Short dark bill; pinkish legs.
dusgro: Large dusky grouse, sooty blue-grey overall with finer mottling; male has a yellow-orange comb over the eye and inflatable neck patches of red or purplish skin set in white-based feathers. Dark squared tail with a pale grey terminal band; dark bill.
dusmoo1: Dark slaty-grey waterhen with a brownish-tinged back, a small red frontal shield and red-based yellow-tipped bill. White undertail coverts split by a dark line; faint white flank streaks. Yellowish-green legs with long toes.
dusmyz5: Small plain honeyeater, uniformly dull greyish-brown overall, slightly paler below, with a faint olive tinge to the wings. Slender slightly decurved dark bill; dark legs. Nondescript, lacking bright colour.
dusnig1: Cryptic dark nightjar, mottled blackish-brown, grey and cinnamon above with intricate vermiculations and a buff hindcollar. Buff throat patch; male shows small white spots in the wings and white tail corners. Tiny bill; large dark eyes; short legs.
duspar1: Stocky dark parrot, mostly dull blue-grey to brownish-grey with a maroon-red lower back and undertail coverts and a reddish-blue wing patch. Pale eye-ring; whitish-horn bill; greyish legs.
duspig2: Medium dark pigeon, slaty blue-grey overall with a purplish-vinous wash on the head and breast and a slightly darker back. Subtle pale eye-ring; dark bill; reddish-pink legs. Plain and uniform.
duspih1: Large uniformly dark slaty-grey cotinga with a slightly paler grey belly and a rather long tail; plumage plain without obvious markings. Stout dark bill, dark legs; robust, upright thrush-like build.
dusrob1: Plain dull greyish-brown Australian robin with paler buffy-grey underparts and a faint pale brow; whitish double wing bars and a small white patch on the folded wing. Dark bill and legs; sturdy, upright posture.
dusscr1: Stocky dark brownish-grey megapode with sooty-brown body, slightly bushy short crest, and bare reddish facial skin. Short rounded wings, strong dull-yellow to greyish legs and large feet; small head, robust chicken-like build.
dusspi1: Small drab rufous-brown spinetail with dull greyish-brown underparts, a plain dusky face, and rufous wings and tail. Long graduated frayed tail; thin dark bill, greyish legs; slender skulking build.
dussun2: Small slim sunbird; breeding male blackish-brown above with a dull violet sheen on rump and shoulders and a dusky throat shading to paler greyish underparts. Long thin decurved black bill, dark legs.
dustap1: Tiny dark slaty-grey tapaculo, almost uniformly dark with paler grey throat and faint brownish flanks; very short cocked tail. Short black bill, brownish legs; rounded skulking mouse-like body.
dusthr1: Medium thrush with grey-brown upperparts, warm rufous-red wash on flanks and underwing, and rufous-edged wings; whitish supercilium and submoustachial stripe framing dark-spotted face. Yellow-based dark bill, pinkish legs.
dusthr2: Medium thrush, dark brown above with bold blackish breast-band and flank spotting on whitish underparts; prominent white supercilium and rufous wing-panel. Yellow-based dark bill, pinkish-brown legs.
dustit2: Sooty blackish-grey tit, almost uniformly dark with slightly paler grey belly and pale-edged wing feathers giving faint wing markings; whitish eye in some birds. Short stout black bill, dark legs.
duswoo1: Smoky grey-brown woodswallow with darker grey wings showing a thin white leading edge, and a short dark tail tipped white. Pale blue-grey black-tipped bill, dark legs; plump short-tailed build.
dutant1: Small dark slaty-grey antbird with blackish wings spotted white, white-streaked breast, and a long blackish tail tipped and edged whitish. Thin dark bill, greyish legs; slender skulking shape.
dutant2: Plain dark slate-grey antshrike with a slightly paler grey throat and concealed white interscapular patch; uniform dusky plumage overall. Stout hooked black bill, greyish legs; sturdy compact build.
dutcan1: Drab greyish-brown canastero, dull grey-brown above with paler buffy-grey underparts and faint dusky throat; long blackish-brown graduated tail. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs; slim long-tailed build.
dutdov1: Medium dove, dark vinous-grey overall with a dusky face, blackish wing-patch spots, and a slate-grey tail with whitish corners; dark eye in pale-grey orbital skin. Dark bill, reddish legs.
dutfla1: Stocky olive flycatcher with rufous-brown crown and wings, dull olive-yellow underparts, and a broad flat bill; rufous flight feathers and rufous-edged tail. Pale lower mandible, greyish legs; large-headed build.
duther1: Small dull hermit hummingbird, bronzy-green above with greyish-buff underparts and a dusky cheek bordered by buff facial stripes; graduated tail with white-tipped central feathers. Long decurved bill, dark legs.
dwajay1: Small jay, deep blue overall with a contrasting black face and throat; slightly paler blue forecrown and underparts. Short black bill, black legs; compact small-billed body.
dwavir1: Small grey-headed vireo, grey-olive above with whitish underparts, a bold white spectacle (eye-ring plus loral line), and two faint pale wing bars. Short stout grey bill, bluish-grey legs.
dwawhi1: Small bird with a striking golden-yellow face and underparts, blue-grey crown and nape, olive-green back, and a black mask through the eye; dark wings. Short dark bill, greyish legs.
dwtman1: Tiny stub-tailed manakin, dull olive-green above and paler olive-yellow below with a plain face and pale eye; very short tail. Short blackish bill, pale legs; rounded compact body.
eaafie1: Sleek glossy black antbird (male) with a concealed white interscapular patch and a bright red eye; plumage otherwise uniform jet-black. Slender black bill, blackish legs; slim long-tailed build.
eacaka1: Small robin-like akalat, olive-brown above with bright orange face and breast fading to whitish belly; grey wash on sides of neck and flanks. Thin dark bill, pale pinkish legs; upright posture.
eaclar1: Stocky lark, boldly blackish-brown scaled and streaked above on a buff ground, with buffy-white streaked breast and plain whitish belly; short crest. Stout pale horn bill, pinkish legs; rounded heavy build.
eacsun1: Brilliant sunbird; breeding male scarlet-red on back, breast, and crown with a metallic green forecrown and moustache, grey belly, and slate wings. Long thin decurved black bill, dark legs.
eactan1: Stocky tanager-finch, olive-brown above with grey head, white throat bordered by a black malar line, and dull yellow-buff underparts; yellow lores and reddish eye. Heavy black bill, dark legs.
eacwar1: Greenish leaf-warbler, bright olive-green above and whitish below with a yellow vent; bold long yellowish supercilium, dark eyestripe, pale median crown-stripe, and one faint wing bar. Dark-tipped pale bill, pinkish legs.
eaosun1: Plain olive sunbird, dull olive-green above and slightly paler yellowish-olive below with a faint yellow pectoral tuft and no bright colours. Long thin decurved black bill, dark legs; slender build.
eaptyr1: Tiny round pygmy-tyrant, olive-green above with rufous-tinged ear-coverts and pale eye, whitish underparts faintly streaked olive on breast; very short tail. Tiny black bill, pale legs.
eardov1: Slim warm-brown dove with greyish head, black spots on wings, iridescent neck patch, and black 'ear' line below the eye; pointed tail with whitish corners. Dark bill, reddish legs.
earpoo1: Small greyish-brown nightjar finely mottled and vermiculated, with a whitish throat-band and pale spots; rounded wings and a white-cornered tail. Tiny dark bill, dark legs; large dark eyes, cryptic build.
earque: Large trogon-like quetzal; male iridescent green above and on breast with a bright red belly, blackish face, and a long broad white-and-blackish undertail; bushy ear-tufts. Stout dark bill, dark legs.
easbeg1: Olive-green greenbul with a shaggy 'bearded' yellowish-buff throat and chest, olive-grey crown, and greyish-olive underparts; rufous-tinged tail. Long heavy pinkish-horn bill, greyish legs; large bristly head.
easblu: Small thrush; male bright deep blue above and on head, with a rusty-orange throat and breast and white lower belly. Short straight black bill, dark legs; plump upright posture.
easbri1: Plain rufous-brown bristlebird with greyish-scaled face and breast, faintly scalloped grey-brown underparts, and a long broad rufous-brown tail. Short dark bill, greyish legs; rounded skulking ground-bird build.
easkin: Sleek black-and-white tyrant, blackish above with a dark head, pure white underparts, and a black tail broadly tipped white; concealed red crown-patch. Stout black bill, black legs; upright posture.
easmah1: Large slim harrier; male pale grey and white below with blackish wingtips, brown-streaked back and head, and a grey tail. Long wings and tail, yellow legs; hooked dark bill with yellow cere.
easmea: Streaky brown grassland icterid with bright yellow throat and belly crossed by a bold black V on the breast; brown-streaked flanks and white outer tail feathers. Long pointed pale bill, pinkish legs.
easmog2: Olive-green mountain greenbul with a contrasting blackish head and throat, olive-yellow underparts, and yellow-green wing and tail edgings. Slim dark bill, greyish legs; medium bulbul build.
easnic1: Robust olive-green bird with a heavy hooked bill, yellow-spotted wing-coverts, whitish-grey throat, and pale grey-and-yellow underparts; yellowish undertail. Strong blackish hooked bill, bluish-grey legs.
easowl1: Small eared owl, finely streaked and barred in grey-brown (or rufous morph) with prominent ear-tufts, a pale facial disc, and yellow eyes. Pale greenish-grey bill, feathered legs; compact stocky build.
easpho: Plain grey-brown flycatcher, darker on head, with whitish underparts washed pale olive on the breast and no eye-ring or wing bars. All-dark thin bill, black legs; upright, fairly long-tailed build.
easros1: Brightly multicoloured parrot with red head and breast, white cheek-patches, yellow-and-black scalloped back, yellow-green belly, and blue wings and tail. Pale horn bill, greyish legs; long-tailed build.
easspi1: Small slim honeyeater; male with rufous nape and breast-band, white throat, black-and-white face stripes, grey-brown back, and a long fine decurved black bill. Dark legs; white-cornered tail.
eastow: Large sparrow-like bird; male with black hood, back, and breast, rufous flanks, white belly, white wing-flashes, and a long black white-cornered tail; red eye. Stout black bill, pinkish legs.
easwah1: Olive-grey honeyeater with whitish underparts, a yellow bare-skin wattle behind the eye, and olive-yellow wing and tail edgings. Slim slightly decurved dark bill, greyish legs; medium slender build.
easwhi1: Dark olive-green bird with a black head and breast crossed by bold white cheek-patches and a short shaggy crest; olive-green back and white belly. Black bill, greyish legs; long-tailed build.
easwpw1: Cryptic grey-brown nightjar, finely mottled with a blackish-streaked crown and a narrow whitish throat-band; male shows white outer-tail tips. Tiny dark bill, dark legs; large dark eyes, flat-headed build.
eawpew: Dull grey-olive flycatcher with whitish underparts washed grey on the breast, two pale wing bars, and a peaked crown; long primary projection. Dark upper and orange-based lower mandible, dark legs.
eaywag: Slender wagtail; breeding male olive-green above with a bright yellow throat and underparts, grey-to-olive crown, and a whitish supercilium; long white-edged black tail. Thin dark bill, dark legs.
ecgdov1: Tiny ground dove, sandy grey-brown above with rufous wing-flashes and dark wing spots, plain pale grey-buff underparts, and a short dark tail. Dark bill, pinkish legs; small rounded build.
eclpar1: Striking large parrot with extreme sexual dimorphism; male is brilliant emerald-green with a candy-orange upper bill and red flanks/underwings, while the female is vivid scarlet-red with a deep blue belly and mantle and an all-black bill.
ecupic1: Tiny olive-brown piculet with a short stubby bill; barred whitish underparts, plain brownish upperparts, and a black crown spotted white (males with orange-red forecrown spots). Short stiff dark tail; grey legs.
ecupie1: Tiny short hummingbird, grass-green above with a greenish-brown crown and green-and-white speckled throat; bold broad white chest band and largely white underparts, with extensive white in the short tail. Straight black bill with yellowish base.
ecutap1: Small dark slaty-grey tapaculo, slightly paler grey below, with brownish flanks and faint barring on the rear flanks and undertail. Short cocked tail, blackish bill, and pale pinkish-brown legs; rounded skulking body shape.
ecuthr1: Plain warm brown thrush, uniform olive-brown above and paler buff-brown below fading to whitish belly; faintly streaked throat. Distinctive yellow-orange bill, narrow yellow eye-ring, and pinkish-yellow legs.
ecutyr1: Small slender flycatcher, olive-yellow with a grey crown, whitish eyebrow and a dark line through the eye, giving a messy face. Two pale yellow wing bars on dusky wings; whitish throat and yellow underparts. Thin black bill.
edcsun3: Small sunbird; breeding male has glittering metallic green head and back, a broad iridescent blue-green throat band bordered by a scarlet-red breast band, and dark belly. Female plain olive-grey above, yellowish-grey below. Slender decurved black bill.
elblar1: Large long-legged lark with a very long slightly decurved bill; richly streaked brown above, buff-white below with a streaked breast, prominent pale supercilium, and rufous wing panel. Rufous rump; long pinkish legs.
elctin1: Plump ground bird with a slender forward-curving wispy black crest; finely vermiculated grey-brown plumage densely spotted and barred white, a whitish throat and pale line behind the eye. Small head, short dark bill, stout grey legs.
elecre1: Boldly marked skulker; rich chestnut-rufous below with a black crescent-shaped breast band across a white throat, brown upperparts, and a striking white supercilium contrasting with a dark crown and ear-coverts. Longish cocked tail; dark bill.
eleeup1: Small stocky finch-like tanager; male glossy blue-black above with a sky-blue crown and nape, a rufous-chestnut forehead, and bright golden-yellow underparts. Female greener overall with bluish crown. Short stubby dark bill.
elepai: Small active monarch-flycatcher, rufous-brown above with a rusty breast and crown, white throat, dark mask, and white wing bars; white-tipped tail often cocked. Plumage variable across the Big Island. Small dark bill; dark legs.
elepai4: Small monarch-flycatcher endemic to Oahu, warm brown above with rufous tones on the crown, a whitish throat and belly, dark face mask, two white wing bars, and a white-tipped tail often held cocked. Small dark bill.
elepai5: Small monarch-flycatcher of Kauai, brown above with rufous-tinged crown, whitish underparts with rusty wash on breast, dark mask, white wing bars, and a white-tipped cocked tail. Small dark bill; dark legs.
elepit7: Stocky, short-tailed, colourful pitta; green back, buff underparts with a black belly patch and crimson vent, a buff supercilium over a broad black eye-stripe and crown sides. Turquoise wing patch and rump; stout dark bill, pinkish legs.
elequa: Small plump quail; soft blue-grey body finely scaled, with a thin upright dark crest, rufous flanks spotted white, and a pale buff face. Males greyer, females browner. Stout dark bill; grey legs.
eleter1: Slender pale tern with a shaggy black crest extending off the rear crown, pale grey upperparts, white underparts, and a long slightly drooping orange-yellow bill. Forehead whitens outside breeding; black legs; deeply forked tail.
eletit2: Small bold tit, glossy blue-black above with a tall pointed black crest, a broad yellow-buff nape patch and cheeks, yellow underparts, and white wing bars on dark wings. Black throat; short stubby black bill; grey legs.
eletro2: Long-tailed trogon; male metallic green head, back and breast separated from a bright rosy-red belly by a white breast band; finely vermiculated grey wing panel and a square white-tipped undertail. Stout yellow bill; female browner.
elewoo1: Slender brown woodcreeper with a long slightly decurved pale bill; warm rufous-brown plumage with fine buff streaking and spotting on the crown, nape, back and breast, and rufous wings and tail. Strong clinging feet; grey legs.
elfowl: Tiny round-bodied owl, grey-brown finely mottled and spotted white and buff, with pale whitish eyebrows, yellow eyes, and a short tail; lacks ear tufts. Stubby pale bill; pale legs; sparrow-sized.
elipig1: Large heavy pigeon, pale grey head and underparts grading to bluish-grey, with a glossy bronze-green and purple sheen on the back and wings, and a broad pale-tipped dark tail band. Reddish bill base; dark red legs.
elllau1: Medium laughingthrush, warm olive-brown overall with a grey crown and nape, fine pale streaking on the face, and a striking bright rufous-and-grey wing panel and rufous-tipped tail. Pale eye; dark bill; brownish legs.
elopar1: Small green parakeet, mostly bright grass-green with a red forecrown band above the bill, reddish shoulders and bend of wing, and a reddish-tinged tail underside. Pale horn bill; greyish legs; long pointed tail.
emedov2: Plump ground-dove with a grey-tinged pinkish head and breast, rich chestnut-brown back, and brilliant iridescent emerald-green wing patches; white shoulder patch and two dark rump bars. Red bill; reddish legs; male has whitish crown.
emedov3: Plump terrestrial dove, pinkish-brown head and breast, chestnut-brown back with shining emerald-green wing coverts, a whitish shoulder bar and dark double rump bands; longer-billed than Asian Emerald Dove. Red bill, reddish legs.
emetan1: Brilliant small tanager, vivid emerald-green overall with a golden-yellow crown, black face mask and lores, and black streaking on the back and wings. Underparts green washed yellow on belly. Short black bill; grey legs.
emlwar1: Small greenish leaf warbler, olive-green above with a paler grey-green crown, a long yellowish supercilium, dark eye-stripe, and two pale wing bars; whitish-grey underparts washed yellow. Dark-tipped pale lower bill; pale legs.
empfai1: Tiny fairywren; breeding male is largely deep velvety blue-black with a brilliant sky-blue crown, cheeks and back, and a long cocked black tail; female has reddish-buff face and rufous-tinged plumage. Black bill; long thin tail.
empgoo: Stocky pale goose with a silvery blue-grey body scaled black-and-white, a clean white head and hindneck, and an orange-yellow throat often stained rusty. Pinkish bill, bright orange-yellow legs; white tail.
epaori1: Slim glossy black oriole with a contrasting bright yellow or yellowish shoulder epaulet (lesser wing coverts) and yellow underwing; otherwise entirely black plumage. Slender pointed bluish-black bill; black legs.
epaori4: Slim oriole, glossy black with a variable rufous-chestnut to orange shoulder patch on the wing; rest of plumage black, including head, underparts and tail. Slender pointed dark bill; black legs.
equant1: Plump round antpitta with a short tail and long legs; uniform rich rufous-brown plumage, slightly paler and more rufous on the belly, with a plain face and a relatively large dark eye. Short stout dark bill; pinkish-grey legs.
equgra1: Tiny warbler-like furnariid, plain grey above and white below streaked grey on the breast and flanks, with a white supercilium and a chestnut-orange forecrown (often hard to see). Plain grey wings; graduated grey tail; thin dark bill.
ercfra: Large dark spurfowl with a chestnut-brown crown, finely black-and-white streaked face, neck and breast, and a dark brown body; bare red facial skin. Heavy pale bill; dull yellowish-grey legs with spurs.
erttyr1: Tiny tody-tyrant, olive-green above with a bold white eye-ring on a greyish face, dusky wings with two pale yellow wing bars, and pale yellowish-white underparts. Short flattish dark bill; pale legs; large-headed look.
esmant1: Small antbird with marked sexes; male dark slaty-grey, blacker on wings with white-tipped wing coverts and a hidden white scapular patch, and red eyes. Female brown above with ochre-brown underparts and stippled throat; red eyes.
eswdov1: Small slim dove, soft grey-brown above and pale pinkish-buff below, with two iridescent emerald-green wing spots, a whitish forehead, and dark double tail bands. Dark bill; reddish legs; long-bodied with a longish tail.
eulfly1: Small plain flycatcher, olive-brown above with two buffy wing bars and a whitish eye-ring, whitish throat, and pale grey-olive breast grading to yellowish belly. Dark upper bill with pale orange lower mandible; dark legs.
eurbla2: Glossy black thrush (Indian Blackbird); male all blackish with a bright orange-yellow bill and yellow eye-ring; female dark sooty-brown with a duller bill. Compact, with orange-yellow legs.
eurmag6: Large pied magpie (Black-rumped Magpie) with a glossy black head, breast and back, white belly and shoulder patches, and iridescent blue-green wings; differs from common magpie in a black (not white) rump. Long graduated black tail; black bill and legs.
eursco3: Small grey-brown scops owl with short ear tufts, finely vermiculated and streaked cryptic plumage, a pale facial disc, and pale scapular spots; warm rufous and grey tones. Yellow eyes; short pale bill; feathered legs.
eurtre3: Small slender treecreeper (Hodgson's), streaked brown above with a whitish supercilium and a rufous rump, and clean whitish underparts; long stiff pointed tail. Long thin decurved bill; pale brownish legs.
evegro: Large stocky finch; male golden-yellow body with a dark brown head, a bright yellow forehead band over the eye, blackish wings with a big white wing patch, and a black tail. Huge pale greenish-ivory conical bill; pinkish legs.
evweye1: Small olive-green white-eye with a conspicuous bold white eye-ring, yellow-green upperparts, a yellow throat and undertail, and greyish-white belly. Slender dark bill; greyish legs; rounded compact shape.
eyethr: Slim grey-brown thrush; grey head with a bold white supercilium and white throat patch, olive-brown upperparts, and orange-buff flanks and breast contrasting with a white belly. Dark-tipped yellow bill; pinkish-yellow legs.
eyrfla1: Plump flycatcher with a broad flat bill and large dark eye encircled by a bold whitish eye-ring; olive-green above with rufous-edged wings, and pale yellow-olive underparts. Broad pale-based dark bill; greyish legs.
eyrthi1: Small brown furnariid with a long wispy ragged tail; chestnut-brown crown, back and wings, a wide white eye-ring on a dark-lored face, an orange chin patch, and greyish-brown underparts. Thin dark bill; dark legs.
eywbab1: Tiny short-tailed wren-babbler, warm brown above heavily spotted and scaled buff and black, with a pale buff supercilium and dark eye-line, and whitish-buff underparts with dark scaling. Slender dark bill; pinkish legs; rounded skulking shape.
fabbri1: Medium plump hummingbird, glittering green above and on the throat, with a fawn-buff to cinnamon belly and breast; male shows a small violet-blue throat patch. Straight black bill; greenish flanks; dark forked tail.
fabtan1: Small tanager with deep violet-blue crown, mantle and wings, contrasting blackish back and face; a black mask bordered above by the pale-blue crown. Underparts warm fawn-buff to cinnamon. Short dark bill, dark legs.
fabwax1: Tiny waxbill with olive-brown back and wings, bright red rump and black tail; greyish to olive face and crown, with pale fawn-buff to whitish underparts. Conspicuous coral-red bill; dark legs.
fabwre1: Medium wren with rich rufous-brown upperparts, plain crown, bold white supercilium and dark eyeline; whitish throat grading to warm fawn-buff lower breast and flanks. Faintly barred wings and tail; slightly decurved dark bill, pinkish legs.
faclar8: Slender sandy-brown lark with rufous-toned wings, streaked back and crown, pale buff underparts with fine breast streaking; white supercilium and dark eyeline. Stout pinkish-horn bill, pale legs.
faecur: Very large curlew, entirely streaked buff-brown above and below with no white rump; pale belly with dark streaking. Extremely long, decurved dark bill (pinkish at base); long grey-blue legs.
faifly1: Tiny, slim flycatcher; blue-grey above, white below, with a black mask and white forehead and supercilium giving a striking face pattern. Black wings and long black tail with white panels and outer feathers. Fine black bill, blackish legs.
faiger1: Tiny warbler-like bird, olive-green above, with a yellow throat and breast and whitish belly; conspicuous white-and-black face pattern with white eyebrow and dark mask. Fine black bill, grey legs.
faimar2: Small swallow with rufous-chestnut cap and rump, white throat and underparts faintly streaked; blue-black back, dark wings and squarish tail. Short black bill, dark legs.
faipit1: Plump, brightly coloured pitta; green back and wings, buff underparts, a broad black eye-mask and buff crown stripes, bright red belly patch. Shows a turquoise-blue shoulder patch and rump; pale-tipped wings. Stout dark bill, pinkish legs.
falduc: Dabbling duck; drake has a glossy bottle-green and chestnut head with a long drooping nape crest, vermiculated grey body, and a black-and-white tertial pattern; creamy throat with green collar. Dark bill, grey legs.
famcha1: Small upright chat, plain grey-brown above and paler buff-brown below, with a tiny white wing flash and a small white eyebrow/lores spot. Rufous-orange rump and tail base with a dark terminal tail band. Black bill, black legs.
fasant1: Stocky antshrike, male finely barred black-and-white overall (zebra-like), with a black crown and a striking red eye; heavy hooked black bill. Grey legs.
faswre1: Large boldly patterned wren, brown above strongly barred black-and-white on wings, back and tail; whitish throat and breast with dark streaking, buff flanks; white supercilium and dark crown. Slightly decurved dark bill, grey legs.
fatcuc1: Slim cuckoo, slaty-grey above and on head and breast, with rich rufous-buff underparts; long graduated tail boldly barred and notched white below. Yellow eye-ring, dark bill, yellow legs.
fatger1: Tiny warbler-like bird, grey-olive above with bright yellow flanks and lower underparts, whitish throat and centre; faint pale eyebrow. Fine black bill, dark legs.
fatrav1: Glossy all-black raven with a notably short, wedge-shaped tail and long broad wings; heavy black bill. Plumage may show purplish gloss; black legs.
fatwar: Warbler with slate-grey upperparts and crown, bright yellow throat and underparts; bold white spectacles (split eye-ring) and a black mask. Yellow rump; fine dark bill, pale legs.
fawbab1: Medium wren-babbler, blackish hooded head and body boldly streaked white; rufous wings and tail with blackish tip, bright white throat with fine black malar stripe, orange nape, and distinctive long sickle-shaped plumes on the lower-back sides. Dark bill, pinkish legs.
fbfeye1: Glossy all-black antbird with a brilliant fiery-red eye; uniformly black plumage, stout black bill and dark legs.
fbtbab1: Dark babbler with chestnut-brown crown and nape, blackish face and breast, and a patch of pale-blue bare skin around the eye; dark fluffy back. Throat and breast blackish with fine pale streaking. Stout dark bill, pale legs.
febant1: Antbird with bright rufous-chestnut back, wings and tail, contrasting slate-grey head, throat and underparts; pale-blue bare orbital skin. Black bill, greyish legs.
fepowl: Tiny owl, rufous-brown to grey-brown above finely spotted, with two black false-eye spots on the nape; whitish underparts streaked rufous-brown, and a long barred tail. Streaked crown, yellow eyes, yellowish bill and feet.
ferant1: Small antbird, male slaty-grey with rufous-chestnut back, wings and rump; long dark tail. White interscapular patch often hidden. Dark bill, grey legs.
ferbab1: Plain babbler, warm rufous-brown above and on wings, paler buff-rufous below with whitish throat; rather featureless face with a faint pale eyering. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
ferfli1: Buffy woodpecker, golden-buff and brown barred above, with a streaked buff breast and barred belly; plain buffy face with a black malar stripe (male). Pale yellowish bill, grey legs.
ferfly1: Small flycatcher, warm rufous-brown above with brighter rufous rump and tail; pale throat and rufous-washed flanks, whitish belly; faint pale eyering. Dark bill, dark legs.
ferhaw: Large pale buteo, rusty-rufous above with pale head and underparts; rufous leg feathers forming a dark V against the white belly in flight, and pale rufous tail. Large yellow gape and bill base, feathered yellow legs.
fernbi1: Streaky brown songbird with rufous-brown crown, heavily dark-streaked buff-brown back and underparts, whitish throat with fine streaking; long, frayed, graduated tail. Fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
fernwr1: Small plump bird, olive-brown above, with a black-and-white streaked throat and breast and a bold white supercilium contrasting with dark face. Whitish belly; short dark bill, pinkish legs.
fespar1: Green parrot (amazon) with mostly bright-green plumage, a red forehead and lores, bluish wash on crown and cheeks, and a yellow-and-red patch on the throat/upper breast; red wing speculum. Horn-coloured bill, grey legs.
fhbfin1: Brushfinch with rich rufous-buff to tawny head, olive-green upperparts, and bright yellow underparts; dark lores. Stout dark bill, dark legs.
fibara1: Toucan-like aracari with black head, chestnut-and-red collar, green-black back, bright yellow breast crossed by a red band and black spot, and a red rump. Bill ivory above with a fiery orange-red lower mandible; blue-grey orbital skin, dark legs.
fibflo1: Tiny flowerpecker, male glossy blue-black above with a brilliant scarlet breast patch and a fine black line down the buff belly; whitish throat. Short dark bill, dark legs.
fibwar1: Small warbler, warm rufous-brown above with a rufous-tinged crown, plain buff-brown underparts and pale throat; faint pale eyebrow. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
ficale2: Robin-like bird, rich rufous-brown above with a slaty-grey crown bearing a concealed white central crest; white throat and breast, grey flanks. Dark bill, pale pinkish legs.
ficale3: Robin-like bird, olive-brown above with a grey crown showing a white crown patch; clean white throat and breast, grey flanks, and white outer tail. Dark bill, pinkish legs.
ficbar1: Compact barbet with black crown and back, bright red forehead/face, yellow rump, and white-spotted black wings; whitish underparts with a yellowish wash and red flank tones. Stout pale bill, grey legs.
ficman1: Tiny manakin, male olive-green above with a fiery red-and-yellow crown stripe, reddish-streaked breast over pale underparts; striped flanks. Short dark bill, pale legs.
fiediu1: Slaty-grey flycatcher-like bird, uniformly grey above and slightly paler below, with a striking fiery red-orange eye. Black bill, black legs.
fiespa: Small sparrow with rufous crown, grey face, plain greyish breast and buff flanks; rusty eyeline and a white eyering giving a gentle expression. Bright pink bill, pinkish legs.
fietop1: Large hummingbird, male glittering crimson-red overall with a golden-green throat and crown, dark wings, and very long curved black outer tail streamers. Black bill, dark legs.
fifser1: Small finch with a black head marked by a flame-red forehead, streaked olive-brown back, yellow-green rump and breast, and streaked whitish flanks. Short conical dark bill, dark legs.
fifthr1: Thrush-like bird, warm olive-brown to rufous-brown above with rufous tail; pale buff throat and grey-brown breast, paler belly. Dark bill, pinkish legs.
fijshr1: Robust monarch-flycatcher, warm brown above and buff-rufous below with a rufous-toned head and tail; pale throat. Heavy, slightly hooked dark bill, dark legs.
finbul1: Plain bulbul, olive-brown above with a greyish head, pale lemon-buff throat and underparts; rather featureless face with a dark eye. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
fineup1: Small finch-like euphonia, male glossy blue-black above and on throat with a yellow forehead/crown, and bright yellow underparts. Short stubby dark bill, dark legs.
finnig1: Cryptic nightjar, mottled grey-brown and rufous above; rich rufous-orange collar across the nape, blackish throat with a white patch, and barred buff underparts. White corners to wings and tail (male). Tiny dark bill, short legs.
fippar1: Tiny stub-tailed parrot, green above and yellowish-green below, with a buff-brown face and crown and bluish wash on the head. Pale tail-feather tips used for climbing; small grey bill, grey feet.
firecr3: Tiny kinglet, olive-green above with a black-bordered orange-and-yellow crown stripe; bold white supercilium, black eyeline and a bronze neck collar; two white wingbars. Whitish below; fine dark bill, dark legs.
fireth1: Robin-like chat, slaty blue-grey above with a brilliant fiery orange-red throat and breast bordered by black; grey flanks, white belly. Dark bill, pale legs.
firgat1: Slender brown ovenbird with whitish supercilium and short crest. Upperparts streaked grey-brown, throat white edged black, underparts dull whitish. Long graduated tail with whitish tip; dark bill, greyish legs.
fiscro: Glossy all-black crow, smaller and slimmer than American Crow, with relatively short bill and squared tail. Plumage uniformly black with faint blue-purple sheen; black bill, legs and feet.
fisfly1: Medium flycatcher. Male glossy blue-black above, white below, with bold white wing patch and white sides to base of tail. Female greyer-brown above. Black bill and legs; upright stance.
fisgre1: Plain olive-brown greenbul with paler greyish-white throat and grey-washed underparts, rufous-brown wings and tail contrasting with olive back. Pale eye, slender dark bill, pinkish legs; nondescript and rounded.
fislov1: Small stocky green lovebird with orange-red face and forecrown shading to olive-yellow neck and breast, green belly and wings, blue rump and tail. White eye-ring, red bill, grey legs.
fisspa: Greyish-brown sparrow with bold black-and-white striped face: white supercilium, black eyeline, white submoustachial, black malar. Plain grey breast, brownish flanks, bicolored bill (black above, yellow below); long rounded tail.
fistur1: Spectacular green turaco with tall bushy crest tipped white and red, blue-black nape, white-edged crimson-tufted face. Glossy green body, violet-blue back and tail, crimson flight feathers in flight; red bill, black legs.
fitbar1: Bright green barbet with grey throat and chest band, yellow-and-red forecrown bearing an erect black-and-orange feather tuft, black eyestripe bordered white. Heavy pale bill, greenish underparts, grey-green legs.
fithum1: Stocky hummingbird with shining green body, brilliant fiery orange-red throat bordered blue-violet on lower breast, dark blue tail with white outer tips. Black bill with reddish base; black mask, white spot behind eye.
fitsun1: Tiny brilliant sunbird. Male crimson-red crown, back and exceptionally long red tail; metallic green forehead, yellow rump, orange-red breast fading to yellow belly. Slender curved black bill, dark legs.
flabul1: Olive-yellow bulbul with greenish-brown upperparts, bright yellow underparts and yellow vent, greyish head with whitish throat. Dark eyestripe, faint pale eyebrow; slim dark bill, dark legs; slightly crested.
flafly1: Olive-brown flycatcher with rufous-edged wings and tail, pale buff wing bars, yellowish-streaked (flammulated) breast and belly. Broad flat blackish bill, pale lower mandible; pale eye-ring, greyish legs.
flafly2: Small olive flycatcher with dusky-olive upperparts, bright yellow underparts streaked olive on breast, two buff wing bars. Pale eye-ring, dark bill with pale base below, dark legs; upright posture.
flalar1: Small cryptic lark, warm rufous-brown above heavily mottled black, rufous wing panel, buff-streaked breast over whitish belly. Short stout pale bill, pale legs; rounded crown, short tail.
flamec1: Tiny kinglet with olive-green upperparts, white underparts, and a bold black-and-white striped face. Crown black-bordered with flame-orange centre, white wing bar; fine black bill, dark legs; rounded body.
flaowl: Small grey-and-rufous owl with dark eyes (unusual among small owls), intricately mottled grey-brown plumage with rufous facial-disc tones, white scapular spots, short ear tufts. Pale bill, feathered legs; compact and cryptic.
flapyt1: Tiny tyrant, olive-brown above, whitish below heavily streaked (flammulated) on breast and flanks. Pale eye, two buff wing bars, broad flat dark bill with pale base; pale legs; rounded head.
flarob1: Small upright robin. Male slaty-grey above with bright flame-orange throat and breast, white forehead spot and white wing bar, grey-and-white tail. Female brown with buff wash. Fine black bill, dark legs.
flatre1: Robust dark foliage-gleaner, rich rufous-brown above with bright rufous wings and tail, blackish crown and back boldly streaked buff. Throat and breast buff streaked dark; stout blackish bill, grey legs.
flawar1: Bright warbler, olive-green above, entirely rich yellow below, with yellow supercilium and dusky eyeline. Plain wings, yellowish face; thin pointed dark bill, pinkish legs; slim and active.
flbsun2: Small sunbird. Male metallic green head and back, brilliant flame-orange-red breast band, dark belly; iridescent purple-blue throat and rump. Female dull olive-yellow. Slender curved black bill, dark legs.
flcman2: Olive-brown manakin with paler buff-yellow throat and belly, and a flattened flame-orange-and-yellow crown crest bordered black, often half-concealed. Greyish face, dark bill, pinkish legs; stocky build.
flctan: Robust tanager. Male flame orange-red overall, brightest on head and underparts, with two whitish wing bars and dusky streaking on back. Female yellowish-olive. Stout dark bill, grey legs; slight crest.
flctan2: Tanager. Male glossy black with bright flame-orange-red crest and crown, sometimes concealed. Female warm rufous-brown above, buff below. Stout blackish bill, dark legs; sturdy build.
flfbar1: Green barbet with blue throat and face, scarlet-orange forecrown and yellowish band, black eyestripe, red-spotted blue cheeks. Yellowish breast band, green underparts; heavy pale bill, greenish legs.
flfshe: Large dark-brown shearwater, uniformly sooty brown above and below, with pale pinkish (flesh-coloured) bill tipped dark and pale pinkish legs and feet. Long slender wings, wedge-shaped tail.
flftan1: Stunning tanager with flame-orange face and forecrown, black-scaled green body, golden-yellow nape, blue-green underparts spangled black. Black mask around eye; short black bill, grey legs; jewel-like.
flocro1: All-black crow with glossy plumage, relatively short stout bill and rounded wings. Uniform black with faint sheen; black bill, legs and feet; medium-sized and compact.
flrtan1: Velvety black tanager. Male with brilliant flame orange-red rump and lower back contrasting sharply with black body. Female duller with orange rump wash. Silvery-grey thick bill, black legs.
flsjay: Crestless blue-and-grey jay with azure-blue head, wings and tail, pale grey back and underparts, whitish forehead and throat, faint blue necklace across breast. Stout black bill, black legs.
fltwar1: Slaty-blue-grey warbler with brilliant flame orange-yellow throat and upper breast, white belly, blackish lores. Plain wings, grey head; thin pointed dark bill, dark legs; small and active.
fluwre1: Tiny short-tailed wren, dark warm brown above, scaled and barred greyish below with finely vermiculated breast. Plain face, long slender slightly decurved bill, pinkish legs; rounded body, cocked stubby tail.
flystd1: Large heavy duck, mostly grey-brown with darker head, paler throat and white belly, white wing speculum visible in flight. Capable of flight unlike other steamer-ducks; orange-yellow bill, orange legs.
focbus2: Stocky bushshrike, green above, with male showing scarlet throat, yellow breast and black band, and green underparts. Black mask, yellow eyebrow. Heavy black hooked bill, grey legs; colorful and robust.
fooant1: Small antwren, grey-brown above, with male grey below and a white throat finely spotted/stippled black. Female buffier. Pale wing spots, dark eye; thin dark bill, pale legs; rounded and active.
fooela1: Small drab elaenia, olive-green above, pale yellowish below, with two whitish wing bars and a concealed yellow crown patch. Faint pale eyering, dark bill with pale base, dark legs; nondescript.
foosco1: Small screech-owl with finely vermiculated grey-brown plumage, dark eyes (or brown-yellow), short ear tufts, indistinct facial-disc rim. Whitish scapular spots, streaked underparts; pale bill, feathered legs; cryptic.
forcan1: Small canary, streaked olive-green above, yellowish below, with dusky-streaked breast, yellow eyebrow and dark eyeline, mottled crown. Conical pale bill, pinkish legs; compact finch shape.
forela1: Drab elaenia, olive above, with pale grey throat and breast, yellow belly, two pale wing bars and concealed white crown patch. Whitish eyering broken, dark bill, dark legs; slim and plain.
forkin1: Striking kingfisher with deep turquoise-blue crown, wings and tail, white underparts and collar, black eyestripe through to nape. Whitish-blue back; large black bill, dark legs; compact tree kingfisher.
formag1: Spectacular long-tailed magpie, deep blue body with black head and breast, yellow eye, white-tipped very long graduated blue tail, white-tipped wings. Bright red bill and red legs; striking.
forowl1: Small stocky owlet, greyish-brown above, white below with bold dark-brown breast band and flank barring, large whitish facial disc with dark eyes, white throat. Heavily banded wings and tail; pale bill, feathered legs.
forrav1: Large heavy all-black raven with thick black bill, glossy plumage, short squared tail and shaggy throat hackles. Uniform black with faint sheen; dark eye, black legs; bulky and powerful.
forrot2: Rock-thrush. Male slaty blue-grey head and upperparts, orange-rufous breast and belly, dark wings. Female warm brown, scaled below. Slender dark bill, dark legs; upright thrush shape.
forter: Pale tern, silvery-grey above, white below, with black cap (breeding), long deeply forked white tail, frosty-white primaries. Orange bill tipped black, orange-red legs; slender and graceful.
forwag1: Distinctive wagtail, olive-brown above, white below with two bold black breast bands (upper complete, lower broken). White wing bars, pale supercilium; thin dark bill, pinkish legs; wags side to side.
forwea1: Weaver, dark olive-brown above, contrasting clean yellow underparts and belly, with dark head and throat. Pale eye, stout conical blackish bill, greyish legs; sturdy, dark-backed appearance.
fotdro5: Glossy black drongo with deeply forked tail flaring outward at tips, slender hooked black bill and red eye. Uniform black with bluish-green sheen; black legs; slim and agile.
fotfly: Striking flycatcher with black cap and grey back, white underparts, blackish wings, and extremely long deeply forked black tail. Black bill, dark legs; elegant elongated silhouette.
fotpyt1: Tiny tyrant, olive-brown above, whitish below streaked olive, with a distinctly forked tail and pale eye. Two buff wing bars, broad flat dark bill, pale legs; rounded head, energetic.
fotsun1: Tiny sunbird, the male with a glossy metallic green crown, throat and back, blackish wings, and a sharply forked tail with elongated central feathers; underparts greyish-olive. Slender, decurved black bill; dark legs.
fotswi1: Slim blackish-brown swift with long scythe-shaped wings and a deeply forked tail; shows a clean white rump band, pale-scaled underparts, and a whitish throat. Tiny black bill, very short dark legs.
fotwoo1: Glittering hummingbird, the male with shining green crown and breast, violet-blue belly, golden-green back, and a deeply forked blue-black tail. Straight black bill; dark feet.
fowthr1: Large dark thrush appearing blackish, with subtle deep blue-violet sheen across head, breast and wings; lacks pale spotting. Stout black bill, blackish legs; dark eye.
foxspa: Large, stocky sparrow with rich rufous-brown to grey upperparts and tail, heavy dark triangular spots converging on white underparts into a central breast blotch. Stout conical bill (yellowish lower), pinkish legs.
fragul: Medium gull with a dark slate hood, broad white eye-crescents, grey back, and black wingtips separated from the grey by a white band, tipped with white spots. Dark reddish bill and legs.
