frbtho1: Rufous-brown ovenbird with bright rufous crown, wings and tail, plain brown back, and a white throat heavily freckled with fine black spots across the breast. Slender dark bill, greyish legs.
frbwoo1: Small black-and-white woodpecker with barred white-and-black back and wings, buffy-white underparts finely spotted/freckled black on the breast; male has a red rear crown. Dark bill, greyish legs.
frenig1: Dark, heavily mottled nightjar, greyish-black overall finely freckled and spangled with white, buff and grey; shows small white throat patches and white spots in the wingtips and tail corners. Tiny bill, short legs.
frifan1: Small slate-grey fantail with a darker head, white throat, pale grey underparts, and a broadly white-edged, frequently fanned tail; fine white eyebrow and wing markings. Small black bill, dark legs.
frimon1: Black-and-white monarch with glossy black upperparts, head and breast band, white nape collar, belly and wing patch, and a bare blue eye-ring forming erectile filamentous 'frills'. Bluish bill, dark legs.
ftpswi1: Very small, slender swift, pale greyish-brown with a whitish belly and rump, and a long, deeply forked tail held closed to a point. Tiny black bill, very short dark legs.
fubwoo2: Pied woodpecker with black-and-white barred back, black crown (male red-naped), bold black moustache, and warm fulvous-buff underparts with fine dark streaking. Greyish bill, dull legs.
fucjuf1: Plain flycatcher with warm olive-brown upperparts, rich orange-buff throat and breast fading to whitish belly, and a faint pale eye-ring on a plain face. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
fuctan1: Glossy black tanager, the male with a concealed orange-tawny crown patch and a white wing-flash; otherwise velvety black overall. Bluish-grey conical bill, dark legs.
fuebou1: Bushshrike with glossy blue-black upperparts, head and tail, contrasting with rich orange-buff to tawny underparts. Heavy black hooked bill, blackish legs.
fuelon2: Pipit-like ground bird with grey-brown streaked upperparts, a bright golden-yellow throat and eyebrow bordered by a bold black breast band, and pumpkin-buff belly; white outer tail corners. Slender dark bill, long-clawed legs.
fulant1: Large, shaggy-crested antshrike; male glossy black finely barred with white above and below, female warm cinnamon-rufous with dense wavy black barring and a darker tail. Heavy hooked black bill, dark legs; orange-yellow eye.
fulowl1: Medium tawny-brown wood-owl with dark concentric facial discs, tawny-and-brown barred underparts, mottled brown upperparts, and dark eyes; lacks ear tufts. Pale yellowish bill, feathered legs.
fulwre1: Small uniformly warm fulvous-rufous wren, rich tawny-brown overall with faint dark barring on wings and tail, slightly paler below; long slender slightly decurved dark bill, dark legs.
fusfly1: Plain flycatcher with dusky grey-brown upperparts, a whitish throat and pale grey breast, two faint wing bars, and a pale loral line/eye-ring on a plain face. Dark bill with pale base, dark legs.
fushon1: Slender honeyeater, dull grey-brown to olive overall with a yellowish gape stripe and a small dark ear patch; faintly streaked underparts. Fine slightly decurved dark bill, greyish legs.
fustan1: Shrike-tanager with a heavy hooked bill; male tawny-rufous overall with a contrasting black head and throat; female plainer olive-rufous. Stout pale-based bill, dark legs.
fuveup1: Small stubby finch-like euphonia; male glossy steel-blue above and on throat with a yellow forehead and breast and fulvous-buff vent; female olive-green above, yellowish below. Short thick dark bill, grey legs.
gabbou1: Bushshrike with glossy blue-black upperparts, head, wings and tail contrasting sharply with pure white underparts. Heavy black hooked bill, blackish legs.
gabfru1: Plump fruiteater; male bright green with a black head bordered by a thin yellow line, black throat, green-streaked yellow belly, and orange-red eyering and bill base. Stout bill, orange legs.
gabgos2: Small slim hawk, grey above and on head and breast, with finely barred grey-and-white lower underparts and a banded tail; a melanistic all-black morph also occurs. Red-orange cere, bill base and legs; red eye.
gabwoo3: Tiny stout crested woodpecker, grey overall with black-and-white scaled back and a very short stubby tail; male shows a red front to the prominent crest. Short dark bill, greyish legs.
gagcoc1: Slate-grey cockatoo with a wispy forward-curling crest; male has a scarlet-red head and crest, female all-grey with faint paler scalloping. Pale grey hooked bill, grey legs.
gagtan1: Pale silvery-grey tanager with a black face and shoulder and messy black-and-gold spotting across the breast and nape. Black conical bill, dark legs.
gagtan2: Brilliant green tanager with a black mask and back streaks, golden-yellow crown and underparts, and green-and-black wings. Short black bill, grey legs.
gagwar2: Warbler with grey crown and nape, olive-green back, and bright golden-yellow underparts; bold yellow supercilium and dark eyeline. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
galah: Pink-and-grey cockatoo with rose-pink face, neck and underparts, a pale pink crest, and grey back, wings and tail. Pale bone-coloured bill, grey legs.
galpet: Large gadfly petrel, blackish-grey above with a dark cap and hindneck contrasting with a white forehead and face, white underparts, and dark underwing margins. Stout black hooked bill, pinkish legs.
gamqua: Plump grey quail with a forward-drooping black teardrop plume; male has a rufous crown, black face bordered white, chestnut flanks streaked white, and a pale belly with a black patch. Short dark bill, grey legs.
ganlew1: Tiny greenish leaf-warbler, olive-green above and whitish below, with a pale yellowish supercilium, dark eyestripe, and a single faint wing bar and pale crown stripe. Fine dark bill with pale base, pinkish legs.
garkin1: Kingfisher with glossy dark green upperparts, head and breast band, a white throat collar, and rich rufous-chestnut underparts; female has a green-spotted breast band. Long heavy black bill, dark feet.
garpit1: Jewel-like pitta, the male with a glossy crimson lower breast and belly, blackish head with a glittering blue crown and nape, and dark greenish-brown upperparts. Stout black bill, pinkish legs.
gartro1: Trogon, the male with a metallic green head, back and breast, a yellow eye-ring, a narrow white breast band, and a bright yellow belly; tail black with fine white barring below. Yellow bill, dark legs.
gawtyr2: Small grey-brown tyrannulet with a shaggy split crest showing a white center when raised; whitish underparts, white supercilium, and a dark line through the eye. Short dark bill, dark legs.
gbesta1: Glossy starling, iridescent blue-green overall with deeper blue ear-coverts and belly, and a row of black spots across the wing coverts. Black bill, dark legs; bright orange-yellow eye.
gbmgem1: Hummingbird with metallic grass-green upperparts; male green-breasted with white feather margins and a white postocular stripe; female buff-throated with greyish underparts and green flanks. Straight black bill.
gbwwre1: Small dark wood-wren, rich brown above and grey below, with a black-and-white streaked face, white throat, and a strong white supercilium; short cocked tail. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
gchwar: Warbler with a bright golden-yellow face bordered by a black eyeline, black crown, throat and upper breast, grey-black streaked back, and white belly with black flank streaks; two white wing bars. Thin dark bill.
gcoroc1: Spectacular cotinga, the male brilliant orange overall with a large half-moon crest covering the bill, black-and-white wings and a fringed orange back; female dull brownish. Orange-yellow bill, orange legs.
gcrfin: Stocky finch, rich cinnamon-brown overall with a grey rear crown and nape, a black forecrown, and pink wing and rump feather edgings. Short conical bill (yellowish in non-breeding), black legs.
gcrwar: Warbler with an olive-green back, grey head with an orange-rufous crown patch bordered by black stripes, and uniform bright yellow underparts. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
gdcsun2: Sunbird, the male with a glittering metallic green head and back, a broad iridescent blue throat band above a wide scarlet breast band, and dark belly. Long decurved black bill, dark legs.
genpen1: Penguin with a blue-black back and white underparts, a black head marked by a broad white bonnet stripe across the crown joining over the eyes, and white eye-spots. Orange-red bill, orange-pink feet.
gepphe1: Dark brownish-grey peacock-pheasant finely spotted buff, with rows of metallic blue-green ocelli on the wings and tail, a bushy crest, and bare reddish-pink facial skin. Dark bill, grey legs.
giaant1: Very large, plump, nearly tailless antpitta with olive-brown upperparts and rufous-brown head. Underparts and breast densely barred blackish on cinnamon-buff. Stout dark bill, long pinkish legs, large dark eye. Stands upright on long legs.
giaant2: Huge antshrike; male slate-grey overall with black crown and bushy crest, fine whitish barring on wings, long graduated tail tipped white. Heavy hooked black bill, red eye, grey legs. Female rufous-brown replacing grey.
giacon1: Stocky, short-tailed conebill with deep rufous-chestnut underparts and face, blue-grey crown, nape and back. Bold whitish wing patch and shoulder. Short pointed black bill, dark legs. Compact, nuthatch-like build.
giacoo1: Massive blackish-grey coot with large white-and-yellow frontal shield grading to deep red base, dark red bill tip. Bulky body, short tail; greenish-yellow legs with large lobed reddish feet. Whitish undertail.
giacou1: Large, long-tailed terrestrial coua, grey-brown above, paler greyish below with rufous-tinged flanks. Bare blue-and-purple skin around eye, black bill, sturdy grey legs. Long broad tail tipped whitish; upright stance.
giacow: Large glossy black blackbird with strong purple-blue iridescence. Male has prominent neck ruff and small head on long neck, long pointed black bill, dark legs, red or dark eye. Long tail, robust build; female duller blackish.
giahum1: Enormous swift-like hummingbird, dull greyish-brown to bronzy-green above with cinnamon-buff underparts and white rump patch. Long straight blackish bill, short blackish legs. Slow heavy wingbeats; largest hummingbird.
giakin3: Large crested kingfisher, black above heavily spotted white, white throat and collar. Male shows broad rufous-chestnut breast band with white-spotted black belly; female chestnut belly. Massive dagger black bill, dark legs.
gialau1: Large laughingthrush, rich rufous-brown overall with bold black scaling and streaking on crown, back and breast. Grey-tinged wings and tail, blackish face mask. Stout dark bill, strong pinkish legs; long full tail.
gianut1: Large nuthatch, pale blue-grey above with broad black eyestripe through pale face, whitish throat and buffy-grey underparts streaked along flanks. Heavy pointed grey bill, short dark legs; oversized, big-headed shape.
giapit1: Large, plump pitta; male glossy deep blue overall with black face and crown, blue crown patch, bright blue underparts paling on belly. Short tail, stout pale bill, long pinkish legs. Female browner with buff scaling.
giasni1: Very large heavy snipe, intricately patterned buff, black and rufous above with bold pale braces. Barred buff underparts, striped crown and face. Extremely long straight pinkish-based bill, greenish legs; broad rufous-barred tail.
giawre1: Large wren, plain greyish-brown above and dull whitish below with little patterning, faint pale supercilium. Long slightly decurved dark bill, dark legs, longish barred-edged tail. Bulky cactus-wren build, unmarked breast.
gietan1: Bright golden-yellow tanager with green-washed back, deep blue throat, belly and flanks, and black mask through eye. Black-and-green wings, black-streaked golden crown. Short black bill, grey legs; jewel-like colourful tanager.
gilbar1: Stocky barbet, glossy black above with bright golden-yellow rump, scarlet throat and breast band, golden-orange face and belly. Heavy pale bill, dark legs. Female has black-streaked underparts. Big-headed, short-tailed.
gilfli: Large brown woodpecker, buffy-brown crown, grey face, pinkish-buff body finely barred black above, black-spotted below with black crescent breast bib. Yellow underwings/tail. Male red moustache. Curved dark bill, grey legs.
gilhum1: Small golden-green hummingbird, glittering bronzy-golden green above and on throat and breast, white belly. Bright coral-red bill with dark tip, dark forked tail, dark legs. Overall shimmering gilt-green appearance.
gilwhi1: Plain grey-brown whistler; male grey above and on head with pale grey underparts, often a dark lores/throat smudge and reddish eye. Stout black bill, dark legs. Female browner. Robust, large-headed, fairly featureless.
gilwoo: Medium tan-brown woodpecker with black-and-white barred back, wings and central tail, plain greyish-tan head and underparts. Male has small red crown cap, yellowish belly tinge. Chisel-tipped dark bill, grey legs.
giwrai1: Large wood-rail, grey neck and breast, rufous-brown back and flanks, black rear belly and undertail. Bare reddish facial skin, stout greenish-yellow bill, long bright red legs and feet. Long-legged, upright, short tail.
glbbec1: Small becard; male glossy blue-black above with white scapular patch and collar, white underparts. Stout grey hooked bill, dark legs, large head. Female greyish-olive with buffy underparts and rufous-tinged wings.
glbcoc1: Large blackish cockatoo, dull brownish-black overall, male with broad scarlet-red panels in the tail; female shows yellow head spotting and orange-red barred tail panels. Heavy dark bill, short crest, grey feet.
glbeme1: Small emerald hummingbird, glittering golden-green above and across breast and belly, deep blue-black forked tail. Bright red bill with black tip, dark legs. Female greener with whitish underparts. Shining green throughout.
glbgro1: Small grosbeak; male soft glaucous slate-blue overall, slightly brighter on crown, with darker wings and tail. Heavy conical blackish bill, dark legs. Female warm tawny-brown. Compact bunting-like shape, pale powdery blue tone.
glbthr1: Glossy all-black thrush; male uniform black with bluish-glossed plumage, bright orange-yellow bill, narrow orange eye-ring and orange legs. Female sooty dark brown with duller bill. Slim upright thrush shape.
glgtan1: Brilliant shining grass-green tanager, glistening emerald overall with contrasting pale silvery-blue ear patch and crescent below eye. Black flight feathers, short black bill, dark legs. Vivid uniform green, jewel-like.
gloant1: Black-and-white antshrike; male glossy black on head, throat, breast and back with bold white wing bars, white belly and white-tipped tail. Short bushy crest, heavy hooked black bill, grey legs. Female brown above, streaked below.
gloflo1: Wholly glossy blue-black flowerpiercer with deep blue-grey gloss. Distinctive upturned hook-tipped black bill, dark legs, dark eye. Slim, all-dark; sexes alike. Velvet-black plumage with bluish sheen in good light.
glteme1: Small emerald hummingbird, shining bronze-green above, glittering green throat and breast with clean white central belly. Reddish-pink lower bill with dark tip, dark slightly forked tail, dark legs. Bright green-and-white below.
glwgul: Large pale gull; white head and underparts, pale blue-grey back and wings with grey (not black) wingtips showing white spots. Heavy yellow bill with red gonys spot, pink legs, dark eye. Pale-winged, stout build.
gnbger1: Tiny warbler-like gerygone, bright olive-green back and wings, grey head, white throat and yellow-washed underparts. Fine pointed dark bill, dark legs, longish tail with pale tips. Small, slender, active foliage-gleaner.
gnbtai1: Small tailorbird, bright green back and wings, rufous-orange crown, greyish face, whitish underparts. Long thin slightly decurved bill, pinkish legs, longish often-cocked tail. Slim, warbler-like; greener-backed than relatives.
gnbtro1: Trogon; male metallic green head, breast and back, bright yellow belly below a narrow white breast band, white eye-ring (pale blue orbital), and broad-barred black-and-white undertail. Stout yellowish bill, dark legs.
gncpar: Small green parakeet, mostly green with scaly grey-brown breast, dull greyish-brown crown, white eye-ring, maroon-red belly patch and blue flight feathers, maroon tail. Pale horn bill, grey legs. Long-tailed, compact.
gnhsun1: Sunbird; male glittering metallic blue-green head, throat and breast, dull olive-grey back and belly, dark wings. Long decurved black bill, dark legs. Female olive above, greyish-yellow below. Bright iridescent hood contrasts grey body.
gnlthr: Large laughingthrush, olive-brown above, white throat and breast crossed by a bold black necklace, rufous flanks and vent. Black eyestripe and white face area, white-tipped dark tail. Stout dark bill, pinkish legs; long-tailed.
gnteup1: Small euphonia; male glossy steel blue-green above and on hood, with green-glossed throat and yellow lower breast and belly. Short stubby dark bill, dark legs. Female yellowish-olive below, green above. Glossy greenish sheen.
gnttow: Olive-green towhee with grey breast and face, bright rufous crown, white throat bordered by dark malar, yellow-green wing edges. Greyish belly, dark conical bill, pinkish legs, longish dark tail. Plump, sparrow-like.
gobbun1: Bunting; male bright golden-yellow below, chestnut back, black-and-white striped head with white supercilium and crown stripe, two white wing bars. Greyish rump, dark conical bill, pinkish legs. Bold yellow-and-stripes pattern.
gobchl1: Stocky chlorophonia, brilliant green above and on throat, golden-yellow belly, sky-blue nape band, and golden forehead/supercilium with black border. Short thick dark bill, pale legs. Vivid green-blue-yellow, rounded shape.
gobcht1: Small chat-tyrant, olive-brown above, bright cinnamon-rufous underparts, and a striking golden-yellow supercilium on a dark face. Rufous wing edgings, short dark bill, dark legs. Upright, perky flycatcher posture.
gobeup1: Euphonia; male olive-green above with grey crown, bright yellow underparts and a small golden forecrown, whitish throat. Short stubby pale-based bill, dark legs. Female greener, paler below. Grey-capped, yellow-bellied.
gobfly1: Flycatcher, olive-brown above with rufous-edged wings and tail, dusky-streaked head with whitish throat and dark malar. Underparts golden-yellow, faintly streaked breast. Broad blackish bill with pale base, dark legs.
gobfru1: Fruiteater; male green above and on head with black face mask, bright golden-yellow breast band fading to yellow-green belly, faint green streaking on flanks. Coral-red bill and legs, red eye. Plump, short-tailed.
gobful1: Tiny, colourful fulvetta; black head with white crown stripe and white ear patch, golden-yellow underparts and yellow wing panel, olive back. Small dark bill, pale legs. Round, active; striking black-and-gold head pattern.
gobger1: Tiny gerygone, plain grey-brown above, bright yellow throat and underparts, grey head with faint pale eyebrow and dark eye. Slender pointed dark bill, dark legs, longish tail with white tips. Small, slim, sulphur-yellow below.
gobgro1: Large grosbeak; male rich golden-yellow on head and underparts, olive-yellow back, black-and-white wings and tail. Very heavy conical pale-grey bill, dark legs. Female duller olive-streaked. Bold yellow, massive billed.
gobmag1: Long-tailed blue magpie, deep purplish-blue above, black head and breast with whitish-spotted crown, white belly, very long blue tail broadly tipped white with black subterminal band. Bright yellow bill, red legs and eye-ring.
gobrob1: Small bush-robin; male orange-yellow below and on rump, blackish back and wings, black face mask, bright yellow supercilium, and orange-edged blackish tail. Slender black bill, dark legs. Female olive-brown, buff below.
gobsal1: Saltator, olive-grey above, grey hood with white supercilium and white throat bordered black, buffy-cinnamon underparts. Distinctive bright orange bill, dark legs. Stout, large-headed; warm orange bill stands out.
gobwar1: Small warbler, olive-green above and bright yellow below including throat. Bold head pattern: rufous crown and ear-coverts split by a broad golden-yellow supercilium edged above with black, plus black lores. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
gobwar3: Small warbler, olive-green upperparts and uniformly bright yellow underparts. Head shows yellow supercilium/eyebrows and a concealed orange central crown stripe; plain face lacking dark stripes or broken eye-ring. Slender dark bill, pale pinkish legs.
gobwea1: Stocky weaver; breeding male has golden-yellow body and rump with a chestnut-and-black face mask and throat, black wings and tail edged yellow. Heavy conical blackish bill, red eye, pinkish legs.
gocfly1: Stout flycatcher with brown-streaked olive upperparts and pale yellow underparts streaked dusky on breast. Dark face with whitish supercilium and a concealed golden-orange crown patch. Broad black bill, dark legs.
gockin: Tiny, plump, short-tailed kinglet, olive-grey above and pale grey-white below. Black-bordered crown stripe is bright yellow (orange-centred in male); bold whitish supercilium and dark eyeline, two whitish wing bars. Fine black bill.
gocman1: Small, plump manakin; male has a black cap and back, brilliant golden-yellow throat, collar and breast forming a beard-like ruff, olive belly and rump. Bright orange legs, short stubby bill. Female olive-green overall.
gocpar2: Medium green parakeet with golden-orange forehead, forecrown and lores, red on the lower belly and underwing edges, blue-tinged flight feathers. Pale horn bill, white eye-ring, grey legs.
gocspa: Large grey sparrow with brown-streaked back; breeding adult has a dull-yellow forecrown bordered by broad black lateral crown stripes. Plain grey face and breast, dusky bill, pinkish legs.
gocspa1: Tiny, big-headed, almost tailless flycatcher; olive-green above, buffy-cinnamon below with an ochre face and dark eye. Male has a flat golden-yellow crown patch (often hidden). Broad flat blackish bill, pale legs.
goctan2: Medium tanager, mostly pale blue-grey; black wings and tail, with a bright golden-yellow lesser-wing-covert patch (epaulet) at the bend of the wing. Stout grey bill, dark legs.
goctan4: Plump dark-blue tanager with a velvety black face and throat and a brilliant golden-orange crown patch; rufous-chestnut lower belly and vent. Short black bill, dark legs.
goctou1: Small toucanet with black head, breast and back, golden-yellow ear-tufts and a yellow crescent on the neck side, red-and-yellow lower belly, green wings, chestnut undertail. Large pale-greenish-and-black bill, green skin around eye.
gocwoo1: Medium woodpecker, barred black-and-white above, plain buffy-grey below; pale-grey face with a golden-yellow forehead and golden cheek patch, red nape (and crown in male). Chisel-shaped grey bill, greyish legs.
gocwoo2: Small woodpecker, dark olive-green above with a golden-yellow nape collar and reddish crown (male), barred olive-and-buff underparts. Short straight grey bill, greyish legs.
godbun1: Slim bunting with a grey head marked by chestnut lateral crown stripes and eyestripe enclosing a grey median crown, rufous-chestnut back and underparts, streaked mantle. Whitish belly, grey-and-pink conical bill, pinkish legs.
goeant1: Medium antbird; male slaty-black overall with a concealed white interscapular patch and bare blue skin around the eye. Female browner with rufous-tinged underparts. Stout black bill, grey legs.
gofgre1: Small greenlet, olive-green above and pale yellow below, with a contrasting orange-yellow forehead and forecrown blending into a grey nape and pale grey face. Short pale bill, pinkish-grey legs.
goflea1: Bright leaf-green leafbird with a golden-orange forehead and crown, black face, throat and upper breast, and a violet-blue malar streak; turquoise shoulder patch. Slightly curved black bill, grey legs.
gofred1: Slender warbler-like redstart, slaty-black above and bright yellow below, with a black face and a golden-yellow forehead and forecrown. White outer tail feathers, thin black bill, dark legs.
goftyr1: Tiny tyrannulet, olive-green above and pale yellowish below, with a distinctive bright yellow face and lores, yellow supraloral and eye-ring giving a 'golden-faced' look. Yellow wing-feather edgings, short bill, dark legs.
gofwoo: Medium woodpecker, finely barred black-and-white above, pale buffy-grey below; pale face with golden-yellow nasal tufts and nape, red crown patch (male), orange-red belly patch. Chisel grey bill.
gogwoo1: Medium woodpecker, golden-olive to yellowish-green above with a green-barred yellowish breast; head shows a golden crown and nape, dark malar and red moustache (male). Pale chisel-shaped bill, greyish legs.
gohcis1: Tiny warbler-like cisticola; breeding male has an unstreaked golden-buff to yellowish nape and underparts with a plain or pale crown, streaked black-and-buff back, short cocked tail. Fine bill, pinkish legs.
gohman1: Small jet-black manakin; male has an entirely brilliant golden-red to flame-red head, white-and-red thighs. Pale whitish bill, pinkish-white legs. Female plain olive-green.
gohque1: Large resplendent-type quetzal; male brilliant metallic green head, back and breast with a golden-bronze sheen on the crown, deep crimson-red belly, green tail with white undertail. Yellow bill, short legs.
gohtan1: Colourful tanager with a golden-tan to buff hood, black face mask, turquoise-blue underparts and rump, black back, and blue-edged black wings. Short black bill, dark legs.
golbab1: Small babbler, bright golden-yellow head and underparts with fine black streaks on the golden crown, olive-green back and wings, rufous-tinged tail. Short pale bill, pinkish legs.
golbow1: Plump bowerbird; male olive-brown above with a brilliant golden-yellow crown, nape and underparts and a yellow tail. Short stout bill, dark legs. Female plain olive-grey.
golmon1: Small monarch flycatcher; male brilliant golden-yellow on head, underparts and rump with contrasting glossy black back, wings, throat-bib and face mask. Bluish-grey bill, dark legs.
golpar2: Tiny parrotbill with a bright golden-orange head and underparts, blackish lateral crown stripes, olive-green back and wings, and rufous wing edgings. Short stubby yellowish bill, pinkish legs.
golpar3: Large, mostly brilliant golden-yellow parrot with green primary flight feathers; bare whitish eye-ring. Heavy pale-horn bill, grey legs, long tapering tail.
golphe: Spectacular pheasant; male has a golden-yellow crest, orange-and-black barred cape (ruff), scarlet-red underparts, golden rump, dark green upper back, and a very long buff-and-black mottled tail. Yellow bill and legs.
golswa1: Small swallow, glossy metallic golden-green to bronze-green above, clean white below, with a slightly forked tail. Small black bill, dark legs.
goltan1: Bright golden-yellow tanager with black ear-covert patch, black-streaked golden back, and black wings and tail edged yellow-green. Short black bill, dark legs.
golvir1: Small vireo, olive-green above and bright golden-yellow below and on the face, with yellow lores and eye-ring and faint yellow wing edgings. Short hooked grey bill, bluish-grey legs.
golwhi1: Robust whistler; male has an olive-green back, black head and broad black breast-band, bright yellow underparts and nape collar, white throat. Stout black bill, grey legs. Female plain greyish-olive.
gonbar1: Plump green barbet with a golden-yellow nape patch, blue throat and face, red-and-black markings on the crown, and a green body. Heavy pale bill with bristles, greyish legs.
gonwoo1: Black-backed woodpecker with a golden-yellow nape and crest, black face, red crown (male), white V down the back, yellowish-buff underparts with a red belly patch. Chisel-shaped grey bill.
goowoo1: Medium woodpecker, olive-green and bronze above, finely barred olive-and-yellowish below; grey crown with red nape, dull-golden cheeks, red malar (male). Strong grey bill, greyish legs.
goppar1: Slender green parakeet with a long pointed tail; golden-yellow ear-tuft plumes behind the eye, red forehead spot, white eye-ring, and reddish underwings. Pale horn bill, grey legs.
goreup1: Stocky euphonia/chlorophonia; male grass-green above with a sky-blue crown and nape, golden-yellow rump and underparts, and a small blue throat patch. Short thick black bill, dark legs.
gortan1: Plump dark-green tanager with a black head bearing a horseshoe-shaped golden-yellow facial ring (supercilium curving behind the cheek to a broad yellow moustache), black throat and a yellow central breast patch. Short black bill.
goseup1: Small euphonia; male glossy steel blue-black overall with diagnostic golden-yellow tufts on the sides of the chest and yellowish lower belly. Short thick black bill, dark legs. Female olive-green.
gospic1: Tiny piculet, olive-brown above with golden-spangled buff spotting, whitish underparts barred and spotted dark; male has a red-spotted forecrown, black-and-white crown. Short pointed grey bill.
goswar1: Small warbler, olive-green above and bright yellow below, with a grey crown bordered by black lateral stripes (a greenish median stripe), bold yellow eye-ring, and two pale wing bars. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
gotbar2: Green barbet with a golden-yellow throat and crown, red forecrown and a red spot at rear of crown, grey-and-blue face with black eyestripe, green body. Stout pale bristled bill, greyish legs.
gotbar3: Leaf-green barbet with a bright yellow throat and crown, red forehead, thick black mask and eyebrow, and a fine black 'necklace' line across the throat. Heavy pale bill, greyish legs.
gotgra1: Slim, long-tailed all-black grackle with a faint bluish gloss and diagnostic golden-yellow tufts at the bend of the wing (shoulder). Slender pointed black bill, black legs.
gotpar2: Small short-tailed green parrotlet with a yellowish-green face, blackish ear-covert patch, and a golden-yellow tail with a black subterminal band visible from below. Pale horn bill, pinkish legs.
gotsap1: Small hummingbird; male glittering green with a violet-blue crown and throat-band and a golden-bronze to coppery-gold tail. Straight black-tipped reddish bill, dark legs.
gotwoo1: Medium woodpecker with bronze-green back finely barred yellowish, and whitish underparts heavily spotted and barred with black. Male has red crown and red malar stripe; female has black-and-white speckled crown. Whitish face with dark moustache, dark bill, grey legs.
goufro1: Small rufous-brown frogmouth mottled and vermiculated with black, buff and white, with bold white spots forming lines on scapulars and breast. Large flat head, huge wide gape, broad pale eyebrows, dark eyes, short hooked pale bill; cryptic bark-like plumage.
gousun1: Brilliant male has scarlet head, back and breast, glittering metallic violet-blue crown, throat and tail, with yellow rump and belly. Female dull olive-green above, yellowish below. Slender decurved black bill, dark legs; small slim sunbird.
goutou1: Stocky toucanet with green back and wings, yellow ear-tufts and yellow flank patch, and a black bill with orange basal patches. Male has black crown and underparts; female replaces black with chestnut. Red undertail, bluish bare eye-skin.
gowbar2: Large green barbet with bright golden-yellow forehead, cheeks and throat, blue crown and supercilium, and a black eyestripe. Underparts green, heavy pale ivory bill with bristles at base; greyish legs. Plump, big-headed shape.
gowcac1: Glossy black cacique with bright yellow shoulder patch and yellow wing-bend, plus chestnut-tinged lower belly and undertail. Sharp pale ivory-blue pointed bill, dark eyes, blackish legs. Slim icterid with long tail.
gowman1: Small velvety-black manakin; male has golden-yellow forecrown and a tufted helmet of orange-and-yellow crest feathers, plus golden patches in the wings. Female plain olive-green, paler below. Short black bill, dark legs; rounded compact body.
gowpar2: Small green parakeet with golden-orange wing patch on the primaries visible in flight, otherwise mostly grass-green with bluish wash on face and crown. Short pointed tail, horn-coloured bill, greyish legs; slim, fast-flying parrotlet shape.
gowqua1: Plump ground quail, rufous-brown above and rich chestnut below (female greyer below), with a black-and-white face, blackish crown and short crest. Distinctive black-and-white crescent collar across the throat; short tail, stout legs, dark bill.
gowspa1: Sparrow with black head lacking pale eyebrow, the black extending into spurs forming a broken breast band, and a long bright yellow bill. Upperparts grey with a green back-and-scapular patch; whitish throat and underparts, grey flanks. Dark legs.
gowtof1: Tiny flycatcher with black crown and face, bold golden-yellow wing patch and wing edgings, and a yellow throat and underparts. Olive-green back, dark wings; pale eye, thin dark bill, pale legs. Long-tailed, upright miniature shape.
gowwar: Small warbler with blue-grey upperparts, white underparts, and a bright yellow crown patch and yellow wing patch. Male has a black throat and black mask through the eye bordered white; female pattern paler grey. Thin pointed black bill, dark legs.
grabul1: Chunky finch with a heavy conical black bill; male glossy black with rufous-chestnut on the chin, eyebrow and undertail coverts. Female dull olive-grey with the same rufous markings. Rounded body, dark legs.
graela3: Small drab flycatcher, olive-grey above with a faint whitish-edged crest, two pale wing bars and pale wing edgings. Pale greyish throat and breast grading to yellowish belly; whitish eye-ring, short dark bill, dark legs.
gragra1: Glossy black grackle with strong purple-blue iridescence, a pale yellow eye, and a long keeled wedge-shaped tail. Long pointed black bill, blackish legs; slim long-tailed icterid.
gramal1: Glossy blue-black weaver with a velvety appearance and a striking pale blue-grey to whitish conical bill. Pale eye, blackish legs; uniformly dark plumage with no contrasting markings, medium-sized and stocky.
gramou1: Plain greyish flycatcher-like bird, uniformly drab grey overall, slightly paler grey below, with darker wings and tail and a faint rufous tinge in the wings. Dark bill, dark legs; nondescript, upright posture with a longish tail.
graori4: Slim oriole, mostly black with bright yellow shoulder patch, yellow rump and yellow undertail coverts and thighs. Sharp pointed black bill, dark legs; long-tailed, restricted yellow-and-black pattern.
grapic1: Tiny grey-and-olive piculet, olive-brown above and barred greyish below, with a dark crown spotted white (male with red-tipped forecrown spots, female all white-spotted). Short stubby black bill, very short tail, grey legs.
grasal2: Heavy-billed saltator, grey above and greyish on the breast with a cinnamon-buff lower belly and undertail. White supercilium and black malar stripe bordering a whitish throat; thick black bill, dark legs.
grasal3: Large grey saltator with a heavy black bill, grey upperparts and greyish breast fading to buffy belly and rufous undertail. Bold white supercilium and black malar framing a white throat; dark legs, sturdy build.
grasal4: Large heavy-billed saltator, predominantly dark grey above and greyish below with a buffy lower belly. Distinct white supercilium and black malar stripe enclosing a whitish throat; only a faint olive tinge. Stout black bill, dark legs.
graspa: Small flat-headed sparrow, buff and brown above streaked black, with a clean unstreaked buffy breast and whitish belly. Pale central crown stripe, yellow lores, buffy face; short pointed tail, pale pinkish bill and legs.
graspa1: Small open-country sparrow, streaked brown above with rufous wing edgings, and plain pale greyish-buff underparts. Grey face with a small yellow loral spot, unstreaked breast; short tail, pinkish conical bill, pale legs.
grathr1: Slim grey thrasher, pale brownish-grey above with two whitish wing bars, and whitish underparts boldly spotted dark on the breast and flanks. Long decurved black bill, yellow-orange eye, pale legs; long graduated tail.
gratre1: Slim grey-brown thrasher-like bird, greyish overall, paler on the throat and breast, with faint streaking. Long slightly decurved blackish bill, dark legs; long tail often cocked, plain trembler shape.
grawar: Grey-headed warbler with grey upperparts, white belly and a bright yellow throat and breast. White eyebrow and broken white eye-ring, two whitish wing bars; fine black streaks on the grey flanks. Thin pointed bill, dark legs.
grbani: Glossy black cuckoo-relative, all black with a faint purple-bronze sheen, and a large laterally compressed bill marked with fine grooves along its sides. Long loose floppy tail, dark legs; ungainly, loose-feathered look.
grbbec1: Small becard, olive-green above with a grey crown and yellowish underparts. Bold yellow eye-ring and yellowish supraloral, dark eyestripe; stout hooked greyish bill, dark legs. Big-headed, short-tailed shape.
grbcam1: Small olive-green warbler-like bird, plain green above with a greyish crown and pale yellowish-grey underparts; rufous wing panel in some birds. Faint pale eye-ring, thin pointed bill, pale legs; often holds tail cocked.
grbcou1: Large heavy black coucal, entirely glossy blue-black including wings and long broad tail, with a stout black bill and dark red eye. Long strong blackish legs; bulky, long-tailed, all-dark cuckoo.
grbcou2: Large blackish coucal with glossy black head, body and tail and contrasting chestnut wings, distinguished by a pale greenish-yellow bill. Red eye, long black tail, strong dark legs; heavy-bodied.
grbcra1: Tiny crake with grey face and breast, rufous-chestnut nape and upperparts, and finely black-and-white barred flanks and undertail. Short greenish bill, reddish eye, dull greenish legs; small rounded skulker.
grbfir1: Small hummingbird with bronze-green upperparts and dull greyish underparts mottled green; male has a glittering iridescent orange-red crown patch. Short straight black bill, white spot behind eye, dark legs; rounded body.
grbhaw1: Large bulky black hawk, almost entirely sooty-black, with a broad white band across the base of the tail and a white tail tip. Bright yellow cere and yellow legs; broad rounded wings, short tail, heavy build.
grbher1: Large hermit hummingbird, bronzy-brown above with a long strongly decurved bill (red lower mandible), buffy below, with a dark mask bordered by buff facial stripes. Elongated central tail feathers tipped white; dark legs.
grbher2: Very large dark heron, slaty grey-brown overall with a paler greyish throat and foreneck and shaggy neck plumes. Massive dusky dagger bill, long dark neck, yellowish-grey legs; tall, long-necked, austere look.
grbher3: Large grey heron, blue-grey above with a white face, black plume stripe over the eye extending into a wispy crest, and rufous thighs. Streaked black-and-white foreneck, heavy yellowish dagger bill, long dark legs; very tall.
grbkin1: Stocky forest kingfisher with green crown and back, buffy-rufous underparts, and a blue-spotted black face mask and collar. Large reddish bill (some with dark upper mandible), short tail, reddish legs; big-headed, short-tailed.
grbmal1: Large slim malkoha, greyish-brown overall with a long graduated green-glossed tail broadly tipped white. Bare red skin around the eye, and a heavy curved pale apple-green bill; greenish-grey legs, long-tailed cuckoo shape.
grbspa1: Olive-and-grey sparrow, plain olive-green above with a grey head marked by blackish lateral crown stripes and a blackish eyeline. Whitish throat and underparts, greyish breast; stout dark bill, pinkish legs.
grbtit1: Small tit with green back, black crown and throat with white cheek patch, and a black central stripe down the yellow underparts. Two white wing bars, blue-grey wings and tail; short black bill, dark legs; like a Great Tit but green-backed.
grbtou1: Small green toucanet, mostly green with a chestnut-maroon crown and nape, blue-grey throat and breast, and chestnut undertail. Bill greenish-grey above and maroon below with grooved texture; bare facial skin, dark legs.
grbtur1: Huge slaty blue-grey turaco with a tall erect blue-black brushy crest, yellow bill tipped red, and a green-glossed back. Yellow breast grading to chestnut belly, broad blackish tail band; long tail, strong dark legs; very large.
grbwoo1: Pale grey-and-brown woodpecker with greyish head and breast, barred brownish back, and a black-and-white barred lower belly with reddish wash. Male has a red crown; female greyer-crowned. Pointed dark bill, grey legs.
grbwoo2: Green-backed woodpecker, olive-green above lightly spotted yellowish, and pale buffy below with fine dark spotting. Male has red crown and malar; female has dark crown spotted whitish. Dark bill, greyish legs; medium-sized.
grbwoo3: Yellowish-green woodpecker barred dark green-and-yellow across the back, with creamy underparts heavily spotted and barred black. Grey crown, red nape and (in male) red malar stripe; long pale bill, grey legs.
grbwre1: Large boldly patterned wren, grey-brown above heavily barred blackish across back, wings and tail, with whitish underparts. Grey crown, blackish eyeline and pale supercilium; longish barred tail, slightly decurved bill, dark legs.
grcbec1: Stocky becard; male grey above with a black crown and a grey collar across the breast, paler grey below. Female cinnamon-rufous above with a grey cap. Stout hooked dark bill, big-headed, short-tailed; dark legs.
grcbri1: Robust hummingbird, glittering emerald-green overall; male has a violet-blue throat spot and green underparts, female green-spangled white below. Straight black bill, white postocular spot, short white leg tufts; large, heavy-bodied.
grccra1: Tall elegant gray crane with a stiff golden crown of bristly feathers, black-and-white face with red cheek patch, pale gray neck and body, and chestnut-and-white wing coverts contrasting with black flight feathers. Bill dark and short; legs long and black.
grcdov1: Medium plump ground-dove with grayish head, warm brown back and wings, and pale grayish-white throat and breast fading to whitish belly. Lacks bold markings; shows faint pinkish-purple sheen on hindneck. Bill black; legs and feet pinkish-red.
grcdov2: Large long-tailed cuckoo-dove with bluish-gray head and underparts, dark maroon-brown back and wings, and a very long graduated dark tail. Bare red skin around eye. Bill blackish; legs reddish.
grcfly: Large crested flycatcher with gray head and throat, olive-brown back, lemon-yellow belly, and bright rufous flashes in the wings and tail. Stout dark bill; gray legs. Bushy crest gives a big-headed look.
grcfly1: Small flycatcher with plain gray cap, white supercilium, olive-brown back, and bright yellow underparts. Wings and tail edged rufous-brown; throat whitish. Short black bill; dark legs. Resembles a small kiskadee but grayer-headed.
grcter1: Large pale tern with shaggy black cap (white forehead), pale gray back and wings, and white underparts. Long slightly drooping yellow bill; black legs. Forked tail. In flight shows pale gray upperwing with darker primaries.
grcwoo1: Medium woodpecker with grayish crown, brown-and-buff barred back, buffy underparts with dark spotting, and a red nape patch (red malar in male). Black bib on upper breast; pale face. Bill dark and chisel-shaped; legs gray.
greani1: Large glossy blue-black cuckoo with a high laterally compressed black bill, long broad tail with bronze-green and purple sheen, and pale whitish eye. Slim long-tailed body; gray feet. Bare blackish facial skin.
greant1: Large heavy-billed antshrike: male glossy black above with white-barred wings, black crown and breast, and white belly; red eye. Massive hooked black bill; gray legs. Female rufous above, white below with rufous crown.
grearg1: Huge brown pheasant; male has enormous broadened secondary wing feathers covered in intricate golden ocelli (eyespots), a very long barred tail, bare blue head and neck, and short black crest. Bill pale; legs reddish.
grebar1: Large stocky barbet with dull blue-green body, dark brown head and breast, yellow-streaked olive belly, and a massive pale yellow bill. Reddish vent; greenish wings. Legs grayish. Heavy big-headed silhouette.
grebar2: Plain olive-green barbet, slightly darker above and paler greenish-yellow below, with faint streaking and a dull whitish eye-ring. Lacks bright colors. Stout pale horn-colored bill; grayish legs.
grebar3: Green barbet with a streaked greenish-and-buff face and breast, green body, and pale green ear-coverts giving the green-eared look. Heavy pale bill with dark tip; grayish legs. Streaked throat and upper breast.
grebow1: Large pale grayish-brown bowerbird, plain sandy-gray overall with fine scaling, paler below, and a lilac-pink nuchal crest on the nape (often hidden). Slim build, long tail. Dark bill; grayish legs.
grebro1: Stocky vivid emerald-green broadbill; male brilliant green with a few black bars on the wing and a small black ear patch, with a tuft of feathers nearly covering the small pale bill. Rounded head; greenish legs.
grecat1: Plump bright green catbird with fine white spotting on the wings, throat, and head, a green crown, and reddish-brown eyes. Heavy pale ivory bill; grayish legs. Stocky bowerbird-relative build.
grecoc1: Striking thrush-like bird: male bright green body with a pale blue crown, black mask through the eye, and a black-and-blue band across the green wings. Bill black; legs pinkish. Female duller green.
grecou1: Large heavy coucal with glossy blue-black head, body, and long broad tail, contrasting bright chestnut-brown wings. Red eye; stout black bill; black legs. Big long-tailed crow-like silhouette.
grecro1: Tiny short-tailed warbler with uniform olive-green upperparts and slightly paler yellowish-green underparts, a plain face with faint pale eye-ring, and almost no visible tail. Slender dark bill; pale legs.
grecur1: Large turkey-sized curassow; male glossy black with a curly black crest, white belly, and a bright yellow knob at the base of the bill. Female rufous-brown with barred crest, variable morphs. Legs gray.
greela: Small grayish-olive flycatcher with a grayish head, faint whitish eyebrow, olive back, and pale yellowish belly. Often shows a partly concealed yellow crown patch and faint wing edging. Short dark bill; dark legs.
greere1: Small warbler with greenish-olive cap and upperparts, bright yellow throat and breast, and a conspicuous pale yellow or whitish eye. Pale belly. Short fine dark bill; pale legs. Plain face.
grefla1: Large golden-backed woodpecker with brilliant golden-yellow and olive back, crimson rump, white-spotted black wings, and a black-and-white scaled neck and underparts. Male has full red crest; long pale bill; grayish legs.
grefla2: Tall flamingo, entirely deep rosy-pink with vivid scarlet wing coverts and black flight feathers. Long sinuous neck; pink legs. Thick downcurved bill, pale pink with a black tip. Most vividly colored flamingo.
grefri: Large blackish seabird with long pointed wings and deeply forked tail; male glossy black with a green sheen and a bright red throat pouch. Female blackish-brown with a white breast. Long hooked gray bill.
gregre1: Large grebe with blackish crown and hindneck, white face and foreneck, dark gray back, and rich rufous-chestnut lower neck and flanks. Long slender body. Long dark dagger bill; dark legs set far back.
greher1: Medium-large hermit hummingbird, dark green above and grayish-green below, with a bold black-and-buff striped face mask, long decurved black bill (red lower mandible), and a long white-tipped tail. Legs small.
grehon1: Slim tanager-like honeycreeper: male glossy turquoise-green with a black head, hood, and chin, bright yellow eye, and a slender slightly decurved yellow bill. Female plain green. Legs dark.
grehon2: Drab grayish-brown honeyguide: male has a black throat, pale yellow shoulder patch, white cheek and outer tail, and a stubby pink bill. Brownish-gray above, whitish below. Female and young plainer. Legs grayish.
grehor1: Huge black-and-white hornbill with a massive curved yellow bill topped by a large flat U-shaped yellow casque, white neck, black body, white belly, and white-barred wings and tail. Bare facial skin; gray legs.
grehyl1: Small plain warbler-like bird, uniform olive-green above and paler grayish-green below, with a faint pale supercilium and an indistinct face. Short slender dark bill; pale legs. Rounded, nondescript shape.
greibi1: Glossy dark ibis appearing blackish, with bronze-green and purple iridescence on the back and wings and a duller blackish-green head and neck. Long decurved greenish-gray bill; greenish legs. Stocky for an ibis.
greior2: Small bird with deep green plumage overall; male blackish-green above and bright green below with two pale yellowish wing bars and a green rump. Short stout bluish bill; grayish legs. Female yellower-green.
grejac2: Large stocky jacamar with brilliant golden-green and coppery iridescent upperparts and breast, a glittering green throat, and a rich rufous-chestnut belly. Heavy straight black bill; short legs. Dazzling metallic sheen.
grejun1: Junglefowl with glossy iridescent green-black plumage; male has a single rounded comb of blue, red, and yellow, glossy bluish-green hackles and back, and a black sickle tail. Bill dark; legs yellowish. Female brown.
grejun2: Junglefowl; male has gray plumage finely spangled with white and yellow waxy spots on the hackles, a serrated red comb, glossy black tail, and orange-buff streaking. Bill horn; legs reddish. Female brown and white.
grekis: Large bold flycatcher with a black-and-white striped head (white eyebrows, black mask), white throat, brown back, rufous-edged wings and tail, and bright yellow underparts. Heavy black bill; dark legs. Concealed orange crown.
grekno: Large stocky sandpiper, breeding adult gray-brown above with bold black-spotted scapulars and a heavily black-streaked breast forming a dense chest band, white belly with arrow spots. Long straight black bill; greenish-gray legs.
grelic1: Large long-tailed cuckoo, grayish-brown above, buff below with a rufous throat and breast, and a very long graduated dark tail boldly tipped white. Bare red eye-ring; stout slightly decurved bill. Streaked appearance; gray legs.
gremag1: Striking magpie with bright apple-green body, a black mask through the eye, chestnut-red wings, a long green white-tipped tail, and a yellowish wash. Heavy red bill; red legs. Green often fades to bluish.
greman2: Small plump manakin, uniform bright green above and slightly paler yellowish-green below, with a plain face and faint pale eye-ring. Short dark bill; dull orange or pinkish legs. Compact, big-headed shape.
grenig1: Very large dark nightjar, mottled grayish-brown and buff, with prominent feather tufts (ear-tufts) on the head, a pale collar across the throat, and long broad wings and tail without white patches. Tiny bill; short legs.
greori1: Medium oriole with olive-green upperparts and yellowish-green underparts, both finely streaked dark, giving a streaked look. Reddish eye; pinkish-red bill. Plain green wings. Legs gray. Streaky greenish overall.
greoro1: Large oropendola with mostly bright moss-green body, chestnut wings and rump, and a yellow tail with a dark central stripe. Distinctive pale greenish-yellow bill with an orange tip; blue-and-pink bare face skin. Legs dark.
grepar: Medium gray parrot, pale dove-gray overall with fine scaling, a bright scarlet-red tail and vent, bare whitish face skin, and a pale yellow eye. Heavy black hooked bill; dark gray legs. Short square tail.
grepar1: Large brown parrotbill with rich rufous-brown upperparts, grayish face and underparts, a faint pale supercilium, and a short stout strongly curved orange-yellow bill. Long brown tail; grayish legs. Big-headed.
grepew: Large gray pewee, dull olive-gray above and paler gray below with a slightly bushy peaked crest. No bold wing bars; faint pale wing edging. Bill with dark upper and orange lower mandible; dark legs. Upright posture.
grepot1: Very large pale potoo, mottled grayish-white and pale brown with fine vermiculations, a large head, and huge dark eyes. Cryptic bark-like plumage, whitest of the potoos. Short wide-gaping bill; short legs.
grepuf1: Small green hummingbird, glittering green above and below, with conspicuous fluffy white leg puffs (powder-puffs) on the thighs, a slightly forked dark tail, and a straight black bill. Greenish underparts; tiny feet.
grerhe1: Very large flightless ratite, grayish-brown overall with a darker crown and upper neck, paler underparts, and loose shaggy plumage. Long bare neck and legs; three-toed gray feet. Small head; flat broad gray bill.
greroa: Large slender ground-cuckoo with long graduated tail held cocked; streaky brown-and-buff upperparts, pale streaked breast, and shaggy erectile crest. Bare blue-and-orange skin patch behind the eye, heavy pale bill, and strong gray legs.
greros1: Large heavy-billed rosefinch; male deep crimson-rose head, breast and rump spangled with silvery-pink spots, contrasting grayish-brown back and wings. Stout conical bill, dark eye, and dull pinkish-brown belly; female plain grayish-brown finely streaked.
greros2: Medium parrot with dull yellowish-green head and body, greener wings, and red frons and vent. Blue cheeks and bend of wing, dark-scalloped green back, and blue tail; pale bill and gray legs.
gresch2: Plain dull olive-green manakin-like bird, slightly paler greenish-yellow below, with a rounded head and short thick blackish bill. Large dark eye, uniform unmarked plumage, and pinkish legs.
grespi2: Large rufous-and-brown spinetail with bright rufous crown, wings and tail, grayish face and throat, and pale buff underparts. Bold blackish streaking on flanks, slender pointed dark bill, and long graduated rufous tail.
grests1: Large swallow with rufous-chestnut cap, rump and ear-coverts, finely streaked buffy underparts, and blue-black back. Dark blackish wings and deeply forked tail, dark bill and legs.
gretea1: Small dabbling duck, mottled gray-brown overall with paler scalloped feather edges, pale gray cheeks and throat, and a bright red eye. Dark crown, gray bill, and dark gray legs; green wing speculum with white border.
gretho2: Large rufous thornbird; bright rufous-chestnut crown, wings and tail, grayish-brown back, and whitish underparts with a paler throat. Slender dark bill and long rufous tail.
grethr1: Very large all-blackish thrush, sooty brownish-black overall, slightly paler below, with bright orange-yellow bill, narrow orange eye-ring, and orange-yellow legs.
gretin1: Large rotund tinamou with no visible tail; rich brown upperparts finely barred blackish, grayish head and neck, and barred buff flanks. Reddish-brown legs and slender slightly decurved bill, plump body.
gretin2: Tiny stubby barbet-like bird, plain olive-green above and yellowish-green below, with a short stout dark bill, plain unmarked face, and a small body; dull greenish overall lacking bold markings.
gretit2: Gray-and-white tit with glossy black head and throat, white cheeks, and broad black ventral stripe down white underparts. Gray back and wings with a white wing bar, gray flanks, dark bill and gray legs.
gretyr1: Small olive-green tyrannulet, greenish above and yellowish below, with a yellowish supercilium and dusky eyeline. Two pale yellowish wing bars, short dark bill, and pale legs.
grewar2: Small greenish leaf-warbler, olive-green above and whitish below, with a long pale yellowish supercilium and dark eyestripe. Two whitish wing bars (lower bolder), dark upper bill, pale lower bill, and dull pinkish legs.
grewoo2: Large slender glossy iridescent green-and-violet woodhoopoe with long graduated blue tail tipped white. Long decurved red bill and red legs, dark eye; metallic green head shading to purple wings.
grexen1: Stocky rufous furnariid with a short heavy upturned silvery bill; bright rufous-chestnut wings, tail and crown, plain pale buff face and underparts, and a bold pale throat. Short tail and pale legs.
greyel: Tall slim shorebird with long yellow legs; gray-brown upperparts spangled white, whitish streaked head and neck, and white belly. Long slightly upturned dark bill, white rump and barred tail.
greyel1: Large green woodpecker with bright yellow crest-nape, olive-green back and wings, and grayish-streaked throat. Chestnut barring on flight feathers, gray-green underparts, dark bill and gray legs.
grfdov1: Plump ground-dove; grayish forehead and crown, pinkish-buff breast, and warm brown back with reddish-rufous wing linings. White belly, dark bill, and reddish legs; rounded tail with white corners.
grfhaw1: Small slim sparrowhawk; pale blue-gray upperparts and head, white underparts faintly washed pinkish-orange, and broad rounded wings. Orange-red eye, yellow-orange cere and yellow legs, dark wingtips visible in flight.
grflan1: Small dark hummingbird with an exceptionally long straight needle-like black bill; glossy dark green plumage, bronzy-green back, and a glittering green forecrown. Dusky underparts, short tail.
grglea1: Robust leafbird; male bright leaf-green overall with a large black throat-and-face mask edged blue-violet malar streak. Stout blackish bill, dark eye, gray legs; female all green with paler yellow-green throat.
grgmac: Very large macaw, mostly bright yellowish-green with red forehead, blue lower back and rump, and blue-tipped wings. Bare whitish facial skin with fine dark feather lines, large blackish bill, dark legs, and long tapering tail.
grgtan1: Striking grass-green tanager with bright leaf-green body, contrasting rufous-chestnut face and throat, and gray crown. Bright red-orange bill and legs, dark eye; uniform green wings and tail.
grhcha1: Large slim brown chachalaca with a grayish head and neck, brownish body, and reddish-rufous in the primaries. Long broad dark tail tipped buff, small head, blackish bill, and reddish bare throat skin.
grhgul: Medium gull with a pale gray hood, white eye-crescents, and pale gray upperparts. White underparts and tail, black wingtips with white spots, pale yellowish eye, and dark red bill and legs.
grhkit1: Medium raptor with a pale gray head, blackish back and wings, and white underparts. Black crown patch, pale gray nape, long barred tail, bluish-gray bill base, and pale gray legs.
grhlar1: Large pale sandy-brown lark with a long decurved bill, bold black-and-white eyestripe pattern, and streaked breast. Long pale legs, and striking black-and-white wing pattern conspicuous in flight; sandy upperparts.
grhori1: Bright oriole with a wholly green head, yellow-green underparts, and olive-green back. Blackish wings and tail with yellow edgings, stout pinkish-red bill, and gray legs.
grhowl: Very large owl with prominent ear tufts, mottled gray-brown plumage finely barred below, and a white throat patch. Rusty-buff facial disc rimmed black, large yellow eyes, and feathered legs to dark talons.
grhpip1: Small plump flycatcher-like bird; olive-green above with a contrasting gray head and crown, yellowish-olive underparts, and a pale eye-ring. Short stout dark bill and pale legs.
grhtan1: Tanager with a soft gray head and short bushy crest, olive-green back, and bright yellow underparts. Plain gray hood, pale eye, stout dark bill, and gray legs.
grhtan2: Spectacular multicolored tanager; bright green head, orange-rufous nape band, black upper back, and turquoise-blue rump and underparts. Black-and-green wings, dark bill, and gray legs.
gripig1: Large pigeon with glossy metallic green upperparts, wings and tail, and pale gray head, neck and underparts. Coppery-green gloss on the back, dark bill with paler tip, and dull red legs.
grnbee3: Slender green bee-eater with bronzy-green plumage, a slim black eye-mask and thin black gorget, and bluish wash on chin and throat. Long slender decurved black bill, and elongated central tail streamers.
grnher: Small stocky dark heron; greenish-black cap, chestnut-maroon neck and breast, and slaty blue-green back and wings. Streaked white throat-front, short yellow-green legs (orange when breeding), and dark dagger bill.
grnjay: Vivid jay with bright green back, wings and tail, a blue-and-black head pattern, and yellow underparts. Black throat and breast bib, blue crown and cheeks, dark bill, and dark legs.
grnkin: Small kingfisher; oily dark green above spotted white, white collar and underparts, and a broad rufous breast band (male) or green-spotted bands (female). Long heavy black bill and short dark legs.
grnpar: Medium all-green parakeet, bright grass-green overall slightly paler below, with a long pointed green tail. Pale horn bill, bare whitish eye-ring, dark eye, and pinkish-gray legs.
grnvie1: Glittering green hummingbird with a violet-blue ear patch and violet-blue patch on the lower throat-breast. Bright green back and underparts, slightly decurved black bill, and a blue-green slightly forked tail.
gropar1: Slim ground parrot, bright grass-green heavily barred and mottled black and yellow, with a red frontal band above the bill. Long slender green tail, pale bill, and pinkish-brown legs.
grothr1: Pale grayish-brown thrush with a whitish face, two black malar stripes, and heavily black-spotted whitish underparts. Pale brownish-gray upperparts with rufous wing edgings, yellowish bill and legs.
grotit1: Small sandy gray-brown tit-like bird with a pale buffy face, plain unmarked grayish-brown upperparts, and pale buff underparts. Slender slightly decurved black bill, dark eye, and blackish legs.
growea1: Heavy-billed weaver; male sooty blackish-brown overall with a small white forehead patch and white wing flash. Massive conical bluish-gray bill, dark eye, and dark legs; female streaked brown.
grpchi: Stocky brown grouse heavily barred buff and dark brown overall, with a short rounded dark tail. Male shows orange-yellow eye combs and inflatable orange neck air sacs framed by long blackish neck pinnae; pale feathered legs.
grpeag1: Huge eagle with a shaggy brown-and-white crest, dark brown back and wings, and whitish underparts. Pale buff head streaked dark, large pale blue eyes, heavy arched grayish bill, and powerful yellow legs.
grpfin1: Large finch, dull olive-green above and grayish below, with a stout conical yellow-and-black bill. Yellow-green wing edgings, gray head and breast, and dull greenish-olive upperparts; pale legs.
grpsni1: Plump shorebird; female (brighter) has chestnut head and neck, white eye-spectacles, green-glossed back, and a white breast band over white belly. Long slightly decurved bill, buff scapular stripes, and greenish legs.
grrpar1: Tiny stubby parrotlet; male bright green with a yellower-green face, bright blue rump, and blue primary coverts. Short pointed green tail, pale horn bill, and pinkish legs; female all green without blue.
grrswi1: Small slim swift with blackish-brown plumage, a contrasting pale gray rump band, and grayish underparts. Short squarish tail, scythe-shaped narrow wings, and small dark bill.
grrwoo1: Very large, robust woodcreeper with rich rufous-brown upperparts, wings and tail; head and underparts paler buff-brown with fine pale streaking on throat and breast. Long, slightly decurved, pale horn-grey bill; dark legs.
grsbab1: Small, plump warbler-like bird with olive-green upperparts and yellowish-green underparts. Grey head with faint pale eyering, short stout pale bill; whitish wing edgings. Pinkish legs; rounded body and short tail.
grsbop1: Crow-sized male is velvety black with iridescent metallic blue-green erectile breast shield and a long blue-green nape cape that fans into a crescent. Short black bill, blackish legs; female brown above, barred buff below.
grsfly1: Slim crested bird, soft grey overall with a pointed grey crest, blackish wings and tail, and a long black-tipped white-banded tail. Yellow undertail coverts, white eye-crescents. Short black bill; dark legs.
grshon1: Medium-sized brownish honeyeater, dull olive-brown overall, faintly streaked, with a conspicuous diagnostic white crescent patch below and behind the eye. Long slender slightly decurved blackish bill; dark legs.
grsplo: Medium plover, sandy grey-brown above and white below; breeding male has rufous breast band and rufous-and-black mask through eye with white forehead. Longish heavy black bill; greyish-green to yellowish legs.
grsvir1: Stocky green vireo with bright yellow-green upperparts and yellow throat fading to grey-green belly. Blue-grey crown and nape, bold yellow supercilium, dark eyeline, red eye. Heavy pale hooked bill; bluish-grey legs.
grswar2: Large plain warbler, warm rufous-brown above and buffy-rufous below with paler throat. Faint pale supercilium, no streaking; long graduated tail. Long pointed dark bill with pale lower mandible; greyish legs.
grswoo1: Huge slaty-grey woodpecker, uniformly dark grey overall with paler throat; male shows a small red moustachial patch. Very long straight pale chisel bill; long neck, dark legs. The largest woodpecker of Asia.
grtcha1: Small warbler-like chat; male has slate-grey upperparts, head and breast with a clean white throat and supercilium, and a bright rosy-red belly and undertail. Thin black bill; dark legs. Female duller buff and grey.
grtdro1: Glossy blue-black drongo with a tufted forehead crest and two extremely long bare tail-shafts ending in twisted racket-shaped webs. Stout black hooked bill, red eye; black legs. Forked tail base above the streamers.
grtgol1: Small hummingbird, glittering golden-green above and below with a coppery-bronze wash on belly; greenish forked tail. Straight black bill with reddish base; tiny dark legs. Female paler with whitish underparts.
grtgra: Large, long iridescent purple-black grackle with a keel-shaped tail and pale yellow eyes; male glossy with blue-purple sheen. Female much smaller, dark brown above, buff below. Long pointed black bill; black legs.
grtjac1: Slender jacamar with glittering golden-green head, back and breast band, white throat (male) and rufous-chestnut belly; long iridescent green tail. Very long straight thin black bill; short dark legs.
grtkne1: Large heavy stone-curlew, sandy grey-brown above and whitish below, with a striking black-and-white face pattern and white forehead. Massive upturned black bill with yellow base; large yellow eyes, stout greyish-yellow legs.
grtlea1: Dark terrestrial bird with deep rufous-brown upperparts, blackish tail, and grey-brown breast; diagnostic clean grey-white throat. Longish straight slender black bill; dark legs; rounded body, short tail.
grtsun1: Small sunbird; male has glittering green crown, maroon-red back and breast, yellow belly, and a long green-blue tail with elongated central feathers. Long decurved black bill; dark legs. Female olive-green and yellowish.
grttra1: Tiny green hummingbird with glittering golden-green upperparts and throat, and an extraordinarily long deeply forked iridescent green tail. Short straight black bill; whitish belly in female. Tiny dark legs.
grwpyt1: Small finch with grey head, olive-green back and wings, scarlet-red face (male), and white-spotted underparts on a yellowish-green breast; bright red rump. Stout reddish conical bill; pinkish legs.
grwsal1: Large stocky saltator, olive-green above with a grey head, bold white supercilium, white throat bordered by a dark malar, and greyish underparts. Heavy black conical bill; dark legs.
grwtyr1: Tiny slim tyrant, grey-brown above and pale yellowish below, with bold white supercilium, white wing edgings and a long graduated black-and-white tail often cocked. Small black bill; dark legs.
gryant1: Small antwren, uniform blue-grey overall, slightly paler below, with a concealed white interscapular patch and faint pale wing markings. Male darker grey, female greyer-buff. Thin black bill; greyish legs.
gryant2: Slender grey antbird; male uniform dark slate-grey with a long graduated tail tipped white. Female greyer-brown with paler buff underparts and white tail tips. Black bill; dark legs.
gryapa1: Small slim warbler-like bird, plain grey above and paler grey below with a whitish throat and belly; long graduated tail often cocked. Pale eye, fine black bill; pinkish legs.
grybun: Stocky bunting; breeding male slaty blue-grey overall with faint chestnut wing edgings and a streaked rufous-brown back. Pale pinkish conical bill; pinkish legs. Female streaky brown.
grybus1: Slim chat; male has grey upperparts streaked black, black mask and wings, white wing patch, white throat, and pale grey breast; rufous rump and tail edges. Thin black bill; black legs. Female warm brown.
grybut1: Grey-and-white butcherbird with grey upperparts, black head and face contrasting a white throat, and whitish underparts; white wing and tail markings. Stout blue-grey black-hooked bill; grey legs.
grycat: Slim slate-grey thrush-like bird, uniform dark grey overall with a black cap and a distinctive chestnut-rufous patch under the tail. Slender black bill; black legs. Long tail often raised.
grycro1: Large dull crow with sooty grey-brown body, blacker wings and tail, and a bare bluish-white skin patch around the pale staring eye. Stout black bill; black legs. Pale grey nape and underparts.
grycur1: Large dark grey crow-like bird with white wing patches, white undertail and white tail tip; sooty grey overall. Heavy black hooked bill; yellow eye; black legs.
grycus1: Slim grey cuckooshrike, uniform pale blue-grey above and on breast, whiter on belly, with blackish flight feathers and a dark mask. Black bill; dark legs. Long graduated tail.
gryela3: Small elaenia, olive-green above with a contrasting grey head and white-edged wing feathers forming two pale wingbars; whitish throat and yellowish belly. Short dark bill; dark legs. Often shows a concealed crown patch.
gryfan1: Tiny active flycatcher, soft grey-brown above and pale buff below, with white throat, two white wingbars, white eyebrow and white-edged broadly fanned tail. Fine black bill; dark legs.
gryfin1: Small finch, streaky olive-brown above and bright yellow below with yellow face; faintly streaked breast sides. Small conical greyish bill; pinkish legs. Yellowish supercilium.
gryfin2: Stocky finch, greenish-grey above and bright yellow below with a yellow face and head; grey wings and tail. Heavy conical greyish bill; pinkish legs. Larger and plainer than other yellow-finches.
gryfin3: Dull finch, greyish-olive above and pale greenish-yellow below with greyish flanks; faint streaking. Small conical grey bill; dark legs. Greenish-yellow face, overall washed-out appearance.
gryfly: Small drab empidonax flycatcher, plain pale grey above and whitish below with a faint white eyering and two faint pale wingbars. Slim bill, dark above with a pale base below; dark legs. Long tail often dipped.
gryfra: Plump grey-brown francolin finely barred and vermiculated grey, buff and brown overall, with a buff throat outlined by a fine dark necklace. Reddish-brown bill base; orange-red legs; rufous primaries.
grygab1: Plain grey go-away-bird, uniform smoky grey overall with a tall shaggy grey crest and a long grey tail. Short black bill; black legs. Slim, long-tailed turaco shape.
gryger1: Tiny warbler-like bird, grey-brown above and pale grey-white below with a faint pale face; red eye and small white tail spots. Fine black bill; dark legs. Plain, slim and active.
grygos1: Pale grey goshawk, uniform soft pale grey above and on head with clean white underparts; some birds pure white morph. Yellow cere and yellow legs; dark wingtips; red eye. Short hooked grey bill.
grygul: Medium gull, uniformly soft grey body with a darker grey hood (breeding), white forehead, blackish wings and a white-tipped black tail band. Black bill; black legs. Distinctly all-grey appearance.
gryhaw2: Compact hawk, pale grey above and finely grey-and-white barred below; grey head. Banded black-and-white tail with broad black bands. Yellow cere and yellow legs; dark hooked bill; dark eye.
gryhaw3: Pale grey hawk, soft grey above and finely grey-barred white below; plain grey head. Black tail with two white bands. Yellow cere and yellow legs; dark hooked bill. Very similar to Gray Hawk.
gryjay: Fluffy grey jay with pale grey upperparts, whitish forehead and face, dark grey nape patch, and pale grey-white underparts. Short stubby black bill; black legs. Rounded fluffy body, no crest.
grykin: Large pale grey kingbird, grey above and whitish below with a faint dusky mask; notched dark tail. Heavy black bill; black legs. Concealed orange-red crown patch; plain pale appearance.
grylon1: Small plain warbler, uniformly grey-green above and below with no markings; short tail extending past wingtips. Long straight thin pale-based bill; pinkish legs. Pale eye, nondescript slim shape.
grymon1: Slim flycatcher, pale grey above and on breast with a whitish belly and throat; blackish wings and tail with white outer tail feathers. Short black bill; black legs. Clean grey-and-white appearance.
grynig1: Cryptic grey-brown nightjar, finely vermiculated grey, brown and black overall, with bold dark scapular streaks; male shows white patches on wings and tail corners. Tiny black bill; brownish legs. Large dark eyes.
grynig2: Cryptic nightjar, grey-brown finely vermiculated with black and buff, dark scapular marks and a buffy collar; male has small white wing and tail-tip spots. Tiny black bill; short brown legs. Large dark eyes.
grypep2: Large brown pheasant with dense buff and black vermiculation; rounded crest and bare reddish-pink facial skin. Upperparts and wings spangled with bold metallic blue-green ocelli, repeated on the long tail. Stout grayish bill; gray legs with double spurs.
grysee1: Small finch with pale gray head, back and breast, fading to whitish belly. Plain wings show a small white speculum at base of primaries. Sexes differ; female buffy-brown. Short, stout conical bill is pale bluish-gray; dark legs.
grysht1: Medium gray-brown songbird with plain gray head and back, pale grayish-white underparts, and faint dusky streaking on throat and breast. Whitish lores and faint pale loral patch. Stout black bill; gray legs. Upright, robust thrush-like shape.
grysib1: Slender gray babbler with darker gray crown and nape contrasting slightly paler gray body, fading to whitish belly. Long graduated gray tail tipped paler. Slim slightly downcurved blackish bill; pale legs. Sleek, long-tailed silhouette.
grytif1: Small slaty-gray flycatcher, uniformly leaden-gray above and slightly paler gray below, often with a faint whitish eye-ring or pale lores. Long, often-cocked dark tail. Fine black bill; dark legs. Slim, active flycatcher shape.
grytin1: Large, plump, ground-dwelling tinamou, overall slaty-gray with fine dark vermiculation; blackish crown and a small white throat patch. Slightly browner wings and rear body. Short slim grayish bill; stout grayish legs. Rounded, almost tailless body.
grytit1: Small gray tit with black crown, nape and throat bib, bordered by bright white cheek patches. Gray back and wings show two whitish wing bars; gray underparts paler on belly. Short black bill; dark gray legs.
grytre1: Long-tailed gray treepie with black face mask, gray crown and underparts, and warm rufous-brown back. Black wings with a small white wing patch; very long graduated black tail with paler base. Stout black bill; dark legs.
gryvir: Plain gray vireo, gray-olive above and whitish below with faint pale flanks. Narrow whitish eye-ring, faint pale lores, and a single faint pale wing bar. Short stout grayish bill; gray legs. Drab, big-headed vireo shape.
grywhi2: Small plain flycatcher, uniformly soft gray above with a slightly browner-olive cast, paler gray throat and breast, and pale yellowish wash on lower belly. Faint pale lores. Short dark bill; grayish legs. Nondescript, rounded shape.
grywoo1: Medium woodpecker with plain gray head and underparts, olive-green back and wings, and a bright red rump and lower belly patch. Male shows a small red crown patch; female all-gray crown. Faint dark eye-line. Gray bill and legs.
grywrw1: Small grayish-brown warbler densely barred with fine dark wavy lines across the gray underparts, giving a scaly look; plainer grayish-brown crown and back. Longish, often-cocked barred tail. Slim black bill; pinkish legs.
gsbfin1: Olive-and-gray brushfinch with blackish head crossed by a bright green-striped pattern: green-yellow median crown stripe and superciliary edges. Olive-green back and wings, gray breast, white belly. Stout black bill; pinkish legs.
gstswi1: Large black-and-white swift with deeply forked tail, glossy blackish upperparts, and a clean white throat and breast separated from white flank patches by a black band. Long scythe-shaped wings. Tiny black bill; dark feet.
gtmgem1: Plump highland hummingbird with bronze-green upperparts and a bright green throat (gorget) edged by a white malar stripe and dark cheek. Grayish-white underparts; dark blue-green tail. Straight black bill; dark feet.
guaiab1: Stocky short-tailed green parrot, bright grass-green overall, palest yellow-green below, with the male's blue lower face and rump scaled blackish on nape and breast. Heavy pale horn-colored bill; gray legs. Dumpy, large-headed shape.
guawoo2: Large black-and-white woodpecker; male has a fully red head and crest, female red only on rear crown with black-and-white face. Black back with white scapular stripes meeting in a V; barred buff underparts. Heavy ivory bill.
gufgle1: Medium rufous-brown foliage-gleaner streaked with buff on the auriculars, mantle and underparts; buffy supercilium and white throat contrast with the dark head. Rufous tail and wings. Straight, slightly upturned pale bill; grayish legs.
guicuc1: Slender scruffy cuckoo with shaggy orange-rufous crest, pale buff streaked head and breast, and dark gray-brown wings and back. Long broad tail blackish at center with broad white-and-buff outer feathers. Stout orange bill; bare yellow facial skin.
guipuf1: Chunky black-and-white puffbird with broad white forehead and throat, black crown and broad black breast band, and white belly. Black back and wings. Very heavy, slightly hooked black bill; dark legs. Big-headed, stubby shape.
guista1: Tiny antwren; male boldly streaked black-and-white over the entire head, back and underparts with a black throat. Female warm orange-rufous head and streaked back, buff underparts. Slim black bill; gray legs. Thin, short-tailed shape.
guitan1: Small tanager; male has glossy black hood and throat, olive-yellow back, bright yellow-orange breast, and white belly. Female olive above with yellow throat and breast. Short pointed black bill; gray legs.
guitou1: Small stocky toucanet, mostly black-headed and dark green above with a chestnut nape collar and red around the eye; underparts black with bold yellow spotting on the flanks. Heavy bill greenish with reddish base; gray legs.
guitur1: Medium green turaco with a tall green crest tipped white-and-black, crimson eye-ring, and red bill. Glossy green head and breast, blue-black wings flashing crimson primaries in flight, and a glossy blue-green tail. Dark legs.
guityr1: Tiny olive-green flycatcher, olive above with a grayer forehead and crown, grizzled face, and pale yellowish underparts. Wing feathers narrowly fringed yellow but lacking distinct wing bars. Small stubby dark bill; longish tail; dark legs.
