guiwaa1: Small antbird; male gray above with rufous-edged wings, white throat and breast finely streaked black, and rufous flanks and belly. Bold white-tipped black tail flicked open. Female browner-buff below. Slim black bill; pinkish legs.
gurcot1: Stocky cotinga; male has a glossy bright red crown and dark maroon-brown to blackish head and back, rufous-brown wings, and a bright red tail with a dark terminal bar. Crimson underparts. Short broad blackish bill; dark legs.
gybant1: Small antbird; male plain gray above and pale gray below with a whitish belly and faint white wing markings; black-and-white scapular spots. Female browner with buff underparts. Slim black bill; pinkish-gray legs. Plump, short-tailed shape.
gybfly1: Small empidonax-like flycatcher, grayish-olive above with a gray breast and crown, white throat, and pale yellowish belly. Bold whitish eye-ring and two pale wing bars. Small bill dark above, pale orange below; dark legs.
gybhaw1: Medium forest hawk; adult slate-gray above and on head with a clean white throat and finely gray-barred white underparts. Long banded gray tail; broad rounded wings. Yellow cere and legs; hooked black-tipped bill; yellow eyes.
gybhaw2: Medium broad-winged hawk, slaty-gray above with a contrasting white head, neck and underparts. Black-and-white barred tail and broad rounded wings. Bare yellow facial skin and yellow legs; black-tipped bill. Bold pied appearance.
gybmar: Medium swallow, glossy steel-blue above on crown, back and wings, with a pale grayish-brown throat and breast grading to whitish belly; slightly forked tail. No breast band. Small black bill; dark feet. Sleek martin shape.
gybmot1: Large mountain-toucan; powdery blue-gray breast and underparts, black crown, golden-yellow nape patch, and olive-bronze back. Red undertail coverts. Massive bill black at tip, red-and-yellow at base; gray legs. Bulky, big-billed shape.
gybpri1: Small slim warbler; breeding adult has a gray crown and gray-brown back, a gray breast band across whitish underparts, and a long graduated tail tipped white. Short fine black bill; pinkish legs. Slender, long-tailed shape.
gybsab1: Large hummingbird, bronze-green above with a uniformly pale gray throat and underparts and bold white tail corners. Broad slightly downcurved blackish bill; thick-set body. Dark feet. Hefty, broad-winged hummingbird.
gybsee1: Plump, partridge-like shorebird, cryptically scaled brown above; male has plain gray face, throat and breast crossed by a black inverted-T breast band and white belly. Short yellowish conical bill; yellow legs. Squat, ground-hugging shape.
gybshr1: Medium shrike with a dark gray back and crown, broad black face mask, and pale buff-white underparts washed warm on flanks. Black wings with small white patch; long black tail. Stout hooked black bill; black legs.
gybspi1: Small plain spinetail, dull gray-brown above with a slightly rufous-tinged crown and wings, and uniform pale gray underparts. Long graduated brown tail. Fine dark bill; grayish legs. Slim, skulking, long-tailed shape.
gybtac1: Tiny flycatcher with brownish-gray upperparts, a short crest and gray crown, a short white supercilium and white eye-ring, and a thin black eye-line on a black-and-white striped face. Two pale wing bars; cinnamon-washed breast. Tiny black bill.
gybtai1: Small tailorbird, gray-backed with a rufous crown, whitish supercilium, and whitish underparts; finely black-streaked face and breast sides. Long, often-cocked graduated tail. Slim slightly downcurved bill; pinkish legs.
gybter1: Medium tern, pale gray above and on the back and rump with a clean white forehead, black cap and black eye-line, and white underparts. Deeply forked gray tail. Slender black bill; black legs. Elegant pelagic tern.
gybtes1: Tiny, almost tailless warbler; bright olive-green above with a gray crown and gray underparts, a yellowish supercilium, and a dark eye-line. Stubby cocked tail. Fine bill dark above, pale below; pinkish legs. Round, plump shape.
gybthr1: Medium thrush; male has uniform blue-gray upperparts, head and breast, a white belly, and bright orange-rufous flanks. Bold yellow eye-ring and yellow bill. Female browner. Pinkish-yellow legs. Upright thrush shape.
gybwow3: Small wren, warm brown above with a bold white supercilium and black-and-white streaked cheeks, a white throat and breast, and grayish flanks. Faintly barred wings and short tail. Slim dark bill; pinkish legs. Compact, perky shape.
gycbab1: Medium gray-brown babbler with a gray crown and a long bold white supercilium over a dark eye-line; white throat and breast, gray-brown back and wings, and a long white-tipped dark tail. Slim slightly downcurved bill; dark legs.
gycbul2: Medium bulbul, olive-brown above with a small bushy crest, gray cheeks, and a whitish throat; pale yellowish-buff underparts. Plain olive wings and tail. Slender dark bill; grayish legs. Slim, crested bulbul shape.
gycfly1: Small flycatcher, olive-green above with a gray crown and face, pale yellowish underparts, and two faint pale wing bars on olive wings. Broad flat dark bill; pale eyes. Grayish legs. Big-headed, flat-billed shape.
gycful1: Small drab fulvetta, warm olive-brown above with a gray crown and nape bordered by faint blackish lateral crown stripes, and pale gray face and buffy-white underparts. Faint pale eye-ring. Small dark bill; pinkish legs.
gycful4: Small fulvetta, warm brown above with a gray crown edged by thin dark lateral stripes, gray face, and buffy underparts; very similar to Morrison's Fulvetta but greener-toned above. Faint pale eye-ring. Small dark bill; pinkish legs.
gycful5: Small fulvetta, olive-brown above with a gray crown bordered by narrow blackish lateral crown stripes, gray cheeks, and pale buffy-white underparts. Faint pale eye-ring. Short stubby dark bill; pinkish legs. Plain, round-bodied shape.
gycgre1: Small drab vireo-like bird with olive-green upperparts, a plain gray head and throat shading to gray breast, and whitish-yellow belly. Pale eye, short straight grayish bill, and pale legs. No wing bars or eye-ring.
gycher1: Tiny hermit hummingbird with bronze-green upperparts, rufous-buff underparts, and a grayish throat. Black mask bordered by buff facial stripes, long decurved bill with yellow-orange lower mandible, and a graduated tail with white-tipped central feathers.
gycill1: Plump dark babbler with rich rufous-brown upperparts, slate-gray head and throat, and gray-tinged chest fading to rufous-brown belly. Stout blackish bill, dark eye, and pinkish-gray legs; rounded short-tailed shape.
gycjuf1: Plain stocky jungle flycatcher with warm olive-brown upperparts, grayish head, and a gray-washed breast fading to whitish belly. Dark eye, short blackish bill, pale legs; no obvious wing bars or supercilium.
gycmin1: Slim minivet; male has gray head and back, orange-red rump, breast and belly, blackish wings with an orange wing patch, and a dark orange-edged tail. Fine black bill, dark legs; female yellower below with grayer crown.
gycnun1: Tiny puffbird with warm rufous-brown upperparts and breast, a contrasting gray cheek and face, and a buffy-white belly. Black bill, narrow bare orbital skin, and dark legs; rounded small-headed shape.
gycpar1: Small green parakeet with grayish-brown cheeks and crown, green body, and bright orange underwing coverts visible in flight. Pale horn bill, dark eye, and grayish legs; slender pointed tail.
gycthr: Slim brown thrush with gray-brown upperparts, a plain grayish face lacking strong eye-ring, and whitish underparts boldly spotted black on the breast. Dark bill with pale yellowish base, pinkish legs.
gyctib1: Small babbler with olive-brown upperparts, a yellow throat and breast finely streaked, gray cheeks, and a warm crown. Pale eye, short pointed bill, and pale legs; rounded body and longish tail.
gyctit1: Plump crested tit with a pointed gray crest, gray-brown back, plain buff-rufous underparts, and a pale gray face with a whitish nape patch. Short black bill, dark eye, and gray legs.
gyctyr1: Tiny tyrannulet with gray cap, olive-green back, two pale yellowish wing bars, and yellow-washed underparts. Faint pale supercilium, short dark bill, and dark legs; compact small-headed shape.
gyctyr2: Small drab tyrannulet with gray head, olive-brown back, whitish throat and yellowish belly. Whitish supercilium broken over the eye, broken pale eye-ring, short dark bill, and dark legs; short-tailed.
gycwar1: Small leaf-warbler with green upperparts, gray crown bordered by black lateral crown-stripes, a broad pale supercilium, yellow underparts, and one faint wing bar. Fine dark bill with pale base, pale legs.
gycwar2: Leaf-warbler with olive-green upperparts, slaty-gray crown and cheeks, a white eye-ring, and a yellow belly with whitish throat. Faint wing bar, thin dark bill, and pale legs.
gycwar3: Stocky warbler with bright olive-green upperparts and breast, a gray crown bordered by a chestnut frontal band, white throat, and yellow belly. Black bill, red eye, and pinkish legs; longish graduated tail.
gycwoo1: Tiny woodpecker with grayish-brown cap, barred black-and-white back and wings, white-streaked buffy underparts with fine streaking, and a white facial stripe. Short dark bill, dark eye; male shows small red mark at side of nape.
gycwor1: Large long-legged wood-rail with gray head and neck, rufous-chestnut breast and flanks, olive-brown back, and a black belly and tail. Bright yellow-green bill, red eye, and long coral-red legs.
gycyel: Yellowthroat warbler with olive-green upperparts, a gray crown and nape, blackish lores, broken white eye-ring, and entirely yellow underparts. Fairly heavy bill, pinkish legs; no black mask.
gyebul1: Plain brown bulbul with olive-brown upperparts, paler grayish-brown underparts, and a grayish-white iris that stands out. Dark bill, dark legs; slim long-tailed shape with no distinct markings.
gyegre1: Small greenlet with olive-green upperparts, a grayish-brown crown and head, and pale yellowish-white underparts. Whitish iris, short straight pale bill, and pale legs; plain face without wing bars.
gyfbuz1: Medium raptor with gray-brown head and gray face, brown upperparts, a white throat with dark central stripe, and white underparts barred rufous-brown. Yellow cere and legs, dark-tipped yellow bill; banded tail.
gyfcin1: Earth-brown ovenbird with dark grayish-brown crown and upperparts, a buff supercilium, white throat scalloped dark, and grayish breast with pale spots fading to whiter belly. Slightly decurved blackish bill, blackish legs; rufous wing patch shows in flight.
gyftib1: Dull babbler with warm orange-brown crown, olive-brown upperparts, a faintly gray face, and yellowish underparts faintly streaked on throat and chest. Short pointed bill, pale eye, and pale legs.
gygbus1: Plump bushshrike with grayish-green upperparts, a gray head, and pale yellowish-green underparts. Stout hooked black bill, dark eye, and grayish legs; rounded short-tailed shape with no bold markings.
gyhant1: Antbird with gray head and underparts, rufous-brown back and wings, and blackish-and-white spotted wing coverts. Black bill, dark eye, and pinkish-gray legs; female browner with buffier underparts.
gyhatt1: Large stout flycatcher with a gray hood and head, rich rufous-brown back, wings and tail, and pale grayish underparts. Heavy slightly hooked black bill, pale eye, and dark legs; upright big-headed posture.
gyhbab1: Babbler with rufous-brown upperparts, a gray head and nape, white throat, and warm brownish underparts. Slender pointed dark bill, dark eye, and pinkish legs; rounded body and medium tail.
gyhbat1: Small batis; male has a gray crown and back, black mask, white throat and underparts crossed by a broad black breast band, and black wings with a white wing stripe. Black bill, red eye, black legs.
gyhbri1: Robust bulbul with olive-brown upperparts, a gray head, bright yellow underparts, and conspicuous bright yellow-green bare orbital skin around the eye. Heavy pale bill, sturdy legs; large rounded shape.
gyhbul1: Small bulbul with olive-green upperparts, a plain gray head, and grayish-olive underparts, with a black throat and a small black-tipped crest. Yellow eye, pale bill, and dark legs.
gyhbul2: Bullfinch; male has a gray back and underparts, black face and cap, white rump, black wings and tail, and a pinkish-red wash on the breast. Stubby black conical bill, dark legs; female buff-brown below.
gyhbun1: Slim bunting with a plain gray head, sandy-brown back, and pinkish-buff underparts, with a bold whitish eye-ring. Pale pinkish bill, pinkish legs; lacks streaking, with a clean unmarked face.
gyhbus1: Large heavy bushshrike with gray head, olive-green back and wings, yellow-orange underparts, and a yellow forehead. Massive hooked black bill, pale eye, and bluish-gray legs; powerful long-tailed shape.
gyhbut1: Medium tanager with a gray head and hood contrasting with olive-green upperparts and yellow underparts. Distinctive pink bill and pink legs; dark eye, plump short-tailed shape.
gyhcaf1: Tiny flycatcher with a gray head, throat and breast, olive-green back and wings, and bright yellow belly. Small crest, white eye-ring, fine dark bill, and pale legs; compact active shape.
gyhdov1: Plump ground-dove with a gray crown and nape, warm brown back, pinkish-buff breast, and whitish belly. Reddish eye with bare red orbital skin, dark bill, and red legs; white-tipped outer tail feathers.
gyhfie1: Large brown eagle with a pale gray head and breast, dark brown back and wings, and a white belly. White tail with a broad black terminal band, heavy dark bill with yellow cere, and pale yellow legs.
gyhfly1: Small flycatcher with a gray hood and head, olive-green back, and rufous-orange underparts. Dark eye, short bill, and pale legs; upright posture and plain face without strong markings.
gyhkin1: Striking kingfisher with a pale gray head and breast, black back and wing coverts, bright chestnut belly, and brilliant turquoise-blue wings, rump and tail. Long bright red bill and red legs.
gyhlap1: Elegant plover-like lapwing with a gray head and neck, brown back, white belly, and a black band across the lower breast. Yellow bill tipped black, yellow eye-ring, and long yellow legs.
gyhlov1: Small green parrot; male has a pale gray head, neck and breast contrasting with a green body, and green underparts. Pale horn bill, dark eye, and gray legs; female all green with green head.
gyhneg1: Finch-like estrildid with a gray crown and nape, black face, throat and underparts, and a black back spotted white on the wing coverts. Heavy black bill, red eye, and dark legs.
gyhpar1: Small parakeet with grayish head and breast, green upperparts, and a yellowish-olive belly washed dull blue. Long pointed tail, pale horn bill, dark eye, and pinkish legs.
gyhpar2: Slender parakeet with a gray head, green body, a maroon-red shoulder patch, and a long blue-green tail tipped yellow. Upper mandible red, lower mandible blackish; pale eye and grayish legs.
gyhpar3: Large parrotbill with rufous-brown upperparts, a gray crown, white throat with a black central stripe, and pale grayish-buff underparts. Short stout yellowish bill, dark eye, and grayish legs.
gyhrob2: Plump ground-robin with olive-brown upperparts, a gray head with white spots before and behind the eye, white throat, and gray breast fading to whitish belly. Black bill, dark eye, and long pinkish legs.
gyhsif1: Stocky finch; male has a gray hood and head, olive-yellow back, and bright yellow underparts. Short conical yellowish bill, dark eye, and yellowish legs; clean contrast between gray hood and yellow body.
gyhsow1: Small sparrow-like weaver with a gray cap, sandy gray-brown upperparts, whitish underparts, and dark-scaled wing coverts. Pale horn conical bill, dark eye, and pinkish legs; plain unstreaked plumage.
gyhspa1: Sparrow with a plain gray head, chestnut-brown back streaked black, gray rump, and pale grayish-white underparts, with a small white wing bar. Stout black bill, dark eye, and pinkish legs.
gyhspi1: Slim spinetail with rufous crown, wings and tail, a gray face and neck, brown back, and pale grayish underparts. Slender pointed dark bill, dark eye, and pale legs; long graduated rufous tail.
gyhtof1: Tiny flycatcher with plain gray head and nape, olive-green back and wings, and bright yellow underparts. Long straight black bill, pale eye, slim body, and pale legs; flat-headed tody-flycatcher shape.
gyhwar2: Small leaf-warbler-like bird with a uniform gray hood and breast contrasting with bright yellow lower underparts, olive-green back and wings, and a pale whitish supercilium. Thin dark bill, pale legs.
gyhwoo1: Medium woodpecker with gray-washed head and olive-bronze barred upperparts, finely streaked grayish-olive underparts, and a red rump. Male shows a red hindcrown; straight gray bill, gray legs.
gyltin1: Plump tailless ground bird with uniform dull dark brown upperparts, slightly paler grayish-brown underparts, and a grayer head and neck. Slender slightly decurved bill; gray legs and feet.
gymwre1: Small slim wren with plain bluish-gray crown, nape and mantle, contrasting rufous-brown wings and tail finely barred black, and pale gray underparts. Thin pointed bill, fine dark eyeline.
gynant1: Plump round antpitta with dark brown crown and upperparts crossed by a broad slate-gray band over the eye and nape, bright rufous-chestnut face and underparts, paler olive belly center. Stout bill, long pale legs.
gyogre1: Plain drab greenbul with olive-brown upperparts, grayish-olive head and throat, and pale buffy-cinnamon belly and undertail. Slender pale bill, dark eye, longish tail; nondescript shape.
gyrtre1: Sleek crested treeswift, pale blue-gray above and on head with a slightly darker gray rump, whitish-gray underparts, very long slender swept-back wings, and a deeply forked tail. Tiny black bill.
gysbuw1: Small skulking warbler with warm rufous-brown upperparts, grayish face and underparts, and grayer flanks. Faint buffy supercilium, dark eyeline, thin pointed bill; long rounded tail, pinkish legs.
gysfly1: Slim gray-brown flycatcher with white underparts heavily streaked gray-brown across breast and flanks, plain grayish head, and pale lores. Long wings, short black bill, dark legs; upright posture.
gysscb1: Long-billed babbler with rufous-brown upperparts, gray crown and sides of neck contrasting with white throat and breast, and rich rufous flanks. Strikingly long decurved pale bill; chestnut ear patch.
gysthr1: Olive-brown thrush with gray-washed head and flanks, gray breast band, white belly and undertail, and a clean whitish supercilium. Yellow bill and orbital ring; orange-yellow legs.
gytbab1: Small babbler with olive-brown upperparts, a dark blackish crown finely streaked white, and a gray throat and breast, whitish belly. Thin dark bill, dark eyeline, pale supercilium; pinkish legs.
gytbar1: Stocky sooty-black barbet with a gray throat, dull blackish-brown body, and tufts of bristly buff feathers at the bill base. Heavy pale ivory bill; bare yellowish skin around the eye.
gytmar1: Small dull brown martin with gray-brown upperparts and head, gray-brown throat and breast washed dusky, and whitish belly, lacking a distinct breast band. Short notched tail, tiny black bill, dark legs.
gytpih1: Plump cotinga, plain leaden gray overall, slightly paler below, with a grayer tail and a faint pale eye-ring. Short broad black bill, dark eye, sturdy body; uniform unmarked plumage.
gyttat1: Medium gray shorebird, uniform gray above and on head with a white supercilium, finely gray-barred white underparts, and a plain gray rump and tail. Straight dark bill with yellow base; short yellow legs.
gyttif1: Small dark flycatcher with slate-gray upperparts and head, a plain gray face, gray throat and breast grading to whiter belly, and an all-dark tail. Short black bill, dark eye, dark legs; upright posture.
gytwaf1: Medium finch with brown-gray upperparts, gray throat and upper breast, rich rusty flanks, and a white belly. Bold white supercilium, white cheeks and chin; conical bill, white in the tail.
gytwar1: Warbler with olive-green upperparts, a gray head with white broken eye-ring, a gray throat and breast band, and yellow lower underparts. Thin dark bill, slim body; pale legs.
gywbla1: Black thrush (male) with a broad pale silvery-gray panel along the closed wing, glossy black body, orange-yellow bill and eye-ring, and dark legs. Slim long-tailed shape.
gywfra1: Mottled brown francolin with finely vermiculated gray-brown upperparts, a gray-washed face and neck, and a chestnut-bordered whitish throat patch. Underparts barred and scaled brown; yellowish legs, dark bill.
gywinf1: Trim gray finch with brownish back, gray head and underparts, whitish belly, and a black face mask. Bright yellow conical bill and yellow legs; white outer tail feathers flashed in flight.
gywroc1: Robin-chat with olive-brown upperparts, blue-gray wing panel, gray crown and face, white supercilium, and bright orange-rufous breast and flanks fading to whitish belly. Thin dark bill, pale legs.
gywtru1: Large long-legged terrestrial bird, velvety black overall with a hunched neck, glossy gray-and-pale plumage panel across the wings, and soft gray inner secondaries. Short pale bill, long gray legs.
habbar1: Stocky barbet with blackish upperparts spotted yellow-white, a black-streaked yellowish face, and pale underparts coarsely streaked black with a dark breast band. Heavy black bill ringed by bristles.
habbul1: Olive-brown bulbul with whitish-tipped feathers giving a faintly scaled look, a pale grayish throat, and bright pale yellowish eyes. Short bill, fine pale shaft streaks; plain longish tail.
habfly1: Small flycatcher, male deep indigo-blue above with a brighter blue forehead, blackish face, and a paler blue throat grading to whitish belly. Pale buffy-blue breast wash; small black bill, pale legs.
hacant1: Antbird with dark gray-brown body, a shaggy black-and-white-spotted crest, blackish face, and bare blue skin around the eye. Underparts gray; heavy black bill, dark legs; upright stance.
hacdro1: Glossy black drongo with iridescent blue-green spangling on the breast and head, a long deeply forked tail with outcurled tips, and fine elongated hair-like crest feathers. Heavy black bill, red eye.
hacspa1: Sparrow with olive-green back and wings, a black head with white throat broken by a black half-collar across the breast, and grayish underparts. Bright orange-yellow conical bill; gray legs.
hadibi1: Large dull glossy-brown ibis with iridescent green-purple sheen on the wing coverts, a grayish-brown head and neck, and a long decurved dark bill marked with a red ridge on top. Dark legs.
haiwoo: Black-and-white woodpecker with a clean white back stripe, black wings spotted white, white underparts, and a boldly black-and-white striped head. Male has a red nape patch; long straight black bill.
halori1: Oriole entirely dull brownish-olive to grayish, lacking bright yellow, with a paler grayish head and faintly streaked underparts. Stout pinkish-red bill, dark eye; medium thrush-like build.
hamerk1: Uniform dull brown wading bird with a distinctive thick backward-pointing crest forming a hammer-shaped head. Plain brown body and wings; stout black bill, black legs; broad rounded wings.
hamfly: Small drab Empidonax flycatcher, grayish-olive above with a grayer head, a bold whitish eye-ring, two whitish wing bars, and a pale yellowish-washed belly. Short narrow bill dark above, pale below.
hanfly1: Small flycatcher with olive-green upperparts, a rich cinnamon-rufous breast and throat, yellow belly, and a gray crown with broken whitish eye-ring. Two buff wing bars; small bill, pale legs.
hanfru1: Plump fruiteater, male bright green above with a black head and throat bordered by a bold yellow band, yellow-streaked green underparts, and red-tipped wing feathers. Short orange-red bill; coral legs.
hapwre1: Bright wren with rich rufous-brown upperparts, a bold white supercilium and black-and-white streaked face, white throat and breast, and warm cinnamon flanks. Long barred tail; slender decurved bill.
harbab1: Grayish-brown babbler with paler gray-streaked underparts, a whitish throat and face, and a conspicuous white rump. Pale eye, slim dark decurved bill, longish tail; pale legs.
hareag1: Massive powerful eagle with slate-gray upperparts, a black chest band, white underparts, and a pale gray head crowned by a double black crest. Huge yellow talons; heavy hooked black bill.
harlew1: Bright olive-green leaf warbler with a broad yellow supercilium, a pale yellowish median crown stripe, and yellow-washed whitish underparts. Two faint wing bars; broad orange-based bill, pale legs.
harqua1: Small quail, male with bold black-and-white face markings, chestnut and black blotched underparts, and cryptically mottled brown upperparts. Black throat patch outlined white; gray bill, pale legs.
harspa: Large sparrow with a black crown, face and bib, gray cheeks and nape, brown-streaked back, and clean white underparts. Pink conical bill; brownish flank streaking, pinkish legs.
hartur1: Glossy green turaco with a tall blue-black crest tipped white, white facial streaks, a red eye-ring, and a deep blue back and tail. Crimson flight feathers flash in flight; stout red bill.
hattyr1: Tiny tyrant with olive-brown upperparts, a grayish head, whitish throat, and pale buffy-white underparts faintly streaked on the breast. Pale eye, short bill, two faint wing bars; slim build.
hauthr1: Plain warm brown thrush, uniform rufescent-brown above and on the head, paler buffy-brown breast, and whitish belly. Few faint dark throat streaks; dull horn bill, dark eye, pale legs.
hauthr3: Dull sepia-brown thrush, uniform brown above and below with a whitish lower belly, faint blackish throat streaks, and a grayish-brown tail. Diagnostic yellow-green bill and orange-yellow eye-ring.
hawama: Small honeycreeper, male bright yellow-green above and below, brightest on the face and underparts, with a dusky lore line. Short decurved black bill; slim warbler-like build, dark legs.
hawcoo: Slate-gray waterbird with a blackish head and neck, white undertail, and a large bulbous white frontal shield extending onto the forehead. White bill, red eye; greenish lobed-toed legs.
hawcre: Small, drab Hawaiian honeycreeper with olive-green upperparts and pale grayish-white underparts, a faint dark mask through the eye, and a whitish throat. Bill short, straight and pointed; legs gray. Round-bodied and warbler-like.
hawduc: Mottled brown dabbling duck resembling a small female Mallard, with buff-edged dark brown body feathers, a slightly paler head, and a greenish to blue speculum bordered with white. Bill olive-green to dusky in male, orange-and-dark in female; legs orange.
hawgoo: Medium goose with a buff-cheeked, black-faced head and black crown and nape; the buff neck shows deep diagonal black furrows. Body grayish-brown with bold pale-barred flanks, black bill and notably long, less-webbed black legs.
hawhaw: Compact broad-winged buteo occurring in two morphs: dark birds nearly uniform sooty brown, light birds dark brown above with creamy, lightly streaked underparts. Tail dark with fine barring; yellow cere and legs, dark hooked bill.
hawpet1: Long-winged gadfly petrel, dark gray-brown above and on the cap, with a clean white forehead, face and underparts; dark M-pattern across the upperwings and a dark underwing border. Bill black and stout-hooked; legs pink.
heegul: Distinctive gull with overall slate-gray body, a white head (smudged gray in winter), and a sharply contrasting bright red black-tipped bill. Wings darker gray with white-trailing edge, black tail tip; legs black.
helcur1: Large, glossy blue-black curassow with a striking pale bluish-gray fig-shaped casque on the forehead above a red-orange bill. Belly and undertail white; some birds reddish. Tail long and black with white tip; legs pinkish-gray.
helfri1: Large dull gray-brown honeyeater with grayer underparts, a bare blackish face, and a prominent backward-pointing knob (casque) at the base of the heavy blackish decurved bill. Legs grayish; long-tailed and gangly.
helgui: Round-bodied gray ground bird densely spotted with white over the whole body, a near-naked bluish head with a bony horn-like casque, bare red-and-blue facial skin and red wattles. Bill short and pale; legs dark gray.
helhor1: Huge hornbill with blackish body, white belly and white-tipped long central tail feathers, and a bare wrinkled red neck. The massive yellow-and-red bill carries a solid red casque forming a thick helmet block. Legs dark.
helman1: Striking manakin (Antilophia galeata): male glossy black with a vivid red crown, erect red forecrown crest and red upper back, plus a long tail. Female and young olive-green. Bill small dark; legs grayish.
helpip1: Streaky brown pipit, buff-and-dark-streaked above, with a pale buffy supercilium, dark malar stripe, and warm buff breast finely streaked dark fading to plain whitish belly. Slender pale-based bill; pinkish legs with long hind claw.
helwoo1: Rich chestnut-rufous woodpecker (Celeus galeata) with a bushy pointed crest, black-barred upperparts and a black throat-and-breast patch; underparts barred buff and black. Bill pale ivory; legs gray; male shows a red malar stripe.
hemhor1: Slim black-and-white hornbill, blackish above with white belly and white-tipped tail; bare red facial and throat skin. Bill large, dull red with a low casque and a paler tip. Legs dark; long-tailed and lanky.
henspa: Small, flat-headed sparrow with a large pale olive-green face and nape, finely streaked rusty-and-black upperparts, and a whitish breast finely streaked black on the sides. Bill pale and stout; legs pinkish; short spiky tail.
heptan: Medium tanager: male brick- to orange-red overall, slightly duskier on the back and face giving a faintly masked look; female yellowish-olive. Bill thick, gray-black with a dark culmen and slightly hooked; legs gray.
heptyr1: Tiny, large-headed olive flycatcher with a short erectile rounded crest, pale eye, and a whitish throat; underparts pale yellowish faintly streaked, upperparts olive with two faint wingbars. Bill small dark; legs grayish.
herthr: Brown thrush with warm reddish-rufous rump and tail contrasting with grayer-brown upperparts, a whitish eye-ring, and white underparts boldly spotted black on the breast and throat. Bill dark with pale base; legs pinkish.
herwar: Warbler with a clean bright-yellow head, plain gray back, and white underparts; throat black in male, paler in female. Two white wing bars; bill thin dark; legs blackish. Compact and short-tailed.
heswoo1: Tiny black-and-white woodpecker with a large pointed black crest, gray-and-buff head, and bold black heart-shaped spots on white wing coverts; black back, white rump, black tail. Short chisel bill pale-based; legs grayish.
hhfgle1: Boldly patterned foliage-gleaner (Clibanornis erythrocephalus) with a bright rufous-henna hood, wings and tail contrasting with an olive-brown back and dull brown underparts. Bill thick, dark and slightly decurved; legs grayish.
hibfly1: Flycatcher (Cyornis whitei): male deep blue above with an electric-blue forehead and shoulder, darker indigo face, a bright rufous-orange throat and breast fading to white belly. Female duller brown-and-orange. Bill black; legs pinkish-gray.
higela2: Dark grayish-olive elaenia (Elaenia obscura) with lighter cheeks, a thin whitish eye-ring, a small dark loral spot, and two dull yellowish wing bars; underparts dull olive-yellow. Bill dark with pale base; legs dark.
higela3: Olive-brown elaenia (Elaenia sordida) with a small-headed, long-tailed look, gray throat and flanks, yellowish belly center, and two pale wing bars. No obvious crest. Bill dark with pale base; legs dark.
higgua1: Mostly blackish guan glossed greenish, with a bare reddish to pinkish face and a small dewlap (red in male, yellowish in female). Long broad black tail; bill blackish; stout red legs.
higmot1: Large green motmot with a pale blue-and-black crown patch, a black eye-mask, turquoise-edged wings, and green underparts shading rufous-tinged. Long blue-green tail ending in two racket-tipped spatulas. Bill heavy, black, serrated; legs gray.
higtin1: Plump rufous-brown tinamou with finely vermiculated dark upperparts, a blackish crown, a grayish face and throat, and warm rufous-buff underparts. Small head, slender slightly decurved bill; sturdy gray legs; near tailless.
hilfra2: Stocky brown spurfowl heavily mottled and streaked, with a buff-and-dark scaled breast, rufous-tinged flanks, and bare red skin around the eye and on the throat. Bill blackish, legs red with spurs.
hilmyn: Glossy black myna with purple-and-green sheen, a bold white wing patch shown in flight, and bright yellow wattles and bare skin on the nape and behind the eye. Bill orange-yellow; legs yellow.
hilpar1: Round partridge with a chestnut-and-black streaked crown, a black-bordered white throat, and a rufous-orange face and breast; flanks rufous-spotted white over gray-brown barred upperparts. Bill black; legs reddish.
hilpri1: Long-tailed prinia (Prinia atrogularis), olive-brown above with a gray head, bold white supercilium, and white underparts with a black-streaked breast and a black throat patch. Bill blackish; legs pinkish; graduated tail.
hilpri2: Long-tailed brown prinia (Prinia superciliaris) with a grayer crown, a long white supercilium, white throat and breast finely streaked dark, and buffy flanks. Bill blackish above, paler below; legs pinkish; strongly graduated tail.
hilsta1: Brilliant starling with iridescent blue-green head, back and breast, a glossy blue belly, and a striking creamy-white eye. Wings and tail glossed violet-blue; bill and legs black. Sleek and medium-sized.
himblu1: Small chat: male slate-blue above with a brilliant cobalt-blue brow, shoulder and rump, blackish face, and white throat with orange-rufous flanks and white central belly. Female brown with blue tail. Bill black; legs dark.
himcuc1: Slim gray cuckoo, plain blue-gray on head, breast and upperparts with a whitish belly boldly barred blackish; yellow eye-ring and barred undertail. Bill dark with yellow base; legs yellow. Hawk-like in flight.
himgri1: Massive pale vulture, creamy-buff to whitish body and wing coverts contrasting with dark brown flight feathers and tail, and a whitish downy head and ruff. Bill pale yellowish-horn, heavy; legs grayish-white; broad-winged.
himmon1: Spectacular pheasant: male iridescent, with a metallic green head, copper-gold nape, purple-blue back, glittering green-and-blue wings, white rump and rufous tail; black underparts. Bare blue eye-skin, dark bill, gray legs.
himowl1: Medium round-headed wood owl, mottled brown and buff above with whitish facial disc rings around dark eyes, no ear tufts; underparts buff with dark cross-bars and streaks. Bill yellowish; feathered legs.
himrub1: Chat-like bird: male olive-brown above with a brilliant scarlet throat bordered by a black breast band, a white supercilium and white malar stripe, and pale buff underparts. Bill black; legs pinkish-brown; rufous-edged tail.
himwoo1: Pied woodpecker, black above with large white shoulder patches and white-barred wings, buffy underparts unmarked or lightly streaked with a pinkish-red vent; male shows a red crown (black in female). Bill blackish; legs grayish.
hislic1: Long, slim lizard-cuckoo, grayish-brown above with a long graduated tail boldly tipped white below; gray throat and breast grading to rufous-buff belly, with bare red eye-ring. Long slightly decurved bill dark above, paler below; legs gray.
hispar1: Stocky green Amazon parrot with black-scalloped neck feathers, a white forehead and forecrown, blue tinges on the crown and wings, red speculum, and a maroon belly patch. Bill pale horn; legs grayish.
histro1: Trogon (Priotelus roseigaster): metallic green upperparts and crown, a gray throat and breast, and a bright red belly; tail dark blue boldly marked with white below, wing coverts vermiculated black-and-white. Bill yellow; legs grayish; orange eye.
hiswoo1: Boldly barred woodpecker, back and wings cross-barred black-and-yellowish, with a grayish-olive face and underparts, a red crown and nape, and a yellow rump. Bill blackish, chisel-shaped; legs grayish.
hoatzi1: Bizarre pheasant-sized bird with a spiky rufous crest, bare blue facial skin and red eyes, a streaked buff neck, dark brown upperparts edged buff, and rich chestnut belly and wing patches. Long broad buff-tipped tail; short bill; gray legs.
hobcuc1: Small bronze-cuckoo (Chalcites basalis) with greenish-bronze upperparts, a white-and-bronze face, a brown eyestripe, and white underparts with incomplete coppery-brown barring on the breast and flanks; rufous in the tail. Bill dark; legs gray.
hobher2: Small hermit hummingbird (Glaucis dohrnii) with greenish-bronze upperparts and warm cinnamon-rufous underparts; dusky face with white supercilium and white malar streak; bronze tail tipped white. Bill gently down-curved, black above, yellowish below; legs yellow.
hobkin1: Stout brown forest kingfisher with a heavy, broad, strongly hook-tipped pale bill; rufous-buff head and underparts, dark brown scaled upperparts, and a dark crown. Eyes dark; legs grayish; large-headed and short-tailed.
hobkit: Slender raptor with a small head and a deeply hooked, long-curved bill; plumage variable from gray above with barred underparts to dark sooty morph. Pale eyes, greenish-yellow cere and legs; broad paddle-shaped wings and barred tail.
hobvan1: Black-and-white vanga (Vanga curvirostris) with a glossy black crown, nape and upperparts, a black breast band, and clean white throat and underparts; a long, heavy, hooked blue-gray bill. Legs bluish-gray; medium-sized.
hodfro1: Medium frogmouth with a broad flat hooked bill and large dark eyes. Male grayish rufous-brown, intricately dappled with black, rufous and white bark-like markings; bold whitish spots on scapulars forming a partial collar, and irregular black-and-white barring across the breast. Cryptic plumage; short legs.
hodhac1: Small accipiter-like cuckoo with slaty-grey upperparts and head, blackish wings and a long grey tail barred with dark bands. Underparts white with bold rufous-and-dark streaking on the breast; throat whitish. Yellow eye-ring, yellow legs, dark bill with yellow base.
hodred1: Slim redstart; male has blue-grey crown and back, black face, throat and wings, and a large white wing patch. Lower breast, belly and rump are bright rufous-orange; central tail feathers dark, sides rufous. Fine black bill and dark legs.
hofwoo1: Medium woodpecker with black-and-white barred back and wings, plain pale buff-grey underparts, and a whitish face. Male has a red crown and nape with a small yellow patch on forehead; female lacks red on crown. Pale grey bill, greyish legs.
hogwea1: Robust, heavy-billed weaver entirely bright golden-yellow, slightly greenish on the back and wings. Lacks any black mask. Distinctive pale yellow or whitish eye, stout black conical bill, and pinkish-grey legs.
homtan1: Large, striking tanager with a black hood, back and throat, brilliant cobalt-blue rump, shoulders and crown patch, and rich golden-yellow underparts. Black wings and tail edged blue; bright red eye. Stout black bill and dark legs.
honeme1: Small green hummingbird; upperparts and crown glittering bronze-green, throat and breast shining emerald-green, lower belly whitish to greyish. Bluish-black forked tail. Bill straight, reddish-pink with a dark tip; legs dark.
hongre1: Plain medium greenbul, olive-brown above and paler olive-grey below, with a slightly darker greyish head. Distinctive bright yellow-white outer tail feathers conspicuous from below. Pale-tipped dark bill, greyish legs; rather nondescript bulbul shape.
hooant1: Tiny, plump, almost tailless antpitta with a bright orange-rufous hood covering the entire head, throat and neck, contrasting with olive-brown back, wings and tail and greyish underparts; small white crescent on the lower throat. Yellowish bill, pale legs.
hoober2: Plump cotinga-like bird with an entirely black hood (head, neck and upper breast), a thin yellow half-collar on the neck sides, chestnut-brown back, olive-green rump, and bright yellow belly. Wings and tail blackish edged olive. Stout dark bill.
hoobut1: Large black-and-white butcherbird with a glossy black head, throat, wings, back and tail, and clean white underparts, nape collar and rump. Heavy blue-grey hooked bill with a black tip; dark grey legs; dark eye.
hoocra1: Tall grey crane with a contrasting white head and upper neck and a small red bare crown patch. Body dark slate-grey, slightly darker on the back. Greenish-grey bill, blackish legs; bustled tertials over the tail.
hoocus1: Pale grey cuckooshrike with black flight feathers and a long black-tipped tail. Male has a full black hood and black upper breast; rest of body soft grey. Black bill, dark legs; slender, long-tailed shape.
hoogre1: Medium grebe with blackish crown, hindneck and back, contrasting white cheeks, foreneck and underparts. A flat-topped black crown with slightly raised rear gives a hooded look; red eye, dark dagger bill, dark lobed feet.
hoogro1: Stocky finch with a massive pale greenish-yellow bill. Male has a black hood, wings and tail, golden-olive back and bright yellow underparts, with white wing patches. Female duller, greyer. Pinkish legs.
hooori: Slender oriole with a long curved bill. Male is bright orange-yellow with a black throat, face mask and tail, and black wings showing two white wing bars. Bill black above, blue-grey below; legs blue-grey.
hoorob1: Small robin with a sharply contrasting pattern. Male has a black head and breast forming a hood, white throat, black back, and white underparts; black wings with a bold white wing stripe; black tail with white sides. Fine black bill.
hoosis1: Small siskin; male has a black hood (head and throat), olive-green back, bright yellow rump and underparts, and black wings and tail with broad yellow wing bars and yellow tail base. Short conical greyish bill; dark legs.
hootan1: Small tanager with a glossy black cap (hood) bordered by clean white face and underparts, pale grey back, and white throat. Reddish or orange-red eye; pale blue-grey bill; pinkish legs. Crisp black-grey-white pattern.
hootin1: Plump, round-bodied tinamou with a contrasting blackish cap and nape forming a hood, rich chestnut-brown to rufous body finely vermiculated darker, and rufous throat. Small head, slender slightly decurved bill, stout grey legs.
hoovis2: Small hummingbird; male iridescent bronzy-green overall with black crown and face sides, a glittering green forehead and throat shading blue-green and bordered black, a narrow white breast band with a golden-orange central spot, and a crimson tail. Short straight bill.
hoowar: Small wood-warbler; male olive-green above and bright yellow below with a striking black hood and bib enclosing a bright yellow face and forehead. White tail spots flash in flight. Thin dark bill, pale legs.
hooyel1: Yellowthroat warbler; male has a black hood/mask across the face and crown bordered above by a grey-blue band, olive-green upperparts, and uniformly bright yellow throat and underparts. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
horbab2: Plain skulking babbler, warm brown above and on the wings, with a greyish face and breast and a whitish throat and belly; faint rufous tones on flanks. Reddish or pale eye, dark bill, pinkish-grey legs; rounded short-tailed shape.
horgua1: Large black guan with glossy blue-black plumage, a white breast band, white-tipped tail, and white belly. Most distinctive is a bright red, upright horn-like casque on the forehead. Red legs and reddish bill.
horscr1: Huge, dark turkey-like waterbird, blackish overall glossed greenish with white-speckled head, neck and wing coverts and a white belly. A long thin upcurved spine-like horn projects from the forehead. Small chicken-like greyish bill; greyish legs with no webbing.
hotbar1: Medium barwing, warm rufous-brown overall with a finely streaked greyish crown and a whitish, finely streaked (hoary) throat. Wings and tail boldly barred black; pale eye-ring. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs; slim long-tailed babbler shape.
hotspi1: Plain rufous-and-grey spinetail; greyish-brown above with rufous crown, wings and tail, and pale grey underparts. Whitish, faintly streaked (hoary) throat. Long graduated rufous tail with pointed feathers; fine dark bill, greyish legs.
houbun2: Small pale bunting with finely dark-streaked sandy-grey upperparts, a streaked greyish head with pale and dark head stripes, and warm rufous-buff underparts and rufous wings. Pale bill with dark culmen, pinkish legs.
houcro1: Slim two-toned crow; glossy black face, throat, wings and tail contrasting with a dusky grey neck collar, nape and breast. Slender black bill, black legs; sleeker and longer-legged than typical crows.
houfin: Small finch with a stout conical bill. Male has bright rosy-red forehead, throat and breast and a red rump, with brown-streaked back, wings and belly; flanks and belly heavily streaked brown. Female plain grey-brown and streaky. Greyish legs.
houswi1: Compact swift with blackish-brown plumage, a contrasting white rump band and a whitish throat. Wings narrow and swept-back, tail short and slightly notched. Tiny black bill, dark feet.
houwre: Small, plain brown wren, warm brown above and pale greyish-brown below, with fine dark barring on the wings, flanks and tail and a faint pale eyebrow. Slender slightly decurved bill, pinkish legs; cocked tail.
hrshaw: Large dark hawk; deep chocolate-brown body with rich chestnut shoulders, wing linings and thighs, and a white base and broad white tip to the long blackish tail. Yellow cere and legs, dark hooked bill.
hubtyr1: Small tyrant-flycatcher; male glossy black overall with a concealed white patch in the wing visible in flight. Reddish-brown eye; small black bill, black legs. Compact, upright stance.
hudcan1: Streaky canastero, brown above with dark-streaked crown and back, rufous wing panel and rufous tail, and pale buff underparts with diffuse streaking on the breast; small rufous-orange throat patch. Long tail, fine dark bill, pale legs.
hudgod: Large long-billed wader; breeding adult has dark brick-red underparts barred darker, mottled brown-and-black upperparts, and a pale eyebrow. Long slightly upturned bill, pink at base with dark tip; black legs. In flight shows black underwings, white wing bar and black-and-white tail.
humant1: Antbird; male evenly slaty-grey with blackish wings showing small white spots forming wing bars and a plain blackish tail; pale grey eye. Female warm brown. Black bill, greyish legs; plump, short-tailed shape.
humlar1: Small short-tailed lark, sandy grey-brown above with fine dark streaking, and whitish underparts with light streaking across the breast. Pale supercilium, short stubby greyish bill, pale legs; compact build.
humowl1: Medium pale desert owl with a rounded head and no ear tufts; plumage warm sandy-buff to pale greyish, finely vermiculated and faintly streaked, with a plain pale facial disc. Large dark eyes, pale bill, feathered legs.
humwhe1: Small white-eye, olive-green above on head, back, wings and tail, with a broad white eye-ring broken at the front and a black lore line. Underparts pale grey with a yellow throat and a yellow band down the centre of the belly. Silvery dark-tipped bill, pale legs.
huncis1: Plump cisticola, rufous-brown above streaked darker on the back, with a rufous crown and a long white eyebrow; underparts buff to whitish with rufous flanks and a streaked breast. Dark bill, pinkish legs; longish tail.
hutvir: Small stocky vireo, olive-grey above and dull olive-yellow below, with two pale wing bars and a broken whitish eye-ring with a short pale loral line forming spectacles. Thick pale-based dark bill, blue-grey legs.
hyamac1: Very large, long-tailed macaw entirely deep cobalt-blue, slightly darker on the wings. Bare bright yellow skin ring around the eye and a yellow crescent at the base of the lower mandible. Massive blackish hooked bill, dark grey legs.
hyavis1: Tiny hummingbird; male iridescent golden-green above with a black crown and face sides, a glittering green forehead, a glittering golden-green to blue throat bordered black and edged by a white band, and a coppery-red to violet tail. Short straight black bill.
hypoco1: Slim, soft grey waxwing-like bird with a long tail; pale buffy-grey overall with a black face mask, black wingtips and a broad black band at the tail tip. Stubby grey bill, dark legs; sleek crested-looking head.
ibisbi1: Unmistakable wader with a long downcurved crimson-red bill. Upperparts blue-grey, face and a broad breast band black, throat white, and lower underparts white separated from the grey breast by a black band. Stout greyish-pink legs.
iheant1: Small antwren; male grey with mainly grey upperparts, black wings spotted white forming wing bars, and a black throat and bib extending onto the breast; paler grey belly. Fine black bill, greyish legs.
iiwi: Striking Hawaiian honeycreeper, brilliant scarlet-red overall with black wings and tail and a white wing patch. Bill long, strongly decurved and salmon-pink to orange; legs orange-red.
ijlwar1: Small greenish leaf warbler, olive-green above and pale yellowish-white below, with a single faint pale wing bar and a bold yellowish supercilium and dark eyestripe. Pale-based dark bill, pale legs; active, slim shape.
imewaa1: Small slim antbird with black crown bearing a white central streak, white supercilium, and black-and-white speckled face; olive-grey upperparts with a white scapular patch, and whitish underparts faintly washed yellow on the flanks. Dark bill and pinkish-grey legs.
immant1: Uniformly slaty-black antbird with a contrasting pale blue bare patch of skin around the eye and lores. Plumage is plain blackish overall lacking wing spots; stout black bill and dark grey legs. Compact, fairly large terrestrial antbird shape.
immwrb1: Tiny, nearly tailless wren-like cupwing, rich rufous-brown overall with no spotting or scaling, slightly paler buffy on the throat and belly. Short fine dark bill, rounded body, very short cocked tail, and pinkish-brown legs.
impsni1: Large dark snipe with blackish upperparts densely barred and spotted rufous-buff, a striped head, and heavily barred buff-and-black underparts. Long straight dark bill, short olive legs, rounded wings, and stocky compact body.
inawoo1: Smallish slim woodcreeper with olive-brown back and rufous wings and tail; head and underparts olive-brown with heavy pale streaking below but plain unstreaked back and crown. Long creamy-white slightly decurved bill, greyish legs.
inbrob1: Male slaty-blue above from crown to tail with a black face and throat, and clean white underparts; a small white throat patch. Slim chat-like robin with fine dark bill and pale pinkish legs.
incdov: Small slender pale grey-brown dove with a strongly scaled appearance, each feather dark-edged giving a scaly look over the back, breast and crown. Long square tail edged white, rufous wing patches in flight, dark bill and pink legs.
incfly1: Small flycatcher with dark olive crown, mottled blackish-and-whitish face with a thin pale eye-ring, olive-green back and rump, and a warm buffy-orange wash across the breast. Two broken rufous wing bars, dark bill, pale legs.
incter1: Striking dark slate-grey tern with a long curved white moustachial plume curling back from the bright red bill across each cheek. Yellow gape wattle, blackish cap, red legs, and a forked grey tail; medium-sized and elegant.
incwre1: Boldly marked wren with rufous-brown crown and back, a long white supercilium, black-and-white streaked face and ear-coverts, white throat and breast, and rich rufous flanks and belly. Fine dark bill, slim cocked tail, grey legs.
indbar1: Plump green barbet with a multicoloured head: blue throat and face patches, red and yellow markings, and black streaking around the crown and ears. Stout pale bill ringed by bristles, green body, short tail, greyish legs.
indbun: Breeding male is entirely vivid deep blue, brightest on the head, fading slightly darker on wings and tail. Small stout finch-shaped body with a short conical silvery-grey bill and dark grey legs.
indbus2: Small streaky lark, sandy-brown above with heavy dark streaking, and pale buff-white below with a streaked breast; shows rufous wing panels in flight. Short stout pale bill, pale supercilium, and pinkish legs.
indbus3: Compact streaked lark, warm sandy-brown above with dark feather centres and a rufous-tinged crown, pale buff underparts with streaked breast, and rufous wing patches in flight. Short thick pale bill, pinkish legs.
indcha1: Plain greyish-brown chat, slightly paler below with a faintly rufous-tinged lower belly and rump. Lacks bold markings; thin dark bill, slim upright posture, and long dark legs giving a robin-like silhouette.
indcuc1: Greyish cuckoo with plain grey head and breast, brown-grey upperparts, and white underparts broadly barred with bold black bars. Long barred tail with a broad black subterminal band, yellow eye-ring, and yellow legs.
indflo1: Small flowerpiercer, entirely deep glossy indigo-blue plumage darkest on the wings and tail, with no contrasting marks. Short upturned hook-tipped black bill characteristic of the genus, and dark grey legs.
indfly1: Small flycatcher uniformly bright indigo-blue, slightly paler and brighter blue on the forehead and brow, with a blackish face mask around the eyes. Short dark bill, dark legs; compact rounded body.
indmac1: Large all-blue macaw, deep slaty greyish-blue overall with a bright yellow bare patch at the base of the lower mandible and a yellow eye-ring. Massive curved blackish bill, long tapering blue tail, dark grey legs.
indnig1: Cryptic greyish-brown nightjar finely vermiculated and spotted, with a buff nuchal collar and bold pale streaks on the scapulars; males show white throat patch and white wing and tail spots. Short dark bill, large dark eyes, short legs.
indpit1: Brilliantly coloured plump pitta with green back, buff underparts, a scarlet-red lower belly and vent, and a striking head pattern of broad black eyestripe, buff crown stripes, and white throat. Blue wing and rump patches, stout pinkish bill, long pink legs.
indrob1: Male glossy blackish overall with a long cocked tail showing bright chestnut-rufous undertail coverts, and a white wing-shoulder patch. Slim upright build, fine dark bill, and dark legs.
indrol2: Stocky roller with a brownish-buff back, lilac-and-blue breast and throat, and brilliant turquoise and deep-blue wings showing bright pale-blue bands in flight. Pale blue crown, heavy black bill, and dull yellow legs.
indsil: Small pale finch, sandy fawn-brown above and whitish below, with a long pointed black tail and a white rump. Conspicuous heavy silver-grey conical bill, unmarked plain plumage, and pinkish legs.
indthk1: Large-headed sandy-brown wader streaked darker, with pale underparts and a bold white wing-bar pattern visible in flight. Huge staring yellow eye, stout black-tipped yellow bill, and long stout yellowish legs.
indtit1: Bright yellow-and-black tit with a black crown and crest, black throat and central breast-stripe, yellow cheeks and underparts, and a black-and-yellow back with white wing markings. Small stout dark bill, grey legs.
indwhe1: Tiny olive-green warbler-like bird with a bold white eye-ring, yellow throat and undertail, greyish-white belly, and yellow-green upperparts. Fine pointed dark bill and pale grey legs; rounded active form.
indyuh1: Small babbler-like bird with rufous-brown crown, greyish face, white throat, and a distinctive black-and-white streaked or scaled collar across the neck sides. Olive-brown back, whitish underparts, fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
inghor2: Slim grey hornbill, brownish-grey overall with paler underparts and a long graduated tail tipped white with a black subterminal band. Long down-curved greyish bill with a small pointed casque, dark legs.
ingori1: Male brilliant golden-yellow with contrasting black wings and a black tail with yellow corners; a black eyestripe extends behind the eye. Stout pinkish-red bill and grey legs; thrush-sized robust body.
inltho1: Tiny grey-brown thornbill with finely streaked forehead, pale eye, plain greyish underparts faintly streaked on the throat, and a contrasting rufous rump. Short dark bill, dark tail-tip, and dark legs.
inpher1: Stocky squat heron, breeding adult with rich maroon-brown back, buff head and neck, and pale yellowish breast; in flight the wings flash brilliant white. Yellow blue-based bill, greenish-yellow legs, hunched posture.
insbab1: Brown babbler with rufous-brown upperparts, a bold white throat and breast, and rufous flanks; the long down-curved yellow bill is the key feature. Plain face lacking strong supercilium, dark eye, and greyish legs.
insowl1: Small grey-brown scops-owl with prominent ear-tufts, cryptically vermiculated plumage, a pale buff nuchal collar, and dark-streaked underparts. Dark eyes, pale facial-disc rim, short pale bill, and feathered legs.
integr1: Medium-large all-white egret with slender neck and rounded head; breeding adult has a short black-tipped yellow-orange bill, plumes on back and breast, and dark legs and feet. Gape line does not extend past the eye.
inwpar1: Small stocky green parrot with a reddish-brown forehead and crown, dull olive-yellow face, and bright blue primary coverts forming a blue wing patch; red shoulder edge. Pale horn bill, short tail, greyish legs.
isabuh1: Large plain rail, olive-brown above and rich rufous-chestnut below, with no barring or streaking. Pale greenish bill, greenish-yellow legs, and a stocky compact body; the largest of its genus.
isashr1: Pale sandy isabelline-brown shrike with a faint darker mask through the eye, pale buff underparts, and a rufous-toned rump and tail. Stout hooked grey bill (often pink-based) and dark grey legs; slim long-tailed form.
isawhe1: Pale sandy-buff wheatear, uniform pale brown above and creamy below with a faint pale supercilium and a dark eye-line. Black-and-white tail pattern with a broad black inverted-T, fairly long blackish legs and upright stance, dark bill.
isbduc1: Large grey-brown dabbling duck with scaly-patterned body, a paler head with dark crown and eye-line, and a green-and-white speculum bordered black. Bill black with a bright yellow tip and orange-red spots at the base; coral-red legs.
isipig1: Large heavy pigeon, pale grey on the head and underparts grading to slaty-grey, with greyish-green glossed upperparts and wings. Dark bill, reddish legs, and a heavy-bodied imperial-pigeon shape.
islfly1: Small flycatcher with bright turquoise-blue upperparts and head, a blackish face mask around the eye, and paler greyish-blue underparts. Short dark bill and dark legs; compact rounded form.
islwar1: Small greenish leaf-warbler, olive-green above with a greyish crown, a yellowish supercilium and dark eye-stripe, and yellow-washed underparts. Fine pointed bill dark above and pale below, pale legs; active slender shape.
istwre1: Plain wren, warm brown above and pale greyish-white below with buffy flanks, a faint pale supercilium, and a finely barred tail. Slim fine dark bill, longish slightly cocked tail, and pinkish legs.
itaspi1: Dull greyish-brown spinetail, plain dusky-grey overall, slightly browner on the wings and tail, with a faint pale throat. Lacks bold markings; fine dark bill, fairly long dark tail, and dark legs.
ivbara1: Small toucan-relative with black head and breast, green-toned back, and a yellow belly crossed by a red band; rump bright red. Distinctive pale ivory-and-black bill, blue-grey bare facial skin, and greenish legs.
ivbpit1: Large robust pitta with glossy black head, neck and underparts contrasting a clean white breast patch, deep blue back and wings, and a scarlet-red lower belly and vent. Pale blue rump patch, stout black bill, long pinkish legs.
ivbwoo1: Medium woodcreeper, rufous-brown overall brighter rufous on wings and tail, with buff streaking and spotting on the head, breast and back. Long pale ivory-coloured straight bill and greyish legs; sturdy build.
izuthr1: Dark thrush with blackish-grey head and upperparts, deep rufous-chestnut flanks and breast sides, and a paler belly. Bright orange-yellow bill and eye-ring, and orange-yellow legs; stocky robust thrush shape.
jabiru: Huge white stork with a naked black head and neck, and a broad bright-red inflatable band at the base of the neck. Massive slightly upturned black bill, black legs, and a bulky heavy-bodied form.
jabwar: Plain olive-brown warbler with paler buffy-grey underparts and a faint creamy supercilium over a dark eyeline. Crown warm brown, throat whitish. Slender pointed bill, dark above, pale below; pinkish legs. Long-tailed, skulking shape with no wing bars.
jabwar1: Small dark olive-brown bush warbler with rufous-tinged crown and upperparts, buffy-grey underparts and a short faint pale supercilium. Plain wings without bars; rounded tail. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs; rounded skulking body shape.
jacpen1: Stout black-and-white penguin with black back and head, white underparts crossed by a single black breast band, and a black horseshoe stripe down the flanks. Bare pink skin patch above the eye; black bill with grey band, dark feet.
jacwin1: Small grey-brown flycatcher, plain grey-brown above and whitish below with a soft greyish wash on the breast. Indistinct paler face, dark eye, fine black bill. White outer tail feathers conspicuous; dark legs. Upright slim posture.
jambec1: Wholly glossy slaty-black becard (male), uniform black over head, back, wings and underparts with a slight blue-black sheen. Stout, hooked black bill; dark grey legs. Stocky, big-headed body with a short tail.
jamcro1: Medium all-black crow with a slightly glossy purple-brown sheen, grey-based feathering visible on the nape and throat giving a slightly shaggy, paler-necked look. Heavy black bill, black legs; robust corvid build.
jamfir1: Small finch with rich crimson-red face, breast and rump contrasting with grey-brown crown, back and wings. Belly greyish-buff; fine white speckling on the red breast sides. Bluish-grey conical bill, dark legs.
jamfla1: Pale pink flamingo with a deeper rosy wash on the neck and back, and bright carmine streaks across the upper body. Black flight feathers; very long pinkish legs. Bill bright yellow-orange with a black tip, downcurved. Slender, small-headed shape.
jamori1: Bright yellow oriole with yellow head, underparts and rump, contrasting black throat patch and black lores. Black wings with a bold white wing patch; black tail. Pointed bluish-grey bill, grey legs.
jamowl1: Rich tawny-brown owl finely barred and vermiculated overall, with a rounded head bearing small ear tufts, plain rufous-brown facial disc and large dark eyes. Cinnamon-buff underparts streaked darker; feathered legs, dark bill.
jamtod1: Tiny plump tody, bright grass-green above with a glowing crimson-red throat patch bordered white. Pale grey-white underparts washed pink and yellow on flanks. Long flat bill, red below and dark above; greyish legs.
jamvir1: Small drab vireo, olive-grey above and pale greyish-white below with a faint yellowish wash on the flanks. Whitish broken eye-ring and pale lores; pale yellowish eye. Short stout grey bill, bluish-grey legs.
jamwoo1: Boldly marked woodpecker with golden-olive upperparts barred black, and a plain olive-buff face and underparts. Male has a red crown and nape; red around the eye. Yellowish underparts; chisel-tipped grey bill, grey legs.
janpar1: Small parakeet with golden-yellow head and breast grading to fiery orange on the belly, contrasting with green back, wings and tail. Blue-tinged flight feathers, reddish underwings. Black bill, grey legs; long tapered tail.
japfly1: Glossy black-headed paradise-flycatcher (male) with purplish-black head, neck and breast, deep chestnut-maroon back, wings and very long streaming tail. Whitish belly; bright blue eye-ring and blue bill. Short dark legs.
japgro1: Large heavy-billed grosbeak, pale silvery-grey overall with a glossy black hood covering the whole head, and black wings and tail. Massive deep-yellow conical bill; pinkish legs. Stocky, big-headed body.
japher1: Small heron, in breeding plumage with white head, neck and breast washed buff-cinnamon, a slate-grey or maroon-tinged back, and white wings and belly. Yellow bill with dark tip; yellow-green legs. Hunched, stocky pond-heron shape.
japqua: Small rotund quail, mottled brown above streaked buff and black, with creamy-buff underparts. Male has rusty-brown face and throat; bold whitish supercilium and dark crown stripes. Short greyish bill, pale legs; rounded tailless body.
japrob2: Small robin with bright orange-red face, throat and breast, a narrow dark border below the breast, and olive-brown upperparts. Belly whitish-grey. Dark eye, fine black bill; pinkish legs. Plump, upright, short-tailed shape.
japspa1: Small compact sparrowhawk, slate-grey above with a paler grey head, and white underparts finely barred rufous-grey (male). Pale rufous wash on breast; yellow eye, yellow cere, dark-tipped bill. Long yellow legs, rounded wings, banded tail.
japthr1: Thrush with glossy black head, throat, breast and upperparts (male) contrasting with white belly boldly spotted black on the flanks. Yellow eye-ring and yellow bill; pinkish-yellow legs. Slim upright thrush shape.
japwag1: Boldly black-and-white wagtail with black crown, back, breast and throat, white face split by a black eyeline, and white belly. Black wings with large white panel; long black tail with white edges. Black bill and legs.
japwax1: Sleek pinkish-grey waxwing with a prominent crest, black mask and chin, and a chestnut wash on the face. Grey upperparts grading to vinous breast; wings grey with white and red markings. Tail tipped bright crimson-red (not yellow). Dark bill and legs.
japwoo1: Green woodpecker with olive-green back and wings, greyish face and underparts faintly barred, and a red crown (male) or partly red crown. Pale yellow-green rump; dark moustache. Grey chisel bill, greyish legs.
jasowl1: Greyish-brown scops-owl with prominent ear tufts, finely vermiculated grey-brown plumage and dark streaking on the underparts. Pale buff facial disc, rufous-buff hindneck collar; bright orange-red eyes. Pale bill, feathered legs.
javfro2: Cryptic mottled frogmouth, rufous-brown to greyish overall heavily marbled and spotted with black, buff and white, with scattered pale spots forming faint shoulder lines. Huge flat wide hooked bill, large dark eyes; broad flat head, short legs.
javful1: Small plain fulvetta, warm rufous-brown above with rufous-edged wings, and pale buffy-grey underparts. Greyish head with a faint pale eye-ring; no strong head pattern. Short stout bill, pinkish legs; rounded compact body.
javmun1: Small munia with dark chocolate-brown head, breast and upperparts contrasting sharply with a clean white belly and flanks. Dark brown wings and tail. Heavy bluish-grey conical bill; grey legs. Plump short-tailed finch shape.
javplo1: Small pale sand-coloured plover, greyish-brown above and white below, with a white forehead, white collar and an incomplete dark breast band. Faint dark eye-mask; rufous tinge on hindneck. Black bill, dull greyish legs.
javspa: Stocky grey finch with a black head and tail, white cheek patches, and a pale grey back and breast washed pink on the belly. Large bright pink-red conical bill; bare red eye-ring; pink legs. Compact, big-billed shape.
javtes1: Tiny near-tailless tesia, olive-brown above with a grey head and breast and a bold white supercilium set off by a black eyeline. Pale grey throat, whitish belly. Long bicoloured bill, dark above and flesh below; pinkish legs. Round-bodied, stub-tailed.
jawpig1: Large dark wood-pigeon, entirely glossy blackish with a green and purple iridescent sheen on the neck and upperparts. Dark slaty underparts; deep red eye and eye-ring. Greyish bill, dark red legs. Heavy-bodied pigeon shape.
jelcht1: Small chat-tyrant, brown above with a bright yellow-orange forehead and short pale brow, plain greyish underparts, and rusty-rufous wing bars. Dark eye, fine black bill; blackish legs. Upright slim tyrant posture.
jerbab1: Plain warm rufous-brown babbler, buffy below with a slightly streaked greyish throat and breast, and a long rufous tail. Pale lores and a soft pale eye; stout yellowish bill, pale orange-yellow legs. Rounded, long-tailed shape.
jerbaz1: Slim raptor, brownish-grey above with a long erect black-and-white crest, and white underparts boldly barred rufous-brown. Grey head, dark crest; rufous breast bars. Yellow eye, hooked dark bill, greyish-yellow legs.
jerbus2: Small streaky lark, sandy-brown above heavily streaked dark, with whitish underparts streaked on the breast. Rufous wing panel in flight; pale supercilium and rufous-buff cheeks. Stout pinkish bill, pale flesh legs.
jerlea1: Bright leaf-green leafbird (male) with a black face, throat and upper breast, bordered by a turquoise-blue moustache and a bright blue shoulder patch. Yellowish wash around the black bib. Dark bill, greyish legs.
jernig1: Cryptic grey-brown nightjar mottled and vermiculated with buff, black and grey, with a buff hindneck collar, and white throat patches. Dark crown streaks; broad flat head. Tiny dark bill, short legs; long wings and tail with white corners (male).
jetant1: Jet-black antbird (male), uniformly glossy blackish-slate over the whole body with a small concealed white patch between the scapulars. Pale grey eye; slender black bill, dark grey legs. Slim, long-tailed body.
jocant1: Large striking antpitta, slaty-grey above with a blackish crown and bold white crescents (eyebrow and below eye) framing the face, and tawny-rufous flanks. White throat and central underparts. Massive black bill, grey legs; rounded, near-tailless body.
johtot1: Tiny tody-flycatcher with a rich rufous-cinnamon head and throat, a grey nape collar, and yellowish-olive back and rump. Black wings edged yellow-olive with two thin buff wing bars; yellow belly. Flat dark bill, pale legs.
jottyr1: Small dull olive tody-tyrant, olive crown and upperparts with two thin yellowish wing bars and yellow-edged coverts, and yellowish underparts streaked olive on the throat and breast. Pale loral line, pale brown eye; black bill, pale legs.
joygre1: Bright olive-green greenbul with a yellowish-green throat and breast and a yellow belly, plain unmarked face with a paler throat. Olive upperparts and tail; dark eye. Slender dark bill, greyish legs; medium bulbul shape.
jubqua1: Small rotund bush-quail, finely barred grey-brown above with a chestnut face, a white supercilium and white throat bordered black (male), and rufous-and-white barred underparts. Short reddish bill, reddish legs; rounded tailless body.
jufpet: Long-winged grey-and-white gadfly petrel, grey above with a darker M-pattern across the upperwings and a grey cap, and clean white forehead, face and underparts. White underwing with thin dark margins. Black hooked bill, pinkish legs.
junbab2: Drab grey-brown babbler, uniformly greyish-brown above and paler below with faint streaking on the throat and breast, and a long graduated brown tail. Pale eye; yellowish stout bill, yellow legs. Untidy, rounded gregarious shape.
juncan1: Chunky streaked canastero, warm reddish-brown above heavily streaked black and buff, with creamy underparts faintly streaked tawny, and an orange chin patch. Long rufous-edged cocked tail. Fine dark bill, dark legs.
junhao1: Small dark hawk-owl, deep chocolate-brown over the head, back and underparts with faint paler spotting, and a rounded head without ear tufts. Dark facial disc, yellow eyes; greenish-yellow bill, yellowish feet. Slim long-tailed shape.
junmyn1: Dark grey myna with a brownish-grey body, a black head and a short shaggy frontal tuft of forehead feathers over the bill. White wing patch and white-tipped tail visible in flight. Orange-yellow bill, yellow legs; pale eye.
junowl1: Tiny owlet, grey-brown above closely barred white and rufous, with a rounded head, no ear tufts, and white-spotted crown. Underparts white barred dark brown; yellow eyes, greenish-yellow bill, yellow feet. Compact rounded body.
junpri1: Slim warbler with long graduated tail. Plain grey-brown upperparts, warmer rufous-tinged wings, and whitish to pale buff underparts. Pale lores and short whitish supercilium; reddish-brown eye. Thin blackish bill; pinkish legs.
juntit1: Small plain grey crested titmouse, uniformly drab grey above and slightly paler pale grey below with no markings. Short pointed grey crest, plain grey face, dark eye. Short stubby black bill; dark grey legs.
kagu1: Pale ash-grey heron-sized bird with a large shaggy erectile crest. Uniform pale grey plumage; in flight wings show bold black, white and chestnut barring. Long bright orange-red bill and long orange-red legs; red eyes.
kalphe: Large blackish pheasant; male glossy blue-black with a tall floppy black crest, bare scarlet facial skin, and a long arched black tail. Whitish to grey rump-edged feathers on some races. Greyish bill; grey legs.
karbus1: Medium drab bustard, grey-brown above suffused pinkish, paler off-white belly. Plain face with a black chin-to-throat patch bordered white (smaller, browner in female); creamy wing patches in flight. Pale greyish bill; yellowish legs.
karlar2: Stocky rufous-toned lark, rich rufous-brown to greyer above, whitish below with heavy dark streaking across the breast. Pale supercilium, streaked crown. Fairly heavy pale horn bill; pinkish legs.
karpri1: Small grey-brown prinia with a long cocked tail. Greyish-brown above, whitish below boldly spotted and streaked black across throat and breast forming a malar streak. Pale yellowish eye; thin black bill; pinkish legs.
karthr1: Plain greyish-brown thrush, uniform grey-brown above, paler greyish breast grading to orange-buff flanks and whitish belly. Bright orange-yellow bill and narrow orange eye-ring; orange-yellow legs.
kasfly1: Small flycatcher; male slate-grey above with a bright orange-red throat and breast narrowly bordered black at the sides, white belly. Blackish tail with white base patches. Small black bill; black legs.
kasrob1: Pale sandy scrub-robin, greyish-brown above with a rufous rump and rufous-based tail tipped black and white. Whitish below; white supercilium and dark eyestripe. Slender blackish bill; pale legs.
kasrob2: Dusky grey-brown scrub-robin, uniform greyish above and on breast with whitish belly. Faint pale supercilium, rufous rump, and dark tail boldly tipped white. Blackish bill; dark legs.
kauama: Small olive-green honeycreeper, yellow-green above and brighter yellowish-green below, brightest on the throat. Faint dark lores. Slender decurved blackish bill; dark legs.
kawpar1: Large green Amazon parrot, mostly bright green with darker feather edging giving a scaled look, plain green head, and a bare reddish patch at the base of the upper mandible. Pale horn bill; grey legs; red wing speculum.
kbopar1: Tiny stocky bird-of-paradise; male brilliant crimson-red above and on head, with a white belly sharply demarcated by a green breast band, and two long wire tail plumes ending in coiled emerald-green disks. Yellowish bill; blue legs.
kebmot1: Green motmot with a turquoise-blue crown patch and black face mask, rufous-tinged green underparts, and a black throat patch. Long bluish-green racket-tipped tail. Broad heavy black bill, finely serrated; dark legs.
kebtou1: Large black-bodied toucan with bright yellow face and breast, red undertail, and a huge multicoloured bill of green, orange, red and blue. White uppertail-coverts; blue bare skin around the eye; blue-grey legs.
kelgul: Large black-backed gull, jet-black mantle and wings with a white trailing edge and white head and underparts. Yellow bill with red gunmetal spot near the tip; greenish to dull yellow legs; dark eye.
kemlon1: Plain warbler-like bird, dull olive-green above and greyish-olive below with a paler whitish throat, lacking obvious markings. Long slender slightly decurved blackish bill; dark eye; greyish legs.
kenwar: Olive-green warbler, plain olive above and bright yellow below, with a black crown patch and bold black sideburns curving down below the eye, broken yellow spectacles. Pinkish stout bill; pink legs.
kerlau2: Olive-brown laughingthrush, warm brown above with a greyer crown and rufous-buff underparts. Bold white supercilium and dark eye-stripe; greyish ear-coverts. Blackish bill; pinkish-brown legs.
kerpet: Robust dark gadfly petrel, variable from uniform sooty-brown to pale-bellied morphs, all showing a conspicuous white flash at the base of the primaries on the underwing. Stout black bill; pinkish legs with dark webs.
kerspa2: Rufous-and-grey sparrow; grey crown, broad rufous-chestnut stripe behind the eye and on the nape, grey-brown back streaked black, and clean pale greyish-white underparts. Black bill; pinkish legs.
killde: Slender plover, brown above and white below with two bold black breast bands and a white collar. White forehead and throat, dark crown and eyeline; bright orange-red eye-ring and orange rump in flight. Black bill; pale legs.
kinpen1: Large penguin, blue-grey back and white belly with vivid orange ear-patches that merge into a yellow-orange wash on the upper breast. Long slender slightly downcurved black bill with an orange lower-mandible stripe; black feet.
kinrai2: Slim greyish rail, grey-brown streaked back, slate-grey face and breast, and faintly barred flanks with white undertail. Long slightly decurved reddish bill darker at the tip; greyish legs.
kinrai4: Large rust-breasted rail, brown-streaked back, bright rufous-cinnamon breast and neck, and boldly black-and-white barred flanks. Long slightly decurved orange-based dark-tipped bill; greenish-grey legs.
kirwar: Bluish-grey warbler streaked black on the back, with a bright yellow throat and breast finely streaked black on the sides, white belly. Broken white eye-ring and black lores. Dark bill; dark legs; wags tail.
klaant1: Tiny antwren; male grey above with white wing-bar spots, whitish underparts, and a black-and-white streaked crown. Greyish face and pale grey underparts. Thin blackish bill; pale grey legs.
klaant2: Slim antbird with a long white-tipped tail; male streaked black-and-white on head, back and breast, with a contrasting rufous rump and rufous belly. Thin black bill; greyish legs.
klacuc1: Small glossy cuckoo; male iridescent emerald-green above and on the head, with white underparts and a small green patch at the side of the breast; white outer-tail spots. Greenish-black bill; grey legs.
klblar6: Large long-billed lark, grey-brown above with dark streaking, plain rufous-tinged wings, and whitish underparts streaked on the breast. Pale supercilium; very long decurved horn-coloured bill; pale legs.
klolew1: Small greenish leaf warbler, olive-green above and yellowish-white below, with a long yellowish supercilium, dark eyestripe, and two pale yellowish wing-bars. Dark upper mandible, pale lower; pale legs.
knohor1: Large hornbill; male black with a rufous-chestnut neck and head, white tail, and pale blue bare facial and throat skin. Huge yellow-orange bill topped by a tall ridged reddish casque. Dark legs; red eye.
knswar1: Plain dark warbler, uniformly dark olive-brown above and slightly paler greyish-brown below, faintly mottled on the throat, lacking obvious markings. Slender dark bill; pinkish-brown legs.
