knytur1: Glossy green turaco with a tall rounded green crest tipped white, a small white spot before the eye and a white line below it, and a red eye-ring. Crimson flight feathers flash in flight. Reddish bill; black legs.
koesco1: Greyish-brown screech-owl with fine dark streaking and vermiculation, prominent ear tufts, and a pale-bordered facial disc. Cryptic bark-like plumage with whitish scapular spots. Yellow eyes; greenish bill; feathered legs.
kokako3: Slate-grey crow-sized bird with a black face mask and a bright blue fleshy wattle at the base of the bill. Long tail, rounded wings, mostly uniform blue-grey body. Short stout black bill; long blackish legs.
kokphe1: Pheasant; male with a dark glossy-green head bearing a long pointed brown and green crest, a white neck patch, and chestnut-and-grey body boldly streaked dark. Greyish bill; greyish legs.
korfly1: Striking flycatcher; male black above with a bright yellow rump, throat and breast, a bold white wing patch and white supercilium, and black tail. White belly. Black bill; black legs.
kospar1: Velvety black-and-yellow bird-of-paradise; male black with a glossy golden-yellow breast shield, and two extraordinary head plumes longer than the body, each lined with sky-blue enamel-like flags. Bill blackish; legs grey.
kurthr1: Olive-brown thrush, greyish-brown above, with a whitish throat heavily streaked black, orange-buff breast and flanks, and white belly. Bright orange-yellow bill and orange legs; faint orange eye-ring.
kvbsun1: Small sunbird; male with iridescent violet-blue upperparts, throat and head, sharply contrasting white underparts. Glossy metallic cap and back; yellowish pectoral tufts. Short decurved black bill; dark legs.
kwqdov: Plump terrestrial dove, rich rufous-brown above with green-purple iridescence on the nape, and a bold white facial stripe below the eye. Pinkish-buff underparts. Reddish bill; red legs.
laaphe1: Spectacular pheasant; male with a black-and-silver-white scaled crest and nape, glossy green mantle, white underparts, red rump, and an extremely long white tail barred black. Bluish bill; blue-grey legs.
labant1: Small antwren, grey above with a black-streaked crown, two whitish wing-bars, and pale grey underparts; whitish supercilium and dark eye-stripe. Long fairly heavy blackish bill; greyish legs.
labcro1: Large all-black crow, glossy black throughout with a notably heavy, deep-based arched black bill and a steep forehead. Slight purple-blue gloss on the plumage; black legs; dark eye.
labger1: Tiny warbler-like bird, grey-brown above and pale greyish-white below with a faint whitish throat. Short white-tipped tail and red eye. Relatively large dark bill; greyish legs.
lablar1: Stout lark, heavily streaked brown above and whitish below with a streaked breast. Short crest, pale supercilium. Very heavy pale pinkish-horn bill, deep at the base; pinkish legs.
labscr2: Small plain scrubwren, warm brown above and buffy-brown below, lacking strong markings. Plain face with a pale eye-ring and pale eye. Relatively long pinkish-based dark bill; pinkish legs.
labter1: Large stocky tern, grey above with a black cap and a contrasting white forehead, white underparts; in flight shows a bold black-grey-white wing pattern. Massive heavy yellow bill; blackish legs.
labwoo: Small woodpecker with black-and-white ladder-barred back, buffy-white underparts with black spotting, and a black-framed buff face. Male has a red crown; female crown is black. Straight grey-black bill, grey legs.
lacwoo1: Medium green woodpecker with tea-green back, olive-green throat and breast grading to dark-streaked yellowish belly, grey face and cheeks, and a black moustache. Male has a red crown, female black. Barred flight feathers; pale grey bill, greyish legs.
laffin1: Robust, dark terrestrial finch with slate-grey head, black throat, forehead and crown sides, dark olive upperparts, and bright olive underparts washed brownish on flanks. Black wings and tail with olive fringes; stout dark bill and very large powerful pinkish legs and feet.
lafpic1: Tiny olive-green piculet with whitish-spotted dusky underparts and a short bill. Crown black, spotted white at rear; male shows red forecrown spots, female all-black-and-white speckled crown. Short stiff tail, dark legs.
laftap1: Small, dark slaty-grey tapaculo with paler grey underparts, often a faint brownish-buff lower belly and flanks, and very large pink legs and feet. Short cocked tail, slender black bill; dark eye, skulking ground-dweller shape.
lagbab2: Large pale grey babbler, brownish-grey above and pale grey below, with a paler face and forehead and a long graduated tail tipped whitish at the corners. Yellow eye, yellowish bill, sturdy build; legs pale yellowish.
lahfla2: Stocky flycatcher with a notably large flattened head, olive-brown upperparts, two rufous wing bars and rufous wing-feather edges, and pale yellowish underparts with faint olive streaking on breast. Broad flat bill; pale eye, greyish legs.
lalbru1: Lark-like ovenbird, sandy-brown above with heavy dark streaking, a pointed erect rufous-tinged crest, pale buff supercilium, and whitish underparts streaked dark on breast. Short conical pale bill, pinkish legs; stocky upright posture.
lalbun1: Small plain sandy-grey-brown bunting, pale buff-grey above with faint streaking, plain unmarked face and unstreaked pale underparts. Pale pinkish conical bill, pale legs; nondescript, washed-out appearance.
lamtan1: Striking mountain tanager, blackish above with blue-edged wings and tail and a blue rump, and rich tawny-orange underparts. Diagnostic isolated yellow cheek and below-eye patches forming a 'teardrop'. Short stout black bill, dark legs.
lanmon1: Tiny rounded puffbird, dark brown above and white below with bold black streaking on breast and flanks, a small white forehead patch and white throat. Short heavy black bill, dark eye; compact short-tailed shape.
larbun: Stocky finch-like sparrow; breeding male jet-black overall with bold white wing patches. Female and nonbreeding male streaky grey-brown above and below with a pale buff wing patch. Heavy bluish-grey conical bill, pale legs.
larcuc1: Large grey cuckooshrike, pale blue-grey above and on breast, fading to whitish belly, with a dark grey face mask through the eye and finely barred underparts in some plumages. Black bill, dark legs; long pointed wings and longish grey tail.
larela1: Large drab elaenia, olive-brown above with two whitish wing bars, a whitish throat and pale yellowish belly, and a faint pale eye-ring; usually no visible crest crown patch. Dark bill with pale base, dark legs.
larfro1: Large cryptic frogmouth, mottled rufous-brown and grey plumage finely vermiculated for bark camouflage, with prominent ear-like tufts behind the eye and a very broad flat hooked bill. Large dark eye, short legs; broad rounded head.
larhac1: Medium hawk-cuckoo resembling a small raptor; dark slate-grey above, whitish below with bold rufous-and-grey barring across the breast and belly, and a grey head. Yellow eye-ring and yellow legs; barred grey-and-blackish tail.
larhac2: Large hawk-cuckoo with grey-brown upperparts and head, whitish underparts boldly barred and streaked rufous-brown across breast, and a broadly barred tail tipped pale. Yellow eye-ring, yellow legs; hawk-like in shape and flight.
larnil1: Large robust flycatcher; male deep blue overall, darker on face and underparts, with brilliant shining blue crown and shoulder patches. Female warm olive-brown with a buff throat and a pale blue neck patch. Stout black bill, dark legs.
larspa: Boldly patterned sparrow with a chestnut-and-white striped head, white supercilium and black-bordered chestnut ear patch, plain pale grey-brown breast with a single dark central spot, and a rounded black tail with white corners. Pale bill, pinkish legs.
larwoo1: Bulky shrike-like bird, grey-brown above with a broad blackish mask through the eye bordered by a pale supercilium, and whitish underparts. Pale rump, stout hooked black bill, grey legs; upright thickset posture.
lasbab1: Large scimitar-babbler, warm brown above with a long decurved yellowish bill, a chestnut face and crown, white throat and breast, and rich rufous-chestnut flanks and belly. Dark eye, sturdy greyish legs; long tail.
lasfly: Medium Myiarchus flycatcher, grey-brown above with a slightly bushy crown, pale grey throat and breast, and a pale yellow belly. Rufous edges on wing and tail feathers; dark bill, dark legs; upright posture.
lasthr1: Stocky thrush with starkly contrasting slate-black head and upperparts and a largely deep orange belly, plus a few white streaks on throat and ear coverts. Bright orange bill and bold orange-yellow eye-ring; dark legs.
latant1: Large heavy antshrike; male blackish overall finely spotted and barred white on wings and underparts, with a bushy crest. Female blackish with bold rufous-buff spotting. Stout hooked black bill, long graduated tail, grey legs.
latdov1: Plain ground-dove-shaped Leptotila with warm brown upperparts, a greyish crown, vinaceous-pink wash on neck and breast, and whitish belly. Long broad tail tipped white at corners; red eye-ring, dark bill, reddish-pink legs.
latlar1: Small streaky lark, sandy-brown above heavily streaked dark, with a whitish supercilium, white-edged tail, and whitish underparts with a streaked buff breast band. Short pale conical bill, pale pinkish legs; rufous wing edges.
latman1: Small round manakin; male velvety black with a brilliant sky-blue back and a red crown patch, and elongated central tail feathers forming a slim point. Bright orange legs. Female olive-green overall with pointed tail and orange legs.
latnig1: Slim nightjar, greyish-brown finely mottled and streaked, with a long graduated tail boldly barred black-and-white (ladder-like), white throat patch and white wing patches in male. Cryptic camouflaged plumage, short dark bill, tiny legs.
latnig2: Large grey-brown nightjar with cryptic mottling, a buff nape band, blackish scapular streaks, and a white throat patch. Male shows white spots on outer primaries and white tail corners. Long tail, tiny dark bill and legs.
latsni1: Stocky snipe, cryptically patterned in warm browns, blacks and buff, with bold pale buff stripes along the crown and back, barred flanks and a long straight pinkish-based dark bill. Short greenish-grey legs; rounded broad-tailed shape.
lattro1: Large trogon; male blackish-green head and breast with metallic green upperparts, a bright red belly below a white breast band, and a black-and-white finely latticed-barred undertail. Pale eye-ring, stout yellowish bill, dark legs.
laufal1: Large pale raptor with a buff-white head and underparts, a broad black mask through the eyes wrapping around the nape, and warm brown back and wings. Buff tail barred dark; heavy hooked black bill, pale legs; big rounded head.
laugul: Medium gull; breeding adult has a black hood, white crescents above and below the eye, dark grey back and wings with black wingtips, and white underparts. Drooping dark-red bill and dark reddish-black legs.
laukoo1: Large stocky kingfisher with a big head; whitish underparts, brown back and wings with pale blue patches, a creamy-white head with a dark eye-stripe, and a rufous tail barred black. Massive black-and-cream dagger bill, short legs.
lavwax: Small grey waxbill washed soft lavender-blue, paler below, with black lores and a black-bordered face, and a contrasting bright red rump, tail and undertail coverts. Crimson black-tipped bill; dark brown legs.
lawbab1: Large brown wren-babbler, rich warm brown above and on flanks with a whitish throat and breast finely scaled or streaked dusky, and a short tail. Long stout pale bill, large sturdy pinkish legs; rounded skulking shape.
lawgol: Small grey finch; male has a grey body, black face and forecrown, bright yellow wing patches and yellow wash on breast, and yellow-edged black wings and tail. Female plainer grey-buff with yellow wing tinge. Pale pinkish conical bill.
lawthr1: Plain dark thrush, uniformly sooty-brown to blackish overall with little contrast, a yellow eye-ring and a bright yellow-orange bill. Dull yellowish legs; medium robust thrush shape.
layalb: Large albatross with a white head, neck and underparts, blackish-grey upperwings, back and tail, and a white rump. White underwing edged broadly black with dark streaks; dark smudge around the eye, large pinkish-grey hooked bill.
laypar1: Green parakeet with a bluish-grey head and back separated by a bright green collar, a broad black chin stripe, and a blue tail tipped yellow. Male has a red upper mandible (lower brown); female all-black bill. Greyish legs.
lazbun: Small finch; male has a bright blue head and upperparts, a cinnamon-orange breast band, white belly, and two white wing bars on dark wings. Female plain warm grey-brown above, buff below with faint wing bars. Conical greyish bill.
lazsab1: Large hummingbird with curved wings; glittering green upperparts and breast, a deep violet-blue throat and chest patch, and a blue-black slightly forked tail with white outer-tail tips. Long straight black bill; dark feet.
lbesta1: Glossy iridescent starling, shining blue-green overall with bluer wings, a contrasting magenta-blue ear patch and a small blackish mask, and a deep blue-green tail. Pale yellow eye; black bill and legs; slim glossy build.
lblwar1: Greenish leaf warbler, olive-green above and dull whitish-grey below, with a long pale supercilium, dark eyestripe, and usually two faint pale wing bars. Notably large dark bill with a pale lower mandible; dark legs.
lbopar1: Spectacular bird-of-paradise; male has a yellow crown and upper back, an emerald-green throat, maroon body, and a huge spray of lacy yellow-and-white flank plumes with two long wire-like central tail feathers. Bluish bill, greyish legs.
lbsfin1: Stocky seed-finch with a very large bulbous blackish bill; male glossy black overall with a small white wing speculum. Female plain warm brown, paler below. Heavy conical bill dominates the rounded head; dark legs.
lbwbab1: Brown wren-babbler with a long slender slightly decurved bill, warm brown upperparts faintly streaked pale, a whitish throat and breast with fine dusky streaking, and rufous-brown flanks. Short tail, sturdy pale legs; rounded shape.
lcptyr1: Tiny pygmy-tyrant with a conspicuous long bushy black-and-grey crest, olive-green upperparts, two pale wing bars, a whitish throat and pale yellowish underparts. Short tail, pale eye, small dark bill; rounded big-headed shape.
leaant1: Small antwren; male pale grey overall with a black throat and upper breast and bold white wing bars and spots. Female grey above, buffy-white below with rufous-tinged crown. Slender dark bill, greyish legs; short tail.
leaauk: Tiny chunky auklet; breeding adult blackish above and white below with dark mottling across the breast, a small upturned red-and-black bill, white facial plumes and a white forehead streak. White eye, greyish legs; very small and round.
leabit: Tiny heron with buff and chestnut plumage; rich rufous neck and wing patches, blackish-green crown and back, pale buff underparts with faint streaking. Large pale buff wing panels show in flight. Long yellow bill, yellowish legs; very small and slender.
leabul1: Small chunky finch; male glossy black overall with bright rufous-chestnut throat, undertail coverts, and a patch above the eye. Female and immature plain grey-brown, paler below. Stout conical blackish bill, dark legs.
leafly: Small grey-olive flycatcher with a bold white eye-ring, two whitish wing bars, and pale greyish throat and breast washed olive, whitish belly. Greyish-olive upperparts, short tail. Small dark bill with pale lower mandible, dark legs.
leafly2: Small flycatcher; male slate-grey above and on head and breast, contrasting white belly, with a slight crest. Female grey-brown above with orange-rufous throat and breast. Dark bill, dark legs; upright posture.
leagre: Very small dark grebe; slaty-grey overall, slightly browner on flanks, with a blackish crown and striking golden-yellow eye. Slender pointed dark bill, fluffy whitish rear. Tiny and dumpy.
leanig1: Small nightjar, cryptic mottled grey-brown, buff, and black above; barred buff underparts. In flight shows a white wing band across primaries and pale tail markings. Tiny bill, short legs; very small and short-tailed.
leapau1: Small cryptic nightjar; greyish-brown plumage finely streaked black, with a broad buff hindneck collar and a white chest band. Wing feathers tipped white, underparts barred, white terminal tail band. Short bill, short legs.
leapyo1: Tiny owl with a rounded head lacking ear tufts; brown upperparts, rufous-brown crown faintly spotted, white underparts with bold rufous-brown streaking. Two black nape false-eyespots, yellow eyes, yellowish bill and feet.
leasal1: Stocky finch-like bird; olive-grey above, greyish head with a faint pale supercilium, white throat bordered by dark malar streaks, and dingy greyish-buff streaked underparts. Heavy dark conical bill, dark legs.
leasan: Tiny sandpiper, brown above with dark feather centers, buff-brown streaked breast band, white belly. Faint pale supercilium, slightly drooped fine dark bill. Distinctive yellowish-green legs; smallest of the peeps, crouched posture.
leasee1: Small quail-like shorebird; mottled brown and buff above, finely vermiculated. Male has grey face and breast bordered by black bands; white belly. Streaked brown crown, short stubby yellowish bill, short yellowish legs.
leater: Small slender tern; pale grey above, white below, with a black cap, white forehead patch, and black line through the eye. Yellow bill with black tip, yellow-orange legs. Long narrow wings, forked tail; very small.
lebfly2: Olive flycatcher; greenish-brown upperparts with faintly whitish-edged wing coverts, a striking bright lemon-yellow eyebrow and yellow underparts. Grey crown, dark eye-line. Short black bill, dark legs; medium-small.
lebfly3: Small flyrobin; greyish-olive above with greyish crown and face, white throat, and bright lemon-yellow underparts. Faint whitish eyebrow, fine dark bill, blackish legs; slender and upright with a longish tail.
lecgre2: Small greenlet; yellow-green crown and upperparts with grey nape, greyish face and throat, yellow breast and white belly. Pale whitish iris contrasting, brownish bill, pale legs; short-tailed and warbler-like.
lecspa: Small flat-headed sparrow with an orange-buff face, grey crown stripe, and grey ear patch. Buffy-orange breast and finely streaked flanks, white belly. Streaked rufous-and-black back. Pinkish-grey bill, pinkish legs; very furtive.
lecter2: Slender medium tern; pale grey above, white below, with a black cap and shaggy nape crest, white forehead in non-breeding. Distinctive orange-yellow bill, black legs. Long pointed wings, forked tail.
lecthr: Pale sandy grey-brown thrasher, plain unstreaked, with a slightly darker tail tipped paler and a faint dark malar stripe. Pale buffy underparts. Long decurved black bill, dark eye, long blackish legs and long tail.
lefeag1: Medium fish-eagle; greyish-brown head and breast, dark brown back and wings, with a white belly and white-based tail crossed by a broad dark subterminal band. Pale grey cere and bill base, dull greyish legs; stocky raptor.
legcuc1: Slender ground-cuckoo; olive-brown above, rufous-buff below, with a striking bare blue-and-yellow patch around the eye. Rufous rump and primaries, long graduated tail. Decurved blackish bill, pale legs.
leglea1: Medium leafbird; male bright leaf-green overall with a black face, throat, and upper breast bordered by a blue-violet moustache, no yellow border. Female all green with bluish throat. Slender black bill, greyish legs.
legrob1: Small robin-like bird; dark brown above with rufous-edged flight feathers, paler underparts, white throat, and a small white spot at the bill base. Compact with short wings and short tail; fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
lemdov2: Plump forest dove; dull greyish-brown above with a vinous-pink wash on the breast, paler buff belly, and a striking bare creamy-white face. Iridescent green-purple neck gloss, reddish bill, red legs and feet.
lenlau1: Medium laughingthrush; warm olive-brown above with a white throat and a narrow black necklace across the breast. White face with a black eye-stripe and black-bordered ear coverts, buff supercilium. Dark bill, brownish legs.
lepchi: Stocky grouse barred buff, brown, and white overall, paler and sandier than relatives. Male shows yellow eye-combs and reddish neck air sacs with elongated pinnae feathers. Short rounded tail, feathered legs, pale bill.
lesbri1: Stout bulbul-like bird; olive-green above with bright yellow underparts and a contrasting yellow lores and eye-area, dark crown. Pale or yellowish eye, heavy pale hooked bill, sturdy pinkish legs; large-headed.
lescou1: Large dark coucal; glossy blue-black head, body, and tail with rich chestnut-rufous wings. Long graduated tail, deep red eye, heavy black bill, dark grey legs; slender and long-tailed, smaller than Greater Coucal.
lescuc1: Small slim cuckoo; grey head, throat, and upperparts, with white underparts boldly barred black. Yellow eye-ring and yellow legs, blackish bill with yellow base. Long graduated grey tail tipped white; hawk-like.
lescus1: Small cuckooshrike; male slaty blue-grey above and on the head, paler grey below grading to white belly with faint barring on flanks. Black eye-line, white undertail tips. Black bill, dark grey legs.
lesela1: Plain olive-brown elaenia; greyish-olive above with two faint whitish wing bars, dull whitish throat, pale greyish-olive breast, and yellowish-tinged belly. Slight crest with little or no white crown patch. Dark bill with pale base, dark legs.
lesfla1: Small slender flamingo; deep pink overall, brighter than Greater Flamingo, with crimson wing coverts and black flight feathers. Distinctive dark crimson-red bill appearing all black at distance. Long pink legs, long curved neck.
lesfri: Slender seabird with long pointed wings and deeply forked tail; male glossy black with a brown wing bar and white spurs onto the flanks, red throat pouch. Female blackish with white breast extending to belly and dark throat. Long grey hooked bill.
lesgol: Tiny finch; male glossy black or greenish-black above with bright yellow underparts, black cap, and white wing patches. Female olive-green above, dull yellow below. Small pointed dark bill, dark legs; white wing flash in flight.
lesgre1: Tiny greenlet; dull olive-green above with a greyish crown and face, pale eye-ring, and whitish underparts washed yellowish on the breast and flanks. Pale iris, short pinkish bill, pinkish legs; small, plain, and short-tailed.
lesgrf1: Slender grass-finch; olive-green above with fine dark streaking on the back, greyish head, and plain pale greyish-buff underparts. Long graduated pointed tail, often blackish-tipped. Conical greyish bill, pale legs.
leshor1: Small ovenbird; grey head with a prominent white supercilium and white throat, rufous-brown back, wings, and rump, tawny-buff breast and flanks fading paler below. Long straight pale bill, pinkish legs; compact.
leskis1: Small flycatcher resembling a tiny kiskadee; brown above, black crown with a concealed yellow crest, bold white supercilium meeting on the nape, black mask, white throat, and bright yellow underparts. Long black bill, dark legs.
lesmaw1: Small weaver; breeding male golden-yellow with a black face mask covering the forehead, eyes, and throat, olive-yellow back. Female and non-breeding dull olive-yellow, streaked above. Pale or reddish eye, black conical bill, pinkish legs.
lesmel1: Small terrestrial songbird; velvety all-black plumage, bulbous head, fairly short tail, and long legs. Male has a red iris, female brown. Slender blackish bill, blackish legs; rounded and short-tailed.
lesnig: Slender nightjar; cryptic mottled grey-brown, buff, and black above, barred buff below, with a buffy throat patch. In flight shows a buff-and-white wing band set closer to the wingtip. Tiny bill, short legs; long pointed wings.
lesnod1: Slim dark tern; sooty grey-brown overall, slightly paler greyish on the back, with a pale whitish-grey forehead and crown blending into the dark nape. Long slender black bill, dark legs; wedge-shaped tail, slender build.
lesroa1: Slim ground-cuckoo; streaked brown, buff, and black above and on the breast, with a shaggy crest and a bare blue-and-orange skin patch behind the eye. Cinnamon-buff lower underparts, long graduated tail. Stout decurved bill, strong legs.
lessca: Diving duck; male has a glossy purplish-black head with a peaked crown, black breast, pale grey vermiculated back, white flanks, and black rear. Female brown with a white patch at the bill base. Blue-grey bill with small black tip, golden eye.
lessee2: Small seedeater; breeding male blackish above and on the head with a white throat, a black band across the upper breast, and white lower underparts; white wing speculum. Female dull buffy-brown. Stout pale conical bill, dark legs.
lessho1: Small skulking chat-like bird; warm brown above, paler buffy-brown below with faint scaly dusky streaking or scalloping on the breast. Short tail, faint pale supercilium, dark eye. Fine dark bill, pinkish legs; rounded body.
lessts1: Slender swallow; rufous-orange crown, nape, and rump, blue-black back and wings, white underparts heavily striped with fine dark streaks. Whitish ear coverts, deeply forked tail with long outer streamers. Small black bill, dark legs.
lestan: Stocky tanager; dull olive-green overall, slightly paler yellowish-olive below, with a contrasting bright yellow ring around the eye and yellow lores forming spectacles. Heavy dark bill, greyish legs; large-headed and robust.
lesvio1: Medium hummingbird; glossy green overall with a striking violet-blue ear patch flaring behind the eye and a violet-blue patch on the throat-breast. Blue-green tail with a dark subterminal band. Straight black bill, dark feet.
leswar1: Plain warbler; warm brown above, unstreaked, with a faint pale supercilium and whitish throat, buffy-brown flanks, and whitish belly. Long broad tail, rounded wings. Long slender dark bill, greyish legs; large reedbed warbler.
leswoo2: Small woodcreeper; rufous-brown overall with an olive-brown back and head finely spotted and streaked buff, plain rufous wings, rump, and tail. Buff streaking on breast. Short, straightish dark bill, greyish legs; slender.
lesyel1: Medium green woodpecker with golden-yellow nape crest, green upperparts, paler greenish underparts faintly scaled, and a chestnut-and-white-bordered face; male shows red over the eye and on malar stripe. Grey-black bill, greenish legs.
letara1: Small toucan with black head and breast, maroon-tinged black upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a red rump. Bill ivory-yellow above with fine dark vertical script-like markings; bare reddish facial skin. Greenish legs.
letbar1: Tiny stout barbet; male has red forecrown, blue and black face, bright lemon-yellow throat, orange-red breast and green back, yellowish belly. Heavy pale bill, greenish legs. Female duller with grey-blue head.
levcuc1: Slim crested cuckoo with glossy black crown and upperparts, white wing patch, white throat and underparts boldly streaked with fine black on the breast. Long graduated black tail tipped white. Blackish bill, dark legs.
lewduc1: Small buffy-brown whistling-duck with chestnut-brown plumage, paler greyish head and neck, chestnut rump and rich rufous flanks. Faint yellow eye-ring; dark slate bill and grey legs. Long-necked, upright stance.
lewhon1: Medium olive-green honeyeater with darker streaked underparts, a small bright yellow ear-tuft behind the eye and yellowish facial markings. Slightly curved blackish bill, grey legs. Dull, plain overall with the yellow ear-patch diagnostic.
lewrai1: Compact rail with rufous-brown crown and nape, grey face and breast, olive-brown black-streaked upperparts, and black-and-white barred flanks and belly. Long slightly downcurved pinkish-based bill, dull pinkish-brown legs.
lewtyr1: Tiny slim flycatcher, pale grey above and whitish-yellow below, with a long graduated tail often cocked, faint pale wing edgings and a short pale supercilium. Small dark bill, dark legs. Dull, washed-out plumage.
lewwoo: Distinctive dark woodpecker, glossy greenish-black above, with a dark red face, pale grey collar and breast, and a rosy-pink belly. Stout straight black bill, dark grey legs. Crow-like, broad-winged shape.
libeat1: Tiny bee-eater, bright green above with a cinnamon-buff lower belly, yellow throat bordered below by a narrow black gorget, and a black eyestripe. Slender decurved black bill, square tail, dark legs.
libher: Medium slender heron, slaty blue-grey overall with a purplish-maroon-tinged head and neck. Long pointed bill, blue-grey at base with a black tip; greenish legs. Immature is all white with dusky wingtips.
librol2: Striking roller with greenish head, lilac throat and breast, pale blue belly, rufous-brown back, and turquoise wings showing dark flight feathers. Long blue tail streamers. Stout black bill, yellowish legs.
licdov1: Small slim cuckoo-dove, rich rufous-cinnamon above and on the head, paler buff below, with a long graduated rufous tail. Faint scaling on the neck; dark bill, reddish-pink legs. Long-tailed, dove-shaped.
licpar: Medium green Amazon parrot with a lilac-blue crown and nape, maroon-red forehead, green cheeks and body, and a red wing speculum. Pale horn bill, greyish legs. Yellowish eye-ring.
licspi1: Small slender spinetail, warm brown above with a contrasting pale whitish crown, rufous wings and tail, and dull buffy-grey underparts. Thin pointed dark bill, greyish legs. Plain-faced.
licspi5: Small spinetail, brown above with rufous crown and wings, a fine pale line across the cheek, and dull greyish-buff underparts. Slender dark bill, pale greyish legs. Plumage varies geographically.
lidjay1: Large dark jay, deep glossy purplish-blue on the head, breast, wings and tail, contrasting with a rich chestnut-maroon back and belly. Heavy whitish bill, pale legs. Bold, robust, distinctive two-tone bird.
lifcan1: Small streaky canastero, dark brown above with fine pale streaking, a rufous wing panel and rufous tail, and a whitish throat bordered by a dark malar; underparts dull buff-brown. Thin dark bill, dark legs.
liffal1: Medium slim forest-falcon, slaty-grey above and on the head, with finely grey-barred white underparts. Bare yellow-orange facial skin and eye-ring, pale eyes. Yellow legs, long barred tail, short rounded wings.
lifgle1: Medium foliage-gleaner, warm brown above, with a buff-streaked dark head and breast giving a lined appearance, rufous wings and tail, and a pale buff throat. Straight dark bill, greyish legs.
ligpig1: Small compact green-pigeon; male bright green with a grey crown, maroon-chestnut mantle and wing coverts, and yellow wing edgings. Pale bluish-based bill, reddish legs. Female all green, lacking maroon.
limlew1: Small olive-green leaf warbler, greenish above and yellowish below, with a bright yellow supercilium, dark eyestripe, yellowish crown stripe and two faint wingbars. Dark upper, pale lower mandible; pale legs.
limpki: Large brown wading bird, dark olive-brown overall heavily marked with white teardrop streaks on head, neck and breast. Long slightly downcurved greyish bill, long dark grey legs. Crane-like ibis shape.
linant1: Boldly barred antshrike; male blackish above and below entirely barred black-and-white, with a black crown. Female rufous-brown above with black-and-white barred underparts. Stout hooked black bill, grey legs.
linbar1: Large green barbet, green-bodied with a brown, white-streaked head, neck and breast creating a lined effect. Heavy pale yellowish bill, bare orange-yellow eye-ring, reddish legs. Plain green wings and back.
linsee1: Small finch; breeding male black above with a white crown stripe, white cheek patch, black throat and a broad white malar, white belly. Stubby pale bill, dark legs. Female plain buffy-brown.
linspa: Streaky sparrow, brown above streaked black, with a buffy breast band finely streaked black, grey face, rufous crown stripes and a buff malar. Whitish belly. Conical greyish bill, pinkish legs.
linwoo1: Large black woodpecker with a flaming red crest, white facial stripes running down the neck to form a V on the back, and black-and-white barred belly. Pale chisel-shaped bill, grey legs.
linwoo3: Slender woodcreeper, rufous-brown above with a plain unstreaked back, head and breast streaked buff-white, and a pale throat. Slim slightly decurved pale bill, greyish legs. Long stiff rufous tail.
linwoo4: Slim woodcreeper, rufous-brown above, with buff streaking on the crown, nape and breast and a paler throat; back largely plain. Slender decurved pale bill, greyish legs. Long rufous tail.
lipfly1: Tiny flycatcher; male glossy black above with a long white supercilium, white wing patch and clean white underparts. Female olive-brown above, buffy-white below. Small black bill, dark legs.
liqdov1: Plump terrestrial dove, rich brown above with a purplish gloss, a bold white facial stripe below the eye bordered by dark lines, and pinkish-buff underparts. Reddish bill base, red legs.
lisfly1: Small flycatcher; male slaty-blue above with a white throat and breast and a white wing patch, buffy flanks. Female brown above, paler below. Small dark bill, dark legs. Compact and short-tailed.
litbar1: Small barbet, green-bodied with a brown white-streaked head and breast, and a yellow ear-patch and yellow throat markings. Heavy pale bill, bare eye-ring, reddish legs. Streaky-headed lined appearance.
litbus1: Small stocky bustard, sandy-brown above finely vermiculated black, with a white belly; breeding male shows a grey face and a black neck with two white bands. Short greyish bill, yellowish legs.
litcor1: Small all-black cormorant with a slight greenish gloss; breeding adult shows fine white head plumes and a few white flecks. Short stubby dark bill, blackish legs. Compact, short-billed, rounded-headed.
litcor2: Small white cockatoo with a short rounded crest, pale pinkish wash on the face, and bluish-grey bare skin around the eye. Pale horn bill, grey legs. Underwing and undertail tinged yellow.
litcuc2: Small slim cuckoo, rich rufous-chestnut above and on the head and breast, paler buffy belly, with a long graduated rufous tail. Yellowish bill base, dark eye-ring, greyish legs.
litcur: Small slim curlew, buffy-brown above mottled and streaked dark, with a buff crown striped dark, pale supercilium, and finely streaked breast fading to pale belly. Short slightly downcurved bill, greyish legs.
litfri1: Plain greyish-brown honeyeater with a paler grey-brown head, whitish throat with a yellowish wash, and dull greyish underparts. Bluish bare skin around the eye, dark curved bill, grey legs. Lacks a knob.
litgra1: Small streaky brown warbler, buff-brown above heavily streaked black, with a finely dark-streaked breast and pale buffy underparts. Faint pale supercilium. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs. Long graduated tail.
litgre2: Small dull greenbul, olive-green above and yellowish-olive below, with a slightly greyer head and faint pale eye-ring. Plain-faced with no strong markings. Slender dark bill, greyish legs.
lither2: Tiny hermit hummingbird, greenish-bronze above, buffy-cinnamon below, with a dark mask bordered by buff face stripes and a short whitish-tipped tail. Long decurved bill, reddish lower mandible base.
litlor1: Tiny green lorikeet, bright green overall with a red face and forehead, yellowish-green underparts and a greenish tail with reddish undertail markings. Slender orange-red bill, greyish legs.
litnig1: Small cryptic nightjar, greyish-brown mottled and vermiculated with buff and black, with buffy scapular stripes; male shows white throat and small white wing and tail patches. Tiny dark bill, short legs.
litpar2: Tiny short-tailed parrotlet, mostly green with a lilac-blue tail, blue underwing and dark blue flight feathers, and a green body with darker scaling around the face. Pale horn bill, greyish legs.
litpen1: Small penguin, slate-blue to indigo-grey above and white below, with a clean white belly meeting the blue at the face. Short greyish-black bill, pinkish-white feet. Smallest penguin, upright stance.
litrav1: All-black corvid with a slight glossy purplish sheen, fairly short throat hackles and a relatively short squarish tail. Stout black bill, black legs. Smaller and slimmer than a true raven, crow-like.
litspa1: Tiny sparrowhawk, slate-grey above with a paler grey head, finely rufous-barred white underparts, and a white-spotted dark tail and rump. Yellow eyes, yellow cere and long yellow legs. Short rounded wings.
litspi1: Drab spiderhunter, olive-green above and pale yellowish below faintly streaked, with a plain face and a yellowish throat. Very long strongly decurved blackish bill, greyish legs. Sturdy, short-tailed.
litswi1: Small compact swift, sooty blackish overall with a broad square white rump band and a contrasting white throat patch. Wings broad and scythe-shaped, tail short and nearly square. Bill tiny and black; small dark feet largely hidden.
littho1: Small slender ovenbird, plain warm brown above and pale buffy-white below. Faint pale supercilium, rufous-tinged forehead and crown, and rufous wing edgings; longish graduated brown tail. Short straight pointed dark bill; pinkish-grey legs.
littin1: Small dumpy ground tinamou, dull plain brown above and grey-brown below with a paler buffy throat and belly; head grey-tinged. Body rounded with a short tail and slender neck. Slender slightly decurved dark bill; greenish-grey legs.
litwat1: Large slender grey-brown honeyeater with pale streaking and spotting above and whitish underparts marked with fine pale chevrons; crown darker brown finely streaked whitish. No wattles; chestnut flash in primaries and pale tail tips show in flight. Long slightly decurved dark bill; grey legs.
litwea1: Small weaver; breeding male bright golden-yellow on head and underparts with a black face mask and throat, and yellowish-olive back and wings. Slender pointed blackish bill; reddish to brown eye; pinkish legs. Female plainer dull yellow without mask.
litwoo2: Small woodpecker, olive-brown above narrowly barred buff, with whitish underparts heavily barred dark. Face greyish; male has a red crown and nape, female plain brownish crown. Short chisel-tipped dark bill; greyish legs.
livbul1: Medium bulbul, olive-grey above and whitish below with a faint greyish breast band. Head black with a bold broad white patch from behind the eye across the nape, and a small white cheek mark. Slim blackish bill; dark legs.
livtur1: Striking glossy-green turaco with a tall pointed green crest tipped narrowly white, a white line below the eye and another before it on red bare facial skin around the eye. Body deep green to violet-blue on wings and tail; crimson primaries flash in flight. Short stout red bill; dark feet.
lizbuz1: Small neat grey hawk, pale grey head, back and breast, with a sharp black vertical stripe down the white throat. Lower belly finely barred grey and white; blackish tail with one or two narrow white bands. Short bill with orange-red cere; red-orange legs.
lobber1: Medium drab warbler-like songbird, plain olive-brown above and pale olive-yellow below, brighter on throat and belly. Indistinct dark eyestripe and a narrow pale olive-yellow supercilium sharper before the eye. Long bill, dark grey-brown above with pale pinkish base below; brownish-grey legs.
lobblf1: Medium flycatcher; male deep ocean-blue above with a paler shining blue forehead, rump and shoulder patches, blackish lores, and bright orange throat and breast fading to whitish belly. Long slender black bill; dark legs. Female brown above with orange underparts and bluish tail.
lobcor1: Mostly white cockatoo with a salmon-pink wash across the face and throat, a short whitish recumbent crest, and pale orange-red feather bases showing on the face and breast. Very long upper mandible giving an extended pale horn-coloured bill; grey legs; bluish bare eye-ring.
lobcro1: Large glossy black crow with purplish gloss and a striking pale whitish iris. Heavy long black bill tapering from a broad base; black legs and feet. Relatively short tail; thickset, powerful build.
lobcur: Very large pale cinnamon-buff curlew, mottled brown above and plain buffy-cinnamon below with fine streaking on neck and breast; bright cinnamon underwings. Extremely long strongly decurved bill, dark with a pink base below; long grey legs.
lobgna4: Tiny long-billed gnatwren, warm brown above and buffy-whitish below with a finely streaked face and throat; long graduated tail often cocked, blackish with white tips. Very long thin straight bill; greyish legs.
lobgna5: Tiny slim gnatwren, rich brown above and pale buffy below with a plain unstreaked or faintly marked throat; long graduated blackish tail tipped white, often held cocked. Very long, thin, straight bill; grey legs.
lobher: Large dull-coloured hermit hummingbird, bronze-green above and brownish-grey below, with a dark mask bordered by buff facial stripes and a buffy throat. Central tail feathers very long and white-tipped. Long decurved bill, blackish above and red at base of lower mandible; small feet.
lobpip1: Large robust pipit, sandy grey-brown above with diffuse streaking and plain warm buff underparts only faintly streaked on the breast. Pale supercilium and buff outer-tail edges. Longish bill, dark above with pale base; pale pinkish legs; upright stance.
lobplo1: Medium plover, grey-brown above and white below with a narrow black breast band and black bar across the white forehead; white throat collar. Long blackish bill; dull yellowish to flesh legs; non-breeding birds duller with brownish band.
lobsta1: Medium hummingbird, bronze-green above with a small white postocular spot and whitish malar stripe; throat an iridescent rose-red patch (gorget) bordered below by greyish underparts. White flank tufts and a notched bronzy-blue tail. Very long straight black bill.
lobsun2: Small sunbird; breeding male metallic green-blue on head, back, throat and breast with a maroon-purple band across the lower breast and a blackish belly; bright yellow pectoral tufts. Extremely long, strongly decurved black bill; dark legs. Female olive above, yellowish below.
lobtai1: Tiny long-billed tailorbird, grey-brown above with a rufous-tinged crown and whitish-grey underparts; face greyish. Slender longish graduated tail often cocked. Long fine slightly decurved dark bill; pale pinkish legs.
lobthr: Large long-tailed thrasher, warm grey-brown above and whitish below boldly marked with blackish teardrop spots and streaks. Greyish face with a pale buff eyebrow and orange-yellow eye. Long, strongly decurved blackish bill; pale grey-pink legs.
lobwoo1: Large slender woodcreeper, rich rufous-brown above and on wings and tail, with a strikingly long whitish-buff streaked throat and breast contrasting a darker crown. Extremely long, slender, slightly upturned pale ivory bill; greyish legs; long neck.
lobwre1: Medium wren, warm rufous-brown above and buffy-white below with a bold white supercilium and finely barred buffy flanks; tail barred dark. Long slightly decurved bill, dark above and pale below; pinkish-grey legs.
loceag1: Slender blackish-brown eagle with a very long, narrow pointed black crest drooping from the head. Plumage mostly dark brown to black with white thigh feathering; in flight shows white wing-base patches and barred grey-and-black tail. Yellow cere and feet; yellow eye.
logkin: Large stout kingbird, dark grey-brown above with a blackish head and a concealed orange-red crown patch; whitish below with a pale grey breast wash. Broad blackish tail tipped white. Heavy black bill; black legs.
logshr: Compact grey shrike, pale blue-grey above and whitish below, with a bold black mask through the eye across the forehead and white-edged black wings showing a white wing patch. Black tail with white outer feathers. Short, stout, hooked black bill; black legs.
lotant1: Slender long-tailed antbird; male black above heavily streaked white on crown sides and back with a hidden white interscapular patch, rufous rump, and black white-tipped wings; underparts whitish boldly streaked black with an orange-buff belly. Slender blackish bill; grey legs.
lotbro1: Vivid broadbill, bright grass-green body with a black cap, yellow face patches and a yellow throat; blue patch on crown and blue-and-black wings; long graduated blue tail. Broad flat greenish-yellow bill; greenish legs; stocky big-headed shape.
lotcin1: Large long-tailed ovenbird, greyish-brown above with a bold broad white supercilium narrowing behind the eye and a dark eye band; throat white, underparts paler grey-brown. Buffy wing bars and edgings; long tail. Fairly long straight dark bill; dark legs.
lotfis1: Large pale grey-and-white shrike with a long graduated black tail; pale grey crown and back, white underparts, and black mask and wings with a white wing patch. Stout hooked black bill; black legs.
lothaw1: Slender long-tailed forest hawk, slate-grey to blackish above and white below finely barred grey, with a long blackish tail crossed by narrow white bands and a conspicuous white rump. Yellow cere and eyes; long yellow legs.
lother1: Large dull hermit hummingbird, bronze-green above and greyish below, with a dark mask bordered by buff stripes above and below the eye and a buffy throat. Very long, white-tipped central tail feathers. Long decurved bill, dark above and reddish below.
lotkoe1: Large slim cuckoo, brown above heavily barred and spotted rufous-buff, with pale buff underparts streaked and barred dark; very long barred tail. Dark crown and a pale supercilium. Slightly decurved dark bill; greenish-grey legs.
lotman1: Small manakin; male glossy black with a sky-blue back (mantle), a bright red crown cap, and two greatly elongated central tail streamers. Orange legs; short dark bill. Female and young olive-green with shorter tail projections and orange legs.
lotmea1: Stout long-tailed blackbird; breeding male blackish-brown above streaked buff with a vivid scarlet-red throat, breast and belly and a red eyebrow; whitish lower belly. Long pointed greyish bill; dark legs. Female duller with paler pink-red wash.
lotmin1: Slim long-tailed minivet; male glossy black on head, upperparts and throat with bright scarlet-red lower breast, belly, rump and tail edges, and a red wing patch with an extra red spur. Slender black bill; black legs. Female grey above with yellow underparts and yellow wing-and-tail markings.
lotmoc1: Long-tailed mockingbird, grey-brown above and whitish below, with a bold blackish mask and a striking broad white supercilium; blackish wings with two white wing bars and white-cornered long tail. Slightly decurved black bill; dark legs.
lotmyn1: Robust glossy-black myna with bright orange-yellow bare facial skin around the eye and a yellow patch on the nape and rear crown; long graduated tail. In flight shows a white wing patch. Stout orange bill; yellowish legs.
lotnig1: Slender greyish-brown nightjar finely vermiculated and spotted, with a very long graduated tail; male shows white outer-tail feathers and a small white wing patch in flight. Cryptic mottled plumage with buff scapular lines. Tiny dark bill; short legs.
lotpar2: Slim long-tailed parakeet, green above and yellowish-green below, with a rose-red face and cheeks, blackish chin and a broad black stripe through the lores and below the cheek; bluish crown wash. Very long narrow blue-green tail. Heavy red bill (male); greyish legs.
lotpot1: Large cryptic potoo, mottled and streaked greyish-brown and rufous-buff resembling bark, with a long tail and a large head with a huge gape and big yellow-orange eyes. Underparts paler with dark streaking. Small dark hooked bill; short feet.
lotros1: Long-tailed finch; breeding male soft rosy-pink overall, brightest on face and rump, with silvery-pink streaking, pinkish-white wing bars and a long pink-and-brown tail. Stubby pale conical bill; brownish legs. Female greyish-brown streaked, with whitish wing bars.
lotsco1: Medium screech-owl with prominent long ear tufts, mottled and streaked grey-brown plumage finely vermiculated, with a pale-bordered facial disc and dark streaking below. Cryptic bark-like pattern. Greenish-grey bill; feathered feet; eyes brown to amber.
lotshr1: Long-tailed shrike, grey crown and nape grading to a rufous-brown back, rump and flanks, with whitish underparts and a bold black mask through the eye; black wings show a white wing patch. Long black tail. Stout hooked black bill; black legs.
lotsib1: Slender grey babbler with a long graduated grey tail tipped white, dark grey upperparts and paler grey underparts, and a small whitish wing patch. Pale eye. Slim dark bill; dark legs; long-tailed elegant shape.
lotsti: Tiny long-legged stint; breeding adult warm rufous-and-black scaled above with rufous-edged feathers, a streaked rusty breast and white belly, and a pale split supercilium. Fine dark bill with pale base; distinctively long yellowish-green legs with long toes.
lotsyl1: Small hummingbird; male glossy green above and below with a brilliant violet-blue throat patch and an extremely long, deeply forked iridescent blue-green tail. Short straight black bill. Female green above, whitish below spotted green, with a much shorter tail.
lottap1: Small dark tapaculo, uniformly dark slate-grey overall, slightly paler grey below, with brownish flanks faintly barred and a brownish rump; longish often-cocked tail. Short blackish bill; brownish legs; rounded skulking shape.
lottit5: Tiny round-bodied tit with a very long graduated tail. Whitish head with broad black lateral crown-stripes meeting over the eye, pale silvery-grey throat, pinkish-buff flanks and belly, blackish back and wings, white outer tail feathers. Short stubby black bill, dark legs.
lottri1: Small slim cuckooshrike; male glossy blue-black above and on head with a clean white belly and undertail, white-tipped long graduated tail and a small white wing patch. Female greyer with fine barring below. Slender black bill, dark legs.
lottyr1: Sooty-black tyrant flycatcher with a pale grey crown and nape; male has two greatly elongated, wire-thin central tail feathers far exceeding the body. White edging on some wing feathers. Short black bill, blackish legs; slim upright posture.
lotwid1: Breeding male jet-black with a huge cascading tail many times the body length; shoulder shows a red-and-buff/pale epaulet on the folded wing. Stout conical bluish-grey bill, dark legs. Females and non-breeders streaky brown sparrow-like.
lotwoo4: Plain brown woodcreeper, warm rufous-brown above brightening to rufous wings and rather long tail; faint pale buff streaking on a duller brown breast and throat. Straight slender dark bill, greyish legs; slim climbing posture.
louwat: Streaky brown-backed warbler resembling a small thrush; bold white supercilium broadening behind the eye, white throat, white underparts boldly streaked dark on breast and flanks with buffy-washed rear flanks. Long pinkish legs, dark pointed bill.
lovfai1: Male brilliant: sky-blue crown, ear-coverts and back, black mask and throat-band, blue-and-black breast, whitish belly, long cocked blue-black tail. Female blue-grey above with bright blue tail and rufous lores/eye-ring. Fine black bill.
lowant1: Small antwren; male blue-grey overall with a black throat and breast, two white wing bars and white-spotted black wing coverts. Female warm buff-brown below, grey-brown above. Slim dark bill, greyish legs; long wings, short tail.
loweye2: Small olive-green warbler-like bird with a bold white eye-ring, yellow-green forehead and throat, greyish-white belly, yellow undertail coverts. Fine pointed dark bill, greyish legs; rounded body, short tail.
lowhar1: Large slender harrier; adult black above and on the hood with a white rump, finely barred grey-and-black tail; underparts white with black breast and barring, or a dark morph all sooty-black. Long yellow legs, yellow cere, hooked bill.
lowowl1: Tiny owlet with prominent long whisker-like facial bristles projecting around the face. Warm brown above mottled buff, paler buffy-brown below with faint streaking, no ear tufts. Large dark eyes, pale bill, feathered legs; very small and short-tailed.
lowumb1: Large glossy black cotinga with a forward-curling crest over the bill and a very long feathered wattle hanging from the throat (longest in male). Heavy black bill, black legs; bulky crow-sized body.
lrtdro1: Glossy blue-black drongo with two extremely long bare tail shafts ending in small twisted rackets well beyond the squarish tail. Slight forehead tuft, red eye, stout black hooked bill, black legs; slim graceful body.
lstlar2: Small pale sandy-brown lark, finely streaked above and on a buffy breast, whitish belly, plain face with pale supercilium and dark eye. Short stubby pinkish-yellow conical bill, pale legs; short-tailed compact build.
ltbwar1: Plain warm rufous-brown warbler with a long graduated, broad rounded tail; faint pale supercilium, paler greyish-buff throat and belly. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs; skulking long-tailed shape.
ltgsta1: Glossy starling iridescent green and violet-blue, with a long graduated metallic tail and a black mask around a pale yellow eye. Underparts violet-blue. Black bill, black legs; sleek long-tailed form.
ltrfin1: Slim long-tailed grass finch; grey-brown above with dark streaking, plain pale buffy-grey underparts, a pale supercilium and whitish throat. Sharp conical greyish bill, pinkish legs; long graduated tail.
ltsfly1: Crested silky-flycatcher; soft grey body with a tall pointed grey crest, yellow forehead and undertail, black-and-yellow wings, and a long black tail edged white. Yellow eye-ring, dark bill, dark legs; sleek elegant shape.
ltwpar1: Plump terrestrial partridge, rich rufous-brown overall finely vermiculated grey, with a long broad tail (unusual for a quail), grey-brown breast and a reddish face. Bright red bill and red legs; rounded heavy body.
luchum: Small hummingbird; male glossy green above with a brilliant violet-purple gorget, buffy flanks, and a long deeply forked tail. Long slightly down-curved black bill. Female green above, buff below, with rufous-based tail.
lucwar: Small plain grey warbler, grey-brown above and whitish-grey below with a chestnut crown patch and a chestnut rump, white eye-ring. Fine pointed dark bill, dark legs; very small, short-tailed.
luebus1: Stocky bushshrike; black or slaty above with a bright tawny-rufous crown and orange-rufous breast fading to whitish belly, dark wings and tail. Stout black hooked bill, blue-grey legs.
lunant1: Plain grey antbird (male) with a white throat and a broad white supercilium contrasting the grey face and body; some bare bluish skin around the eye. Stout black bill, pale legs. Female warmer brown-tinged with the same white throat and brow.
lytnig1: Dark mottled nightjar; male has two enormously elongated, ribbon-like outer tail feathers far exceeding the body. Plumage cryptic rufous-brown vermiculated black and grey, with a buffy collar. Small dark bill, short legs.
mabara1: Small toucan (aracari); blackish head, green back, red rump, and a yellow breast crossed by multiple black-and-red bands. Large bill black above and ivory-yellow below with a serrated edge. Bare reddish facial skin, dark legs.
mabeat1: Slim bee-eater, mostly bronzy-green above with a chestnut-tinged crown and nape, blue-washed face with a black eye-mask and white forehead, and elongated central tail streamers. Long down-curved black bill, dark legs.
mabfly1: Small flycatcher; male turquoise-blue above and on the head with a deeper blue breast grading to whitish belly, a darker blue face. Slender black bill, pale legs. Female duller blue-brown with a buffy throat.
mabfly2: Small flycatcher; male deep blue above and on the throat/breast with a rich rufous-orange belly and undertail, blue face. Black bill, dark legs. Female browner with a paler rufous wash below.
mabpar: Small green parakeet with a dull brownish-maroon forehead and crown, scaly grey-and-brown wash across the breast, a maroon belly patch and reddish tail tip. Greenish-blue flight feathers. Pale horn bill, grey legs.
mabphi2: Compact flycatcher-like vanga; male deep glossy blue overall with a velvety maroon throat and breast patch and a black mask. Female plainer rufous-brown above, paler below. Stout black bill, dark legs.
macdov1: Small long-tailed cuckoo-dove, warm reddish-brown to greyish-brown above with a long plain brown tail; breast feathers black-based giving a distinctly spotted/scaled appearance on a pinkish-buff breast. Short dark bill, reddish legs.
machon2: Honeyeater olive-brown above with bold buffy-white streaking on the head, neck and breast, a plain olive crown, and a small orange-yellow ear patch. Fine slightly down-curved dark bill, greyish legs.
mactan1: Striking tanager, deep velvety crimson with a contrasting black mask, black throat, lower belly, wings and tail. Whitish lower mandible base on the silvery-and-black bill, dark legs; vivid scarlet plumage.
macwar: Olive-green above, yellow below; male has a grey hood and breast with a black smudge around the eyes and broken white eye-arcs (no full eye-ring). Throat slaty-grey. Fine pointed bill, pinkish legs.
madant1: Small antwren; male warm brown with a bright rufous back patch, grey face and breast, and a black throat finely stippled white. Female buffier below. Slender dark bill, greyish legs; short-tailed.
madbul1: Slim dark bulbul, slaty grey-brown to blackish overall, slightly paler greyish below, with a loose shaggy crown. Bright orange to reddish bill and orange-red legs are diagnostic; longish tail.
madbuz1: Medium broad-winged buteo; brown above, white below with brown barring on the breast and belly, and a barred brown-and-grey tail. Yellow cere and yellow legs, dark hooked bill; broad wings, short tail.
madcis2: Small streaky brown cisticola; rufous-brown crown, dark-streaked sandy-brown back, plain buffy-white underparts, and a rufous-edged tail tipped pale with a dark subterminal band. Fine pointed bill, pinkish legs.
madcou1: Large long-tailed coucal; glossy black head, body and long broad tail with rich chestnut-rufous wings. Red eye, heavy black down-curved bill, blackish legs; bulky skulking shape.
madcuc1: Slim grey cuckoo; plain blue-grey head, breast and upperparts, with a white belly finely barred dark grey. Yellow eye-ring, long barred grey-and-white tail. Slightly down-curved dark bill yellow at base, yellow legs.
madflu1: Tiny rail-like flufftail; male rich rufous-chestnut head, neck and breast, blackish body finely spotted and barred white, and a short rufous tail. Short dark bill, brownish legs; rounded skulking body.
madhoo1: Cinnamon-pinkish hoopoe with a long erectile fan crest tipped black, bold black-and-white barred wings and tail, and a pinkish-buff head and breast. Long slender down-curved blackish bill, short grey legs.
madlar1: Small stocky lark, sandy grey-brown streaked above, with a chestnut hindneck and shoulder patch, a buffy-white face and breast, and a dark malar mark. Short stout conical pale bill, pinkish legs.
madnig1: Cryptic nightjar mottled greyish-brown vermiculated black and buff, with a buffy collar across the nape; male shows white outer-tail corners and small white wing spots. Tiny dark bill, short legs; flat broad head.
madpet: Small grey-and-white gadfly petrel; grey above with a darker M across the upperwings and a dark cap, clean white underparts and white underwing with dark margins. Short stout black bill, pinkish legs.
madsco1: Small rufous-morph scops-owl, warm rufous-brown finely vermiculated and streaked darker, with small ear tufts and a faintly rimmed facial disc. Yellow eyes, pale greenish bill, feathered legs; compact.
madwag1: Slim long-tailed wagtail; grey crown and back, black breast-band, white throat and supercilium, and a pale yellow lower belly and undertail. Black-and-white wings, long black tail edged white. Fine dark bill, dark legs.
madwhe1: Small white-eye, olive-green above and on the head with a bold white eye-ring, greyish-white throat and breast, and pale yellowish flanks/undertail. Fine pointed dark bill, greyish legs.
mafpar1: Large dark-green parrot (macaw-like) with a maroon-red forehead band, red shoulder/thigh patches and a long pointed tail. Underwing yellowish. Heavy black bill, dark grey legs; bare pale eye-ring.
mafpar3: Small green parakeet with maroon-red lores and forehead, white ear-coverts, a blue-washed crown, brownish scaling on the breast, and a maroon belly patch with a reddish tail. Pale horn bill, grey legs.
magant1: Medium antbird; male slaty-grey overall with bold white tips to wing coverts forming spots and white-tipped tail; female browner-grey above, paler grey below. Dark eye, stout black bill, dark legs.
magfri: Large all-black seabird with very long pointed wings angled at the wrist and deeply forked tail; male glossy black with inflatable scarlet throat pouch, female with white breast and dark head. Long grey hooked bill.
maggoo1: Large goose-like waterbird, glossy black head, neck and wings contrasting with white belly and back; tall knobbed black bill, distinctive bony crown bump, long orange legs and partly webbed feet.
maghor2: Slim grey hornbill, brownish-grey above and paler greyish below with white-tipped tail; long curved pale yellowish bill without large casque. Greyish overall with white belly, dark legs.
maghum1: Large dark hummingbird appearing blackish; male with glittering violet-purple crown and emerald-green throat (gorget), dark green body, white spot behind eye; long straight black bill. Female greyish below, green above.
maghum2: Large dark hummingbird; male with violet-blue crown and brilliant blue-green glittering throat, blackish-green underparts, white postocular spot; long straight black bill. Female green above, grey below, very similar to Rivoli's.
maglar1: Pied black-and-white songbird; black back, breast band and crown contrasting with white face, throat, belly and wing patches; white-and-black wings, pale eye, slender pointed pale bill, dark legs.
magoys1: Large black-and-white oystercatcher with all-black head, breast and upperparts and clean white belly; long straight orange-red bill, yellow eye with red eye-ring, pale pinkish to whitish legs.
magpen1: Medium penguin, black above and white below, with two black bands across the white chest and a black face bordered by a white horseshoe band curving around the cheeks; black bill, black feet.
magrif3: Large velvety black bird-of-paradise; male iridescent, with brilliant metallic blue-green triangular breast shield and a glittering blue-green crown; long curved black bill. Female rufous-brown above, buff barred below.
magrob: Pied robin-chat; male glossy blue-black on head, breast and upperparts with a white belly and bold white wing stripe, black-and-white tail often cocked. Female greyer above. Slender black bill, dark legs.
magtan2: Striking large black-and-white tanager; glossy blue-black head, throat, back, wings and long tail contrasting sharply with pure white belly and lower back. Pale yellow eye, stout black bill, dark legs.
magtap1: Tiny dark tapaculo, uniformly slaty-grey to blackish overall, often with brownish flanks and faint barring on the rear flanks and undertail. Short cocked tail, fine dark bill, brownish legs.
magwar: Small warbler; grey crown, black mask and back streaks, yellow underparts with bold black necklace-streaking, white eye-ring arcs, white wing patch and a distinctive white-banded black tail. Thin dark bill.
magwoo1: Very large black woodpecker; male with entirely crimson-red head and crest, glossy black body, white wing patch. Female black-crested with red only at base of bill. Long ivory-grey chisel bill, dark legs.
malbrw1: Plain drab warbler, uniform grey-brown above and paler buffy-grey below with no strong markings; long graduated tail often held cocked. Slender dark bill, pale pinkish legs, dark eye.
malhac1: Slim hawk-like cuckoo, grey-brown above, grey head and throat, with rufous-tinged white underparts faintly streaked; broadly barred tail. Yellow eye-ring, short hooked bill, yellow legs.
malia1: Medium babbler-like bird, plain olive-green above with a yellowish head and yellow underparts; wings and tail rufous or olive depending on region. Pinkish-brown stout bill, dark legs.
malkin1: Tiny brilliant kingfisher; bright blue back, crown and crest finely barred black, rufous-orange underparts and cheeks, white throat and neck patch; long bright red bill, red feet.
mallar1: Small rufous-toned crested lark, warm sandy-brown above with bold dark streaking, short pointed crest, buff underparts streaked on the breast, rufous wings; pale stout bill, pale pinkish legs.
mallau1: Olive-brown laughingthrush with a grey crown and nape, rufous-tinged wings and tail, buffy-grey underparts and white throat; pale eye, dark slender bill, pinkish legs.
malnig1: Large dark eared-nightjar, intricately mottled brown, grey and rufous cryptic plumage with prominent feathered ear-tufts on the head; no white wing or tail spots. Tiny bill, large dark eyes.
malpar1: Slim long-tailed parakeet, mostly bluish-grey body with green wings, a narrow black-and-bluish collar; bright red upper bill with yellow tip, very long blue-and-yellow tapering tail, grey legs.
malplo1: Small pale sand plover, sandy-grey above, white below with an incomplete dark breast band, white forehead and collar, thin black eyeline and a black-and-rufous crown bar. Black bill, greyish-pink legs.
malsta1: Small grey starling with a pale whitish head and underparts, grey back and wings, and rufous-buff vent; pale eye, bluish bill with dark tip, pinkish legs.
malsun1: Long-tailed sunbird; breeding male entirely brilliant metallic emerald-green with elongated central tail feathers and yellow shoulder tufts; long curved black bill. Female dull olive-yellow below, greyish-olive above.
maltro1: Striking trogon; male with black head and breast, white breast crescent, crimson-red belly, cinnamon-rufous back and finely vermiculated black-and-white wing panel. Bluish bill, blue eye-ring, grey feet.
malwoo1: Stocky grey woodshrike, brownish-grey above with a darker mask through the eye, whitish underparts and a pale eyebrow; stout hooked dark bill, dark legs, pale eye.
mamrob1: Pied magpie-robin; male glossy blue-black on head, breast and upperparts with white belly and white wing patch, dark tail. Female greyer-brown. Slender black bill, dark legs.
manant1: Medium antbird; male plain slaty-grey overall with white-tipped wing coverts and white-tipped tail; female rufous-brown above, buff-orange below. Dark eye, stout dark bill, grey legs.
manbuw1: Plain warm rufous-brown bush warbler, unmarked above, with buffy-white underparts, a pale buff supercilium and dark eyeline; broad rounded tail. Slender pale-based bill, pinkish legs.
mancuc: Slim long-tailed cuckoo, grey-brown above, buffy-cinnamon below, with a black mask through the eye; long graduated tail with bold white spots below. Decurved bill, yellow lower mandible.
manduc1: Compact duck; male with rich chocolate-brown head and neck, finely speckled grey body, dark mane on nape, black-and-white speckled breast. Female mottled brown with white eye-stripes. Short grey bill.
manger1: Tiny warbler-like bird, plain grey-brown above and whitish below, with a faint white eyebrow and white-tipped dark tail; thin pointed bill, slender legs, red eye.
manher1: Stocky rufous night-heron, rich chestnut-brown overall with a black crown and shaggy crest, finely vermiculated wings and a black-streaked throat; thick dark bill, greenish-yellow legs, large eyes.
manhon1: Medium olive-grey honeyeater, greyish-brown above, with yellowish-streaked throat and breast fading to whitish belly, and a yellow plume behind the eye; slightly curved dark bill, dark legs.
manpit1: Plump short-tailed pitta; buff-brown head with black crown stripes and eyestripe, green back and wings, buff underparts, scarlet-red belly patch, blue wing patch; very large stout black bill, pinkish legs.
manrai1: Large grey-brown rail, brownish above with grey face and breast, barred black-and-white flanks; very long slightly decurved orange-and-grey bill, dull pinkish legs, red eye.
manrew1: Plain reed warbler, warm brown above and buffy-white below, with a distinct whitish supercilium bordered above by a faint dark line; flat forehead, pointed bill with pale lower mandible, pinkish legs.
manswa1: Small swallow with iridescent green-blue upperparts, clean white underparts and rump, and a small white line above the lores; slightly forked tail, tiny black bill, dark legs.
manvir1: Small drab vireo, greyish-olive above, dull whitish-yellow below, with pale yellowish spectacles (eye-ring plus loral line) and faint pale wing bars; stout hooked grey bill, grey legs.
manwhi1: Plain grey whistler, uniform brownish-grey above, pale grey-buff to whitish below, with a whiter throat and no strong markings; sturdy dark bill, dark legs, dark eye.
mao1: Large dark honeyeater, blackish on the head and breast grading to olive-green on the wings and body, with a dark greenish mark below the eye; long curved black bill, black legs.
mapfly1: Paradise-flycatcher; male variable, often rufous-and-black with a glossy blue-black hooded head, rufous back, wings and very long tail streamers, or a white morph; bright blue bill and eye-ring. Female rufous, shorter-tailed.
mapfly2: Small paradise-flycatcher; male with glossy blue-black head, rufous-brown back, wings and tail, and greyish underparts, with modestly elongated tail; bright blue bill and eye-ring. Female similar but duller.
maphor1: Large pied hornbill, glossy black with white belly, white wing tips and white outer tail; massive yellowish casqued bill with a dark patch. White facial skin, dark legs, red eye in male.
mapphe1: Medium peacock-pheasant; male dull brown finely speckled, with metallic blue-green ocelli (eyespots) on the wings and tail, a short crest and bare pinkish-orange facial skin. Female smaller, duller, fewer ocelli.
maqcan1: Slender long-tailed canastero, warm rufous-brown above with a plain unstreaked back and brown cap, buffy underparts and a rufous chin spot, with yellow at the base of the bill; long slim tail, dark legs.
marbab1: Slim long-legged ground bird; rich rufous-brown crown, nape and underparts, brown back, with a long white-bordered black stripe down the neck side and white throat; long slender black bill, long pinkish legs.
marbab2: Small warm brown babbler, rufous-brown above and tawny-buff below with fine dark streaking on the throat and breast; pale supercilium, slender pointed dark bill, pinkish legs, dark eye.
marcre1: Small ground bird with a bold black face mask and chest crescent, white supercilium and throat, rich chestnut underparts, and brown back; wings strongly marked with white feather edges. Dark bill, pinkish legs.
marfro1: Large frogmouth with a huge flat hooked bill and big yellow eyes; cryptic plumage mottled grey-brown to rufous with bold white ocellated spots and dark streaks resembling bark. Short legs, broad head.
margod: Large long-legged shorebird, warm cinnamon-buff overall with dark barring above and on underparts; long slightly upturned bill pink at base and dark at tip. Grey legs; cinnamon underwing in flight.
margra1: Small streaky warbler, warm reddish-brown above heavily streaked black, paler buff-white below with faint streaking; long graduated tail with pale-edged dark feathers. Fine pointed bill, pinkish legs.
margua1: Large dark guan, glossy blackish overall with greenish gloss, scaly white-edged feathers on neck and breast; bare red throat dewlap and red orbital skin. Dark bill, reddish legs.
marmur: Small chunky alcid; breeding adult dark brown above and heavily mottled brown below, cryptic and barred all over. Short slender dark bill, dark eyes, dark legs; compact short-necked shape.
marori2: Striking oriole with deep maroon-crimson body and black hood, wings and tail; underparts dark maroon. Pale bluish-grey bill, dark legs; staring red eye and bold black-and-maroon contrast.
marsee1: Small finch-like seedeater; male has slate-grey head and back, white throat and broad white collar, and rich chestnut-rufous breast and underparts. Stubby pale bluish-grey conical bill, dark legs.
marspi3: Small drab spinetail, plain grey-brown above with a rufous crown and rufous wing panel; greyish face, whitish throat, and dull greyish underparts. Long graduated brownish tail, slender dark bill.
marspi4: Compact spinetail with bright reddish-chestnut crown and short crest edged black, rufous back, wings and tail; grey face, white throat, greyish-buff underparts. Long pointed tail, slender bill.
marsto1: Huge bald-headed stork with pinkish bare head and neck, a long pendulous pink throat pouch, blackish-grey upperparts and wings, and dirty-white underparts and neck ruff. Massive pale wedge-shaped bill.
marsun2: Slender sunbird; male is iridescent metallic green on head, back and throat, with a broad maroon-red breast band and blackish belly. Long decurved black bill, dark legs.
marswi: Tiny dark swiftlet, sooty grey-brown above and slightly paler grey-brown below; faintly paler rump, short slightly forked tail and narrow swept-back wings. Tiny black bill, dark legs.
martap1: Small dark tapaculo, blackish-grey overall with a slightly paler grey throat and breast, and brown-tinged flanks and rump with faint barring. Short cocked tail, slender black bill, brownish legs.
marthr2: Medium thrush, dull olive-brown above; whitish throat and breast heavily streaked dark, grading to plain pale buff-white belly. Yellow bill, yellow eye-ring, and yellowish-brown legs.
marwar4: Small leaf-warbler, bright green above and yellow below, with blackish lateral crown-stripes and a grey median crown-stripe; bold yellow eye-ring, faint wing-bar, white in outer tail. Fine bill, pale legs.
marwoo1: Stocky woodpecker, deep maroon-chestnut overall with a darker head; uniform rich reddish-brown plumage lacking bold markings. Pale yellowish heavy chisel bill, greyish legs; male shows red neck patch.
marwre: Small marsh wren, warm brown above with a black-and-white streaked back and blackish crown; whitish supercilium, white throat and breast, buffy flanks. Slender dark bill, often-cocked tail, pinkish legs.
masboo: Large white booby with sharply contrasting black flight feathers and black tail; bare blackish facial mask around the eyes. Stout pointed yellowish bill, yellow eyes, greyish legs.
mascan1: Streaky canastero, brown above heavily streaked black; underparts whitish with extensive dark streaking, and a rufous wing patch and chin spot. Pale eye, long graduated rufous-edged tail, slender bill.
masflo1: Vivid deep-blue flowerpiercer with a black mask around the eye and forehead; bright pale blue eye, glossy violet-blue body. Short upturned black bill with hooked tip, dark legs.
masgna1: Small slender gnatcatcher, blue-grey above and pale grey below; broad black mask through the eye and across the forehead. Long black tail edged white, thin black bill, dark legs.
maslap1: Large plover, brown back and wings, white underparts and neck, with a black crown; large bright yellow facial wattles and yellow bill. Yellow legs, red wing carpal spur.
maslau1: Plain greyish-brown laughingthrush with a bold black mask through the eye over a pale forehead; paler buff-grey underparts and warm rufous-brown vent. Stout dark bill, reddish-brown legs.
masmar1: Stocky martin, grey-brown above and whitish below with diffuse grey-brown streaking on throat and breast; slightly notched tail. Short black bill, dark legs; broad-winged swallow shape.
masowl2: Small scops-owl, rufous to greyish-brown finely vermiculated and streaked; short ear-tufts, pale facial disc rim, and bold dark streaking below. Bright yellow eyes, dark bill, feathered legs.
maspar2: Large stocky parrot, mostly green with a blackish-blue face mask around the eyes and bill; bluish wash on crown, reddish thighs. Heavy pale grey hooked bill, grey legs.
massal1: Large grosbeak-like saltator, slate-grey above and on hood with a black mask, white throat and a black band across the upper breast; grey belly. Heavy black conical bill, dark legs.
mastan1: Bright tanager, turquoise-blue with a black mask, black scaled back, and black streaking; pale blue underparts and crown, yellowish wash. Stout black bill, dark legs.
mastit1: Stocky pale tityra; male is silvery-white with a black crown, mask and flight feathers; bare red skin around the eye and base of bill. Stout black-tipped red bill, dark legs.
mastro1: Trogon with metallic green upperparts and head, a black face mask, fine white breast band, and bright red belly; finely white-barred wing coverts. Yellow bill, dark legs; black-and-white barred undertail.
maswar1: Plain warbler, warm brown above and buffy-whitish below with an indistinct pale supercilium; unstreaked plumage and a long rounded tail. Long slender dark bill, greyish legs.
maswhe3: Small white-eye, olive-green above and greyish-buff below with a yellowish throat and vent; faint pale eye-ring (lacking the bold white ring of others). Fine dark bill, greyish legs.
maswoo1: Sleek woodswallow, blue-grey overall with a black face mask and black throat; paler grey underparts. Stout blue-grey black-tipped bill, dark legs; broad triangular wings, short tail.
masyel2: Warbler with olive-green upperparts and bright yellow underparts; male has a black mask bordered above by a blue-grey band. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
matpar2: Small green parakeet with maroon-red lower belly patch and maroon tail; scaly dusky-edged breast feathers and reddish on the wing bend. Pale horn hooked bill, grey legs.
mattap1: Small dark tapaculo, slate-grey overall with a paler grey throat and breast and brownish flanks and rump faintly barred. Short cocked tail, slender black bill, brownish legs.
mauala: Small honeycreeper, olive-green above and bright yellow below, brightest on the throat and breast; plain face with faint pale lores. Straight pointed dark bill, dark legs.
maubul1: Dark bulbul, sooty grey-brown to olive overall with a slightly shaggy crown and paler grey throat; uniform dusky plumage. Bright orange bill and orange legs, dark eye.
maufod1: Small weaver-finch; breeding male is dull olive-brown with a bright orange-red head, throat and breast and a red rump. Stout pointed dark conical bill, brownish legs.
maupar1: Large green parakeet, uniformly bright green with a faint blue wash on the head and a narrow black collar; long tapering green tail. Heavy red hooked bill, grey legs.
mauwhe1: Small white-eye, olive-green above and pale greyish-white below with a yellowish-green throat; bold white eye-ring. Slender slightly decurved dark bill, greyish legs.
mawbab1: Cryptic terrestrial wren-babbler, rich brown above and below with fine pale-and-dark marbled scaling and spotting all over; short tail. Stout dark bill, sturdy pale pinkish legs.
mawqua1: Plump terrestrial quail, mottled brown overall finely vermiculated and barred, with a rufous-brown crest and bold pale-buff supercilium; bare red orbital skin. Dark bill, dark legs.
mawthr1: Large dark thrush, deep blue-black overall with brilliant cobalt-blue shining patches on the forehead and shoulders. Long black bill, blackish legs; glossy indigo iridescence in good light.
