mawtyr1: Pied tyrant, white head and underparts with a broad black mask through the eye to the nape and black back, wings and tail; clean white-and-black contrast. Slender black bill, black legs.
mbopar2: Spectacular bird-of-paradise; male has a yellow nape cape, dark green iridescent breast shield, maroon-brown back, yellow wings, and two long curled blue-green central tail wires. Bluish bill, bluish legs.
mccfin1: Boldly patterned finch, grey-brown above with a black-and-white striped face, white supercilium and black malar stripe; whitish underparts washed buff with rufous flanks. Stout dark conical bill, pale legs.
mccfly1: Small olive flycatcher, dull olive-green above and pale yellowish below with faint dusky streaking on the breast; plain face with a faint eye-ring. Short bill dark above, pale below; pale legs.
mcclon: Stocky longspur; breeding male has a black crown, black breast crescent and black malar, white face, grey upperparts and chestnut wing shoulder patch. Pinkish conical bill, dark legs; white-sided tail with black T.
mccspi1: Small drab rufous-and-grey spinetail; brown back, plain greyish underparts, rufous crown and rufous wing panel, often a small dark throat patch. Long graduated brown tail, slender dark bill, greyish legs.
mcgcus1: Medium grey cuckooshrike; grey crown, back and rump, black forehead, face and chest, white belly. White shoulder patch and long white stripe down the dark wing, white outer tail feathers. Black bill and legs.
mcgdov1: Small plump dove; male slaty blue-grey overall with deep maroon-chestnut breast patch, two iridescent dark wing bars, white face streak below eye. Short dark bill, reddish legs; female browner.
mckbun: Mostly white Arctic bunting; breeding male nearly pure white with small black wingtips and a few black tail marks, faint pale buff on back. Stubby yellow-pink bill, black legs; very pale snow-coloured overall.
mcptit1: Tiny dull penduline-tit; greyish-buff above, paler whitish-buff below, plain pale grey head with no mask. Pale lores, faint pale eye-ring, short conical grey bill, greyish legs; small rounded body.
mcrtyr1: Tiny brightly multicoloured tyrant; green back, black-and-yellow striped head, bright yellow throat and breast, scarlet vent. Bold black eyestripe and yellow supercilium, white wing patch, blackish tail. Fine dark bill.
meabun1: Streaky brown bunting; rufous-brown back, warm rusty-buff underparts, chestnut crown and ear-coverts framed by bold black-and-white face stripes and white submoustachial. Streaked flanks, white outer tail. Conical greyish bill, pinkish legs.
meapar: Large bulky green amazon parrot; overall dull green with a frosted whitish 'mealy' bloom on nape and back, small yellow crown patch, red wing speculum. Pale eye-ring, horn-coloured bill, grey legs.
megsta1: Glossy iridescent starling; deep metallic blue-green body and head, long graduated tail, rich purple-blue gloss on wings. Bright yellow-orange eye, slender black bill, dark legs; slim long-tailed shape.
megtan1: Small bright tanager; mostly shining metallic green body, blackish lores and around eye, with a coppery-buff or golden crown patch. Black on flight feathers, green-edged wings. Short dark bill, greyish legs.
melbla1: All-black thrush-sized blackbird; uniform glossy bluish-black plumage with no markings. Dark eye, fairly long pointed black bill, black legs; slim, slightly long-tailed icterid.
melfly1: Small flycatcher; male glossy blue-black head, throat and upperparts, contrasting white belly and undertail, fine white markings on wing. Slight crest, broad flat bill, dark legs; female greyer with rusty wash.
melkin1: Stocky kingfisher; greenish-blue crown and upperparts, broad whitish-buff collar and underparts, dark eyestripe through to nape. Large dagger-like blackish bill with paler lower base, dark legs.
melscr1: Plump dark megapode; sooty brown-black body, slightly greyer head with short erectile crest, bare reddish facial skin. Stout pale-tipped bill, large strong reddish-brown legs and big feet; short tail, heavy build.
melthr: Warm brown laughingthrush; rufous-brown overall with fine dark streaks on crown and nape, plain rusty underparts. Striking pale blue-white eye-ring extending into a thin white plume behind the eye. Yellowish bill, pinkish legs.
mertap1: Small dark tapaculo; uniform slaty-grey body, slightly paler grey throat and belly, faint brownish wash on flanks and rear. Short cocked tail, fine black bill, pinkish-brown legs; rounded skulking shape.
metpig1: Large heavy pigeon; dark slaty-grey body with strong green-and-purple iridescent gloss on neck and breast, paler grey forehead. Whitish or pale throat in some, red eye, reddish legs, dark bill with pale tip.
metsta1: Glossy black starling; iridescent green-and-purple sheen overall, long pointed graduated tail. Bright glaring red eye, slender black bill, black legs; slim, long-tailed shape (juvenile whitish below, streaked).
mewgul2: Small grey-and-white gull; pale grey back and wings, white head and underparts, black wingtips with white spots ('mirrors'). Rounded head, dark eye, thin unmarked yellow bill, yellow-green legs.
mexchi: Grey chickadee; grey back, pale grey-white underparts with greyer flanks, black cap and large black bib. Broad white cheeks, no white wing edging. Short black bill, dark legs; typical chickadee shape.
mexduc: Mottled brown dabbling duck; uniformly dark brown body, buffy-edged feathers, slightly paler buff face and throat, dark crown and eyeline. Blue-purple speculum bordered black (no white), yellow-olive bill (male), orange legs.
mexjay3: Large crestless blue jay; deep azure-blue head, wings, rump and tail, pale grey-brown back, dull pale grey underparts. No crest, dark eye, stout black bill, black legs; long-tailed bold jay.
mexjay4: Large crestless jay; dull blue head, wings and tail, brownish-grey back, pale grey underparts. No crest, no necklace, dark eye, stout black bill, black legs; chunky long-tailed jay.
mexpar1: Tiny stub-tailed parrotlet; mostly bright green, paler yellowish-green face and underparts, male with turquoise-blue rump and blue wing patch. Short pointed tail, small pale horn bill, pinkish legs.
meypar1: Stocky brown-and-green parrot; dusky brown head, back and breast, bright green belly and rump, yellow crown patch and yellow shoulders/thighs. Pale eye, dark grey hooked bill, grey legs.
mfbtyr1: Small olive bristle-tyrant; olive-green above, yellowish below, grey crown contrasting with face. Bold blackish ear-patch and white eye-ring/supercilium giving marbled face look, two yellowish wing bars. Small dark bill.
micmyz1: Small bright honeyeater; male glossy scarlet-red head, breast and rump with black wings, tail and belly patches. Slender slightly downcurved black bill, dark legs; female duller olive-grey with reddish tinge.
micsta1: Stocky dark starling; dull glossy greenish-black body with subtle bronze sheen, plain overall. Striking pale yellow-white eye, heavy black bill, dark legs; sturdy short-tailed island starling.
midflo1: Very small flowerpecker; male dark glossy blue-black above, white underparts, sometimes a faint dusky breast streak. Tiny stubby dark bill, short tail, dark legs; compact rounded body.
migtyr1: Tiny olive flycatcher; bright olive-green crown and upperparts, pale yellow underparts with yellower throat. Rusty-ochre forehead and lores, whitish eye-ring and faint supercilium, faint yellowish wing bars. Small bill, long tail.
migwar: Plain brown grasshopper-warbler; uniform olive-brown above with faint streaking, buffy-white below, whitish throat and faint pale supercilium. Rounded brown tail, slender dark bill, pinkish legs; skulking unstreaked shape.
milmac: Large mostly-green macaw; bright green body, scarlet-red forehead patch, blue flight feathers and lower back. Bare whitish facial skin with fine dark lines, large blackish bill, grey legs; long tapered tail.
miltap1: Small dark tapaculo; mostly uniform slaty-grey, paler grey throat and breast, brownish wash and faint barring on rear flanks and undertail. Short cocked tail, fine black bill, pale brownish legs.
minhor1: Small hornbill; black body and wings, rufous-buff to whitish tail with broad black band, creamy-white face and throat. Pale yellowish bill with reddish ridges and pale casque, bare pinkish eye-skin (male), dark legs.
miosun2: Small double-collared sunbird; male metallic green head, back and throat, narrow red breast band above a pale grey belly, with yellow pectoral tufts. Slender downcurved black bill, dark legs; female plain olive-grey.
miowrw3: Small barred warbler; greyish-brown above, whitish below finely barred dusky across breast and flanks, plain greyish head. Longish often-cocked tail, fine dark bill, pinkish legs; slim scrub warbler.
miskit: Slim grey raptor; pale grey head and underparts, darker grey back, blackish wings and tail. Pale whitish-grey secondaries showing as paler wing panel, red eye, small black hooked bill, yellow legs.
misrob1: Slim scrub-robin; warm brown back, whitish underparts, rufous rump and tail with blackish subterminal band and white tips. White supercilium and dark moustachial stripe, fine dark bill, pinkish legs; cocked tail.
mistle1: Small flowerpecker; male glossy blue-black above, bright scarlet throat and breast, white belly with black central stripe, red undertail. Short stubby dark bill, dark legs; compact rounded body (female greyer).
mistyr1: Tiny pale tyrannulet; greyish-olive above, pale yellowish-white below, greyer head with whitish lores and faint eye-ring. Plain wings or faint wing edging, small dark bill, dark legs; small long-tailed shape.
mitpar: Medium green parakeet; mostly green body, scattered red-and-maroon feathers on face, forehead, throat and bend of wing. Bare white eye-ring, long pointed tail, pale horn bill, greyish legs.
mobela1: Large crested elaenia; olive-brown mottled-streaked back, grey throat and breast, pale yellow belly. Distinctive spiky bushy white crest with horn-like dark tufts and white crown patch, two whitish wing bars. Dark bill.
mobfin1: Bold brushfinch; olive-green back, grey head with rufous crown, bright yellow throat and breast, olive-yellow belly. Conspicuous white moustachial/loral stripe (white face mark), short conical black bill, dark legs.
mobpar1: Medium partridge; warm brown upperparts marked with chestnut and buff, buffy underparts heavily spotted with dark crescents. Bold white supercilium, rufous cheek, dark eyeline, stout greyish bill, greenish legs.
mobtan1: Striking dark tanager; deep moss-green back and wings, glossy blue-black head, throat and underparts, golden-yellow forehead and rump. Black wings edged green, stout black bill, dark legs; chunky body.
mocant1: Plain grey antshrike; male uniform mouse-grey overall, slightly paler below, with concealed white interscapular patch and faint pale wing-feather tips. Stout black hooked bill, grey legs; female brownish.
moccha1: Bold cliff-chat; male glossy blue-black head, breast and back, contrasting bright chestnut-rufous belly, vent and rump. White wing-shoulder patch, blackish tail, slender black bill, black legs; robin-like build.
mocsun2: Plain dull sunbird; male sooty grey-brown overall, slightly paler below, with faint metallic greenish-blue gloss on crown and shoulders. Slender downcurved black bill, dark legs; nondescript drab sunbird.
moctap1: Small uniform tapaculo; overall plain bluish-grey, slightly paler grey throat and belly, faint brownish tinge on rear flanks. Short cocked tail, fine black bill, brownish legs; rounded skulking shape.
mocthi1: Small drab thistletail; grey-brown body, plain greyish underparts, faint rufous forehead and small rusty throat patch. Long narrow pointed brown tail, slender dark bill, greyish legs; slim furtive shape.
moctyr2: Small slender flycatcher, olive-green above and yellow below, with a grayish crown, bold yellow eye-ring, dark mottled cheek patch, and two faint yellowish wing bars. Thin dark bill; pale legs; long slim tail often cocked.
mofgle1: Warm rufous-brown ovenbird with bright buff-ochre eye-ring and broad buff supercilium, finely streaked buffy throat and breast, rufous wings and tail contrasting with browner back. Straight slim bill; grayish legs; medium size.
mofsco1: Small grayish-brown screech-owl with short ear-tufts, finely vermiculated cryptic plumage streaked darker below, pale facial disc rimmed dark, and yellow eyes. Greenish-gray bill; feathered legs to feathered toes; compact rounded shape.
mogjay1: Pale sandy-buff ground-jay with black crown patch, glossy blue-black wings showing a large white wing flash, and black-and-white tail. Long downcurved black bill; long blackish legs; slim, long-legged terrestrial build.
mogwar1: Large robust warbler, rich rufous-brown above and buffy below, with a rufous crown, bold black-and-white moustachial stripe bordering a white throat, and a long broad graduated tail. Stout dark bill; pinkish legs.
mohcuc1: Slim hawk-like cuckoo, gray-brown above and white below with bold rufous-brown barring across breast and belly, a grayish head, dark moustachial stripe, and a long barred tail. Yellow eye-ring; yellow legs.
moipig1: Large heavy pigeon, vinous-maroon and gray above with a pale gray head and breast, deep chestnut belly and undertail, and a broad pale terminal tail band. Reddish bill; dark red legs; bulky long-tailed form.
molfly1: Compact monarch flycatcher; male glossy blue-black on head, throat and upperparts with a contrasting cinnamon-rufous belly and breast. Broad-based bluish-black bill; dark legs; small rounded body with short crest.
molpit1: Stocky short-tailed pitta, deep green above with a black head, glossy blue shoulder patch and rump, and a bright scarlet-red belly. Stout black bill; pinkish legs; rounded plump body, very short tail.
molsta1: Glossy starling, uniformly dark blackish with strong green and purple iridescence over the whole plumage. Bright red eye; straight black bill; black legs; medium size with a square tail and pointed wings.
momwar1: Small dark scrubwren-like bird, rich dark brown above and pale buffy-brown below with a white throat, some races showing a dark chest band. Slim dark bill; pale eye in male; pinkish legs; rounded short-tailed shape.
momwoo1: Medium green-backed woodpecker, spotted and barred yellowish-green above, finely spotted whitish below; male with a red crown and nape, female with crown spotted. Grayish bill; greenish legs; barred greenish tail.
monlar2: Small plain lark, sandy gray-brown above with dark streaking and pale fringes, whitish below with a streaked breast band, and a short pale supercilium. Short stout pale bill; pinkish legs; rounded, compact build.
monnig1: Cryptic gray-brown nightjar finely vermiculated and spotted, with a grayish crown, buff nape collar, and a dark throat bordered by white patches; male shows white wing flashes and white tail corners. Tiny bill; short legs.
monoro1: Large oropendola; chestnut-and-black body with bare blue-and-pink facial skin, a black head and breast, and a bright yellow outer tail. Massive pale orange-tipped black bill; blackish legs; long-bodied, long-tailed.
monqua: Plump round quail; male with bold black-and-white harlequin face pattern, blue-gray streaked back, and a cinnamon belly flanked by black-spotted white sides; bushy crest. Short dark bill; gray legs; very rotund body.
monwoo1: Slender woodcreeper, warm rufous-brown above with a buff-streaked back and crown, finely streaked buffy underparts, and rufous wings and tail. Long slightly decurved pale bill; grayish legs; slim climbing posture.
morepo2: Small dark hawk-owl, dark brown above spotted white, heavily mottled and barred rufous-brown and white below, with a rounded head (no ear-tufts) and yellow eyes in a pale-flecked face. Dark bill; yellowish feet.
mosbou1: Robust bush-shrike, glossy blue-black above and rich buffy-rufous to tawny below, lacking wing stripe. Stout hooked black bill; dark legs; medium plump body with a longish rounded tail; sexes similar.
mosfin1: Sparrow-like finch; male gray overall with a darker hooded head, faintly streaked back, white belly, and yellowish lower mandible. Conical bill; yellowish legs; whitish wing and tail edgings; medium-small build.
mosowl1: Medium scops-owl with prominent ear-tufts, variable gray-brown to rufous cryptic plumage finely vermiculated and streaked black, with a pale-rimmed facial disc and yellow-orange eyes. Dark bill; feathered legs.
mosowl2: Small scops-owl with short ear-tufts, warm rufous-brown to gray cryptic plumage spotted buff on the crown and shoulders, pale facial disc, and pale yellow eyes. Pale bill; bare yellowish toes; compact build.
motduc: Mottled brown dabbling duck, body buff-and-dark scaled throughout, with a plainer paler tan head and neck, dark eyestripe, and a violet-blue speculum. Yellow-olive bill (male) or orange-spotted (female); orange legs.
motflo1: Tiny flowerpecker with lavender-gray face, chest and belly, the chest showing brown mottling, and grayish-olive upperparts; black at bill base and lores. Short stubby blackish bill; dark legs; very small, short-tailed.
motowl: Medium woodland owl with no ear-tufts, dark brown above mottled buff, whitish below with bold brown streaking, and a plain brown facial disc set off by whitish eyebrows; dark brown eyes. Pale bill; feathered legs.
motpet: Gray-and-white gadfly petrel, ashy-gray above with a dark M across the wings, white belly contrasting with a dark gray breast band and underwing bar. Black bill; pinkish legs; medium, fast-flying seabird.
motpic1: Tiny piculet, olive-brown above finely barred dusky, buffy below mottled and barred darker; male with a red-spotted forecrown, black hindcrown spotted white. Short straight black bill; gray legs; stubby tail.
mouant: Very small antwren, gray above and paler gray below, the male with a black throat patch and white-tipped wing coverts; female buffier. Short thin bill; pale legs; tiny, short-tailed, active warbler-like shape.
mouant1: Plump round antpitta, olive-brown above with a gray crown and nape, rich buff-ochre throat and breast, and a bold buff-and-black scaled or moustachial pattern on the malar. Stout dark bill; long pinkish legs; near tailless.
moubab1: Medium babbler, warm rufous-brown above with a grayish crown, whitish throat and breast streaked dusky, and a faint dark moustachial stripe. Fairly long blackish bill; pale gray-brown legs; longish rounded tail.
moubar1: Plump green barbet, mostly bright green with a black-and-blue head pattern, blue throat, and red and yellow facial spots; finely streaked greenish underparts. Heavy pale bill with bristles; greenish legs; stocky build.
moubar2: Stocky green barbet with a blue face and throat, a black band and red patches above and below the eye, and an orange-red forecrown. Heavy pale ivory bill; greenish-gray legs; plump short-tailed body.
moublu: Slender thrush-like bird; male entirely sky-blue, brightest above and paler below fading to whitish on the lower belly; female gray-brown with bluish wings, rump and tail. Thin black bill; black legs.
moubul2: Crested bulbul, olive-brown above with a pointed brown crest, rufous-tinged face and ear-coverts, gray throat and breast, and yellowish belly. Slim dark bill; pinkish legs; medium slim body, longish tail.
moubuz2: Compact buzzard, dark brown above and on the breast, whitish below with bold dark-brown barring and blotching across belly and flanks; barred tail with a dark subterminal band. Yellow cere and legs; hooked black bill.
moucac1: Striking blackbird; glossy black with a bright golden-yellow lower back, rump and wing patch. Pale ivory-blue conical pointed bill; pale blue eye; blackish legs; medium slim body with a longish tail.
mouchi: Small chickadee, gray above and pale gray below, with a black cap broken by a distinctive white supercilium (eyebrow), white cheeks, and a black bib. Short black bill; dark legs; round-bodied, short-billed.
moudov: Slim long-tailed dove, soft grayish-buff and pinkish-brown, with black spots on the wing coverts, a small black ear spot, and a long pointed white-edged tail. Slender black bill; pinkish-red legs.
mouela1: Plain olive flycatcher, grayish-olive above and pale yellow below with a whitish throat, faint bushy crest, whitish eye-ring, and two pale yellowish wing bars. Small dark bill with pale base; dark legs; slim build.
mouflo1: Slender flowerpiercer; male blackish with a contrasting tawny-rufous moustachial stripe and chestnut undertail; black hooked upturned bill. Dark legs; red eye; small slim body adapted for nectar feeding.
mouful1: Small plain babbler, warm olive-brown above with a gray crown bordered by faint blackish lateral stripes and a pale eye-ring, buffy-white below. Short dark bill; pinkish legs; rounded, short-tailed, nondescript.
mougra1: Slim glossy black blackbird with a chestnut-rufous patch on the underwing coverts (often hidden). Pointed black conical bill; black legs; long graduated tail; slender elongated body, all-dark at rest.
mouhae1: Large powerful eagle, dark brown above, whitish below heavily streaked and barred rufous-brown, with a short crest and a boldly barred tail. Feathered legs; yellow feet; hooked black bill; broad rounded wings.
mouill1: Skulking brown babbler, warm rufous-brown above with rufous wings and tail, a grayer crown and face, whitish throat, and grayish-buff breast and flanks. Slim dark bill; pinkish legs; rounded shape, longish tail.
moupar2: Small slender parakeet, mostly green with a yellow face, throat and underparts, and dull bluish-green wings and crown. Pale horn bill; pinkish-gray legs; long pointed tail; compact long-tailed form.
moupel1: Striking black-and-white bird; black above with a large white shoulder patch, white throat and breast, and a fiery red rump and undertail. Broad flat blackish bill; dark legs; upright short-tailed posture.
mouplo: Pale sandy plover lacking breast band; sandy-brown above, white below, with a white forehead, thin dark loral line and dark forecrown bar in breeding plumage, and a faint dark subterminal tail band. Black bill; dark legs.
moupuf1: Plump puffbird, rufous-brown above, buffy-rufous below faintly streaked, with a bold white moustachial stripe and a whitish forehead and lores. Heavy hooked black bill; dark legs; large-headed, short-tailed, fluffy.
mouqua: Plump round quail, slaty blue-gray on the breast and brown above, with a chestnut throat patch bordered white, barred chestnut-and-white flanks, and two long straight black head plumes. Dark bill; dark legs.
mourob1: Plain thrush, uniform sooty olive-brown above and slightly paler grayish-brown below, with a pale throat finely streaked dusky. Dull yellowish to orange bill; yellowish legs; orbital ring yellowish; medium robin shape.
moutai2: Small warbler-like bird with bright rufous-orange crown, olive-green back and wings, grey face and breast, and yellow belly and vent. White throat, fine dark bill, long graduated tail often cocked, and pinkish legs.
moutin1: Tiny olive-green tinkerbird with yellow rump, black-and-yellow streaked face, and a distinctive whitish moustachial stripe below the eye. Underparts dull yellowish-olive; short stubby black bill and dark legs.
moutre1: Large grey treeswift with long swept-back wings and deeply forked tail; pale grey body, white throat, and dark blue-grey upperparts. Male shows white moustachial and supercilium-like facial stripes; tiny bill, very short legs.
moutro1: Trogon with metallic green head, back and breast, sharply divided from a bright red belly by a white breast band. Wing-coverts finely vermiculated grey, undertail boldly barred black-and-white, eye-ring narrow, and bill yellow.
moutur1: Large chunky rufous-brown tapaculo (turca) with cinnamon-brown body, white barring on breast and belly, grey-brown crown, dark eyestripe, bold white supercilium and broad white moustachial throat-stripe. Black bill and legs, long cocked tail.
mouvel1: Hummingbird with glittering green upperparts, a velvety blackish breast and belly, and an iridescent green throat patch. Tail dark blue-black; long, decurved black bill and small body.
mouwag1: Slim grey-and-white wagtail with pale grey upperparts, clean white underparts, a black breast band, white supercilium and dark eyeline, and white wing edgings. Long black tail with white outer feathers; black bill and dark legs.
mouwar: Olive-green above and bright yellow below, with a slate-grey hood covering head, nape and breast, and blackish mottling forming a crepe-like patch on the upper breast. No eye-ring or wingbars; pinkish legs and dark bill.
mouwar2: Small olive-green leaf warbler with yellow underparts, a long pale yellow supercilium, dark eyestripe, and a pale yellowish median crown-stripe bordered by darker lateral crown-stripes. Thin pointed bill and pale legs.
mouwhe1: Sooty-black chat with contrasting white shoulder patch (lesser wing-coverts) and white rump and outer tail base. Overall blackish-brown body, slender black bill and black legs; female browner.
mouwre1: Small warm rufous-brown wren with finely barred wings and tail, a distinct pale buff supercilium, and buffy-rufous underparts. Faint barring on flanks, short cocked tail, slender dark bill and pinkish legs.
mouwre2: Rich rufous-brown wren with grey-streaked cheeks, a black-and-white streaked malar (moustache) area, white throat and orange-buff underparts. Finely barred wings and tail, slender bill, often cocked tail.
mowowl1: Medium owl with rich rufous-brown plumage densely spotted and barred with white ocelli on head, back and underparts. Rounded head without ear tufts, dark eyes, pale facial disc, and feathered legs.
moywar1: Bright yellow warbler-like bird with olive-green upperparts and uniformly rich yellow underparts and face. Faint yellow supercilium, plain wings, slender dark bill and dark legs.
mugfly: Small flycatcher with blackish upperparts, a bold white wing patch and white tail-base sides, an orange-rufous throat and breast fading to white belly, and a short white supercilium behind the eye. Small dark bill, dark legs.
mulpar1: Slim long-tailed parrot, bright green overall with a yellow forehead band, turquoise-blue shoulders, a red belly patch and yellow-orange thigh marking. Pale bill, greyish legs; female duller and greener.
munwow1: Small dark wood-wren with olive-brown upperparts, grey breast and faintly barred whitish belly, a long white supercilium and heavily black-and-white streaked face and cheeks. Short tail, slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
musduc1: Bulky dark sooty-grey duck with finely vermiculated plumage; male has a large fleshy black lobe hanging beneath the bill. Stiff tail often held flat on water, broad body sitting very low, dark legs set far back.
muslor1: Mostly green lorikeet with a red forehead and forecrown, blue crown, yellow-green collar on the nape and red lores. Underparts green, bill orange-red tipped, legs grey.
muswre2: Stout rufous-brown wren with warm chestnut upperparts, a rich orange-rufous breast and throat, and finely black-barred lower belly and flanks. Bluish-grey bare orbital skin, stout dark bill, short cocked tail.
nabant1: Slate-grey antwren with two bold white wingbars, a large black bib on throat and breast, and silvery-white flanks. Outer tail feathers narrowly tipped white; relatively long tail, fine narrow bill, dark legs.
nabtod1: Tiny chunky tody with bright green upperparts and crown, a vivid red throat patch, white breast and pale yellowish-pink flanks. Long flat bill red below and dark above, pale grey legs, large head.
nabwoo1: Slender woodcreeper with olive-brown back, a heavily black-streaked rufous-buff crown and nape, whitish-streaked breast, and rufous wings, rump and tail. Long, slim, slightly decurved pale bill; greyish legs.
nacnig1: Large pale nighthawk with sandy grey-brown mottled upperparts, a white throat, and a strikingly clean white belly contrasting with barred chest. White wing patches conspicuous in flight; broad wings, short bill, brown legs.
nafbar1: Sooty grey-brown barbet with a bare blackish face and pale yellow-rimmed bare skin around the eye, a tuft of bristles at the bill base, and pale streaking on the breast. Stout pale horn bill, dark legs; chunky build.
nampri1: Small slender warbler (prinia-like) with grey-brown upperparts finely streaked, whitish underparts streaked on breast, a long graduated tail, and a faint pale supercilium. Thin dark bill, pale legs.
namsan1: Sandy-brown sandgrouse with cryptic mottled upperparts, a long pointed tail. Male has a yellowish throat and face and a white-and-chestnut breast band; pale belly. Short legs, small dark bill, plump body.
narfly1: Small flycatcher, bright green-olive above and yellow below, with a yellow throat, narrow yellow eye-ring, and yellow-green edged wing-coverts. Lacks white wing patch; short dark bill, dark legs.
narfly2: Striking flycatcher with black upperparts, a bright orange-yellow throat and breast fading to white belly, a bold yellow-orange eyebrow patch, and a large white wing patch and white rump. Small dark bill, dark legs.
nartap2: Small dark grey tapaculo, slate-grey overall with browner flanks and faint barring on the rear flanks and undertail. Rounded body, short cocked tail, slender dark bill and pinkish-brown legs.
nartro1: Trogon with bright iridescent green head, breast and upperparts, and a vivid crimson-red belly. Bright blue-green skin around the eye, undertail barred and tipped white; bill grey, body slender.
naswar: Small warbler with a grey head, bold white eye-ring, olive-green back and wings, and bright yellow throat and underparts. Often shows a concealed rufous crown patch; no wingbars, thin pointed bill, dark legs.
natfra2: Large brown spurfowl with finely vermiculated grey-brown plumage heavily marked with white V-shaped streaks below, a bare red face and throat skin, and a red bill. Bright red legs with spurs; stout game-bird build.
natsla1: Fairly small antshrike; male grey overall with a black cap and whitish belly centre, two white wingbars and a graduated white-tipped tail. Female has cinnamon-rufous cap and back, grey-buff underparts; stout hooked bill.
navwre1: Chunky brown wren with a rich brown crown and upperparts, a tawny-brown face, a whitish throat and upper breast grading to pale grey then grey-brown vent, with faint scalloping on the chest. Very long blackish bill, sooty flanks.
ndcsun2: Small sunbird; male has a glittering metallic green head, throat and back, a broad scarlet breast band bordered above by a narrow blue-violet band, and blackish belly. Long decurved black bill, dark legs.
nebtap1: Small dark grey tapaculo, uniformly slate-grey above and below with brownish flanks and faint barring on the rear flanks and undertail-coverts. Rounded body, short cocked tail, slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
necspi1: Small spinetail, olive-brown crown and back with a white forehead, white supercilium and black eyeline, a white throat streaked brownish across the upper breast forming a necklace. Cinnamon rump and flanks, bicoloured rufous-and-black tail, fine bill.
necwhi2: Whistler with olive-green upperparts and yellowish-green underparts, a plain greenish head, and no strong markings. Stout dark bill, greyish legs; compact body with a fairly large head.
negeag1: Very large powerful grey-brown eagle with greyish-brown upperparts, pale grey-and-white barred underparts, a short bushy crest, and a deep heavy bill. Long broad rounded wings, long tail, powerful pale yellow legs and feet.
nehhon2: Boldly streaked black-and-white honeyeater with blackish upperparts streaked white, white-streaked underparts, a striking white iris, yellow wing and tail panels, and a small white ear-tuft. Slender slightly decurved black bill, dark legs.
nelwea1: Weaver with a black head and throat forming a hood, olive-green upperparts, a yellow nape collar and yellow underparts, and chestnut undertail-coverts. Pale eye, stout conical black bill, pinkish legs.
neocor: Slender blackish cormorant with glossy dark plumage, a long tail, and a sharply pointed yellowish-buff throat pouch edged with whitish feathers in breeding plumage. Hooked grey bill, dark legs; smaller and thinner-necked than other cormorants.
nepful1: Small drab fulvetta with grey-brown upperparts, a grey head with blackish lateral crown-stripes bordering the crown, a pale eye-ring, and buffy-white underparts. Short stubby bill, pale legs; plain rounded wings.
nezbel1: Olive-green bird with greenish-yellow underparts and a faint purplish iridescence on the head; male darker olive-green, female browner with a white-and-purple malar streak. Slightly decurved dark bill, dark legs, red eye.
nezfan1: Small fantail with grey-brown upperparts, a buff-orange belly, a black-and-white facial pattern with white throat and brow, and a long broad tail edged whitish that is constantly fanned. Tiny bill, thin dark legs.
nezkak1: Large stocky olive-brown parrot with greenish-brown upperparts, a grey-scaled crown, crimson-red underwing and rump, and orange-red flushed belly. Strong dark grey decurved bill, grey legs; loose untidy plumage.
nezrob2: Small dark robin (North Island); slate-grey to blackish above and on the breast, with a sharply demarcated whitish-yellow lower breast and belly. Upright stance on long thin dark legs, fine black bill, large dark eyes.
nezrob3: Small dark robin (South Island); dark slate-grey upperparts and breast grading to a pale yellowish-white belly with a less sharp division than its northern relative. Upright posture, long thin dark legs, fine black bill.
nibkiw1: Large flightless brown kiwi with shaggy, streaked greyish-brown hair-like plumage, a rounded body, no visible wings or tail, and a long pale ivory decurved bill. Stout grey-brown legs and feet, small eyes.
nicgra1: Medium blackbird with long keel-shaped tail; male glossy black with violet-blue iridescence and pale yellow eye. Female much smaller, warm brown above, buff below with paler throat and supercilium. Bill and legs black.
nicwre1: Small wren, warm rufous-brown above, whitish below with grayish flanks; bold white supercilium and black-and-white streaked cheeks. Faintly barred wings and tail. Slender slightly decurved blackish bill, pinkish legs.
nigwre1: Tiny, very short-tailed wren, uniform dark sooty-brown above, slightly paler grayish-brown below with faint scaling on throat. Long slender straight blackish bill, pinkish-brown legs; rounded compact body.
nilfly2: Compact flycatcher; male deep slaty indigo-blue overall, brighter on forehead and shoulder, with blackish lores and face. Female grayer-brown with rufous wash. Short black bill, dark legs; rounded head, upright posture.
nisfin1: Stocky finch with massive deep conical blackish bill. Male entirely glossy black; female plain warm brown above, buffy-cinnamon below. Dark legs; large-headed, heavy-billed silhouette.
nivsun2: Tiny slender sunbird with very long graduated central tail feathers (male). Breeding male metallic green crown and back, yellow underparts with reddish-orange breast patch. Female grayish above, pale yellow below. Thin decurved black bill.
noacha1: Slim all-sooty-black chat with conspicuous white wing patches flashing in flight. Sexes similar, dull black overall. Slender black bill, long black legs; upright stance, rather thrush-like shape.
nobfly1: Slim all-blackish flycatcher, sooty-black overall with slight gloss, long slightly notched tail. Sexes alike; resembles a drongo but bill straighter. Black bill, black legs; upright perched posture.
nobsni1: Large dark snipe, heavily patterned brown, buff and black above with cream stripes; barred flanks and dense breast streaking. Very long straight bill, greenish legs; cryptic, stocky-bodied.
nobtyr: Tiny, dull olive-gray tyrannulet with bushy peaked crest, faint pale eyebrow and two buffy-cinnamon wing bars. Pale grayish underparts. Short stubby bill with pinkish base, dark legs; nondescript and small-billed.
nobwoo1: Large robust woodcreeper, warm brown overall with fine blackish barring across head, neck, breast and back; rufous wings and tail. Straight heavy dark bill, gray legs; broad-bodied with stiff tail.
noccur1: Large rufous-chestnut curassow with black head and shaggy curly crest, bare blue-gray facial skin, and a knobbed bright yellow-orange bill. Buffy belly, barred tail. Pinkish legs; bulky, ground-dwelling.
nohcuc1: Medium hawk-like cuckoo, slaty-gray above, with rich rufous-orange unbarred underparts and gray throat. Yellow eye-ring and yellow legs. Gray tail with dark bands; pointed wings, accipiter-like profile.
noifri1: Large drab gray-brown honeyeater with bare blackish facial skin, a prominent casque-like knob at base of the stout decurved bill, and a silvery-white nape. Pale underparts; long tail, lanky build.
noimin1: Gray honeyeater with black crown and face mask, bright yellow bare skin behind eye, yellow bill and legs. White scalloping on nape, yellowish wing patch. Pale gray underparts; slim, long-tailed.
noipit1: Plump, short-tailed, long-legged pitta; green back and wings, buff underparts with black central belly and scarlet-red vent. Blackish head with buff crown stripe, pale blue wing patch. Stout dark bill, pinkish legs.
nopowl: Tiny owl, grayish-brown above with white spots, white underparts boldly streaked dark. Two black 'false-eye' nape spots, finely spotted crown, yellow eyes. Long barred tail often cocked; yellow bill and feet.
norben1: Tiny flycatcher with distinctive sharply downcurved, broad black bill. Olive-green above, pale yellow below with faint olive streaks on breast; gray throat. Yellow eye, pale eye-ring; pinkish legs; large-headed.
norbob: Small round quail; male rufous-brown mottled body with white throat and bold white supercilium bordered black through eye. Female buffy throat and eyebrow. Short gray bill, gray legs; chunky, short-tailed.
norboo1: Medium brown hawk-owl, dark brown above, whitish below boldly streaked and blotched rufous-brown. No ear tufts, rounded head, yellow eyes. Dark bill, yellowish feet; slim with long barred tail.
norbro1: Plain drab bulbul, olive-brown above, paler buffy-gray below with whitish throat and reddish-brown wash on wings and tail. Reddish eye, slender dark bill, pinkish legs; nondescript, rounded.
norcar: Male brilliant red overall with prominent pointed crest, black face mask around stout conical orange-red bill. Female warm buff-brown with reddish crest, wings and tail. Reddish bill, pinkish-brown legs.
norcro1: Tiny, very short-tailed warbler-like bird, grayish-brown above, buffy-white below with pale supercilium and dark eyeline. Stub tail gives stocky look. Short pointed bill, pinkish legs; compact and round.
noremt1: Bright grass-green toucanet with large bicolored bill (yellow upper, black lower), blue-tinged throat, and chestnut-tipped tail. Bare red-orange skin around eye. Greenish-gray legs; sleek, long-billed.
norfan1: Slim long-tailed fantail, brownish-gray above, with white throat and pale buffy underparts; dark breast band and white eyebrow. Broad fan-shaped tail with white-tipped feathers. Small dark bill, dark legs.
norfis1: Black-and-white shrike, glossy black above with white scapular stripe and white underparts; long black tail edged white. Female has rufous flank patch. Stout hooked black bill, black legs; upright perch.
norfli: Large brown woodpecker, buffy-brown back with black barring, spotted underparts and black crescent bib. Red (or black) malar stripe, gray-and-brown head; yellow or red wing/tail shafts, white rump. Long slightly curved bill.
norgre1: Medium-large pale shorebird, gray-brown above with whitish spotting, clean white underparts; slightly upturned bicolored bill (yellow base, black tip). Short yellowish legs; stocky build, white rump and lower back.
norhar2: Slim long-winged, long-tailed harrier with white rump. Male pale gray above, white below with black wingtips; female brown, streaked below. Owl-like facial disk, yellow eyes; small bill, long yellow legs.
norjac: Rich chestnut body with black head, neck and breast; greenish-yellow flight feathers flash in flight. Yellow frontal shield and bill base, with leading-edge wing spur. Extremely long greenish toes and legs.
norlog1: Plump rounded ground bird with spiny tail feathers; rufous-brown above, white throat and breast, with bold black scalloping on flanks. Sexes differ in throat tone. Short stout bill, strong pinkish legs.
normoc: Slim gray songbird, pale gray above, whitish-gray below, with two white wing bars and large white wing patches and white tail edges flashing in flight. Yellowish eye, slender slightly decurved black bill, dark legs.
norpar: Tiny warbler, blue-gray above with greenish-yellow back patch and two white wing bars. Yellow throat and breast crossed by rufous-and-black band; white belly, broken white eye-ring. Dark upper, yellowish lower bill.
norpot1: Large cryptic nightjar-like bird, mottled gray-brown, buff and black with streaked underparts and dark mustache. Huge head, large yellow-orange eyes, wide gape. Short bill, tiny legs; upright tree-stump posture.
norpuf1: Compact bush-shrike; male glossy black above with conspicuous fluffy white rump and gray-white underparts, red eye. Female warmer brown with buffy underparts. Stout hooked black bill, dark legs; puffball appearance.
norscr1: Large gray goose-like bird with black hindneck, bushy crest, and bare red facial skin and throat. White cheeks and foreneck, gray body, white belly. Short reddish bill, pinkish heavy legs; bulky build.
norshr4: Large pale gray shrike, gray above, whitish below faintly barred; black face mask, black wings with white patch, black tail edged white. Strongly hooked black bill, black legs; big-headed, long-tailed predator.
norsla1: Antshrike with stout hooked bill. Male slaty-gray overall with black wings spotted white and white-tipped tail. Female warmer brown above, grayer below. Pale eye, dark bill, gray legs; sturdy, short-tailed.
norwat: Streaky brown warbler, plain olive-brown above, whitish-yellow below heavily streaked dark; bold buffy-yellow supercilium narrowing behind eye. Streaked throat. Pointed dark bill, pinkish legs; ground-loving, tail-bobbing.
nosbir1: Plain brown bird with long graduated tail often cocked; dark brown above with fine barring, paler below with dark-scaled throat and breast. Short rounded wings. Slender dark bill, strong pinkish legs; secretive, thrush-like.
nosfly1: Drab gray-olive flycatcher, grayish above, pale yellowish-white below with dusky breast wash; faint pale eye-ring and short pale eyebrow. Short straight bill with pale base, dark legs; nondescript, peaked-headed.
nrwswa: Plain brown swallow, dull grayish-brown above, dingy white below with brownish wash across throat and upper breast. Slightly notched squared tail, broad wings. Tiny black bill, dark legs; rough-winged, no contrasting marks.
nstspa: Small sparrow with bright orange-buff face and breast contrasting gray cheek patch and gray nape; back streaked black-and-white, faint breast streaking. Gray crown stripe; short conical bill, pinkish legs; flat-headed.
nswowl: Tiny earless owl, reddish-brown above with white spotting, whitish underparts streaked rufous-brown. Round head, pale facial disk with whitish Y between yellow eyes. Dark bill, feathered feet; very small, big-eyed.
nubwoo1: Spotted woodpecker, green-brown above heavily spotted white, whitish underparts boldly spotted black. Male red crown and malar; female has black-spotted crown. Greenish-gray bill, gray legs; medium, barred tail.
nutfly: Medium Myiarchus flycatcher, gray-brown above with bushy crest, pale gray throat and breast, lemon-yellow belly; rufous edges in wings and tail. Straight blackish bill, dark legs; upright, peaked head.
nutman: Small finch; warm chestnut-brown above and on face, throat; underparts white boldly scaled with dark crescents on breast and flanks. Stout blue-gray conical bill, gray legs; rounded, short-tailed.
nutwoo: Small black-and-white woodpecker; barred black-and-white back, white underparts with black spotting on flanks; black-and-white striped face. Male has red hindcrown patch. Small chisel bill, gray legs; ladder-backed.
nwfowl1: Pale gray owl with whitish facial disk boldly bordered black, prominent black-tipped ear tufts, and bright orange-red eyes. Finely vermiculated gray upperparts, pale streaked underparts. Dark bill, feathered feet.
oahama: Small honeycreeper; male bright yellowish-green, brighter yellow below, with black lores and slightly decurved blackish bill. Female duller grayish-green, paler below. Dark legs; warbler-like, active and slim.
oaktit: Small plain crested titmouse, uniform grayish-brown above and slightly paler gray below, with no strong markings; short pointed gray crest, plain face with a beady dark eye, short stubby dark bill, and gray legs.
oaxspa1: Medium sparrow with brown black-streaked upperparts, plain grayish underparts, and a chestnut crown; strong face pattern with white supercilium, broken white eye-ring, black lores and black malar stripe, all-black bill and dull legs.
obbfin1: Olive-green above with yellowish belly, and an entirely rich rusty-ochre head, throat and breast; dark eye, conical blackish bill, and dark legs, giving a striking orange-headed, olive-bodied appearance.
obfdov1: Plump green fruit-dove with mostly green head and upperparts and gray-green breast, marked by a contrasting orange-yellow patch on the lower belly and a yellow undertail; short reddish-based bill and reddish legs.
obfgle2: Warm brown foliage-gleaner with rufescent crown and back, ochre-buff throat and breast fading to paler belly, and a plain buffy face with faint eye-ring; rufous wings and tail, slender pale-based bill, grayish legs.
obfgle3: Plain olive-brown foliage-gleaner with rufous-brown wings and tail, dull buff throat contrasting slightly with olive-brown underparts, and a plain face; fairly straight dark bill and grayish legs.
obfrob1: Small forest robin, slaty-gray above with blackish tail, bright orange face, throat and breast, and white belly; bold white spot before the eye, dark slender bill and pale pinkish legs.
obnthr1: Olive-brown above with plain grayish underparts and pale buffy belly, lacking strong markings; most distinctive is the bright orange bill, orange eye-ring and orange legs setting off the otherwise plain plumage.
ocbant1: Tiny rounded antpitta, olive-brown above with a grayish crown, and bright ochre-orange breast boldly scaled or scalloped with dark crescents; large dark eye in a buffy face, short stout bill and long pinkish legs.
ocbdov1: Medium ground-dove, warm brown above with pinkish forehead and gray crown, and pale ochraceous-buff underparts; iridescent neck patch, dark bill, and reddish legs, with white tail corners visible in flight.
ocbfly1: Small dull flycatcher, olive-green above with rufous-edged wings and tail, and plain ochre-buff underparts darkest on the breast; large dark eye, broad flat dark bill with pale base, and dull legs.
ocbpip1: Streaky pipit, buffy-brown above heavily streaked black, with warm ochre-buff breast and flanks finely streaked dark and a whitish belly; pale supercilium, fine pointed bill, and pinkish legs with a long hindclaw.
ocbtan1: Stocky olive tanager, olive-green above and on the head with a slightly grayer face, and dull ochre-yellow underparts brightest on throat and breast; stout dark bill and grayish legs.
ocbthi1: Small slim furnariid with dull brown upperparts, grayish-brown underparts, and a long spiky rufous tail; distinctive orange-ochre face and chin, slender dark bill, and dark legs.
occpic1: Tiny piculet, olive-green back and barred black-and-white underparts, with rich ochre-buff face and hind-neck collar; black cap spotted white (males with red forehead spots), black tail with white central bar, short bill.
occspi1: Small spinetail, grayish-brown above with a contrasting rufous crown and rufous wing patch, and pale grayish underparts with a buffy throat; longish dusky tail, slender dark bill, and grayish legs.
oceant1: Large antbird with scaly black-and-buff spotted ('ocellated') upperparts and breast, rufous-brown crown and wings, and bold bright blue bare skin around the eye; black throat, stout dark bill and pinkish legs.
ocecra1: Tiny secretive crake, rufous-brown above finely spotted and barred with white, with rufous breast and black-and-white barred flanks and belly; short greenish bill and dull legs.
ocepic2: Small piculet, olive-brown above finely spotted whitish, with whitish underparts heavily barred or scaled dark; black crown spotted white (red-fronted in males), short dark bill and grayish legs.
ocepoo1: Small grayish-brown nightjar mottled and vermiculated, with a buffy collar across the hindneck, large dark eye, and white throat patch; short bill with broad gape, rounded tail with white corners, short legs.
ocequa1: Plump quail with intricately spotted plumage; male has a bold black-and-white harlequin face pattern, blue-gray body covered with round white spots, and rich chestnut breast; short stout bill and stout legs.
ocetap1: Distinctive tapaculo with rufous-chestnut crown and forehead, blackish body finely spotted ('ocellated') white throughout, and a rufous lower belly and vent; short upturned tail, stout bill and strong pinkish legs.
ocethr1: Large gray-brown thrasher with whitish underparts heavily marked with bold round dark spots, plain grayish-brown upperparts and pale face; pale yellow eye, long decurved blackish bill, and stout legs.
ocetur1: Large iridescent turkey with bronze-green and copper body plumage barred with blue and gold ocelli on the tail; blue unfeathered head dotted with orange-red caruncles, no beard, and pink legs with spurs.
ocewoo1: Medium woodcreeper, warm brown with rufous wings and tail, olive-brown crown and back finely streaked and spotted buff ('ocellated'), and buff-streaked underparts; straight dark bill and grayish legs.
ocfant1: Small antpitta, olive-brown above with a distinctive ochre forehead and face, and ochre-buff breast scaled or streaked dusky fading to whitish belly; large dark eye, short pale-based bill and pinkish legs.
ocftap1: Small dark tapaculo, slaty-gray on head, breast and upperparts, with contrasting ochre-rufous flanks and lower belly and a brownish back; short cocked tail, short stout bill and pinkish legs.
ocftof1: Tiny tody-flycatcher, greenish mantle with gray crown and bright buffy-ochre sides to the head, set off by an olive spot on the ear-coverts; pale yellowish underparts, two yellow wing bars, flat dark bill.
ochatt1: Stout-billed attila, olive-brown above with rufescent rump and tail, and bright ochraceous-yellow underparts brightest on the breast; pale eye, heavy hooked dark bill, and dark legs.
ochbul2: Medium bulbul, olive-brown above with an ochraceous-buff face and underparts, slightly bushy crown, and pale whitish throat; dark bill, fairly long tail, and grayish legs.
ochpic1: Tiny piculet, plain ochraceous-brown overall, warmer below, with sparse pale spotting; black crown spotted white (red-fronted in males), short dark bill and grayish legs.
ochwre1: Small warm-toned wren, rich ochraceous-brown above and below with faintly barred wings and tail and a buffy supercilium; fine dark barring on flanks, slender dark bill and pinkish legs.
ocrant1: Small antbird; male slaty-gray with a black-and-white streaked throat and breast and a contrasting bright ochre rump and lower belly; thin dark bill and pale legs.
ocrbun1: Breeding male reed-bunting with an entirely black head, chestnut-brown back and nape, whitish sides of neck, and pale buff underparts streaked black on breast and flanks; ochre rump diagnostic, conical bill, pinkish legs.
ocsant1: Plump antpitta, olive-brown above, with ochre-buff underparts boldly streaked dark giving a striped breast; large dark eye in a plain face, short stout bill and long pinkish legs.
ofrcha1: Dark compact robin-chat with brown to gray upperparts, a pale eyebrow and pale throat, olive-tinged flanks, and rufous on the belly, rump and tail; slender dark bill and dark legs.
oilbir1: Large brown nightjar-like bird with rich reddish-brown plumage densely spotted with white diamond-shaped spots and barred dark; large dark forward-facing eyes, strong hooked pale bill, and short legs.
olbeup1: Small euphonia; male glossy blue-black above with olive-green back, yellow forecrown, and rich tawny-orange underparts; female olive-green above and yellowish-olive below; short stubby bill, dark legs.
olbori1: Medium oriole, olive-green above and on the head, with whitish underparts heavily streaked dark giving a streaked appearance; red eye, pinkish-red stout bill, and dark gray legs.
olbpip: Compact pipit, plain olive-green above with faint streaking, and whitish underparts boldly streaked black on breast and flanks; bold buff-and-white supercilium with a white spot behind ear-coverts, pink bill base and legs.
olbsun3: Tiny sunbird; male has a glossy metallic green head, throat and upper breast, olive-green back, and olive-yellow belly with a small yellow rump patch; thin decurved black bill and dark legs.
olbsun31: Tiny sunbird; male olive-green above with iridescent blue-black forehead, throat and upper breast bordered by yellow tufts, and a bright yellow belly; thin decurved black bill and dark legs.
olbtai1: Small grayish tailorbird with a rusty-red crown, cheeks and throat, faint olive-green wash on the upperparts, and a whitish belly tinged yellow; long thin pinkish bill, often-cocked tail, pinkish legs.
olbtan1: Bulky tanager, mostly coppery-olive plumage tending yellowish, with a contrasting dark gray face and paler gray throat; stout silvery-gray bill and grayish legs.
olbwoo1: Medium woodcreeper, olive-brown with rufous wings and tail, olive-toned head and back finely streaked buff, and buff-streaked olive underparts; fairly straight dark bill and grayish legs.
olbwoo2: Medium woodpecker, olive-green above with a barred greenish back and reddish wash, and dull olive underparts; males show a red crown patch, with a pale chisel-tipped bill and grayish legs.
olccre1: Small crescentchest, olive-brown above with an olive-tinged crown, a bold black breast crescent below a buffy throat, and rufous-buff lower underparts; white supercilium, short dark bill and pinkish legs.
olcfly1: Small flycatcher, olive-green above with olive-brown wings and tail, and a dusky olive breast band contrasting with a paler yellowish belly; faint pale eye-ring, broad flat dark bill, dull legs.
olcwar1: Small warbler, blue-gray above with a contrasting olive-yellow cap, yellow throat and breast, and whitish belly; white eye-crescents, two white wing bars, thin dark bill and dark legs.
olcyel1: Yellowthroat; male olive-green above with a bright yellow underparts extending from throat to belly, and a broad black face mask across forehead and ear-coverts; thin dark bill and pinkish legs.
olehem1: Small, dingy tanager with olive-green upperparts and head, and dull yellowish underparts. Shows a faint yellowish supercilium and a relatively slender, pointed dark bill. Dark eyes, grayish legs; overall plain and warbler-like in shape.
olgcam1: Small, plump warbler with bright olive-green upperparts and wings, contrasting with a grayish head and pale grayish-white throat and breast. Short cocked tail, rounded body, fine dark bill, and pinkish legs.
olgtan1: Medium tanager, uniformly bright olive-green above and slightly yellower-green below, with a faint dusky mask. Stout conical blackish bill, dark eyes, and gray legs; plain, robust-bodied appearance.
olgtyr1: Tiny, slender tyrannulet, bright olive-green above and yellowish below, with a pale yellow supercilium and dusky cheek patch. Two yellowish wing bars, thin dark bill, and dark legs; long-tailed, delicate shape.
olibul1: Small drab bulbul, uniform dull olive-brown above and paler buffy-olive below with a slightly yellowish belly. Has reddish-brown eyes, a short slender dark bill, and dark legs; nondescript and rounded.
olibus1: Plump bushshrike with olive-green upperparts, gray head, and dull yellowish-olive underparts. Some have a faint pale supercilium; stout hooked black bill, dark eyes, and grayish legs. Robust, large-headed shape.
oliela1: Medium olive-brown elaenia, grayish-olive above with two whitish wing bars and a pale grayish-white throat and breast grading to pale yellow belly. Lacks an obvious crest; short dark bill, dark legs, faint pale eye-ring.
olifin1: Dark finch with blackish-olive upperparts and breast, a rich chestnut crown and nape, and slaty-gray belly. Short conical dark bill, dark eyes, and dark legs; compact, ground-feeding shape.
olifla3: Plump flatbill with olive-green upperparts, broad flat bill, and a large head. Yellowish-olive underparts with pale lores and a faint pale eye-ring; broad-based bill with pale lower mandible, dark eyes, and grayish legs.
oligre1: Small, plain greenlet, uniform olive-green above and dull yellowish-olive below, with no wing bars or eye-ring. Pale eyes, a short stout pale-based bill, and pinkish-gray legs; nondescript and rounded.
olioro1: Large oropendola with olive head, breast, and back grading to chestnut on the lower back, wings, and belly. Bright yellow outer tail feathers, a pink bare cheek patch, and a stout pale yellowish-tipped black bill; blue eyes.
olipic1: Tiny piculet, olive-green to grayish-olive above and finely barred or scaled grayish below. Male shows a small red-flecked black cap (yellow-spotted in female). Short straight blackish bill, stubby tail, and gray legs.
olisis1: Small dark siskin, deep olive-green to blackish above and on the head with bright yellow underparts and a yellow wing patch and rump. Short conical dark bill, dark eyes, and dark legs; compact finch shape.
olispa: Plain sparrow with grayish-olive upperparts, a gray head marked with two dull brown lateral crown stripes and a brown eyeline, and whitish-gray underparts. Short conical bill, dark eyes, and pinkish legs.
olispi1: Slender spinetail, warm olive-brown above with a faint rufous tinge on wings and a plain face, and pale buffy-olive underparts. Long graduated brownish tail, fine dark bill, and grayish legs; nondescript furnariid.
olitan1: Robust olive tanager, uniformly dull olive-green above and slightly paler yellowish-olive below, often with a faint yellowish throat. Stout dark conical bill, dark eyes, and grayish legs; plain, heavy-bodied appearance.
olithr2: Medium thrush with uniform olive-brown upperparts, a grayish head, and orange-buff lower belly contrasting with a streaked grayish throat. Bright orange-yellow bill, orange eye-ring, and orange legs.
oliwar: Slender warbler-like bird, gray above with a tawny-orange head and breast (male; yellowish in female) and a blackish ear patch. White wing bars, white belly, fine sharp dark bill, and dark legs; long-bodied shape.
oliwhi1: Plump whistler, dull olive-gray above with an olive-green wash, a grayish head, and a whitish throat bordered by a faint gray breast band over olive-buff underparts. Short stout dark bill and gray legs; large-headed.
oliwoo1: Small, slim woodcreeper with a grayish-olive head and underparts contrasting with a rufous-brown rump, wings, and tail. Short straight slender bill, dark eyes, and grayish legs; plain, climbing-tree shape.
oliwoo2: Medium woodpecker, olive-green above with a gray head and grayish-olive underparts. Male shows a red crown and nape; female has a gray crown. Greenish-yellow rump, chisel-shaped dark bill, and gray legs.
olsfly: Large robust pewee, dark olive-gray above and on the sides of the breast, leaving a whitish stripe down the center of the underparts (vest pattern). Peaked head, broad dark bill with pale lower base, dark legs; white tufts on rump sides.
olshum1: Small green hummingbird, bright golden-green above and below with a paler grayish-white belly center and olive-flecked flanks. Straight blackish bill, dark eyes, and an olive-tinged forked tail; compact shape.
oltcuc1: Slim long-tailed cuckoo, dark olive-brown above and on the head with paler buffy underparts, often faintly barred. Very long graduated tail with pale tips, slightly curved dark bill, and dull legs.
oltpar1: Small green parakeet, bright green overall with a dull olive-brown throat and breast wash, a pale bare eye-ring, and a long pointed tail. Pale horn-colored bill, dark eyes, and grayish legs.
oltthr1: Plump scaly thrush, rich olive-brown above heavily marked with black crescentic scaling, and whitish underparts boldly scaled with black crescents. Dark bill, large dark eyes, and pinkish legs.
olwbul1: Drab olive-brown bulbul, grayish-brown above with olive-yellow wing edges and a dull whitish-gray throat and breast grading paler below. Reddish eyes, a short slender dark bill, and dark legs; nondescript and rounded.
omao: Solitaire-like thrush, uniform dark grayish-brown above and slightly paler gray below, with grayish lores and a plain face. Short straight blackish bill, dark eyes, and dark legs; upright, plump posture.
oncbec1: Stocky becard, glossy slaty-blue-black above and on the head, paler gray below (male); female rich rufous-brown above and cinnamon below. Large head, stout hooked dark bill, dark eyes, and gray legs.
opcman1: Tiny bright grass-green manakin with an opalescent whitish-to-bluish crown and nape, yellow belly and undertail-coverts. Whitish bill, yellow iris, yellow thighs, and pale pinkish legs; plump, short-tailed shape.
oprtan1: Stunning tanager, velvety black face and throat with shimmering opaline silvery-blue crown, nape, and underparts, and a contrasting bright orange-rufous rump. Black wings and tail, short stout black bill, and gray legs.
orabis1: Small weaver, breeding male flame orange-red with a black face, crown-front, and lower belly, and brown wings and tail. Heavy conical blackish bill, dark eyes, and pinkish legs; short-tailed, plump finch shape.
oraori1: Medium oriole, brilliant orange head, body, and rump with a black face mask, throat, back, wings, and tail. White wing edgings, a pointed grayish-black bill, dark eyes, and gray legs.
orbant1: Tiny antwren, gray above and on the head with a contrasting bright orange-rufous lower back and rump patch, and pale grayish underparts. Two faint pale wing bars, fine dark bill, and pale legs; small and slender.
orbbun1: Small bunting, male turquoise-blue crown and upperparts, bright green back, and a vivid orange-yellow throat and underparts. Whitish belly, short conical bill, dark eyes, and grayish legs; compact finch shape.
orbeup1: Small euphonia, male glossy steel-blue-black above and on the throat with a yellow forecrown and rich orange-yellow underparts. Short stubby dark bill, dark eyes, and gray legs; round-bodied.
orbfal1: Powerful compact falcon, slaty-black above and on the head with a white throat and upper breast, a black-barred breast band, and rich rufous-orange belly, thighs, and undertail. Yellow cere and legs, dark hooked bill.
orbflo1: Tiny flowerpecker, male slaty blue-gray above with a bright orange-yellow rump and underparts, and a grayish throat. Stubby slightly decurved dark bill, dark eyes, and dark legs; very small and plump.
orbfly1: Small dark flycatcher, dark olive-brown above with a concealed orange-yellow crown patch and two broad buffy-ochraceous wing bars. Yellow-olive breast grading to yellow belly; orange lower mandible with dark tip, pale iris, pale eye-ring.
orbfru1: Plump fruiteater, male bright green above with a black face and forecrown and a vivid orange throat and breast fading to yellowish-green belly. Short stout reddish-orange bill, dark eyes, and pinkish legs.
orblea1: Leafbird, male bright leaf-green with a black face, throat, and breast, a violet-blue moustache, blue-washed wings and tail, and an orange-tinged belly. Slightly decurved dark bill, dark eyes, and gray legs.
orbman1: Tiny velvety-black manakin, male with a snow-white forecrown, deep azure-blue lower rump and uppertail-coverts, and a bright orange-yellow belly and undertail. Small dark bill, dark eyes, and dark legs; plump, short-tailed.
orbowl1: Distinctive owl with a heart-shaped, ear-tufted chestnut-and-buff facial disc, rich chestnut-brown upperparts spotted with black and white, and buffy-pink underparts spotted dark. Dark eyes, pale bill, and pinkish legs.
orbpig1: Plump green pigeon, male green above and on the head with a lilac-gray nape, a broad orange-rufous breast band bordered by lilac, and pale green belly. Maroon shoulders, bluish-based pale bill, and reddish legs.
orbspa1: Striking sparrow, olive-green above with a black head marked by a white central crown stripe and white supercilium, and a white throat and underparts. Bright orange bill, dark eyes, and pinkish legs.
orbsun2: Slender sunbird, male iridescent metallic green head and throat with a bright orange breast band and olive-gray belly, and long blue-green central tail feathers. Long decurved black bill, dark eyes, and black legs.
orbtho1: Rufous-toned furnariid, warm rufous-brown above with a brighter rufous crown and wings, a whitish throat, and pale buff underparts with a faint orange-rufous breast wash. Long rufous tail, slender pale bill, and gray legs.
orbthr1: Plump thrush, olive-brown above with a striking white-and-black face pattern and bold black-and-white scaling on the breast and flanks over whitish underparts. Orange bill, dark eyes, and pinkish legs.
orbtro2: Trogon, male with an olive-yellow head and upper breast, a bright orange-yellow belly, dark green back, and finely black-and-white vermiculated wings. Black face, blue bare eye-ring, pale blue bill, and black undertail with white tips.
orbtro3: Bright troupial, vivid orange head, underparts, and back contrasting with black throat, wings, and tail, and a bold white wing patch. Bare blue eye patch, pointed black-and-blue-gray bill, and dark legs.
orbwoo1: Large woodpecker with crimson-red crown and crest, golden-orange back and wing coverts, blackish flight feathers, and barred buff-and-black underparts. Pale ivory bill, dark eye, grey legs; white-striped black face.
orcfly1: Small olive flycatcher with olive-brown upperparts, two buff wing bars, and pale buff-yellow underparts faintly streaked olive on the breast. Concealed orange crown patch; dark bill with pale base, pale eye-ring, dark legs.
orcman1: Small stocky manakin; male has a black cap and back, bright golden-orange throat and breast forming a beard, olive-green belly and wings, and bright orange legs. Short tail, stubby bill. Female plain olive-green.
orcman3: Compact olive-green manakin with dark olive upperparts and paler yellowish underparts; male shows a flame-orange central crown stripe and pale throat. Short black bill, dark legs, short squared tail.
orcori: Small slim oriole; adult male deep chestnut below and on rump with a solid black hood, back, wings and tail, plus a single white wing bar. Slender pointed blackish bill, blue-grey legs.
orcori1: Striking oriole with bright orange-yellow body and rump, a black face mask, throat, back, wings and tail; orange crown contrasts the black face. White wing edgings; pointed grey-black bill, grey legs.
orcpar: Small green parakeet with bright green plumage, a brownish-olive wash on crown and shoulder, a tiny orange chin spot, and dull blue in the flight feathers. Pale horn bill, long pointed tail, pinkish-grey legs.
orcpar2: Stocky green parrot with a blackish head, bright orange-yellow cheeks and ear patch, scaly buff-edged nape, and orange-red bend of wing and underwing. Pale horn bill, dark grey legs, short tail.
orcwar: Plain small warbler, dull olive-yellow overall, slightly greyer on the head with faint breast streaking and a concealed orange crown patch. Thin pointed dark bill, broken pale eye-ring, dark legs; yellow undertail coverts.
orcwax: Tiny finch, soft grey-brown above and buff below, with a bright orange face and cheeks, a red rump and bill, and a blackish tail. Pale eye-ring, brownish legs; short conical red bill.
orefly1: Small olive flycatcher with olive upperparts, a greyish crown, buff lores and ear coverts, pale eye-ring, and greyish-buff throat grading to a yellowish belly. Distinctive pale orange iris; broad flat dark bill, dark legs.
oretho1: Brownish ovenbird with bright rufous forehead and forecrown, dark olive-brown back and crown, rufous tail and wing-bases, and a dark rufous throat over olive-brown underparts. Orange iris, black-and-grey bill, greyish legs.
orfbar1: Stocky barbet with a black head and back heavily scaled with white, an orange forehead patch, white throat and breast, and yellowish belly. Heavy pale bill, dark eye, grey legs; bold scaled pattern.
orfpar: Medium green parakeet, paler yellow-green below, with an orange forehead and forecrown, a blue tinge on the crown and flight feathers, and bare yellow eye-ring. Pale horn upper bill, dark lower; long tapering tail.
orfplu2: Small slender furnariid, olive-green above and dull yellow below, with a bright orange forecrown and forehead. Yellowish wash on face; thin pointed dark bill, orange-yellow legs and feet, long tail.
orfscr1: Large dark grey-brown megapode with a small bare reddish head, a short erect crest, sooty-brown body and broad rounded wings, and a short tail. Heavy dull yellow bill; very large bright orange legs and feet.
orgthr1: Plump thrush with rich rufous-orange head, throat and underparts, slaty-grey back, wings and tail, and a white belly. Bold white-spotted wing coverts and dark eye with pale eye-ring; yellowish bill, pinkish legs.
orheag1: Powerful raptor with black crown and pointed crest, rich rufous-orange sides of neck, white throat with black malar stripe, blackish upperparts, and white underparts boldly barred black on belly and legs. Heavy black-tipped yellow-based bill, yellow legs.
orhtan1: Small tanager with a warm orange-buff head and breast, greyish-olive back, and pale whitish belly. Plain face with dark eye; conical grey bill, grey legs; overall soft, unmarked appearance.
orhthr1: Striking thrush with entirely bright orange head, nape, throat and underparts, contrasting with slate-blue back, wings and tail. White belly center and white wing patch; yellowish bill, pinkish legs.
oribla1: Large blackbird with a brilliant golden-yellow head, neck and upper breast, a black eye patch and lores, and glossy black body, wings and tail. Long pointed black bill, black legs; bold yellow-and-black contrast.
oricuc2: Slim grey cuckoo with plain grey head, throat and breast, grey upperparts, and white underparts finely barred black; rufous-buff vent. Long barred grey tail tipped white, yellow eye-ring and feet, blackish bill.
origoo1: Striking goose-like duck with a pale buff-white head and neck, blackish-brown body, chestnut breast and flanks, and a dark green-glossed back. Bold white wing patch in flight; short pinkish-red bill, long red-pink legs.
origre: Stocky finch, olive-brown overall with a greyish nape, greenish-yellow rump and underparts, and bright yellow wing patches and tail-base. Heavy pale pinkish conical bill, dark wings, pinkish legs.
orihob2: Medium raptor with a small slender head, variable plumage from pale to dark brown, barred underparts, and a dark gorget; broad wings and a long tail with broad dark bands. Slim grey bill, yellow legs, dark eye.
orimag1: Large pied corvid with glossy black head, breast, back and tail, a white belly and white shoulder patch (scapulars), and iridescent blue-green sheen on the long wedge-shaped tail and wings. Stout black bill and legs.
oripip1: Slim ground pipit, streaked brown above and buff below with dark streaking on the breast, a pale supercilium, and buff outer tail feathers. Slender pinkish-based bill, long pale pinkish legs and long hind claw.
oriplo1: Slim long-legged plover; breeding male has a buff-brown face and crown, white forehead, and a broad orange-chestnut breast band bordered below with black, over a white belly. Brown back, dark bill, long yellowish legs.
oripra: Slender tern-like wader, soft greyish-brown above and on the breast, with a creamy throat ringed by a thin black border, and a white belly with rusty undertail. Short red-based black bill, forked tail, dark legs.
orisal1: Stocky saltator, grey above with a blackish crown and face, a bold white supercilium and white throat, and a greyish breast grading to buff belly. Heavy black bill, dark legs; clean-faced sturdy build.
orisky1: Small streaky lark, buff-brown above heavily streaked dark, with a short crest, pale buff underparts streaked on the breast, and a pale supercilium. Rufous-edged wings, short stout pinkish bill, pale legs.
orisof1: Inconspicuous brown ovenbird, rufescent-brown above with buff-streaked face and faint pale supercilium, rufous-chestnut wings, rump and tail, and an orange chin spot on a brown breast. Slender dark bill, greyish legs.
oriwar1: Small warbler-like bird, plain olive-grey above with a bright yellow face, throat and breast, and a paler grey belly. Dark eye, thin pointed dark bill, dark legs; unmarked clean plumage.
oriwar2: Boldly patterned warbler with a glossy black head and throat, bright golden-yellow body, breast and underparts, and olive-yellow back and wings. Long graduated tail, slender black bill, dark legs.
oriwhi1: Oriole-like whistler; male bright olive-yellow below with an olive-green back, blackish head and wings, and yellow underparts. Stout blackish hooked bill, dark eye, grey legs; robust thrush-like build.
ornant1: Tiny antwren, brownish above with rufous-tinged wings, two buff wing bars, and a black-and-white speckled (stippled) throat over greyish underparts. Buff flanks; thin pointed dark bill, pale legs.
ornfly1: Striking small flycatcher with a slate-grey head bearing a concealed yellow crown patch, white eye-ring, olive-green back, bright orange-rufous rump and breast, and yellow belly. Small dark bill, dark legs.
orntin1: Plump ground bird, buff and brown above heavily barred and streaked black, with a finely streaked head and neck and buff-spotted underparts. Curved slim greyish bill, slender yellowish legs, very short tail.
orphor1: Medium pied hornbill, glossy black with white belly, white wingtips and white-tipped tail, and a large pale yellowish-white bill topped by a low casque. Bare blue-white facial skin around the eye; dark legs.
orrfra2: Compact francolin, buff-brown above mottled and barred, with a rufous-streaked breast, a buff throat outlined by a dark gorget necklace, and a pale supercilium. Reddish-based dark bill, yellowish to reddish legs.
orrwar1: Large plain warbler, warm brown above and buff-white below with buff flanks, a long pale supercilium, and a flat-headed look. Long stout dark bill with pale lower base; greyish legs, rounded tail.
orsowl: Small eared owl, finely vermiculated grey-brown (or rufous morph) with blackish streaks, prominent ear tufts, and a streaky cryptic face. Yellow eyes, small dark bill, feathered legs with greyish feet.
ortlon1: Robust pipit-like bird, dark-streaked brown above with a bright orange throat bordered by a black breast band, and buff-yellow underparts. Pale supercilium, stout dark bill, long pale legs and long hind claw.
orttan1: Large tanager with a glossy black head, throat and upperparts, a brilliant orange throat patch and breast, and olive-yellow lower underparts. Blue-grey wing edgings; heavy black bill, grey legs.
orwpar: Large green amazon parrot with a yellow-and-blue forehead and crown, scaly green body, an orange wing-speculum patch on the flight feathers, and a red shoulder. Pale horn bill, grey legs, broad green tail with yellow-green tip.
oustyr1: Tiny slender tyrannulet, olive-green above and yellowish below, with a grey-and-yellow face pattern, a pale eye-ring, and dusky ear-covert crescent. Two yellowish wing bars; thin dark bill, dark legs, long tail.
ovenbi1: Plump warbler, plain olive-brown above and white below boldly streaked black on the breast and flanks, with a rusty-orange central crown stripe bordered by black, and a bold white eye-ring. Pinkish bill and legs.
pabant1: Large plump round antpitta with a black forecrown and face, blackish-brown crown becoming olive-brown back, dark chestnut wing coverts, and light grey underparts with a black chin and faint black-tipped breast feathers. Pale ivory bill, blue-grey legs.
pabcra: Small dark crake, slate-grey below and on the face with an olive-brown back, faintly barred flanks, and rufous-chestnut rear flanks and vent. Short bright red-and-yellow (paint-like) bill, coral-red legs, red eye.
pabduc1: Medium brown dabbling duck with a buff face crossed by a dark crown stripe and a dark eye-stripe, scaly dark-brown body, and a glossy green wing speculum bordered white. Dark grey bill, dull yellow-grey legs.
pabflo1: Tiny, plump warbler-like bird with dull olive-grey upperparts and whitish underparts faintly washed greyish-olive. Stubby pale pinkish bill, short tail, dark legs. Lacks bold markings; very small size and short bill are key.
pabfly2: Medium flycatcher; male is uniform dull slaty-blue overall, slightly brighter blue on forehead and shoulder, greyer below. Black bill, dark legs. Female duller grey-brown with rufous-tinged wings and tail. Upright stance, fairly large for a Cyornis.
pabill1: Skulking, plump-bodied babbler with warm rufous-brown upperparts and rusty wings and tail. Pale greyish throat and breast, buffy-white belly. Brownish-grey face, dark eye, dark bill, pinkish legs. Lacks streaking or wing bars.
pabmon1: Slender monarch flycatcher; male bright azure-blue head, breast and upperparts with a small black throat patch and a thin black half-collar across the breast, whitish belly. Pale blue bill ringed black, dark legs. Female duller greyish-blue.
pabmou1: Medium dull flycatcher-like bird, mostly grey-olive head and upperparts with slightly darker crown; two faint pale grey wing bars. Grey throat and breast becoming dirty yellowish belly, rusty-tinged flanks. Dark bill with pinkish mandible base, roundish head.
pabqua1: Small plump quail; male has slaty-grey crown and nape, bold white supercilium and throat bordered by black, rich chestnut-and-white streaked underparts, brown vermiculated back. Bright red bill and red legs. Female plainer brownish.
pabsee1: Small finch with a notably massive, deep, pale yellowish bill. Breeding male grey above with blackish wings, white wing patch, buffy-cinnamon underparts and a grey hood. Female buffy-brown overall. Heavy bill is the key feature.
pabspi1: Slender spinetail with grey-brown upperparts, rufous crown and rufous wing panel. Whitish throat with a small dark central spot, pale greyish underparts. Long, often cocked, narrow brownish tail. Thin dark bill, grey legs.
pabthr1: Plain thrush with uniform olive-brown upperparts, greyish head, and pale buffy-grey underparts shading to whitish belly. Throat finely streaked dark on white. Yellow bill, yellowish-pink legs, narrow yellow eye-ring. Faint pale wing flash in flight.
pabtin1: Plump, nearly tailless tinamou; greyish-brown upperparts finely vermiculated black with barred wings and flanks. Brown crown, prominent white supercilium, white throat, greyish to buffy underparts. Bright coral-red legs and reddish bill base.
pabtre1: Large robust furnariid with warm rufous-brown upperparts, rufous wings and tail, and a bold whitish supercilium. Underparts buffy with fine dark scaling or streaking on the breast. Long slightly decurved pale bill, sturdy legs.
pabwar1: Large dull bush warbler with olive-brown upperparts and brown crown, greenish-yellow to buff underparts. Pale buff supercilium with a dark eyestripe. Long, bright orange bill and orange legs are diagnostic.
