pabwoo1: Very large woodpecker; glossy black back, wings and underparts with a flaming red crest and a white stripe down each side of the neck meeting on the back. Pale ivory bill, grey legs. Male shows red throat; female black throat.
pacant: Tiny antwren; male grey overall with a black throat and upper breast and two white wing bars on black wings. Female greyish above, buffy-white below. Thin dark bill, greyish legs. Small, active, short-tailed.
pacbaz1: Medium raptor with grey head and short black crest, grey throat and breast, and broad rufous-and-white barring across the belly. Slaty-grey upperparts, banded tail. Yellow eye, dark bill, grey legs. Pigeon-like head shape.
pacblf1: Medium flycatcher with greyish-olive upperparts and head, a pale grey face with a faint whitish eye-ring, and a pale chin and throat grading to buffy-olive underparts. Black bill, pinkish legs. Plain, lacking bold blue tones.
pacela1: Small dull flycatcher; olive-green upperparts with two pale wing bars and yellow-edged flight feathers, often a concealed yellow crown patch. Whitish throat, faintly streaked yellowish-olive breast, pale yellow belly. Short dark bill, dark legs.
packin1: Stocky kingfisher with greenish-blue to turquoise crown, back and wings, a buff to whitish collar and underparts, and a dark eyestripe. Large heavy black bill with pale lower base, dark legs. Plumage varies from greenish to bluer.
pacloo: Slender loon; breeding adult has pale silvery-grey crown and nape, black throat patch with vertical white neck stripes, and a black back chequered with white squares. White underparts. Thin straight dark bill held level, dark legs.
pacpar1: Medium green parakeet, overall bright grass-green, paler yellowish-green below, with scattered red flecks possible on neck. Long pointed tail, pale horn bill, bare whitish eye-ring, grey legs. Larger and stockier than similar Aratinga parakeets.
pacpar2: Tiny stub-tailed parrotlet; male grass-green with bright cobalt-blue on the rump, wing coverts and behind the eye, and a greyish-blue wash on the nape. Female all green lacking blue. Pale pinkish bill, pinkish legs.
pacreh1: Medium heron occurring in two morphs: a uniform slaty-grey morph (often with a thin white throat line) and an all-white morph. Stout yellowish bill, greenish-yellow legs, shaggy nape plumes when breeding. Short-legged, hunched build.
pacrob1: Tiny robin; male has black head, throat and upperparts with a bold red-orange breast and belly, a small white forehead spot and white wing patch. Female brownish-grey with paler buff-orange wash below. Thin dark bill, dark legs.
pacswa4: Small swallow with blue-black glossy upperparts, a rufous-chestnut forehead and throat, and dusky-grey underparts shading paler on the belly. Slightly forked dark tail without long streamers. Black bill, dark legs.
pacswa5: Small swallow with glossy blue-black upperparts, a rusty-chestnut forehead and throat, and pale greyish underparts. Shallowly forked dark tail lacking long outer streamers. Black bill, dark legs. Very similar to Pacific Swallow.
pacwoo1: Medium pale woodpecker with a buffy-cinnamon to pale tawny head and bushy crest, finely barred buff-and-black back, wings and tail. Pale yellowish-buff underparts. Male shows a red malar streak. Pale ivory bill, grey legs.
pacwre1: Tiny dark wren; rich dark brown overall, finely barred blackish on wings, flanks and the short cocked tail. Indistinct paler buff supercilium, dusky-brown throat and breast. Thin dark bill, pinkish-brown legs. Rounded, stubby body.
paebla2: Medium all-black blackbird with a glossy bluish sheen, the body uniform black. Striking pale yellowish-white eye, slender pointed black bill, black legs. The pale eye against black plumage is the key field mark.
paefly1: Medium Myiarchus flycatcher; grey-brown upperparts, grey throat and breast, and a bright yellow belly. Rufous edging on the wings and tail, two pale wing bars. Bushy-crested grey-brown head, blackish bill, dark legs.
paethr1: Slim dark thrush; male glossy blackish-slate overall with a contrasting pale whitish or pale grey eye. Female duller dark brown with the same pale eye. Dark bill, dark legs. The pale eye is diagnostic.
pafdov1: Small plump fruit-dove; mostly green with a grey head and breast, a purplish-pink crown patch, and a yellow-and-orange-red lower belly and undertail. Greenish-yellow wing edgings. Short greenish-yellow bill, reddish legs.
pafear1: Medium furnariid, dark earthy-brown above with a bold pale buff supercilium and a pale throat. Dull brown underparts with faint, indistinct breast markings. Short, slightly decurved blackish bill, dark legs. Darker than related earthcreepers.
pafswa1: Small dark swallow with blue-black glossy upperparts and a blackish head, blackish throat and breast contrasting with a whitish lower belly. Notably pale yellowish to pinkish-buff feet and legs. Short forked tail, black bill.
pagplo: Medium plover; breeding adult has gold-spangled blackish upperparts, a black face, throat and belly bordered by a broad white stripe from forehead down the neck sides. Slim build, longish dark legs, short black bill.
pagwar1: Streaky brown warbler; rufous-brown upperparts boldly streaked blackish, a warm rufous rump, and a pale buff supercilium. Whitish underparts with buff-washed, faintly streaked breast and flanks. Dark-tipped tail with pale corners. Pinkish bill base, pale legs.
pahros1: Medium parrot with a pale creamy-yellow to whitish head, blue cheeks, and pale blue underparts. Black back feathers broadly scalloped yellow, blue wings, yellow-green rump, blue-and-green tail. Pale horn bill, grey legs.
pahwoo1: Medium woodpecker with a pale greyish-buff head and a red crown patch (male), olive-brown to greenish barred upperparts, and dull olive underparts. Pale yellowish bill, greenish legs. The pale plain head contrasts with darker body.
paibun: Small finch; male unmistakable with a violet-blue head, lime-green back, and bright red throat, breast and rump. Red eye-ring. Female and immature plain bright yellow-green overall. Stout silvery-grey bill, greyish legs.
paibut: Small plump buttonquail; cryptic mottled grey-brown above with bold white spots edged black on the wings and flanks, and a rufous wash on the breast. Whitish belly, pale eye, short stubby pale bill, pale fleshy legs.
paifra1: Plump partridge; finely barred and spotted black-and-white below over a buff ground, chestnut-brown crown and upperparts, white throat outlined by a black-spotted necklace. White facial spots, red bill, reddish legs.
paihon1: Striking honeyeater with black upperparts boldly marked with bright yellow wing edgings, white underparts heavily spotted black on the flanks, and a black head. Pink to pinkish bill, dark legs. Slender, contrasty black-yellow-white pattern.
paipar2: Small green parakeet; mostly green with a brown crown and cheeks, a maroon-red forehead, scaly buff-and-dusky breast, blue in the wings, and a dull red belly patch and rump. Long pointed reddish-brown-and-green tail, pale bill.
paipig1: Large heavy pigeon; pale grey head and underparts, greenish-bronze gloss on the back and wings, and a darker tail. Pale grey forehead, often a yellowish wash below, dark bill with a fleshy cere, reddish legs. Bulky build.
paired: Slim black-and-white warbler; glossy black head, breast and upperparts with a large white wing patch and white outer tail feathers, a bright crimson-red lower breast and belly, and a white crescent below the eye. Thin black bill, dark legs.
paisto1: Large white stork with black flight feathers, a pink tertial wash forming a pink lower-back band, and a black breast band. Bare yellowish-orange face and a long heavy yellow-orange downcurved bill. Pinkish-red legs. Long-legged, long-necked.
palbat1: Small flycatcher-like bird; male grey above with a black mask and white underparts crossed by a single broad black breast band. White wing markings, dark bill, dark legs. Female has a rufous-buff breast band instead of black.
palbun: Small bunting; breeding male has a black head and throat, a broad white moustache and collar, pale sandy-grey nape, and streaked grey-brown upperparts with whitish underparts. Pale bicoloured bill, pinkish legs. Female streaky brown-headed.
palcoc1: Very large all-black cockatoo with a tall backswept crest, smoky slate-black plumage, and a huge blackish hooked bill. Bare red facial cheek patches that flush brighter when excited. Dark grey legs. Massive, imposing build.
palcro1: Medium all-black crow with a slight purplish gloss, the plumage uniform dull black. Black bill and black legs, dark eye. Smaller and duller than other crows; nasal bristles cover the bill base. No pale markings.
palcuc1: Slim grey cuckoo; uniform pale brownish-grey head, breast and upperparts with a paler grey belly, a yellow eye-ring, and a fine dark line through the eye. Long grey tail tipped and notched white. Dark bill, yellowish legs.
paldov1: Medium ground-dove with plain grey-brown upperparts and a faintly purplish wash on the hindneck; whitish forehead and pale buff-white underparts. Dark eye with bare red-pink orbital skin, dark bill, and reddish-pink legs.
palhor3: Rufous-and-brown ovenbird with bright rufous-chestnut back, wings and tail, a greyish-brown crown, and a bold whitish supercilium over a dark eyeline. Whitish throat and pale buff underparts; slender dark bill and pale flesh-coloured legs.
palila: Plump Hawaiian honeycreeper with a bright golden-yellow head and breast, grey back, whitish belly, and yellow-edged dark wings. Heavy finch-like dark grey bill, dark lores, and dark grey legs.
palmch1: Stocky brown songbird with olive-brown upperparts and heavily dark-streaked dirty-white underparts. Plain brownish head, reddish-brown eye, stout pale yellowish bill, and dark legs.
palsan1: Sandy-buff sandgrouse with finely barred grey-brown upperparts, an orange-buff face and throat, pale grey breast crossed by a narrow dark band, and a black belly patch. Pointed elongated central tail feathers, feathered legs, and small grey bill.
palspi1: Small spinetail with rufous crown and wings, plain grey-brown back, and pale greyish-buff underparts. Faint pale supercilium, slender pointed dark bill, and pinkish-grey legs; tail rufous and graduated.
paltan1: Drab grey-green tanager appearing greyish overall with a paler greenish-grey body, dusky wash on wings, and contrasting blackish flight feathers showing a yellow-green panel. Stout dark grey bill and dark legs.
palthr1: Plain brown thrush with uniform grey-brown upperparts, paler greyish flanks, and a whitish belly and undertail. Whitish throat finely streaked, dark eye with thin pale eye-ring, yellow lower bill, and yellowish-pink legs.
paltyr2: Tiny olive tyrannulet with grey-olive upperparts, yellow wing-feather edgings, and pale yellow-washed greyish underparts. Greyish head with a short pale supercilium, pale eye, short dark bill, and dark legs.
paltyr3: Tiny olive-green tyrannulet with greenish upperparts edged yellow on the wing feathers, greyish head, and pale yellowish underparts. Short pale supercilium, pale eye, stubby dark bill, and dark legs.
palwar: Streaky warbler with brown-olive upperparts, a rufous-chestnut crown, yellow supercilium, and yellow underparts streaked rusty on the breast and flanks. Bright yellow undertail coverts; thin dark bill and dark legs, often pumping its tail.
palwar1: Olive warbler with greyish-olive upperparts, a dark crown bordered by a bold orange-buff supercilium, and yellow throat and breast fading to whitish belly. Thin dark bill and notably pale pinkish legs.
panfly1: Medium Myiarchus flycatcher with grey-brown upperparts, a slightly bushy greyish crown, pale grey throat and breast, and pale lemon-yellow belly. Brown wings with faint pale bars, mostly dark tail, blackish bill, and dark legs.
papfly1: Small monarch flycatcher; male glossy blue-grey above with a darker face, white throat and white underparts. Slender black bill, dark eye, and dark legs; rather long tail.
papgon1: Striking bushshrike with glossy black upperparts, head, wings and tail, and brilliant scarlet-red underparts. A golden-yellow crown patch and golden shoulder patch; stout black bill and dark legs.
paplor1: Spectacular long-tailed lorikeet, mostly bright red with green wings and a green-based tail tipped yellow, with greatly elongated yellow central tail feathers. Black patch on rear crown streaked blue at the front, narrow black hindneck band, blue rump, yellow flank patch, dark belly patch; orange-red bill and yellow-orange eye.
pappit1: Small, plump, short-tailed pitta with green upperparts, a black head with a brown crown stripe, and a small bright red belly patch on otherwise buff-brown underparts. Bluish shoulder patch, stout blackish bill, and long pinkish legs.
papscr1: Small dull scrubwren with olive-brown upperparts, a greyish face with a paler eyebrow, and dingy buff-brown underparts. Pale eye, fine dark bill, and pinkish legs.
paptre1: Brown treecreeper with rufous-brown upperparts, a buffy supercilium and pale-spotted face, and buff-brown underparts with faint darker scalloping. Fine slightly decurved dark bill, stiff tail, and dark legs.
paqthr1: Ground-dwelling quail-thrush; male with brown crown, back and tail, a black face mask bordered by a white facial stripe, black throat and chest, white belly, and rich orange-chestnut flanks. Dark bill and pale legs.
paradi2: Large crow-like bird of paradise, entirely sooty blackish-brown with a slight purplish gloss and contrasting rufous-brown primaries visible in the wing. Stout black bill, red eye, and black legs.
parant1: Small antwren; male slaty-grey overall with a bold black throat and breast streaked or bordered with white down the sides. Slender pointed dark bill and dark legs; female browner with whitish-streaked underparts.
parant2: Small antbird; male uniform dark slaty-grey, slightly paler below, with concealed white interscapular patch and faint pale wing markings. Slender dark bill and dark grey legs; female greyer-brown.
parfog1: Robust foliage-gleaner with warm rufous-brown upperparts, brighter rufous wings and tail, and a contrasting buff-orange throat. Olive-brown underparts, faint pale eye-ring, stout dark bill, and greyish legs.
parhem1: Compact tanager with dark slate-grey upperparts, head and cap, and bright yellow-orange face, throat and underparts. Dark wings and tail; short stout dark bill and dark legs.
parjac1: Glossy dark jacamar with iridescent blue-black upperparts, head and breast, and a contrasting white throat. Long slender black needle-like bill, chestnut belly, and short dark legs; long graduated tail.
parpip1: Slim streaky pipit with brown, dark-streaked upperparts, a buff supercilium, and buff-yellow underparts streaked dark across the breast. Thin dark bill, pale lower mandible, and pinkish legs with a long hind claw.
parrif1: Iridescent bird of paradise; male velvety black with a brilliant metallic blue-green throat shield and crown, and a glossy green central breast triangle. Long decurved black bill, dark legs; females rufous-brown above with barred buff underparts.
parsee1: Small dull seedeater, uniformly dusky grey-brown overall with a faintly rufous rump and dark wings and tail. Short conical pinkish-grey bill and dark legs.
parshe1: Large shelduck; female with a pure white head and chestnut-red body, male with a dark glossy-green head and dark grey vermiculated body. Both show a white wing patch and chestnut underwing; dark bill and legs.
parspi1: Slim spinetail with rufous crown, wings and tail contrasting with greyish-brown back and pale greyish underparts. Faint pale supercilium, fine pointed dark bill, and greyish legs.
partan1: Dazzling multicoloured tanager with bright apple-green head, black back, scarlet rump and lower back, turquoise-blue underparts, and a violet-blue throat. Black wings and tail; short black bill and dark legs.
partap1: Small dark tapaculo, uniformly dark slaty-grey overall with very faint barring on the rear flanks and undertail. Short blackish bill, dark eye, and brownish legs; short cocked tail.
partap2: Small slate-grey tapaculo, dark grey above and on the underparts with rufous-and-black barring on the flanks, vent and rump. Short dark bill and pale brownish legs; short upturned tail.
partap4: Small mouse-grey tapaculo, dark grey above and below with a few white feathers on the shoulder and faint rufous-and-black barring on flanks, vent and rump. Short dark bill and pinkish-brown legs; short cocked tail.
parwar1: Tiny olive leaf warbler with greenish upperparts, a bright yellow rump, two yellowish wing bars, and a yellow median crown stripe bordered by dark lateral crown stripes. Yellow supercilium, fine dark bill, and pale legs.
pasfin1: Plump sierra finch; male with slate-grey head and breast, olive-yellow back, yellowish underparts, and bright yellow-edged wings and rump. Short conical yellowish bill and dark legs.
pasmar1: Small pale brown martin with sandy grey-brown upperparts, a diffuse greyish-brown breast band, and whitish underparts and throat. Slightly forked tail, small dark bill, and dark legs.
pasowl4: Medium screech-owl, greyish-brown with fine dark streaking and vermiculation, prominent ear tufts, and a pale facial disc rimmed dark. Yellow eyes, small greyish bill, and feathered greyish legs.
patbar1: Hermit hummingbird with bronze-green upperparts, a buff-and-blackish striped face, rufous-buff throat and breast, and a pale-tipped graduated tail with white outer feathers. Long decurved bill, dark above and reddish below.
patfly1: Tiny tody-flycatcher with a black crown and back, bright olive-yellow wings with yellow wing bars, a yellow rump, and entirely bright yellow underparts. Flat broad black bill, pale eye, and pale legs.
patmoc1: Slim grey-brown mockingbird with greyish upperparts, a pale supercilium and dark eyeline, and whitish-buff underparts faintly streaked on the breast. Dark wings with pale edgings, long white-cornered tail, slim dark bill, and dark legs.
pattyr2: Small dumpy tyrant with grey-brown upperparts, a greyish head, two faint pale wing bars, and pale greyish underparts washed buff. Short stubby dark bill and dark legs.
pattyr3: Tiny pale tyrannulet with grey-olive upperparts, pale-edged wing feathers, whitish throat and pale yellowish belly, and a long whitish-tipped tail. Short dark bill, pale eye, and dark legs.
pavcuc1: Slim long-tailed cuckoo with rufous-brown upperparts, a buff supercilium and dark eyeline, a crested rufous crown, and pale buff underparts with a faint breast band. Long broad graduated tail; slim dark bill and pale legs.
pavpig2: Medium pigeon with a vinous-pink head and breast, grey-brown back and wings glossed bronze, and a contrasting whitish lower belly and vent. Pale grey rump, dark-tipped reddish bill, red orbital skin, and red legs.
pavque1: Glittering green quetzal; male with iridescent emerald-green head, back and breast, a brilliant crimson-red belly, and a dark blue-black tail with white-barred undertail. Yellow bill, dark eye, and short dark legs.
pavthr1: Plain dark thrush, uniform dusky brown above and on the breast with a contrasting whitish lower belly and vent. Whitish throat with dark streaks, dark eye, dark bill, and pale pinkish legs.
pawsta1: Glossy black starling with iridescent blue-green sheen and bright orange-buff primaries that flash conspicuously in flight. Pale creamy-white eye, slender black bill, and black legs.
pawtru2: Large rounded trumpeter with a velvety black head, neck and breast glossed purple, a grey-and-white patch over the back and inner wing, and black underparts. Short stout pale bill, dark eye, and long greenish-grey legs.
payfin1: Smallish drab finch in attractive mix of grey and yellow; grey-brown back and head, yellowest on throat and belly, with noticeable yellow shoulders. Long-winged build. Pale conical seedeater bill and dark legs; females duller and greyer.
payrob1: Plump small robin with grey crown and nape, plain olive-grey upperparts and bright pale lemon-yellow underparts and rump. Short straight black bill, dark eye in plain face, and dark legs; upright rounded posture.
pbgdov1: Tiny plain ground dove, sandy grey-brown above and on the head with a soft pinkish-buff breast lacking spots; small dark wing spots and rufous in the spread wing. Short tail, dark-tipped pinkish bill, pink legs.
pbipig1: Large heavy imperial-pigeon with pale grey head and neck, glossy green-bronze back and wings, and a distinctive pinkish-grey breast fading to whitish belly. Stout grey bill, red eye-ring and reddish legs.
pbmtou1: Striking mountain-toucan with black crown, slaty-blue back and breast, golden-yellow rump and flanks, white throat and red undertail. Diagnostic large yellow bill with a raised pale plate near the base; blue facial skin and red legs.
pbqdov1: Plump terrestrial dove with iridescent purplish-bronze back and wings, blue-grey crown and nape, and a buffy-white face with dark malar stripe. Cinnamon-buff underparts, dark bill, and red legs; rounded body, short tail.
pbtman1: Small dull manakin, olive-green above and on the head with a faint pale eye-ring and short erectile yellowish crown patch. Pale whitish-yellow throat and belly, washed olive on breast. Short dark bill, pale legs.
pcttyr1: Tiny crested tyrant; black-and-white streaked head with a tall recurved black crest, white throat, and yellow underparts with bold black breast streaking. Grey-brown streaked back, two whitish wing bars; tiny dark bill, pale eye, dark legs.
peaant1: Plump antshrike, plain grey overall, slightly paler below, with bold rows of large pearly-white spots and bars across blackish wings forming wing bars and spotted coverts. Heavy hooked black bill, pale eye, grey legs.
peadov1: Small slender dove, soft grey-brown above finely barred black, with a pale blue-grey face and finely barred grey breast and neck. Whitish belly, blue eye-ring, long tail with white corners; dark bill, pinkish legs.
peakit1: Tiny falcon-like kite, slate-grey above with a white forehead and face, white underparts, and a buffy-orange patch on the flanks and thighs. Black-and-white wings, short black bill, yellow cere and legs.
peatre1: Small rich rufous-brown treerunner; brown crown and back, bright rufous wings and tail, and a bold whitish supercilium. Underparts heavily scaled with white teardrop spots edged black. Thin slightly upturned bill, strong feet.
pebcon1: Small slim tanager, uniform pale blue-grey above, slightly paler whitish-grey below with whitish belly; little contrast between back and underparts. Sharply pointed dark-grey bill, dark eye, and pale pinkish legs.
pebcuc1: Slender grey-brown cuckoo, plain greyish-brown above with whitish underparts and no rufous in wings. Long tail dark above with bold white spots and tips below; slim slightly decurved bill with yellow lower mandible, no eye-ring.
pebfin1: Small slate-grey finch, uniformly grey becoming paler on the belly. Distinctive thin, long, slightly upturned bill with black upper mandible and yellow lower mandible; dark legs. Females duller olive-brown.
pebsee1: Small seedeater; male has a neat black cap, grey-brown back washed cinnamon, black wings and tail, and pale grey to whitish underparts. Pale conical bill, dark legs; female plain buffy-brown.
pecant1: Tiny antwren, grey above with a black crown, bold white supercilium and black eyestripe; black-and-white streaked back. White underparts with a black streaked breast patch; black wings with white-spotted wing bars. Thin dark bill.
pecpip: Small streaky pipit, boldly streaked black on a warm buff-brown back with two pale whitish braces (mantle stripes). Whitish underparts with heavy black breast and flank streaking; pale supercilium, pinkish legs and pinkish-based bill.
pecspa3: Handsome sparrow with a black head striped with a white central crown and white supercilium, grey nape, and olive back. White underparts with a narrow black breast band; orange bill, pinkish legs.
pedlau1: Plain warm grey-brown laughingthrush, fairly uniform with paler greyish underparts and a slight rufous tinge. Dark lores and faint pale face; rounded dark-tipped tail, slightly decurved blackish bill, and pinkish legs.
peethr1: Large pale-eyed thrasher, grey-brown above with heavily diffuse-streaked greyish-white underparts and pale-tipped wing feathers. Long tail with white corners; striking pale whitish-yellow eye, stout pale-based bill, pinkish legs.
peflov: Small stocky lovebird, bright green overall with a rosy-peach face, forehead and throat, and a brilliant blue rump contrasting with green back. Pale horn-coloured bill, white eye-ring faint, and grey legs.
pefpar1: Small green parakeet with a bright orange forehead and forecrown, dull orange around the eye, and greenish-yellow underparts. Blue in the flight feathers, black bill, bare orange-yellow eye-ring; grey legs.
peipig1: Large dark imperial-pigeon, blackish-slate overall with a glossy green-bronze sheen on the back and wings and a slightly paler grey head and breast. Stout dark bill, red eye, and dull reddish legs.
pepowl1: Tiny owl, warm sandy-brown to greyish above finely spotted whitish, with whitish underparts streaked brown. Pale eyebrows, two black false-eye nape spots, yellow eyes; small yellowish bill, feathered yellow feet.
peptyr1: Tiny pygmy-tyrant, olive-grey above with a greyer head, two pale wing bars, and pale yellowish-white underparts faintly streaked on the breast. Conspicuous whitish-staring eye, tiny flat bill, pale legs.
perant1: Small rounded antpitta, warm rufous-brown above and on the head with a buff face. Underparts buffy-white with a scaled or streaked brown breast. Almost tailless, long pale legs, short dark bill, large dark eye.
permea1: Dark blackish-brown meadowlark with a vivid scarlet-red throat and breast and red on the bend of the wing; pale supercilium reddish in front. Sharply pointed grey bill, streaked brown back, dark legs.
pernut1: Stocky rock nuthatch, blue-grey above with a long bold black eyestripe extending well down the neck, white throat and cheeks, and buffy-cinnamon flanks and undertail. Long heavy pointed black bill, no white in tail, grey legs.
perpla1: Stocky finch-like bird; male grey-brown above streaked darker, with a rufous-cinnamon forehead, breast and belly, and a crest. Heavily streaked back, pale wing bars; short conical serrated grey bill, red eye, dark legs.
perrec1: Largish rufous-brown furnariid; reddish-brown overall with rufous-streaked throat and upper breast, faint narrow rufous supercilium, and dark reddish-brown crown. Heavy wedge-shaped slightly upturned pale bill; greenish-grey legs.
persco1: Small grey-brown screech-owl, finely vermiculated and streaked grey-brown above and below with a paler grey face bordered dark, short ear tufts, and yellow to orange eyes. Pale bill, feathered legs; cryptic bark-like plumage.
pertap1: Small dark tapaculo, slaty blackish-grey overall, slightly paler grey below, with brownish flanks and rufous-brown barring on the rear flanks and undertail. Short cocked tail, slender black bill, brownish legs.
perthi1: Small slim furnariid, dark brown overall with rufous-tinged wings and a long pointed graduated tail. Rufous-orange throat patch, faint streaking on breast; slender dark bill, dark legs.
pertyr1: Small tyrannulet, olive-green above with a greyer crown, faint pale supercilium and dark eyeline, and yellowish underparts. Yellow edgings on the wing feathers (no obvious wing bars); small dark bill, dark legs.
perwaa1: Small antbird; male grey above with black-streaked back and white-spotted black wing coverts, white throat and grey breast, and rufous-buff flanks and belly. Slim dark bill, pinkish legs; female buffier below.
perwre1: Medium rich rufous-brown wren, deep reddish-brown overall with fine dark barring on wings and tail and a faintly mottled grey face. Underparts warm cinnamon-brown; slender slightly decurved dark bill, dark legs.
pesowl1: Tiny owl, warm grey-brown above heavily spotted white, with whitish underparts boldly streaked brown. White eyebrows, two black false-eye spots on the nape, bright yellow eyes; yellowish bill and feet.
petkne1: Large long-legged thick-knee, sandy grey-brown streaked above with a bold white supercilium bordered by black lines and a grey wing panel. Whitish underparts, large staring yellow eye, stout black-and-yellow bill, long yellowish legs.
pettwi1: Small dark finch; slate-grey head and back, deep crimson-red face, throat and breast, and a black belly densely spotted with round white pearls on the flanks. Dark conical bill, dark legs; vivid red rump.
pettyr1: Very small flycatcher, plain olive-green above with whitish wing bars, and white underparts marked with thin grey streaks. Pale eye-ring, tiny flat dark-tipped bill, pale legs; rounded compact shape.
pfbwar1: Small skulking warbler, plain warm olive-brown above and buffy-brown below with whitish throat and belly. Faint pale supercilium, plain face; thin pointed dark bill and conspicuous pale pinkish-flesh legs and feet.
phaino: Slim glossy black songbird (male) with a tall wispy crest and a distinctive bold white wing patch flashing in flight. Long tail; staring red eye, short slender black bill, dark legs. Female slate-grey with paler wing patch.
phbfin1: Brushfinch with a pale rufous-buff crown and nape, olive-green back, and a black face mask. Throat and underparts yellow, greyish on the flanks; dark conical bill, dark legs; rounded long-tailed shape.
phbwar1: Small plain bush warbler, warm rufous-brown above with a faint buffy supercilium and dark eyeline, and buffy-white underparts washed brown on the flanks. Thin pointed bill, pale pinkish legs; long rounded tail.
phcdov1: Long-tailed cuckoo-dove, rich cinnamon-brown overall, darker above with faint barring and a pinkish-buff face and underparts. Very long graduated tail; slender dark bill, red eye and reddish legs.
phecou2: Large long-tailed coucal; breeding adult mostly blackish with a glossy bluish sheen on head and underparts, and rufous-and-black barred wings and back. Very long broad blackish tail; heavy black bill, red eye, dark legs.
phecuc1: Slim long-tailed cuckoo, brown above mottled buff with a bold buffy-white supercilium and dark crown, and creamy underparts with a streaked breast band. Very long broad graduated tail, slender bill, pale legs.
phepig1: Large dark ground pigeon with a strongly laterally compressed, fan-like tail held high; glossy black head, neck and underparts and rich chestnut-brown back and wings. Bare red eye-skin, stout dark bill, red legs.
phhcuc1: Hawk-like cuckoo, grey-brown above with a grey head, and whitish underparts boldly streaked and barred rufous-brown across the breast. Long tail with broad dark bars; yellow eye-ring, slightly hooked bill, yellow legs.
phibul1: Medium drab bulbul, olive-brown to grey-brown above and paler greyish below, with a slightly shaggy dark crown and faint pale throat. Short blackish bill, dark eye, dark legs; slim build with a moderately long tail.
phicou1: Large heavy-bodied coucal with glossy blue-black head, body and long broad tail, contrasting with bright chestnut-rufous wings. Stout blackish decurved bill, deep red eye, and strong blackish legs.
phidrc1: Slim glossy blue-black cuckoo resembling a drongo, with a fairly long slightly forked tail and white barring on the undertail, thighs and underwing. Slender curved black bill, dark legs; sleek upright shape.
phifab1: Striking fairy-bluebird: male glossy ultramarine and violet-blue on crown, back, rump and underparts with black face, wings and tail; female duller blue-grey. Short black bill, red eye, dark legs.
phifro1: Large cryptic frogmouth, mottled rufous-brown, grey and buff with fine vermiculations and pale scapular spots resembling bark. Huge flat triangular hooked bill, enormous dark eyes, broad flat head; squat shape.
phihao1: Small rufous-brown hawk-owl, warm brown above and white below boldly streaked and barred with rufous-brown, plain whitish belly. Rounded head lacking ear tufts, large yellow eyes, yellowish bill, feathered legs.
phihap1: Tiny stubby parrot, mostly bright green with a red crown and rump, golden-orange wash on nape and mantle, and a bluish throat patch (varies by race). Short pale bill, short tail, pinkish legs.
phimar1: Bold pied robin-chat: glossy blue-black head, back, throat and upper breast contrasting with clean white belly and white wing patch; black tail edged white. Slender black bill, dark legs; often cocked tail.
phinig1: Cryptic nightjar mottled grey-brown, buff and rufous with dark streaking, a pale throat patch and small white wing and tail corners. Tiny dark bill with wide gape, large dark eyes, very short legs; long wings and tail.
phiori1: Bright golden-yellow oriole with black lores or face mask, olive-yellow back and yellow underparts; wings and tail blackish edged yellow. Stout pinkish-red bill, dark red eye, grey legs.
phipif1: Small black-and-white fantail, sooty black above and on head with a black breast band, white throat and white belly; broad fanned tail tipped white with white outer feathers. Fine black bill, dark legs.
phitro1: Vivid trogon: male with rosy-red to orange-red breast and belly, dark grey-brown head and upper breast, finely vermiculated wing coverts, and long square red-tipped white-barred tail. Stout bright orange bill, bare red eye-ring.
phivir: Small drab vireo, olive-green above and pale yellow-washed below (brightest on throat and breast), with a white supercilium and dark eyeline, and grey crown. Short stout grey bill, blue-grey legs; no wing bars.
phlwar1: Small olive-green leaf warbler, greenish above and yellowish-white below, with a long yellowish supercilium, dark eyestripe, and faint or no wing bar. Fine dark bill with pale base, pinkish legs.
phseag1: Medium raptor, dark brown above and below densely spotted white, with a short black-and-white crest and bare yellow facial skin and cere. Pale-and-dark barred tail and underwings, yellow eyes, yellow feet.
phtjac1: Striking jacana with white face and foreneck, golden-yellow nape patch bordered black down the neck, blackish-brown body and very long sickle-shaped black tail. Slender grey bill, long greenish legs and immense toes.
piapia1: Slim glossy black crow-relative with a long graduated tail and a thick pale pinkish-horn to bluish bill with a black tip. All-black plumage, dark eye, black legs; slender pointed shape.
pibgre: Stocky brown grebe with a short thick pale bill encircled by a black band, black throat patch, and dusky-brown body paler below. Dark eye, blunt head; lobed greenish feet set far back.
piblar3: Slim sandy-grey lark, greyish-brown above with fine streaking, white below with a pale pinkish-buff wash and faint breast streaks, and rufous in the wings and tail edges. Pale conical bill, pale legs.
pibros2: Rosy-pink rosefinch: male with deep pink underparts, rump and brow, brown streaked back, and a bright pink supercilium contrasting with darker cheeks; female brown and streaky. Stubby conical bill, dark legs.
picpig2: Large grey pigeon with a vinous-grey head and breast, scaled blackish edges on neck and wing coverts, broad dark band across the wing, and pale grey rump. Dark-tipped bill, red eye, red legs.
piebar1: Boldly patterned barbet, black above with white spotting and a broad white eyebrow, white below; some races with red on forehead. Black throat, heavy pale bill, dark legs; chunky big-headed shape.
piebus1: Small stonechat: male glossy all-black with a bold white wing patch and white rump and vent; female dusky brown. Fine black bill, black legs; compact upright posture.
piebut1: Crisp black-and-white butcherbird with black head, throat and upper back, white collar, underparts and rump, and black-and-white wings. Stout blue-grey black-tipped hooked bill, dark legs.
piecro1: Large glossy black crow with a clean broad white breast and collar, the rest of the plumage black. Heavy black bill, dark eye, black legs; bulky build.
piecuc1: Slim crested cuckoo, black above and white below, with a pointed black crest, white wing patch and white tips to the long graduated tail. Slender black bill, dark legs.
piecur1: Large sooty-black butcherbird-relative with white patches at the wing base, white undertail coverts and a white-tipped tail. Stout black bill, bright yellow eye, black legs.
pieduc1: Distinctive grey duck with a large square pinkish-grey bill flanked by a pink ear-spot, finely barred grey-brown body, and a pale belly. Dark eye, greyish legs; oversized bill gives a unique profile.
piefan1: Small fantail, dark grey-brown above and on the head with a blackish breast band, white throat and supercilium, white belly, and a long fanned tail tipped white. Fine black bill, dark legs.
piehar1: Elegant harrier: male strikingly black, white and grey with a black head, breast and back, white underparts and pale grey wings with black wingtips. Slim yellow-ceres hooked bill, long yellow legs.
piehon1: Pied honeyeater: male glossy black above and white below with white wing and tail patches and a small blue-grey wattle below the eye. Slender slightly decurved black bill, dark legs.
pieoys1: Large bold oystercatcher, glossy black head, breast and upperparts with a clean white belly. Long straight bright orange-red bill, red eye-ring, and stout pinkish legs.
piepuf1: Small chunky puffbird, black above with white spotting and a white collar and forehead, white below with a dark breast band. Heavy black bill, big head, short tail; dark legs.
piesti1: Tall slim wader with a white head and underparts, black hindneck and black wings and back, contrasting cleanly. Long needle-thin black bill, and extremely long pink-red legs.
pietri1: Small black-and-white triller: male glossy black above with a white eyebrow, white wing patch and white underparts, grey rump. Short black bill, dark legs; slender shrike-like shape.
piewhe1: Wheatear: male with black face, throat and wings, creamy-white crown, nape and underparts, and a white rump with a black-and-white inverted-T tail. Fine black bill, black legs.
pifshe: Large pale shearwater, grey-brown above and white below with a dusky-mottled neck and flanks. Distinctive pinkish bill with a dark tip, and pale pinkish feet; broad-winged heavy build.
pigdov1: Tiny pale ground dove, grey-brown above and pale pinkish-grey below with iridescent blue-black wing spots and white-edged outer tail feathers. Short dark bill, reddish-pink legs.
piggui: Sooty black-and-white seabird (breeding): all blackish-brown with a large bold white wing patch crossed by a dark wedge. Slender black bill, bright red mouth-lining and red legs.
pihwar1: Vivid warbler with a deep pink-red body and underparts contrasting with a silvery-pink to whitish hood on the crown and face. Fine dark bill, dark legs; small and brightly coloured.
piipig1: Large clean white pigeon with contrasting black flight feathers and a black tip to the tail. Pale bluish-grey to yellowish bill, dark eye, bluish-grey legs; bulky build.
piipig2: Large dark imperial-pigeon with a grey head and breast, a broad whitish or pinkish-grey breast band, dark glossy green-black wings and back, and a pale grey terminal tail band. Dark bill, red eye, red legs.
pilfin1: Small finch: male grey with a bright crimson-red erectile crown bordered black, whitish throat and belly; female warmer brown. Short conical pinkish bill, pale legs.
pilfly1: Small olive-grey flycatcher with a bushy pointed crest, two pale wing bars, a pale eye-ring, whitish throat and pale yellowish belly. Small bill dark above with pale lower mandible, dark legs.
pilgra1: Small slender grey furnariid, mostly pale grey with a blacker cap and a pale supercilium, paler grey underparts, and a long tail. Fine dark bill and conspicuous bright pink legs and feet.
pilotb1: Small rufous-brown ground bird, warm brown above and rich rufous on the face and breast which is scalloped with dark crescents, paler belly. Fine dark bill, pale legs; rounded plump shape with longish tail.
pilwoo: Large crested woodpecker, mostly black with a flaming red pointed crest, white neck stripe and white throat; male has a red moustache. Long chisel-shaped grey bill, white wing linings, dark legs.
pinbun: Bunting: male with a white crown and cheek bordered chestnut, chestnut throat and breast band, white underparts streaked rufous, and a streaked brown back. Conical greyish bill, pinkish legs.
pincoc1: Large pale pink cockatoo, soft salmon-pink on head and underparts, whitish back and wings, with a broad forward-curling crest banded red, yellow and white. Pale bill, dark eye, grey legs.
pinfly1: Small greenish-grey Empidonax flycatcher, olive above and pale yellowish below, with a bold whitish eye-ring and two pale wing bars. Bill dark above with an orange-yellow lower mandible, dark legs.
pinjay: Stocky, short-tailed dusky-blue jay, uniformly powder-blue overall, deepest blue on the head and brightest on the crown, fading to grayer body and a whitish-streaked throat. Long, straight, sharply pointed black bill and black legs; lacks any crest.
pinpig2: Plump pale dove with pinkish-gray head, neck and underparts, rich rusty-brown back and wings, and a broad dark maroon-brown tail. Bare dark-red facial skin and reddish bill with paler tip; deep pink-red legs.
pinpig3: Small plump green pigeon; male has a gray head fading to a rosy-pink neck and upper breast, orange-yellow lower breast, green back and wings, and rufous undertail coverts. Female plain green. Pale bluish bill, reddish legs.
pinrob1: Small dumpy robin; male has sooty-black head, throat and upperparts with a bright rose-pink lower breast and belly, and a small white forehead spot. Female warm gray-brown with paler pinkish wash below. Thin black bill, dark legs.
pinsis: Small, heavily brown-streaked finch with streaked whitish underparts and back, and distinctive yellow flashes in the wings and at the base of the notched, forked tail. Sharply pointed conical bill; thin streaking throughout. Dark legs.
pinspi1: Small dark furnariid with a rich rufous-chestnut crown, nape, wings and tail contrasting with brownish-gray back and dusky-gray face and underparts; whitish throat with faint streaking. Long graduated spiny tail. Slender dark bill, dark legs.
pinwar: Plump warbler with unstreaked olive-yellow upperparts, bright yellow throat and breast blurrily streaked on the sides, and a white belly. Two white wing bars, white tail spots, and broken pale eye-ring. Fairly long, sturdy dark bill.
pipcis2: Small warm-toned cisticola with a plain rufous-brown crown and nape, grayish-brown unstreaked back, and buffy-white underparts. Short, often cocked rufous-edged tail with dark subterminal spots and pale tips. Fine pointed bill, pinkish legs.
pipcro1: Medium pied crow, glossy black on the wings, tail, back, face and throat, contrasting sharply with a clean white nape, neck-sides, breast and belly. Stout black bill, black legs, and dark eyes.
piphor1: Large black-and-white hornbill; mostly glossy black with a white belly, white-tipped flight feathers and broad white tips to the tail. Black casque and bill; bare facial and throat skin; dark legs. Sexes similar in pattern.
pipipi1: Small plain brownish songbird with grayish-brown upperparts, a grayer head, and pale buffy-gray underparts faintly streaked on the breast. Pale narrow eye-ring; short fine dark bill. Drab and nondescript overall, dark legs.
pipplo: Small, very pale sandy-gray-brown plover with a clean white forehead and underparts, a single narrow dark breast band (often incomplete), and a thin black bar over the forecrown in breeding birds. Stubby orange bill with black tip; orange legs.
pirfly1: Medium flycatcher with olive-brown upperparts, a dusky cheek patch, bold whitish supercilium, and a blackish malar stripe bordering a white throat. Underparts whitish with diffuse dusky streaking on the breast. Short broad blackish bill, dark legs.
pitbec1: Small compact becard; male glossy black on the crown, back and wings with a rose-pink throat patch and gray underparts. Female grayer with rufous tones. Short, broad, thick black bill and large head; short tail, dark legs.
pitman1: Tiny manakin; male glossy black above with a flaming red crown and rump, white throat and underparts, and a long, thin, spike-like central tail point. Female olive-green with the same elongated pin tail. Short pale bill, orange legs.
pitsni: Cryptic, dumpy snipe with intricately barred and streaked brown, buff and black plumage, a striped head, and very long straight bill. Distinctive narrow, stiff, pin-like outer tail feathers. Pale buff legs; rounded short tail.
pitwhy: Tiny finch-like bird; breeding male glossy black above with white underparts, a bright red bill, and four extremely long black central tail plumes far exceeding the body. Female streaky brown sparrow-like with a reddish bill. Pale legs.
piwtyr1: Small pied tyrant, clean white on the head, throat and underparts with a contrasting black back, wings and tail; a black line runs through the crown and nape onto the mantle. Slender black bill and black legs.
plaant1: Small plain antbird; male slaty-gray overall, slightly paler below, with a darker crown and a concealed white patch between the shoulders; faint pale eye-ring. Female browner with rufous-tinged crown. Short hooked dark bill, gray legs.
placha: Large slim drab chachalaca, grayish-brown above and paler buff-gray below, with a small bare reddish throat patch and a long broad olive-brown tail tipped buffy-white. Small dark head, short dark bill, gray legs.
placuc1: Slender cuckoo; adult has a plain gray head, throat and upper breast, gray-brown upperparts, and warm rufous-orange lower breast and belly. Long graduated tail with white spots; yellow eye-ring and yellow legs. Slim dark bill.
placuc3: Slim small cuckoo, uniformly slaty-gray over the head, upperparts and breast, slightly paler gray on the belly. Long graduated dark tail with white spotting beneath; yellow eye-ring and yellow legs. Thin slightly decurved dark bill.
plaflo1: Tiny stub-tailed flowerpecker, plain olive-gray-green above and pale grayish-buff below, lacking strong markings; faint paler throat. Short, slightly decurved dark bill and dark legs. Drab and nondescript overall, very small.
plaflo2: Tiny plain flowerpecker, dull olive-green above and pale grayish-white below with an olive wash on the flanks; no bold markings. Short fine slightly curved dark bill, dark legs. Extremely small and stub-tailed.
plagre2: Medium dull greenbul, uniformly olive-green above and paler yellowish-olive below with a slightly grayer throat; lacks wing bars or bold marks. Dark eye, fairly stout dark bill, grayish legs. Plain and unmarked overall.
plaher1: Medium hermit hummingbird; bronzy-green above, warm cinnamon-buff below, with a buff supercilium and dark cheek stripe. Long decurved bill with reddish lower mandible, and a long graduated tail with elongated white-tipped central feathers.
plamar1: Small plain brown martin, dull grayish-brown above and on a diffuse breast band, with a pale dusky-white throat and whitish belly. Short slightly forked tail; small dark bill, dark legs. Lacks any gloss or bold pattern.
plapar1: Small all-green parakeet, bright grass-green overall, slightly paler and yellower below, with bluish tinges in the flight feathers and a long pointed green tail. Pale horn-colored bill, pinkish legs. Plain unmarked plumage.
plapri1: Small slim warbler-like bird, plain warm grayish-brown above with a faint whitish supercilium, and pale buffy-white underparts. Very long, graduated, narrow tail often cocked, tipped with pale and dark spots. Fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
plasla1: Medium antshrike; male barred black-and-white overall with a black crown and concealed white back patch. Female rufous-brown above with a chestnut crown and buffy underparts. Stout hooked blackish bill, gray legs.
plasof1: Plain olive-brown furnariid with a rufous crown bordered by a buffy supercilium, rufous wings and a graduated rufous tail; whitish throat and indistinct frontlet. Yellowish iris and a thick gray bill; dark legs.
plaswi1: Slim all-dark swift, uniformly sooty blackish-brown overall with slightly paler grayish throat, long scythe-shaped wings, and a moderately forked tail. Tiny black bill, dark legs. Plain and unmarked apart from faint pale throat.
platap1: Small dark tapaculo, slaty-gray overall with brownish flanks and rump faintly barred dusky, and a paler gray throat and breast. Short cocked tail; short stout dark bill and pinkish-brown legs. Skulking, plain dark plumage.
platyr1: Tiny plain tyrannulet, grayish-olive above and pale whitish-yellow below, with a faint pale supercilium and indistinct eye-ring; two faint pale wing bars. Short dark bill and dark legs. Nondescript and unmarked overall.
platyr2: Small tyrannulet, olive-gray above with a grayer crown, a short whitish supercilium, and two pale wing bars edged whitish. Underparts pale yellowish-white. Short dark bill and dark legs; compact body.
plawoo1: Large stout woodcreeper, warm olive-brown overall with a rufous-chestnut rump, wings and tail, and fine dark barring on the head, throat, breast and belly. Long, fairly straight, dark bill; grayish legs and strong feet.
plawre1: Plain warm-brown wren, rufous-brown above and clean whitish below with buffy flanks, a bold white supercilium, and dark-and-white streaked cheeks. Long slightly decurved dark bill; pinkish-gray legs. Fairly large unbarred wren.
plawre3: Plain rich-brown wren, warm rufous-brown above and whitish below washed buff on the flanks, with a clean white supercilium and finely streaked cheeks. Long slim decurved dark bill; pale legs. Lacks barring on the back.
plaxen4: Tiny xenops, warm brown above with a rufous-tinged wing patch and tail, and a bold white crescent stripe on the cheek; underparts grayish-brown with faint pale streaking. Short, wedge-shaped, slightly upturned bill; gray legs.
plbant1: Plump round antpitta, uniform olive-brown above and on the head with no scaling, and dull buffy-olive underparts faintly mottled, often with a paler throat. Very short tail, long pale legs, and a short stout dark bill.
plbpip1: Slim plain pipit, grayish-brown above with an almost unstreaked back, a buffy supercilium, and pale buff underparts only faintly streaked on the breast. Long pale pinkish legs; thin dark bill. Plainer-backed than most pipits.
plbspa1: Sparrow with a bright chestnut crown and nape, gray cheeks, a black eye-stripe, and a yellow-washed face and breast; plain unstreaked grayish-brown back and pale underparts. Stout pale bill; pinkish legs. Male brighter than female.
plbthr2: Bluish slate-gray thrush, gray above and on the head with a dark-streaked white throat, a white throat crescent, pale gray breast fading to white belly, and orange-buff flanks. Striking white-to-bluish iris, pale yellow bill and legs.
plbwoo1: Plain dark woodcreeper, uniform dull grayish-brown overall with slightly rufous wings and tail and a faint dusky eye-mask; lacks streaking or spotting. Straight, fairly stout dark bill; gray legs and strong feet.
plcela1: Crested elaenia, grayish-olive above with a bushy peaked crest showing a partly concealed white crown patch, two pale wing bars, and pale grayish-white underparts. Short dark bill, dark legs. Plain face with faint eye-ring.
plcjay1: Striking jay, deep violet-blue and black above with a black face, throat and breast, a stiff erect crest, and pale creamy-yellow underparts and tail tip. Bold pale blue eyebrow and lower eye spots; stout black bill, black legs.
plcsee1: Small dull seedeater, plain grayish-brown overall, slightly paler below, with faint diffuse streaking on the back and rufous undertail coverts. Short, thick conical pale-based bill; dark legs. Nondescript and unmarked.
plcspi1: Plain rufous-and-brown spinetail with a warm rufous crown and wings, brown back, and pale buffy-gray underparts with a whitish throat. Long graduated brown tail. Slender dark bill, grayish legs. Plain unmarked face.
plcsta: Large hummingbird, bronzy-green above with a whitish rump band and white-tipped tail corners, dull grayish-white below, a bold white malar stripe, and a glittering rose-red gorget. Very long, straight black bill; dark legs.
plctan1: Small plain tanager, soft uniform pale blue-gray overall, slightly darker on the wings and back and paler on the underparts, with a darker face. Short stout dark bill; dark legs. Unmarked and evenly toned.
plctyr1: Small olive flycatcher with a plumbeous-gray crown and nape contrasting with greenish back. Pale yellow underparts, whitish throat, faint pale eyering and short supercilium, two dull whitish wing bars. Small dark bill, dark legs.
plgrol1: Striking ground-dweller with cobalt-blue crown and nape, black face mask, and white throat and supercilium edged in blue. Bronzy-green upperparts, rufous breast band, white belly with buff flanks. Stout dark bill, long pinkish legs.
plhpar1: Slim long-tailed parakeet; male has a plum-red to bluish-purple head with a narrow black neck-ring, yellowish-green body, and a red shoulder patch. Long blue-green graduated tail tipped white. Yellow upper, dark lower mandible; pinkish legs.
pllwar1: Small plain leaf warbler, olive-brown above with a long whitish supercilium, dark eyestripe, and two pale wing bars. Whitish underparts washed buff on flanks. Dark upper mandible, pale lower mandible, and distinctive pale pinkish legs.
pllwar2: Tiny, dull leaf warbler with grayish-brown upperparts and dingy whitish-buff underparts. Short pale supercilium, faint dark eyestripe, no wing bars and no rump patch. Short dark bill with pale base, dark legs; very plain overall.
plover3: Tiny hummingbird; male has a glossy green crown bearing a long erect pointed crest, white-tipped, and an iridescent violet-blue throat patch. Green upperparts, gray underparts. Short straight black bill, blackish legs.
plover4: Tiny hummingbird; male has a long pointed crest and a deep violet-purple crown and throat, glittering green back, and grayish underparts. Short straight black bill, dark legs. Similar to Green-crowned but with purple crown.
plsfin1: Uniformly slate-gray finch, slightly paler gray below, with no streaking or bold marks. Conical, stout grayish-yellow bill; dark legs. Female browner and faintly streaked. A plain, leaden-gray high-altitude finch.
plsvir: Sturdy vireo with leaden-gray head and back, bold white spectacles (eyering joined by white loral line), and white throat and underparts. Two white wing bars and yellowish-washed flanks. Stout hooked gray bill, gray legs.
pltant1: Small antwren; male gray overall with a concealed white interscapular patch, blackish wings with white-tipped coverts forming bars, and a whitish throat. Female browner with buffy underparts. Slender dark bill, pale grayish legs.
pltsun2: Stocky sunbird; male has iridescent green crown and upperparts with a glossy purple throat and shoulder, dull yellow belly, and brownish wings. Female olive above, yellow below. Stout, slightly decurved dark bill, dark legs.
pltswi1: Tiny swiftlet, glossy blackish-blue above and grayish-brown below with a slightly paler rump and a shallowly forked tail. Feathered toes give a plumed look. Small black bill, blackish legs; plain and uniform in flight.
pltwar1: Small bright leaf warbler, green above with a yellow crown stripe bordered by dark lateral crown stripes, broad yellow supercilium, dark eyestripe, and two yellow wing bars. Yellow underparts. Dark upper, pale lower mandible; pale legs.
pltwre2: Large wren, rich rufous-brown above with a bold white supercilium and black-streaked face. Whitish throat and breast grading to buff belly, and a plain rufous tail lacking barring. Slim dark bill, grayish legs.
pluant1: Antbird; male dark slaty-gray overall with a contrasting deep rufous-chestnut belly and lower underparts, blackish wings with small white covert spots. Female browner with rufous below. Stout blackish bill, pale grayish legs, bare bluish eyering.
pluant3: Stocky antvireo; male uniformly dark slate-gray with a partly concealed white interscapular patch and small white wing spots. Female slaty-gray, paler below. Pale staring eye, heavy hooked dark bill, gray legs.
plueup1: Small finchlike tanager; male glossy plumbeous slate-blue overall, slightly paler bluish-gray below, with a small black face. Stubby conical dark bill, dark legs. Female duller olive-gray. Compact and short-tailed.
pluhaw: Compact raptor, uniformly dark slate-gray with faint white barring on flight feathers and a black tail crossed by one or two narrow white bands. Bright orange-yellow cere and legs, dark hooked bill, reddish eye.
pluibi1: Large gray ibis; pale gray neck and underparts, slaty-gray wings and back with a glossy greenish sheen, and a whitish forehead. Long decurved blackish bill, red facial skin, red legs, and red eye. Bold and stocky.
plukit1: Small slender kite, pale gray on head and underparts shading to darker slate-gray back and wings, with rufous patches in the primaries visible in flight. Black tail with narrow pale bands. Black bill, yellow cere, red eye, dark legs.
plupig2: Plump pigeon, uniformly dull plumbeous-gray with a faint vinous-purple wash on the head and breast and slightly browner wings. Dark reddish bill, red eye with pale eyering, dull red legs. Plain, unmarked overall.
plurai1: Slim rail with slaty-gray head, neck and underparts, olive-brown back and wings, and a plain unbarred body. Long greenish bill with a red-and-blue base, bright red eye, and coral-red legs. Short cocked tail.
plured1: Plump redstart; male slaty plumbeous-blue overall with a rufous-chestnut tail and undertail. Female grayish-brown above with two white wing bars and a white-based tail. Small black bill, blackish legs; frequently fans its rufous tail.
plusee1: Small seedeater; male blue-gray above and on the head with a whitish throat and white belly, a gray breast, and a white wing speculum. Stubby conical pale bill. Female plain buffy-brown. Compact finchlike shape.
plushc1: Distinctive small tanager with a glossy black face and forecrown and a striking golden-yellow erect plush crown patch. Deep rufous-chestnut underparts and gray back. Very short stubby blackish bill, dark legs.
plwant1: Antshrike; male uniformly slate-gray with plain unmarked wings (lacking white covert spots) and a concealed white interscapular patch. Female rufous-brown with grayish head. Heavy hooked black bill, gray legs, pale eye.
plwduc1: Upright brown whistling-duck with long lanceolate off-white flank plumes boldly edged in black. Chestnut breast finely barred black, pale buff belly, white rump. Pinkish-red bill and legs, yellow eye; long neck.
plwwoo1: Plain woodcreeper, warm brown overall with slightly rufous wings and tail, an unstreaked, unmarked body, and a faintly grayer face. Straight dark bill of medium length, dark legs. Stiff brown tail used as a prop.
pmtspi1: Tiny long-tailed spinetail, grayish-brown above with faint dark streaking, a streaked crown, and pale buffy underparts. Long graduated brown tail with frayed tips. Short fine dark bill, grayish legs; small and slender.
pnbfin1: Brushfinch with a pale creamy-white nape and crown center bordered by black lateral crown stripes, gray face, olive-green back, and yellow underparts with a whitish throat. Conical dark bill, dark legs.
polpar1: Robust parrot with green body, a dull blackish head, a bright yellow band across the lower belly, and a prominent yellow hindneck collar. Blue cheeks and wing edges, long blue-green tail. Pale gray-horn bill, gray legs.
polsta1: Medium dark starling, glossy blackish with a greenish-bronze sheen above and slightly duller below. Pale yellow to whitish eye stands out on the dark face. Stout black bill, dark legs; plain glossy overall.
poltri1: Small cuckooshrike; male glossy blue-black above with a white supercilium and white underparts marked with fine dark spotting on breast and flanks. Female browner. Short black bill, dark legs; trim and upright.
polvan1: Large big-billed vanga with a complete black hood and black upperparts; male white below, female pale rufous below. Massive laterally compressed bluish-gray bill, dark legs. Boldly hooded, heavy-billed appearance.
pomcot1: Stunning cotinga; male deep wine-purple to magenta overall with contrasting silvery-white wings (white inner flight feathers and coverts). Pale whitish-yellow bill, dark legs. Compact, vivid, and unmistakable.
pomgrp2: Plump green pigeon, mostly yellowish-green with a gray crown and forehead, greenish-yellow underparts, and maroon-chestnut mantle and wing patch on the male. Bluish-gray bill, reddish-pink legs; rounded short tail.
pomgrp5: Green pigeon with an ashy-gray head shading into yellow-green body, a lilac-pink wash on the shoulders of the male, and yellow wing-feather edges. Pale bluish bill, coral-red legs; plump and short-tailed.
potpal1: Slender rufous-and-black bird with a bright rufous head, neck and wings, and a black-and-white streaked back and underparts. Long pointed tail. Straight pale dark-tipped bill, dark legs; striking patterned plumage.
powowl1: Very large hawk-owl, grayish-brown above with bold dark V-shaped barring on the whitish underparts. Rounded head without ear tufts, large yellow-orange eyes, and a yellowish bill. Heavily built; yellow feet.
powwoo1: Large black woodpecker with a red crest (full red crown in male, black forecrown in female), buff face and throat, and a buff-and-black barred belly. Black back, ivory-white bill, dark legs; powerful and stocky.
prafal: Pale sandy-brown falcon; brown above, whitish below with dark spotting, a pale head with a narrow brown mustache and dark eyeline. Distinctive dark axillary 'armpit' patches in flight. Yellow cere, feet and eyering; hooked gray bill.
prawar: Small yellow warbler; olive-green above with chestnut streaks on the back, bright yellow face and underparts, black streaks along the flanks, and a dark eyeline with a yellow crescent below the eye. Two faint wing bars, thin dark bill, pale legs.
prbbar1: Plump barbet, olive-green above and dull orange-buff below and on the head, with a small black tuft above a stout pale bluish, slightly hooked bill. Bare grayish eye area, gray legs. Rounded, big-headed shape.
pregrs1: Ground-sparrow with a black face mask and white throat bordered by a black breast crescent, white cheek crescents, a rufous crown, olive-brown back, and grayish-white underparts. Conical dark bill, pinkish legs.
pregrs2: Ground-sparrow with a rufous crown, black-and-white patterned face showing white crescents around a black mask, white throat, olive-brown upperparts, and pale grayish underparts. Conical dark bill, pinkish legs.
prhlau1: Laughingthrush, warm olive-brown overall with a gray crown and nape, rufous-tinged wings and tail, and a contrasting white throat and white-streaked face. Whitish lower belly. Dark bill, pinkish legs; rounded tail.
pribat1: Tiny black-and-white flycatcher-like bird; male has a black crown and face, gray back, white underparts, and a broad black breast band, with white wing markings. Female has a rufous breast band. Small black bill, dark legs; bright pale eye.
prowar: Brilliant golden-yellow warbler with a deep orange-yellow head and breast, plain blue-gray wings and tail, white undertail, and unstreaked greenish back. Long heavy black bill and black legs; large dark eye on a plain golden face.
prsowl: Small dark owl without ear tufts, rich dark brown above mottled with white, and tawny-rufous below with whitish streaking. Rounded head, dark eyes, pale facial disk edge. Pale bill, bare yellowish legs.
przros1: Streaky brown finch resembling a rosefinch; male brown and streaked above with a bright rose-pink wash on throat, breast and belly. Long graduated tail with shorter outer feathers. Slender pointed dark bill, dark legs.
ptpfin1: Large long-tailed finch with olive-green back and wings, grey head, and a distinctive whitish throat contrasting with pale grey underparts. Stout yellowish-and-black conical bill, yellow bend of wing and lores, dark legs.
pubbul1: Olive-brown bulbul with a short erectile crest, brown-streaked head, and pale whitish throat. Underparts dull buffy-grey, undertail coverts pale; distinctive white puffy tufts on the lower back/rump. Dark bill and legs, reddish eye.
pubjay1: Large jay with deep purplish-blue upperparts, wings and tail, blending to dark blackish-blue head and breast. Bulky body, heavy black bill, black legs; pale eye in adults.
pubsun4: Tiny sunbird; breeding male iridescent metallic green head and back with a glossy green throat, bordered below by a purple breast band and a maroon-red band, then dark belly. Slender decurved black bill, dark legs.
pubtho1: Tiny short-billed hummingbird; male glittering bronze-green above with a brilliant violet-purple back patch, green throat, and forked dark tail. Very short straight black bill, dark legs.
puclor1: Small green lorikeet with a deep purple-blue crown, orange-red forehead and cheeks, and a yellow-green nape collar. Blue underwing and red underwing patch in flight; orange bill, grey legs.
puctur2: Glossy turaco with a tall glossy purple crest, green face and throat, and purple-blue back, wings and breast washed pink-red. Dull crimson primaries flash in flight; red eye-ring, black bill, dark legs.
pucwoo1: Tiny hummingbird; male bronze-green above with a brilliant green-and-violet gorget bordered by a purplish collar, white below. Short straight black bill, forked dark tail, dark legs.
pughon1: Greyish-olive honeyeater with finely streaked grey crown and breast, a black mask through the eye, and a bright lilac-purple gape stripe. Yellow ear tuft, dark bill, grey legs.
pumtan2: Tanager with a glossy purplish-blue crown and nape, black face and throat, and rich orange-rufous breast and belly. Bluish-purple mantle and wings; dark bill, dark legs.
punibi1: Large dark glossy ibis, blackish with deep purple, green and bronze iridescence on wings and back. Long decurved dusky-pink bill, dark red legs, pale bluish facial skin; reddish eye.
punmin1: Small sandy-brown miner with pale grey-brown upperparts, whitish underparts, and a faint pale supercilium. Rufous wing patch shows in flight; short straight dark bill, pale legs.
punsni1: Cryptic snipe with boldly patterned buff-and-black streaked upperparts, barred flanks, and a whitish belly. Long straight blackish bill, dark crown with pale central stripe, greenish-yellow legs.
punsun1: Spiderhunter with olive-green upperparts and yellowish underparts heavily streaked dark; male has a glossy purple crown and nape, and a purple malar streak. Long decurved blackish bill, dull legs.
puntap1: Small dark slate-grey tapaculo, nearly uniform blackish-grey overall with browner, faintly barred flanks and lower belly. Short cocked tail, stout black bill, brownish legs.
puntea1: Dabbling duck with a dark blackish-blue crown contrasting sharply with a clean white face and throat, finely vermiculated grey body, and a buff-spotted flank. Blue bill, dark green speculum, grey legs.
punthi1: Slim long-tailed furnariid with rich rufous-brown upperparts, a rufous forehead and throat patch, and greyish-brown underparts. Long graduated frayed tail; thin dark bill, dark legs.
puntin1: Stocky terrestrial tinamou; greyish body finely barred and vermiculated, with bold black-and-white streaking on the head and neck and a rufous-buff belly. Short stout decurved bill, yellowish legs.
purbul1: Plump dark bullfinch, sooty black overall with rufous-chestnut patches on the throat, above the eye, and under the tail. Heavy black conical bill, dark legs.
purfin: Stocky finch; male raspberry-red wash over head, breast, back and rump, brownest on wings, with a whitish belly and blurry pink-brown flank streaking. Conical pale bill, brown legs.
purfly1: Plain Myiarchus flycatcher, grey-brown above with a slightly bushy crown, pale grey throat and breast grading to pale yellow belly. Faint wing edging, dark bill, dark legs.
purgal2: Striking rail with iridescent purple-blue head, neck and underparts, bronze-green back and wings, and white undertail coverts. Pale blue frontal shield, red-and-yellow bill, long yellow legs and toes.
purgre2: Small waxbill; male has a cinnamon-brown body, deep cobalt-blue rump and belly patch, a purplish-blue face, and a red eye-ring. Stout red-and-black bill, dark legs.
purhon1: Small tanager; male glossy deep purple-blue with a black throat, black wings and tail, and bright yellow legs. Long decurved black bill.
purjac1: Slim jacamar with glossy oily-purple to greenish-bronze upperparts, throat and breast, and a white throat patch on the male. Long needle-like black bill, dark legs, white belly.
purjac2: Stout chestnut jacamar, almost entirely deep chestnut-chocolate with dark bronzy forehead, crown, wings and tail. Stout pinkish-red bill, reddish bare eye skin, red feet.
purjay1: Large dull purplish-blue jay with a darker blackish face and throat, paler purplish-grey underparts. Heavy black bill, black legs; dark eye.
purlic1: Large slim lizard-cuckoo, grey-brown above with a long graduated tail boldly tipped white below, rufous wing patch, and pale grey throat grading to cinnamon belly. Red eye-ring, long bill, grey legs.
purmar: Large swallow; male entirely glossy blue-black with a forked tail, broad pointed wings, and a short black bill. Dark legs.
purnig1: Cryptic nightjar mottled grey-brown and buff with fine black vermiculations, a dark crown, and a buff throat band. Male shows white tail corners; tiny dark bill, dark legs.
pursun3: Small sunbird; male has a metallic green crown, dark maroon back, bright purple rump and throat, and yellow underparts. Whitish flank tufts, slender decurved black bill, dark legs.
pursun4: Tiny sunbird; breeding male glossy metallic purplish-black overall with a dark maroon breast band and yellow-and-red pectoral tufts. Slender decurved black bill, dark legs.
purswa2: Large purple-blue swamphen with a blackish-blue head and neck, bluish back, and white undertail coverts. Massive red bill and frontal shield, long red legs and toes.
purswa3: Large swamphen with a pale grey head and neck contrasting with a purple-blue body, and white undertail coverts. Heavy red bill and frontal shield, long red legs and toes.
purswa6: Large swamphen with a black head and upperparts and deep purple-blue throat and breast, with white undertail coverts. Massive red bill and frontal shield, long pinkish-red legs.
purtan1: Plain dark tanager-like bird, sooty greyish-brown above and paler grey below with a whitish throat and a small white wing speculum. Dark bill, dark legs; pale eye.
purtod1: Tiny plump tody, bright emerald-green above with a brilliant red throat, white breast, and pale yellow flanks washed pink. Long flat bill with red lower mandible, pinkish-grey legs.
purvir1: Small plain vireo, greyish-olive above and pale grey-white below washed yellowish on the flanks, with a pale eye-ring and faint lores. Stout grey bill, grey legs.
purwoo1: Woodpecker with black upperparts, a white forehead and face, and a red crown and nape (red throat patch in male). Dusky-brown to reddish underparts; chisel black bill, grey legs.
putbab1: Brown babbler with rufous crown, olive-brown upperparts, and whitish underparts boldly streaked dark on the breast and flanks. Buff-white throat, pale supercilium, slender bill, pinkish legs.
putbul1: Olive-brown bulbul with a brownish crest, greyish face, and a puffy white throat contrasting with yellowish underparts. Olive wings and tail; dark bill, brownish legs.
putcus1: Cuckooshrike; male glossy blue-black overall with a purple sheen and a small yellow-orange gape patch at the bill base. Black bill, dark legs.
puteup1: Small euphonia; male glossy blue-black above and on the throat with a violet sheen, and bright yellow forehead, breast and belly. Stubby dark bill, dark legs.
putfru1: Stocky black cotinga; male all glossy black with a large fan-shaped iridescent crimson-purple throat ruff. Heavy black bill, dark legs.
putsun1: Hummingbird with bronze-green upperparts, a glittering green crown, and a brilliant violet-purple gorget edged with a buff breast band. Forked dark tail, short straight black bill, dark legs.
putsun3: Small sunbird; male has a metallic green crown, deep maroon-purple back and breast, and a glittering purple-red throat, with dull olive belly. Slender decurved black bill, dark legs.
puvill1: Plump skulking babbler, warm brown above with rufous-tinged crown and wings, a white throat, and pale grey buff-washed underparts. Pale eye with thin white ring, short curved bill, pinkish legs.
pvttyr1: Tiny tody-tyrant, olive-grey above with two pale wing bars, and whitish underparts faintly streaked greyish on the breast. Conspicuous pale eye, short dark bill, dark legs.
pybfly1: Very small flycatcher; male slate-blue above with a bright rufous-orange throat and breast and white belly. Short dark bill, dark legs.
pygant1: Tiny short-tailed antwren; male grey above streaked black on the crown and back, with white-tipped wing coverts, and pale buffy-white underparts faintly streaked. Slender dark bill, pale legs.
pygbat1: Tiny stub-tailed flycatcher-like bird; male grey above with black mask through eye, white throat and a broad black breast band across white underparts, white wing stripe. Female has rufous breast band. Black bill, dark legs, bright red eye.
pygflo1: Very small, dumpy short-tailed flowerpecker; dull olive-green above, pale buffy-grey below with whitish throat and faint streaking on breast. Stubby dark bill, dark legs. One of the smallest birds in its range.
pygnut: Tiny short-tailed nuthatch; blue-grey back and wings, brownish-grey cap reaching down to eye, dark eyeline, pale creamy-buff underparts and whitish throat. Sharp straight grey bill, grey legs.
pygsun2: Slender long-tailed sunbird; breeding male metallic green head and back, bright yellow belly, with greatly elongated central tail streamers. Female plain greyish-olive above, pale yellow below. Thin decurved black bill, dark legs.
pygwoo1: Small brown-and-white woodpecker, barred brown above with white-spotted wings, dirty-white underparts with brown streaking, and a broad pale stripe down the side of the brown-capped head. Male has a tiny red spot behind eye. Greyish bill, grey legs.
pyrrhu: Slim grey cardinal with a thin upright crest; male grey overall washed rose-red on face, crest, breast and belly. Stubby strongly curved yellowish bill. Female buffy-grey with reddish wash. Long tail, dark legs.
pywbab1: Tiny rounded babbler with virtually no tail; warm brown above, whitish underparts heavily scaled with dark crescents. Short fine dark bill, pinkish legs. Looks like a small ball of feathers.
quailf1: Tiny stout finch; male grey-brown above, finely barred black-and-white on flanks and breast, with a black face and throat and reddish bill. Female plainer with less barring and orange bill. Stubby conical bill, pinkish legs.
rabcur2: Large glossy black turkey-like bird with a tall curled black crest; deep blue-black plumage, chestnut-red lower belly and undertail, and a vivid red-and-yellow ridged knobbed bill. Red legs.
rabeat1: Slender bright-green bee-eater with golden-buff crown and nape, black eye mask, pale blue-green throat and a black gorget above golden-buff underparts; coppery-orange wing patch in flight. Black tail with two thin elongated streamers. Long decurved black bill, dark legs.
rabgro1: Heavy-billed tanager-grosbeak; male glossy black on head, throat, wings and tail with the rest of the body rich crimson-red. Female has slate-grey head and red body. Massive pinkish-red conical bill, dark legs.
radshe1: Striking white shelduck with rich rusty-chestnut breast band and back, white head, neck and belly, dark blackish wingtips and a glossy green-black flight-feather panel. Pink bill, pink legs, white eye.
radwar1: Plump dingy olive-brown leaf warbler; brownish above, buffy-yellow below with rusty-buff flanks, and a long buffy-cream supercilium. Stout pale-based bill, distinctively orange-yellow legs.
rafmal1: Large slim cuckoo-relative; rich rufous-chestnut overall, slightly darker greyish on the head, with a long graduated chestnut tail. Stout greenish bill, bare green skin around the eye, greyish legs.
ragmac1: Huge long-tailed macaw, mostly brilliant scarlet-red with green wing-coverts grading to blue on the wings and a long red-and-blue tail. Bare white face with fine red feather lines. Massive hooked bill, pale upper and black lower mandible, grey legs.
railor4: Medium parrot with deep blue head, green upperparts and a yellow-green collar; orange-red breast with dark barring, green belly. Orange-red bill, grey legs. Very similar to Rainbow Lorikeet.
railor5: Vivid parrot with violet-blue head, green back, wings and tail, and an orange-yellow collar across the nape; bright orange-red breast, dark blue belly, green flanks. Coral-red bill, grey legs.
railor7: Medium parrot with blackish-blue head, green upperparts and a broad orange-red collar across the hindneck; orange-yellow breast and dark blue-black belly. Orange-red bill, grey legs.
raiqua1: Small buff quail; male sandy-brown above with dark streaks, buffy below with a black throat patch, bold black streaks on the breast and a black-and-white facial pattern. Female plainer, lacking black throat. Grey bill, yellowish legs.
raista1: Large hummingbird with a long straight black bill; body shining green, throat and breast iridescent golden-coppery, forehead glittering blue-violet, and a rufous-orange forecrown patch. Dark legs.
rampig1: Large dark pigeon, slaty blue-grey overall with a purplish gloss, heavily spangled with small white spots on the wings, neck and underparts. Bare yellow eye-ring, yellow bill and yellow legs.
ratcis1: Plain greyish-brown cisticola, streaked darker above on a greyer crown, with whitish underparts and buffy flanks; rufous wing-edges. Tail brown with subterminal dark spots and pale tips. Thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
rattre1: Slender glossy black treepie washed velvety grey, with a pale grey forehead and a long graduated tail that ends in racket-like broadened tips. Stout black bill, black legs.
rattre2: Dark grey-black treepie, sooty below paling on the head, with a distinctive long graduated tail whose feather tips curl upward giving a ratchet or notched look. Heavy black bill, dark legs.
rawant1: Plump round antpitta with rich rufous crown, nape, back and face; underparts blotched red-rufous and white with a paler belly. Bare greyish-blue eye-ring. Short dark bill, long pale pinkish legs, almost tailless.
rawcra1: Small crake; rich rufous-chestnut head, neck and breast, dark olive-brown back and wings, and a contrasting white lower belly. Short greenish bill, reddish legs, red eye.
rawspi2: Small spinetail; bright rufous crown, back, wings and tail with paler rufous rump, black lores and a dusky eyeline; clean white underparts. Spiny-looking tail tips. Thin pointed bill, pale legs.
rawwre1: Bold wren; rich rufous crown and upperparts contrasting with clean white underparts and white throat, a bold white supercilium and black-bordered face. Wings and tail finely barred dusky. Slightly decurved dark bill, greyish legs.
raybar1: Spectacular barbet; black head spotted white with a red crown and face, black-spotted yellow back and breast, red rump and a yellow-and-red bib. Bright orange-red eye and red bill, blackish legs.
