supfai1: Tiny long-tailed wren; breeding male brilliant with a rich blue crown, cheeks and mantle, black throat and nape band, and pale underparts. Female plain brown with bluish tail and reddish bill area.
suphem1: Small tanager-finch, grey above and buffy-cinnamon below, with a slaty-grey head and a bold white supercilium. Slender dark bill and dark legs.
suplyr1: Large brown pheasant-like songbird with grey-brown body, paler underparts, and a spectacular long lyre-shaped filamentous tail in the male. Strong dark bill and powerful grey-brown legs.
supowl1: Tiny pygmy-owl, rich rufous-brown above finely barred, with whitish underparts streaked brown and two black false-eye spots on the nape. Yellow eyes, yellowish bill and feathered yellowish feet.
suppar1: Slim long-tailed parrot, bright green overall; male has a yellow face and throat with a red band across the upper breast. Long pointed green tail, orange-red bill and grey legs.
supsta1: Brilliant glossy starling, iridescent blue-green head and back, bright chestnut-orange belly separated from the breast by a white band, and a white undertail. Black bill and legs, pale eye.
supwre1: Small slender wren, rufous-brown above and white below with rufous flanks. Bold white supercilium contrasts with blackish eyestripe and brown crown; cheeks finely streaked black-and-white. Tail barred. Slim slightly decurved blackish bill, pale legs.
surfbi: Stocky shorebird with short stout yellow-based dark bill and yellow legs. Breeding adult mottled slate-grey above with rusty scapular patches, whitish underparts heavily marked with dark grey chevrons. White rump and tail base with broad black terminal tail band.
surfly1: Tiny flat-billed flycatcher, olive-brown above with rich tawny-buff wing markings and a brilliant lemon-yellow rump. Underparts buffy with pale eye-ring on plain face. Broad flat black bill, pale legs; often fans tail showing rounded shape.
surtro1: Stout trogon; male glossy steel-blue head, breast and upperparts with a violet-blue sheen, bright red belly separated by a white breast band. Yellow bill, dark face. Undertail finely vermiculated black-and-white. Female greyer above with red belly.
susowl1: Small scops-owl with short ear-tufts, mottled rufous-brown and grey plumage finely vermiculated and streaked, with a paler facial disc and dark eyes. Underparts barred and streaked. Pale buff scapular line; short feathered legs, dark bill.
susowl2: Small grey-brown scops-owl with short ear-tufts, finely vermiculated plumage and a buffy nuchal collar. Underparts greyish with fine dark shaft-streaks and cross-bars. Pale buff scapular spots, dark eyes, pale facial disc, feathered legs.
sutspi1: Slim spinetail-like furnariid, warm cinnamon-brown above with dark brown patches on the wings and a striking white supercilium. Underparts whitish with fine grey streaking and a diagnostic sulphur-yellow chin and throat. Slender pointed bill, pale legs.
suwpar1: Small green parakeet with scaly olive-and-buff barring on throat and breast, dull reddish forehead and crown patch, and bright sulphur-yellow underwing-coverts visible in flight. Dull red on belly; horn-coloured bill, greyish legs, dark blue in wing.
swafly1: Medium Myiarchus flycatcher, grey-brown above with rufous edgings in wings and tail, pale grey throat and breast blending to pale yellow belly. Slight bushy crest, plain face. Two-toned bill blackish above with pale pinkish base below, dark legs.
swafly3: Plain greyish-brown flycatcher, dull brown above and dirty greyish-white below with faint diffuse streaking on the breast. Pale lores and indistinct eye-ring on a plain face. Small dark bill, dark legs; upright compact posture.
swafra2: Large grey-brown spurfowl finely vermiculated and streaked, with a bare bright red face and throat patch. Upperparts brown with pale shaft-streaks, underparts streaked. Heavy blackish bill, dark red legs with spurs; sturdy gamebird build.
swagre1: Robust greenbul, olive-brown above with a contrasting whitish throat and breast and bold white outer tail tips. Greyish-brown head, plain face, pale underparts. Stout dark bill, dark legs; thrush-like proportions.
swahar1: Large slim harrier; adult brown above with paler buff-streaked head and breast, rufous-buff underparts streaked darker, and a pale whitish rump. Long wings held in shallow V; yellow eyes and legs, dark wingtips, dark bill.
swahaw: Large slim Buteo; light morph adult dark brown above with white face, dark brown breast band over white belly, and long pointed wings. In flight white wing-linings contrast with dark flight feathers; finely barred tail. Yellow cere and legs.
swanig1: Cryptic greyish-brown nightjar mottled and vermiculated, with buff-spotted scapulars and a buff nuchal collar. Male shows white outer-tail corners and small white throat patches. Tiny dark bill, large dark eyes, short legs.
swapri1: Long-tailed grass-babbler, olive-grey above with bold dark streaking from forehead over crown and back, fading rearward. Underparts greyish-white, greyer on flanks. Faint buff undertail-coverts. Slender dark bill, pale legs; large prinia-like build.
swaspa: Medium sparrow with rich rufous wings, grey face and breast, and a rusty crown (streaked in non-breeding). Buffy flanks, whitish belly, plain greyish unstreaked underparts. Streaked brown back, dark eyeline; dark bill, pinkish legs.
swaspa2: African grey-and-brown sparrow with a grey head and underparts, brown back and wings, and a rufous-chestnut rump. Darker, blackish on head and shoulders than similar grey-headed sparrows. Stout dark bill, pale pinkish legs.
swatan1: Striking tanager-like bird; male glossy turquoise-blue with a black face and throat, black flank barring and a white belly patch. Short stubby bill, dark legs; compact body. Female bright grass-green with paler greenish underparts.
swathr: Plain brown thrush, olive-brown above with a warm buffy face and bold buffy eye-ring and lores (spectacled look). Whitish underparts with buff-washed breast boldly spotted blackish. Dark bill with pale base, pinkish legs.
swawar: Plain warbler, warm brown above with a rusty-brown crown and a long pale buff supercilium over a dark eyeline. Underparts dull whitish washed buff, unstreaked. Long heavy pointed pinkish bill, pinkish legs; flat-headed look.
swewax3: Tiny waxbill, olive-green above and pale grey below with a black face and tail. Male has black lores and throat; rump bright crimson-red. Short conical bill black above and red below, dark legs; rounded compact shape.
swfgle: Medium foliage-gleaner, warm rufous-brown above with slaty-grey wings, a buff throat and a pale buff eye-ring and supercilium. Underparts olive-brown with diffuse paler streaking. Straight pointed bill, greyish legs; long rounded tail.
swipar1: Slim green parrot with crimson around the face, throat and undertail, blue crown patch, and red shoulder. Bright yellow on underparts and red wing-pit. Long tapering reddish tail, short pale bill, dark legs; streamlined fast-flying shape.
swisni1: Stocky snipe, cryptically patterned brown, buff and black above with bold pale braces and a dark-and-buff striped head. Whitish belly, barred flanks. Very long straight dark bill, greyish-olive legs; relatively short broad-based tail.
swiwhe1: Small warbler-like bird, plain olive-green above and pale yellow-washed below with a bright white eye-ring. Greyish flanks, yellow throat and undertail. Fine dark bill, greyish legs; rounded active shape.
swspet: All-dark sooty-brown storm-petrel with a slightly paler greyish wing-bar across the upperwing-coverts and a forked tail. No white rump. Black bill with tubenose, black legs; slender long-winged build.
swtgul1: Large elegant gull; breeding adult has a dark grey hood, scarlet eye-ring, pale grey body and a deeply forked white tail. Bold black-white-grey wing pattern with white triangle. Black bill with pale tip, red legs, large dark eyes.
swthum1: Large hummingbird, glossy green body with a deep blue head and throat, and a long deeply forked steel-blue tail. White vent. Straight slim black bill, dark legs; long elegant streamer-tailed silhouette.
swtkit: Striking raptor with snowy-white head and underparts contrasting with black back, wings and long deeply forked black tail. Black bill with bluish cere, pale legs; long pointed wings and graceful buoyant shape.
swtman1: Plump manakin; male velvety black with a sky-blue back and a scarlet crown patch, and elongated central tail feathers. Pale legs, short dark bill. Female and immatures uniform olive-green with the long pointed central tail spikes.
swtnig1: Cryptic brown nightjar mottled and streaked, with a rufous nuchal collar. Male has extremely long ribbon-like outer tail streamers tipped white. Buff-spotted wing-coverts, dark face, large dark eyes, tiny bill, short legs.
swwpuf1: Distinctive puffbird, sooty black overall with a small white belly and white vent, and broad white wing-band across the flight feathers visible in flight. Short heavy black bill, large head, dark legs; swallow-like long wings.
swwqua1: Plump terrestrial quail, rich brown above finely vermiculated and barred, with bold white spotting on the wing-coverts. Grey face and breast, rufous-brown flanks, bare red orbital skin. Stout dark bill, greyish legs; rounded body.
swyrob1: Small robin-chat; slaty-grey above with a black-and-white half-collar mark on the neck-side, orange-rufous breast and white belly. Plain grey face, dark wings. Slender black bill, dark legs; upright chat-like posture.
sybhor1: Medium hornbill, grey-brown above with white-spotted wing-coverts and a white belly and back-stripe. Bare pinkish facial skin and dark eye. Long down-curved yellow bill without a casque; dark legs. Pale eye, slender build.
sykwar2: Small plain warbler, greyish olive-brown above and whitish below washed pale buff. Short pale supercilium, faint dark eyeline, plain face. Fine dark bill with pale base, dark legs; slim Hippolais-type shape lacking wing markings.
tabbab1: Small babbler, rufous-brown above with a rufous crown, and rich tawny-orange underparts brightening on throat and breast. Plain face, whitish or buff belly. Short stout dark bill, pale legs; rounded skulking shape with longish graduated tail.
tabher1: Large hermit hummingbird, bronzy-green above with rich tawny-buff underparts and rump. Dark face mask bordered by buffy supercilium and malar stripe. Long decurved bill with reddish lower mandible; long white-tipped central tail streamers.
tabowl1: Stocky round-headed owl without ear-tufts, dark chocolate-brown above with a tawny-buff facial disc and bold buff eyebrows forming a spectacled look. Underparts buff with a dark brown breast band. Dark eyes, pale bill, feathered legs.
tabsco1: Small screech-owl with short ear-tufts, warm tawny-brown plumage finely vermiculated, with a tawny-buff facial disc and dark eyes. Underparts buffy with fine dark streaks and cross-barring. Pale scapular line, greenish bill, feathered legs.
tabscr1: Dark stocky megapode, sooty grey-brown overall with a slight crest and bare reddish facial skin. Large strong yellowish to dull legs and feet. Short dark bill; rounded body and short tail; fowl-like terrestrial build.
tabsee1: Tiny finch; male grey above with a grey head and uniform rich tawny-cinnamon underparts from throat to belly. Plain wings. Short conical pale grey or bluish bill, dark legs. Female plain buffy-brown, paler below.
tabtin1: Plump tinamou, rich rufous-brown above finely barred and vermiculated dusky, with a blackish crown and tawny-rufous breast. Buffy belly, plain face. Slim slightly decurved bill, greyish legs; rounded compact body, very short tail.
taceup1: Small stout euphonia; male glossy steel-blue above and on throat and breast, with a complete tawny-orange (russet) cap and rich yellow belly, white undertail-coverts. Stubby dark bill, dark legs. Female olive-green, yellower below.
tacfly1: Tiny flycatcher, olive-green above with two pale buff wing-bars and a whitish eye-ring. Bright yellow underparts with a tawny-buff wash across the chest, whitish throat. Small dark bill with paler base, pale legs; compact upright shape.
tacgre2: Small greenlet, olive-green above with a contrasting ochre-orange crown and nape, and a greyish face with pale eye-ring. Underparts dull buffy-yellow to greyish. Short stout pinkish bill, pale legs; plain vireo-like build.
tachon1: Slim honeyeater, grey-brown above with a tawny-orange crown and a black-and-white striped face pattern. Underparts whitish, finely streaked grey. Slender slightly decurved black bill, dark legs; yellow wing-edge flash in flight.
tacnig1: Cryptic brown nightjar mottled and vermiculated, with a rufous-tawny hindcollar and buff-spotted wing-coverts. Male shows white tail corners and a small white throat patch. Large dark eyes, tiny dark bill, short legs.
tacpyt1: Tiny scrub-tyrant, dull greyish-brown above with a faint rufous-tinged crown and a pale eye. Underparts whitish washed pale buff on the breast, plain face with faint eye-ring. Small dark bill, pale legs; upright slim posture.
tacpyt2: Tiny cinnamon-brown flycatcher with a buffy-orange wash over the face and forehead and a small hidden rufous crown patch. Two buff wing bars, whitish throat, and pale cream underparts. Dark upper bill with pale pinkish lower mandible; bluish-gray legs.
tactan1: Small velvety-black tanager with a contrasting bright tawny-orange crest and crown extending down the nape. Rest of plumage glossy black, including underparts. Stout black conical bill; dark legs. Female is dull olive-brown overall.
tafgna1: Small slender gnatwren with brownish-gray upperparts, a rich tawny-cinnamon face and throat, and gray breast finely spotted whitish. Long thin straight black bill and short cocked tail. Pinkish legs.
tafpri1: Small slim warbler-like bird with grayish-brown upperparts, a pale buffy supercilium, whitish throat and pale tawny-buff flanks and undertail. Long graduated tail often cocked, tipped dark-and-white. Thin black bill; pinkish legs.
tafqua1: Plump rounded ground quail with rufous-brown upperparts and a distinctive striped face pattern: rufous and black facial bands. Male has gray breast with rufous tones, female buffier and barred below. Short stubby dark bill; dull legs.
tahswa2: Small swallow with brown upperparts, a tawny-rufous cap and forehead, and whitish underparts with a buffy wash on throat and breast. Slightly notched tail. Short black bill; dark legs.
taibap1: Plump rounded partridge with mottled brown upperparts, gray face and breast, and warm buff-rufous flanks boldly marked with black and chestnut scaling. Bold whitish supercilium and throat. Short pale bill; reddish legs.
taibar2: Stocky green barbet with a multicolored face: blue cheeks and throat, red and yellow forehead and crown patches, and a black band across the breast. Bright green body. Heavy pale ivory bill with bristles; gray legs.
taibuw1: Small skulking dark olive-brown warbler with rufous-tinged brown upperparts, a faint pale supercilium, and grayish-buff underparts with a paler throat. Long rounded tail. Slender dark bill; pinkish legs.
taifly1: Small gray-brown flycatcher with pale grayish-white underparts and a white-sided black tail it frequently flicks up. Breeding male shows a small orange-red throat patch bordered gray. Black bill with dark base; dark legs.
taiful1: Tiny plain fulvetta with grayish-brown upperparts, a grayer head, and a thin whitish eye-ring. Pale grayish-buff underparts and rufous-tinged wings. Small stubby dark bill; pinkish legs.
taihwa1: Plain warm brown babbler-thrush with grayish streaking on the head and breast and a buffy-brown body. Lacks the bold white eye markings of mainland Hwamei. Yellowish bill; pale legs.
taipar1: Plump rounded partridge with olive-brown upperparts, a bold black-and-white streaked face, white throat and chestnut-and-gray underparts with black spotting on the flanks. Red orbital skin. Dark bill; reddish legs.
taiscb1: Long-tailed scimitar-babbler with rufous-brown upperparts, a bold white supercilium and white throat contrasting with a chestnut-and-gray streaked breast. Long down-curved blackish bill; gray legs.
taiwrb1: Tiny near-tailless rounded cupwing with dark rufous-brown plumage heavily scaled black-and-buff above and on the breast. Compact dumpy shape. Short thin dark bill; pinkish legs.
taiyuh1: Small crested yuhina with a pointed reddish-brown crest, gray face with black moustachial stripes, brown upperparts and whitish underparts washed buff. Reddish-brown nape. Small dark bill; pinkish legs.
tamcon1: Slim warbler-like tanager, mostly blue-gray with a striking rufous-orange supercilium, throat-bib and undertail coverts, plus a white wing flash. Sharply pointed dark bill; dark legs.
tamcro: Medium all-glossy-black crow with faint purple sheen, slim build and a relatively slender straight black bill. Black legs and feet.
tamdov1: Small dove with olive-brown upperparts, two iridescent blue-green wing spots, and clean white face and underparts. Male shows whiter face. Short dark bill; purplish-red legs.
tancoc1: Mostly white cockatoo with a pale salmon-pink wash on the face and forehead between bill and eyes, and yellowish underwings. Short erectile white crest, gray bill; gray legs.
tanfin1: Chunky finch-like bird with olive-green upperparts, a slate-gray hood and breast, and a bold black face mask. Yellowish-olive flanks. Stout conical pale bill; pinkish legs.
tanhen1: Large bulky dark gray-brown rail-like bird with olive-brown back, slaty-gray underparts, a white patch on the flank, and a short cocked black tail. Stout greenish-yellow bill; thick red legs. Flightless.
tapowl1: Tiny owl with grayish-brown to rufous upperparts spotted whitish, pale underparts with dark streaking, and a rounded head lacking ear tufts. Two black 'false-eye' nape spots. Yellow eyes; greenish-yellow bill; yellow feet.
tartyr1: Tiny dull olive tyrannulet with grayish-olive upperparts, a contrasting tawny-cinnamon rump, faint pale wing bars and a pale eye-ring. Pale yellowish-gray underparts. Small dark bill; dark legs.
tasscr1: Small dark olive-brown scrubwren with a blackish face mask, white eye and faint pale brow, dull buffy-brown underparts. Slightly streaked breast. Slender dark bill; pinkish legs.
tastho1: Tiny olive-brown thornbill with a grayish head, pale scalloped forehead, white throat and undertail, and buff-washed underparts. Dark-tipped tail with a pale band. Fine dark bill; dark legs.
tatdot1: Plump plover-like wader with brown-streaked upperparts, a gray face, and a distinctive rufous-cinnamon throat patch. Pale belly with a black-and-buff belly patch. Short dark bill; yellowish legs.
tatlea1: Dark rufous-brown leaftosser with a contrasting rufous-chestnut throat and rump and a blackish tail. Slender slightly down-curved dark bill; dark legs.
tatspi1: Small slim ovenbird with cinnamon-buff face, tawny-rufous forehead and brown crown, brown upperparts and pale buff underparts. Long graduated spiny tail. Fine dark bill; dark legs.
tattin1: Small ground tinamou with plain grayish-brown upperparts, a dark gray head and neck, whitish throat and barred lower belly. Reddish bill and bright red legs.
tawant3: Plump rounded antpitta with uniform warm tawny-olive plumage, slightly paler tawny-buff underparts and a faint pale eye-ring. Almost tailless, long-legged. Dark bill; grayish legs.
tawfro1: Large gray frogmouth with intricately mottled and streaked gray-brown plumage resembling tree bark. Huge broad flat hooked olive-gray bill, bristly forehead, and large yellow eyes. Short legs.
tawgra2: Large long-tailed grassbird with heavily streaked dark brown upperparts, a rufous-tinged crown, buffy supercilium, and pale buff streaked underparts. Long graduated tail. Slender dark bill; pinkish legs.
tawgra3: Streaky brown grassbird with a warm rufous-tinged crown, blackish streaking on the back, buff supercilium, and plain buffy underparts. Long tail. Slender dark bill; pinkish legs.
tawlar1: Small crested lark with rufous-brown upperparts streaked dark, a short crest, buff supercilium, and pale buff underparts streaked on the breast. Rufous wing panel. Slender pale bill; pinkish legs.
tawwoo1: Plain rufous-brown woodcreeper with tawny-rufous wings and tail contrasting with a grayer-brown head and back, and a faint pale eye-ring and throat streaking. Straight dark bill; gray legs.
tbsfin1: Small chunky seed-finch, male glossy black overall with a white wing speculum and a very large swollen blackish bill. Female warm brown with paler underparts and the same heavy bill. Dark legs.
tebfin1: Brushfinch with olive-green upperparts, a black face mask and crown, yellow throat and breast, and olive flanks. Whitish belly. Stout dark bill; pinkish legs.
tembab1: Medium babbler with rufous-brown upperparts, a gray crown, a rufous-and-white face pattern with rufous cheeks, white throat and buffy underparts. Dark bill; pinkish legs.
temsee1: Small seedeater, male blue-gray above and on the head with white underparts and a whitish wing patch; distinguished by a thick yellowish curved bill. Female brownish. Dark legs.
temsun1: Tiny brilliant sunbird, male scarlet-red on head, back and breast with a violet crown and tail and yellowish belly, plus a yellow rump band. Female olive-green above, yellow below. Fine curved black bill; dark legs.
tenwar: Small slim warbler with plain olive-green upperparts, a gray head, a whitish supercilium and dark eyeline, and clean white underparts. White undertail coverts. Fine pointed dark bill; gray legs.
tepant1: Plump rounded antpitta with warm brown upperparts, grayish face, white throat and grayish-white underparts blurrily streaked olive-brown. Nearly tailless. Dark bill; pinkish-gray legs.
tepgre1: Small vireo with olive-green upperparts, grayish crown, a pale yellowish supercilium and eye-ring, and yellowish-washed whitish underparts. Faint wing bars. Stout pale-based dark bill; gray legs.
teppar1: Tiny all-green parrotlet, bright grass-green overall with paler yellowish-green underparts and a short tail. Small pale horn bill; pinkish legs.
tepred1: Slim warbler with slaty-gray upperparts, a chestnut crown patch, black face, and bright yellow underparts. White outer tail feathers conspicuous in flight. Thin black bill; dark legs.
tepspi1: Small spinetail with rufous crown and wings contrasting with grayish-olive back, a faint pale supercilium, and pale grayish-buff underparts. Rufous-tinged tail. Fine dark bill; gray legs.
terbro1: Plain greenish-brown bulbul (brownbul) with olive-brown upperparts, a grayer head, whitish throat and dull buff-gray underparts. Rufous-tinged wings and tail. Slender dark bill; pinkish legs.
thbeup1: Small stocky euphonia, male glossy steel-blue-black above and on the throat with a bright yellow forecrown and entirely yellow underparts. Female olive-green above, yellowish below. Thick stubby dark bill; gray legs.
thbflo1: Tiny dull gray-brown flowerpecker with grayish-olive upperparts, dull whitish underparts streaked grayish, a pale vent and a faintly streaked throat. Short stout dark bill; dark legs.
thbkin: Large gray-and-yellow kingbird with dark gray head, dusky face mask, and pale gray throat and breast blending to pale lemon-yellow belly. Brownish-gray upperparts and dark wings; long heavy black bill; black legs. Stocky, big-headed flycatcher shape.
thbpar: Large stocky green parrot with deep red forehead, fore-crown, shoulder bend, and thighs, plus a red eyestripe. Bright green body, blackish flight feathers, yellow underwing band. Massive heavy black bill; orange-yellow eye; gray legs.
thbpig1: Plump green pigeon with grayish-green head and neck, yellow-green underparts, and maroon-chestnut back and wing coverts (male). Pale yellow wing-edgings; pale gray undertail. Stout pale-tipped bill with bright red-pink base and bluish-green cere; coral-red legs.
thbplo1: Small slim plover with brown back, white underparts crossed by two black breast bands separated by a white band, and a gray face with white forehead and supercilium. Red eye-ring and red bill base with black tip; pinkish legs.
thbsal1: Large dark saltator, dull olive-green above with slate-gray head, bold white supercilium, and white throat bordered by dark malar. Grayish underparts with olive-yellow flanks. Very heavy, deep, blackish bill; dark legs.
thbsis1: Small finch with blackish-brown head and upperparts, bright yellow rump, breast, and belly, and yellow wing patches and tail-base flashes. Heavy conical pale pinkish bill; dark legs. Stocky siskin shape with notched tail.
thbspi1: Drab olive-green spiderhunter, plain olive above and yellowish-olive below with faint streaking, lacking strong markings; large dark eye. Distinctively short, thick, slightly decurved blackish bill; grayish legs. Stocky-billed for a sunbird relative.
thbvir: Plain olive-gray vireo, olive-brown above and pale yellowish-white below with yellowish flanks. Two whitish wing bars; dull yellow spectacles (eye-ring plus loral line) around a pale eye. Notably thick, hooked bluish bill; gray legs.
thbwar1: Large bulky warbler, warm brown above and buffy-whitish below with buff-washed flanks, lacking streaking. Plain face with faint pale lores and dark eye, no supercilium. Short, deep, thick-based pinkish bill; pinkish legs. Rounded tail.
thbwar2: Small warbler with gray head marked by black lateral crown stripes and a rufous-orange central crown stripe and supercilium; white eye-arcs. Olive-green back, bright yellow underparts. Thin dark bill; pinkish legs.
thiant1: Plump tailless antpitta, warm brown above and on crown, with whitish throat and belly and brown-streaked grayish-brown breast; pale buffy eyering and faint pale mark near eye. Stout dark bill; long pinkish legs. Rotund upright shape.
thitin1: Stocky ground bird with rounded body and tiny head, cinnamon-brown upperparts with dark barring on wings, paler grayish-buff underparts and rusty flanks; finely dark-vermiculated. Short slightly decurved bill; pinkish-gray legs.
thlant2: Round tailless antpitta, plain olive-brown above with a grayer crown and a small pale spot behind the eye; throat and belly whitish, breast and flanks washed olive-gray with diffuse streaking. Stout dark bill; long pinkish legs.
thlant3: Dull tailless antpitta, brown above including crown and wings, with a small white spot behind the eye and grayer lores; white throat and belly, breast with grayish-brown streaks, olive-gray flanks. Stout dark bill; long pinkish legs.
thlsch2: Plain medium-sized passerine, uniformly warm olive-brown above and slightly paler grayish-olive below, with no wing bars or strong markings; prominent large dark eye. Fairly stout, slightly hooked bill; grayish legs. Thrush-like upright posture.
thlsch3: Plain olive-brown bird, uniform warm brown above and paler grayish-olive below, lacking wing bars or face pattern; large conspicuous dark eye. Fairly heavy, slightly hooked vireo-like bill; dull gray legs. Upright, big-eyed thrush-like shape.
thlsch4: Plain brown passerine, mostly olive-brown with warmer rufous tones on the wings and crown, paler below; large dark eye, no wing bars or face markings. Fairly large hooked vireo-like bill; grayish legs. Upright thrush-like posture.
thlsch7: Plain olive bird, fairly uniform olive-green above and paler olive-yellowish below, lacking wing bars or strong facial markings; large dark eye. Stout slightly hooked bill; gray legs. Upright, big-eyed thrush-like shape.
thlsch8: Plain bird with olive-brown body and warmer rufescent-bronze tones on the wings and tail, paler grayish-olive below; large dark eye, no wing bars. Stout slightly hooked bill; grayish legs. Upright thrush-like posture.
thlwre1: Large brown wren, plain warm brown above and dull buffy-white below with sparse fine dusky spotting on the breast; indistinct pale supercilium and dark eyestripe. Long slightly decurved dark bill; grayish legs. Long broad tail.
thsfly2: Black-and-yellow flycatcher with black crown bordered by a bold white supercilium meeting across the forehead, white throat, and a black face mask. Olive-green back, bright yellow underparts. Short black bill; dark legs.
thswar1: Small olive warbler with a black-striped head: dark lateral crown stripes and dusky cheeks framing a buff-to-yellowish median crown and supercilium. Olive-green above, dull yellow underparts. Thin dark bill; pinkish legs.
thswar5: Small warbler with blackish head sides and crown stripes enclosing a buffy-orange central crown and supercilium, and a dark ear patch. Olive-green back, yellow underparts. Thin dark bill; pinkish legs.
thtjac1: Slender jacamar with glossy blackish-green upperparts, head, and breast, a white throat, and white lower belly. Long thin sharp black bill; very short legs with only three toes. Long graduated dark tail; dark eye.
thtray1: Small active bird with rufous-and-black head pattern: dark crown, broad rufous supercilium and face, blackish back streaked rufous, and whitish underparts. Rufous wings; distinctive very long spiny, frayed blackish tail. Fine dark bill; dark legs.
thwbel: Large white-headed cotinga: male has pure white head, neck, and breast and a chestnut-brown body, with three long worm-like black wattles dangling from the base of the bill. Heavy black bill; dark legs. Robust, big-headed shape.
tibfly3: Small flycatcher; male bright blue above (crown, back, tail) with vivid blue forehead and shoulder, blackish lores, an orange-rufous throat and breast, and white belly. Black bill; dark legs. Upright, large-eyed posture.
tibfly4: Small flycatcher; male dull blue above with a brighter blue brow and shoulder, blackish face, orange-rufous throat and breast fading to whitish belly. Black bill; pale legs. Very similar to Tickell's but duller, paler blue.
tiblar1: Large heavy-billed lark, sandy gray-brown above with dark streaking, and whitish underparts with a dark patch on each side of the upper breast. Bold whitish supercilium; large head. Thick pale yellowish bill; pale legs.
tibsno1: Large grayish gamebird, gray-brown above with fine vermiculation, whitish underparts boldly streaked black on the breast and belly. White face and throat with a gray collar; chestnut neck stripes. Pale bill; reddish legs.
ticdor1: Small tyrant flycatcher with bright yellow underparts and plain olive-brown upperparts, a soft rusty-tinged cap, and pale buffy wing bars. Plain face with dark eye; small pointed dark bill; dark legs. Slim, long-tailed shape.
ticthr1: Plain pale thrush; male uniform bluish-gray above and on the head, with a whitish throat finely streaked, gray breast, and white belly and undertail. Yellow bill, narrow yellow eye-ring, and yellow legs.
tigshr1: Compact shrike; male has a blue-gray crown and nape, broad black mask, and rich rufous-brown back, wings, and tail finely barred black. White underparts. Stout hooked black bill; dark legs. Short-tailed, big-headed.
tildac2: Small finch-like tanager; male entirely deep vibrant blue with slightly darker wings and tail, and dark eye. Short, stubby, unhooked black bill; black legs. Compact, tit-like body shape with a short tail.
timstu1: Tiny rotund warbler with an almost invisible stub tail, warm brown above and buffy-whitish below; pale buff supercilium and dark eyeline on a plain face. Fine dark bill; pinkish legs. Rounded, nearly tailless silhouette.
tincis1: Small wetland warbler, rufous-brown above with blackish streaking on the back and a rusty-tinged crown, and plain buffy-white underparts. Faint pale supercilium; rufous wing edges. Fine dark bill; pinkish legs. Short rounded tail.
tinhaw1: Very small compact accipiter, slate-gray above and on the head, with white underparts finely barred gray and a rufous morph variant. Faint pale supereyebrow; staring orange-yellow eye. Short yellow legs; small hooked bill. Tiny round-headed hawk.
titber1: Tit-like songbird; male has a black cap, throat-bib, and a large yellow cheek patch bordered black, with green back and wings spotted yellow, yellow belly, and blue-gray tail. Short stout dark bill; dark legs.
titman1: Tiny short-tailed manakin, plain olive-green above and slightly paler yellowish-olive below, with no bright markings; large dark eye on a plain face. Small pale bill; pale legs. Compact, rounded, very small body.
tobcat2: Stocky brown bowerbird, plain olive-brown above and buffy-white below heavily streaked and mottled dark brown, giving a scalloped look. Plain brownish face; pale eye. Short, heavy, slightly tooth-edged dark bill; gray legs.
tobhum1: Medium hummingbird, bronzy-green above and grayish-white below boldly spotted and streaked dark green, with a white postocular spot. Distinctive straight black bill with a hooked, finely toothed tip; dark legs.
tobwre1: Small slim wren, plain gray above and paler gray below, with a finely streaked or scaled grayish face and crown and a pale supercilium. Fine slightly decurved dark bill; pale legs. Long thin barred tail held loosely.
tocspa1: Large sparrow with a gray head boldly striped black: dark lateral crown stripes, pale median crown stripe and supercilium. Olive-brown upperparts; whitish underparts with a buffy-gray wash. Stout dark bill; pinkish legs.
toctou1: Huge black toucan with a white throat and breast, red undertail coverts, and a blue bare eye-ring. Enormous bright orange bill with a black spot at the tip. Black legs; bulky body and short tail.
todant1: Small antwren; male gray-headed with a black crown finely spotted white, a white supercilium, and a dark eyeline. Grayish-olive back, white underparts; black wings with white spots and bars. Thin dark bill; gray legs.
todmot1: Small dumpy motmot, green above with a rufous crown and a black face mask edged pale, and pale greenish-buff underparts. No long tail rackets; short squarish green tail. Short broad flat black bill; grayish legs.
toldov1: Plump ground-dove relative, warm brown above with a pinkish-gray crown and nape, and pale pinkish-buff underparts with a whitish throat. Pale forehead; dark eye with reddish orbital skin. Dark bill; reddish legs.
tomtit1: Tiny round robin; male has a black head and upperparts with a small white forehead spot, a white wing bar and wing patch, white outer-tail edges, and a clean white breast and belly. Small black bill; dark legs.
torcro2: All-black glossy crow with a slight bluish-purple sheen, slim build, and a fairly long deep black bill. White or pale bluish-white eye; black legs. Sleek, longer-billed shape than smaller crows.
torimp1: Large pale pigeon, mostly creamy-white with a black-spotted vent and undertail, black flight feathers, and a black tail tip. Whitish head with grayish hindneck. Pale gray bill with bulbous base; bluish-gray legs. Stout, big-bodied.
tortyr1: Small slim flycatcher, pale grey overall with a darker grey crown, whitish throat and pale grey underparts; short thin black bill, white eye, and dark wings and tail. Compact body, often pumps tail.
toubar1: Stocky, colourful barbet with thick pale bill; black face and throat, broad red breast and belly, grey collar and back, golden-yellow nape patch, and a small erect black crest. Olive-grey wings, blue-grey rump, dark legs.
tousun1: Small hummingbird with glittering green body, a brilliant rose-pink iridescent throat (gorget) bordered below by a buffy band, and dark forked tail. Short straight black bill; sexes differ, male most vivid.
towshe2: Medium shearwater, sharply two-toned: blackish-brown above and on the cap extending below the eye, clean white below including face and undertail edge; dark underwing borders. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
towsol: Slim grey thrush-relative, uniform soft grey overall with a bold white eye-ring, buffy wing patch visible in flight, and white outer tail feathers. Short black bill, long tail, upright posture.
towwar: Striking warbler with black throat and cheek-patch framed by bright yellow face, yellow supercilium and crescent under eye, olive-streaked back, yellow breast with black side streaks, and two white wing bars. White belly.
tremar2: Small swallow, glossy blue-black crown and back, grey-brown rump, dingy white underparts, and a pale forehead spot; short slightly forked tail. Tiny dark bill, dark legs, faint dusky throat streaking.
treswa: Sleek swallow, iridescent blue-green above and on the head to below the eye, pure white underparts with a clean demarcation; slightly forked tail, dark wings. Tiny black bill, dark legs.
tribla: Glossy black blackbird with a bright red shoulder patch bordered below by a broad white (not yellow) band; pointed black bill, black legs. Sleek body, the white epaulet edge distinctive from Red-winged.
tribrf2: Dark brushfinch with bright rufous-orange crown, olive-green upperparts, blackish face and throat, and yellowish-olive underparts. Stout conical black bill, dark legs; sometimes a small white wing speculum.
tribun1: Streaky bunting with boldly striped head: blackish crown with whitish central crown-stripe, white supercilium, dark eyestripe and white submoustachial stripe; rufous-brown back streaked black, white throat, rufous-streaked breast. Pinkish bill.
tricis1: Small warbler-like cisticola, rufous-brown crown, plain greyish-brown upperparts faintly streaked, warm buff flanks and whitish belly; short tail with dark subterminal band and pale tips. Fine pointed bill, pinkish legs.
trieup1: Small finch-like euphonia, glossy steel-blue-black upperparts, hood and throat, with a contrasting bright yellow forecrown, yellow breast and belly. Short stubby bill; female duller olive-green.
triher: Slender dark heron, blue-grey head, neck and back with a rufous-maroon neck stripe, white belly and white line down the foreneck; long thin yellowish bill, yellow lores, long greenish legs. Whitish plumes when breeding.
trimun: Small munia with glossy black head, neck and breast, rich chestnut-brown back and wings, and a black belly patch; the body chestnut with black hood and central belly. Heavy pale blue-grey conical bill.
trista1: Glossy starling, dark slate-black with strong purple and bronze-green iridescence, and a large rufous-orange wing patch obvious in flight; pale yellow-white eye, slender black bill and black legs.
tritap1: Small dark tapaculo, uniformly slaty blackish-grey overall, slightly paler grey below, with faint brownish flanks; short tail often cocked, very short fine black bill, sturdy brownish legs. Skulking, near-uniform plumage.
trobou1: Boldly pied bush-shrike, glossy blue-black above and white below, with a hidden white wing stripe; the underparts can show a faint pinkish wash. Stout black hooked bill, dark legs.
trobou2: Black-and-white bush-shrike, glossy blue-black upperparts and head with a white wing stripe, and clean white underparts often tinged pink; heavy black hooked bill, blackish legs. Very like Tropical Boubou.
trogna1: Tiny slender gnatcatcher, blue-grey above with a black cap reaching the eye in the male, white underparts, and a long black tail with white outer feathers; thin black bill, white eye-arc, dark legs.
trogna2: Small gnatcatcher, blue-grey upperparts, white underparts, and a long black-and-white tail; male shows a black cap above a prominent white supercilium. Thin black bill, dark legs, slim active body.
trokin: Robust grey-and-yellow kingbird, grey head with a dusky mask, pale grey throat and breast grading to bright yellow belly, olive-grey back, and a notched brown tail. Heavy black bill, dark legs.
tromoc: Slim grey mockingbird, plain grey above and paler grey-white below, with a faint dark eyeline, white wing bars and white-cornered long tail. Slender slightly decurved black bill, long dark legs.
tropar: Tiny warbler, blue-grey above with a greenish-yellow back patch, no eye-ring; bright yellow throat and breast washed orange, yellow belly, and two white wing bars. Black mask through the eye in males.
tropew3: Small dull pewee, grey-brown above with a slightly peaked crown, pale greyish throat and breast, whitish belly with faint yellowish wash, and faint pale wing bars. Dark bill with pale orange base below, dark legs.
troshe5: Small shearwater, blackish-brown above and white below with a clean face demarcation around the eye, white undertail coverts and dark underwing edges. Slim dark bill, pinkish legs; compact build.
trsowl: Small grey screech-owl with prominent ear-tufts, finely vermiculated greyish-brown plumage with bold black streaks below and a black-edged facial disc; yellow eyes, greenish-yellow bill. Rufous morph also occurs.
trspet: Medium dark storm-petrel, sooty brownish-black overall with a pale greyish diagonal bar across the upperwing coverts, slightly forked tail and pale legs trailing. Short black bill with tubenose, dark feet.
trufin2: Stocky pale finch, sandy buff-grey overall washed pink on the breast and rump in breeding males; large stubby pinkish-orange (red) bill, dark wings with pink-edged feathers, pale pinkish legs.
truhor1: Large black-and-white hornbill, glossy black body and head, white belly and white-tipped tail, with a tall greyish casque atop a heavy bill; bare pinkish skin around the eye and throat, dark legs.
truman1: Iridescent black bird-of-paradise relative, glossy blue-green and purple sheen overall, with elongated wiry head plumes (twin tufts) and a long graduated tail; stout black bill, dark legs, bright red eye.
truswa: Huge all-white swan with a long straight neck; entirely black bill with a straight culmen meeting the eye (red border on lower mandible), black facial skin, and black legs. Largest North American waterfowl.
truter: Pale graceful tern, white overall with a pale grey back and wings, and a distinctive black mask through and behind each eye (not a full cap); yellow-tipped black bill, orange-yellow legs, deeply forked tail.
tsctap1: Small slate-grey tapaculo, uniform dark grey overall with slightly browner flanks and a paler grey belly; short cocked tail, very fine pointed black bill, sturdy pale brownish legs. Skulking, near-uniform.
tubmot1: Spectacular motmot, turquoise-blue brow and wing-edges, rufous crown and back, black face mask and black throat stripe edged blue, green-blue underparts; long tail with bare shafts ending in racket tips.
tucpar1: Green amazon parrot with a red forecrown and lores, red bend of wing, and yellowish-green underparts; scalloped green plumage, dark-tipped feathers, and red in the wing speculum. Horn-coloured bill, grey legs.
tufant1: Large dark antshrike, male sooty black overall with a shaggy pointed black crest; heavy hooked black bill, dark grey legs. Robust body; female rufous-brown with the crest, paler below.
tuffly: Small flycatcher with a prominent pointed crest, warm cinnamon-buff underparts and face, brownish-olive back, and rufous wing-bar edgings; small black bill, dark legs. Compact, large-headed shape.
tufjay1: Striking large jay, deep blue body and wings, blackish face and throat, white-tipped tail and white belly, and a bushy upright dark blue-black crest; bold white spots above and below the eye. Black bill, dark legs.
tuftit: Small grey songbird with a pointed grey crest, plain grey upperparts, whitish underparts with rusty-buff flanks, and a black forehead patch above the bill; large black eyes, short black bill, dark legs.
tui1: Glossy dark honeyeater, blackish plumage with green and bronze iridescence and lacy white filamentous neck feathers, plus two distinctive white tufts at the throat; slightly decurved black bill, dark legs.
tuipar1: Small green parakeet, bright green overall with a yellow forecrown and orange-yellow chin, blue in the flight feathers; pale horn bill, pinkish legs. Short pointed tail, compact body.
tumspa1: Streaky sparrow, grey head with bold dark-bordered rufous crown stripes and a rufous eyeline, grey nape, brown back streaked black, plain grey-white underparts; pale conical bill, pinkish legs. Rufous shoulder patch.
tuqdov1: Plump terrestrial dove, rich rufous-brown upperparts with purple-violet gloss on the nape and back, pale grey forehead, buff-white underparts, and a pale facial stripe below a dark cheek; reddish legs, dark bill.
turjay1: Brilliant turquoise-blue jay with a black mask around the eyes and across the forehead, blue crown and throat, and slightly paler blue underparts; stout black bill, black legs. Medium-sized, vivid all over.
turpar1: Small bright parrot, turquoise-blue face and throat, green crown and back, yellow underparts, and a deep blue shoulder with a red wing patch in males; pale bill, grey legs. Long tapered tail.
turtan1: Vivid tanager, turquoise-blue head, breast and rump, black back and wings edged blue, and a bright yellow belly; black mask through the eye, stout dark bill, grey legs. Small and colourful.
turvul: Large dark soaring vulture, blackish-brown body with silvery flight feathers below, a small bare red head, and an ivory-tipped hooked bill; long broad wings held in a shallow V, pinkish legs, long tail.
tutspi1: Small slim furnariid with a rufous crown and a pointed forehead tuft, greyish-brown upperparts, pale buff-grey underparts, and a long graduated rufous-edged tail; fine pointed bill, pale legs.
tuttyr1: Tiny tyrant with a wispy black forecrest, black-and-white streaked head, staring pale yellow eye, grey-brown back, whitish underparts streaked dark on the breast, and two pale wing bars; tiny black bill.
twbplo1: Small plover with sandy-brown upperparts and white underparts marked by two black breast bands; white forehead and supercilium, blackish mask and crown patch. Short black bill and dull pinkish-grey legs.
twbwar1: Small warbler with olive-green upperparts and bright yellow underparts; bold white supercilium contrasting with a blackish eyestripe and dark crown sides. Thin dark bill and pale legs.
twwbop1: Crow-sized bird-of-paradise; male glossy black above with iridescent green-margined breast shield, brilliant lemon-yellow flanks bearing twelve curling wire-like filaments curving forward. Bright pink legs and long pale bill; bare yellow-green face skin.
tylvan1: Medium vanga with a glossy black hood covering head and throat, narrow white nape collar, olive-brown upperparts and buffy to rufous-washed whitish underparts. Stout blackish bill and dark legs.
tylwar1: Slim leaf-warbler, greyish-olive above and whitish below; long bold whitish supercilium, dark eyestripe, no wing bars. Notably long fine dark bill with pale base and dark legs.
tyrmet1: Tiny hummingbird with glittering green body; male shows a brilliant violet-red (tyrian) gorget and a coppery-bronze, slightly forked tail. Short straight black bill.
tyrwoo1: Plain dark woodcreeper, uniformly dusky brown with a faintly rufous-tinged tail and wings; lacks streaking or spotting. Straight dark bill and grey legs; subtly paler greyish throat.
ugawow1: Small olive-green woodland warbler, brighter yellow on belly and supercilium; faint pale eyering, dusky eyestripe and a hint of yellow on wing edges. Thin dark bill, pale legs.
ultfly1: Small flycatcher; male deep ultramarine-blue above with white underparts, a long white supercilium and white patches on neck sides. Females duller brown. Small black bill, dark legs.
ultgro1: Stocky finch-like bird; male entirely deep ultramarine-blue, brighter on forehead and shoulders, appearing blackish in poor light. Heavy conical blackish bill and dark legs.
undant1: Large plump antpitta, olive-brown above; greyish head and pale underparts boldly scalloped with fine dark crescentic barring (undulations). Stout dark bill, long pinkish legs and very short tail.
undant2: Large antshrike; male blackish overall with fine pale undulating barring across body and wings, and a bushy crest. Heavy hooked black bill and grey legs; female browner with barring.
undtin1: Plump ground bird with rounded body and small head; greyish-brown plumage finely vermiculated and barred (undulated) darker, paler below. Slightly decurved bill, dull yellowish legs.
uniant1: Small antwren; male uniform slaty-grey overall, slightly paler below, often with a concealed white interscapular patch. Thin dark bill and grey legs.
uniant2: Antshrike; male uniform dark slate-grey throughout with a slightly paler grey belly, lacking wing markings. Stout hooked black bill, grey legs; female warm rufous-brown.
unibla2: Medium blackbird (icterid); male entirely glossy black with subtle bluish sheen, dark eyes. Sharp pointed conical black bill and black legs; females duller brownish-streaked.
unicra1: Plain rail-like crake, rich chestnut-brown above and cinnamon-rufous below, slightly paler throat; lacks barring. Greenish-yellow bill and bright coral-red legs.
unifin1: Small finch; male uniform slaty blue-grey overall, marginally paler below. Short conical bill with pale base and dull legs; female warm brown and faintly streaked.
unijay1: Medium jay, entirely deep blue with a darker blackish face and forehead; no crest or white markings. Stout black bill and black legs.
unitap1: Small dark tapaculo, uniform dark slate-grey overall, sometimes faintly browner on flanks; cocked short tail. Small black bill and pinkish-brown legs.
unitre1: Foliage-gleaner-like furnariid, uniform dull olive-brown with a slightly rufescent tail and faint paler throat; lacks streaking. Stout straight dark bill and greyish legs.
uniwoo1: Large robust woodcreeper, uniform warm rufous-brown overall with brighter rufous wings and tail, lacking spots or streaks. Heavy straight reddish bill and dark legs.
unstit1: Small tit-tyrant, greyish-olive above and pale yellowish below; plain face with dark eye, lacking bold streaking. Short dark bill, slim build with a longish tail and dark legs.
uplant1: Antshrike; male slaty-grey with blackish crown and white-tipped wing coverts forming faint bars; concealed white back patch. Heavy hooked black bill, grey legs; female rufous-brown.
uplgoo1: Large goose; male white head, neck and underparts with finely barred black-and-white flanks and grey-black back, white belly. Female cinnamon-brown with barred flanks; both have pink-black bill and yellow-and-black legs.
uplpip1: Robust pipit, heavily streaked dark brown and buff above; buffy breast boldly streaked dark, paler belly. Stout pale-based bill and long pinkish legs; fairly long-tailed.
uplsan: Slim, small-headed sandpiper with long thin neck and long tail; mottled buff-brown above, buffy breast with fine dark streaking, plain belly, large dark eye. Short straight bill, yellow legs.
upmtap1: Small tapaculo, mainly dark grey, slightly browner on belly and flanks with rusty barring on lower flanks and rump. Short cocked tail, small black bill and brownish legs.
uswowl1: Small compact owl with rounded head lacking ear tufts; warm brown upperparts, buffy-brown underparts with faint streaking, plain buffy facial disc and dark eyes. Pale bill, feathered legs.
vabtyr1: Tiny tyrannulet, olive-green above with two buff-yellow wing bars; variegated face pattern of yellowish and dusky markings, pale eyering. Small dark bill, pale legs.
vadvan1: Large heavy-billed vanga; male has black hood, blackish-grey upperparts and clean white underparts, female only a black cap. Massive pale bluish-grey bill and dark legs.
vahsun1: Tiny sunbird; male iridescent metallic green-blue head, back and throat with a purplish breast band and bright yellow belly. Fine decurved black bill; female olive above, yellowish below.
vanfly1: Small monarch flycatcher; male glossy blue-black above with a rufous-orange throat and breast grading to whitish belly, or rufous-toned overall. Broad pale blue-grey bill, dark legs.
varant1: Variable antshrike; male slaty-grey with a black crown and white-spotted wing bars, white interscapular patch. Stout hooked black bill, grey legs; female rufous-brown above, buffy below.
varant2: Large antpitta, rich brown above with a greyish crown; whitish underparts heavily streaked and scalloped dark brown, with a black-and-white moustachial pattern. Stout bill, long pinkish legs, very short tail.
varbun: Small finch; male richly multicolored with purplish-red nape, blue crown and rump, reddish underparts and dark face. Stout grey conical bill, dark legs; female plain warm brown.
varcha1: Large slender guan-like bird, olive-brown above with a rufous lower belly and bright rufous primaries; bluish bare facial skin and reddish dewlap. Short dark bill, long broad tail, grey legs.
varfai1: Tiny long-tailed fairywren; breeding male has bright blue crown, ear-coverts and back with chestnut shoulders, black throat and breast, whitish belly, blue cocked tail. Fine black bill, brown legs.
varfai5: Tiny fairywren; breeding male shows sky-blue crown and ear-coverts, blue-violet back, chestnut shoulders, black throat and breast band, white belly and long cocked blue tail. Fine black bill.
varfly: Streaky flycatcher, brown above with pale-edged wing feathers; whitish underparts heavily streaked dusky, bold whitish supercilium, dark mask and concealed yellow crown patch. Dark bill, dark legs.
vargos1: Slim accipiter; grey above with a darker head, finely rufous-and-white barred underparts, longish barred tail. Hooked black-tipped bill with yellow cere and long yellow legs.
varind1: Tiny finch-like indigobird; male glossy blue-black overall with a slight greenish sheen. Whitish or pinkish bill and pinkish-red legs; female sparrow-like, streaky buff-brown.
varlau1: Boldly patterned laughingthrush; rufous-and-grey body with a black-bordered grey crown, black throat, white-tipped tail and contrasting variegated grey, white and chestnut wing pattern. Dark bill, pinkish legs.
varoys1: Large stocky black oystercatcher, entirely sooty-black plumage (some intermediates pied). Long stout bright orange-red bill, red eye-ring and thick pinkish legs.
varpit2: Medium-sized bird, variable but typically rich rufous-orange head, breast and underparts with a black or dark cap, blackish wings and tail. Stout black bill and dark grey legs.
varsch1: Plump drab manakin-relative, almost entirely cinnamon-rufous, brighter on rump and tail, paler below, with variable grey around the eye and face. Short dark bill, dull greyish legs.
varsee3: Small seedeater; male glossy black above and on hood with a white wing speculum and white belly patch, variable black-and-white underparts. Stout conical bill; female plain olive-brown.
varsit8: Tiny nuthatch-like bird, grey-brown above with streaked underparts, variable head pattern (dark cap or face); bold orange-buff wing patch and white tail corners in flight. Short upturned bill, orange legs.
varsol1: Slim thrush-relative, mostly slaty blue-grey with a paler grey face and underparts, white eye-arcs and a thin black mask. Bright orange bill and orange legs.
varsun2: Small sunbird; male metallic green head and back with a violet throat, broad yellow breast and belly, and reddish breast band, plus yellow pectoral tufts. Fine decurved black bill; female plain yellowish.
varthr: Robin-sized thrush; male slate-blue above with rich burnt-orange throat, breast, supercilium and wing bars, plus a bold black breast band crossing the orange underparts. Dark eyeline through orange face; dark bill, pinkish legs.
vartin1: Plump, rounded tinamou with small head and short tail; dark rufous-brown overall, the back and wings narrowly barred buff and black, head and neck blackish, throat paler. Slender slightly downcurved grey bill, greyish legs.
vartit1: Small tit with creamy-white forehead and cheeks contrasting a black crown, throat and nape; nape band buff. Bluish-grey back and wings, rich chestnut breast and belly. Short black bill, blue-grey legs.
vartit3: Small Taiwan tit like Varied Tit but underparts deep uniform chestnut; creamy-white face and forehead, black crown, throat and nape, bluish-grey upperparts. Short black bill, blue-grey legs.
vartit4: Small dark tit; creamy forehead and cheeks, black crown, throat and nape, slaty-grey back and wings, and dull rufous-chestnut underparts (darker, duskier than Varied Tit). Short black bill, grey legs.
vartri1: Small cuckooshrike; male glossy black above with bold white supercilium and white wing edgings, white-tipped tail; underparts white, finely barred dark on flanks and belly. Black bill, dark legs.
vaspar1: Large stocky parrot, entirely sooty grey-brown plumage; broad pale bare eye-ring and pale bill (horn to whitish), greyish legs. Heavy build with short squarish tail.
vauswi: Tiny cigar-shaped swift with stubby square tail; sooty grey-brown overall, paler greyish-white on throat, chin and rump. Long swept-back wings, very short black bill, dark legs.
vddhor1: Small hornbill, whitish head and underparts, dark grey-brown back and wings spotted white, black tail. Long downcurved bill bright red-orange (male) or blackish (female); bare pinkish skin around eye, dark legs.
veeowl1: Very large owl, pale brownish-grey finely vermiculated above and below; broad rounded head with ear tufts, and distinctive pink eyelids. Dark eyes, pale facial disc with dark border, feathered greyish legs.
veery: Small thrush, uniform warm reddish-brown above; whitish underparts with soft buffy wash on breast bearing faint, blurry brownish spots. Faint greyish face, indistinct eye-ring, pale lower mandible, pinkish legs.
vefeup1: Small stocky euphonia; male glossy steel-blue above with a small yellow forecrown patch, dark throat, and rich yellow breast and belly. Dark underside of tail. Short stubby black bill, dark legs.
vefgra1: Slim, long-tailed medium blackbird, entirely glossy black with purplish-blue iridescence; diagnostic patch of short, dense bristly feathers on the forehead. Dark eyes, fairly short pointed black bill, black legs.
vefnut1: Small compact nuthatch, violet-blue upperparts, black forehead and lores, and a short black eyestripe; pale buffy-white underparts. Striking red bill and yellow eye, with whitish throat; short legs.
vehpar1: Tiny short-tailed parrot, bright grass-green overall with a vivid red rump and red bill; pale blue throat patch (male) and turquoise wash to underwings. Greenish-yellow undertail, pale legs.
venfly1: Medium Myiarchus flycatcher; grey-brown above, pale grey throat and breast grading to yellow belly, with rufous edges in wings and tail. Slight bushy crest, blackish bill, dark legs.
ventro1: Large striking oriole-like icterid; brilliant orange body with black head, throat, back, wings and tail, and white wing patch. Bold pale blue bare eye-ring, pale eye, pointed black bill, dark legs.
venwoq1: Plump, round-bodied quail; dark brown above mottled and barred, rich rufous-chestnut underparts, with a tawny throat and dark crown. Short crest, stout dark bill, dark legs.
vepcor1: Medium hummingbird looking blackish in poor light but flashing deep purple crown and belly, turquoise sides, greenish back, and rufous underwings; bright white tail flashes. Short straight black bill, small white spot behind eye.
vercar1: Cardinal with long pointed crest; male entirely brilliant scarlet-vermilion, slightly darker on back. Heavy conical pale ivory bill, dark legs. Females greyer with reddish crest and wings.
verdin: Tiny grey songbird with a yellow head and throat and a small chestnut shoulder patch; pale grey body, slightly darker above. Short sharp black conical bill, dark legs; small slim build.
vereme1: Small hummingbird, glistening green above and on throat and breast, with a contrasting white belly; tail blue-green. Bill straight, lower mandible pinkish with dark tip, dark legs.
verfly: Small flycatcher; male brilliant scarlet-red crown, crest and underparts contrasting a blackish-brown mask, back, wings and tail. Thin black bill, dark legs. Females greyish-brown with streaked breast and pinkish belly.
verfly4: Small flycatcher entirely bright turquoise-verditer blue, slightly brighter on forehead; black lores form a small dark mask. Underparts paler blue. Short broad black bill, dark legs.
verhum1: One of the world's smallest birds; dull metallic green above, dingy greyish-white below with green-flecked flanks. Short straight black bill, dark legs; tiny, with a short slightly forked tail.
versco2: Small screech-owl, mottled grey-brown and rufous-brown with fine streaking and vermiculation; short ear tufts, dark eyes, and a pale-bordered facial disc. Pale bill, feathered greyish legs.
vesowl: Small screech-owl, finely vermiculated greyish-brown above and below with dark streaking; short ear tufts, dark brown eyes, pale facial disc with dark rim. Pale greenish-yellow bill, feathered legs.
vesspa: Streaky brown sparrow with a whitish eye-ring, a small chestnut shoulder patch, and white outer tail feathers conspicuous in flight. Streaked breast and back, pale underparts. Pale pinkish bill, pinkish legs.
vibhum1: Small hummingbird; male glittering green above and on throat with a brilliant violet-blue belly and breast; blue-black forked tail. Bill straight, lower mandible reddish-pink with dark tip, dark legs.
vibsta1: Myna with whitish head and a thin black bandit mask, medium-grey back, grey-pink underparts, pale grey rump and white tail tip; white wing patches flash in flight. Bicolored orange-and-black bill, yellow legs.
vibsta2: Small starling; male glossy iridescent violet-purple above, on head and breast, with a sharply contrasting pure white belly and undertail. Yellow eye, slender black bill, dark legs. Females streaky brown.
vichum: Medium hummingbird; bright violet-blue crown contrasting clean white underparts, bronze-green back. Long straight bill red with a black tip, dark legs; greenish-bronze tail.
vicrif1: Bird-of-paradise; male velvety black with intense iridescent metallic green-blue throat shield and crown, and a glossy green central tail. Long downcurved black bill, dark legs. Females brown, barred below with pale supercilium.
vicwoo2: Medium hummingbird; male shining green crown, throat and breast with a deep violet-blue belly and central belly patch; green back, blue-black deeply forked tail. Straight black bill, dark legs.
viebar1: Plump short-tailed barbet; bright red head with red-and-white mottled neck and breast, dark brown upperparts marked by a yellow back stripe, yellowish underparts with dark flank spots. Thick dark grey bill, grey legs.
viecut1: Bulky thrush-like babbler; male blue-grey crown and wing panel, black mask, orange-rufous back and rump, and white underparts boldly barred black. Heavy hooked dark bill, reddish legs. Female duller, browner.
viegre2: Stocky finch with a heavy conical pearl-grey bill; male black head, back, wings and forked tail with a narrow bright yellow collar and yellow throat, shoulders, breast and belly. Pale flesh-coloured legs.
viewea3: Medium weaver; male entirely glossy jet black plumage with a contrasting pale yellow or whitish eye. Stout conical black bill, dark legs. Female olive-brown above, yellowish below.
vifbri1: Medium robust hummingbird; male glittering green overall with a brilliant violet-blue forehead and a small glittering green throat patch; blue-black forked tail. Straight black bill, dark legs.
vigswa: Small swallow, glossy bronze-green crown and back with violet-purple rump and wings; clean white underparts, white extending up onto the face and sides of the rump. Tiny black bill, dark legs, short notched tail.
vihhum1: Small hummingbird; male glittering violet-blue head and throat contrasting bright green back and greyish underparts; small white spot behind eye. Short straight black bill, dark legs.
vilind: Tiny finch; breeding male entirely glossy blue-black plumage with a bright red bill and red or orange-red legs. Compact, short-tailed. Female streaky brown with pale bill.
viltap1: Small dark tapaculo, uniform blackish-grey overall, slightly paler grey throat and belly; faint brownish wash on flanks and rump. Short slender black bill, dark legs; short cocked tail, rounded body.
vilwea1: Medium weaver; breeding male golden-yellow with a black hood (face and throat) and chestnut nape band, black-and-yellow mottled back. Pale eye, stout black conical bill, pinkish legs. Female dull olive-yellow.
vimwea1: Small weaver; breeding male golden-yellow with a black face mask and throat, a chestnut-orange wash across the crown and breast, and yellow-olive streaked back. Red eye, black conical bill, pinkish legs.
vindov1: Slim pale dove; soft pinkish-vinaceous head, breast and underparts, greyish-brown back and wings, with a narrow black half-collar on the hindneck. Dark bill, reddish legs; long tail with white-tipped outer feathers.
vinpar1: Large green amazon parrot with a vinaceous-purple wash over the breast and belly; red forehead and lores, scalloped neck feathers, blue cheeks, and red wing speculum. Pale horn bill, greyish legs.
viocuc1: Small slim cuckoo; male glossy iridescent violet-purple over head, breast and upperparts, with a white lower belly; orange-yellow eye-ring and red eye. Yellow downcurved bill, dark legs.
vioeup1: Small stocky euphonia; male glossy violet-blue above and on throat and head, with bright golden-yellow forecrown, breast and belly. Short stubby black bill, dark legs.
viojay1: Large jay, entirely deep violet-blue plumage, slightly blacker on the face and throat; pale violet wash on crown and nape. Dark eye, stout black bill, black legs; long broad tail.
viosab1: Large hummingbird with brilliant deep violet-purple head, throat, breast and back, blending to greenish on the lower belly; long, slightly downcurved black bill and white spot behind the eye. Blue-black tail with broad white outer corners; flattened, thickened black wing shafts.
viotro2: Small trogon; male has glossy green back and breast, violet-blue head and nape, and bright yellow belly, separated by a narrow white chest band. Bare yellow eye-ring, pale blue bill, finely barred black-and-white underside of the squared tail.
viotro3: Small Amazonian trogon; male glossy green above and on breast with violet-blue head, lemon-yellow belly and a white breast band. Pale blue bill, complete yellow eye-ring, and densely vermiculated black-and-white undertail.
viqdov1: Plump terrestrial dove with deep violaceous-purple iridescent mantle and nape, contrasting pale whitish face and throat, and clean white-to-buff underparts. Reddish bill base, dark red legs, rounded body and short tail.
virmet1: Small high-Andean hummingbird, overall dark green with a glittering green throat patch (gorget); shining bronzy-green to coppery iridescence on the back and a forked, blue-green to bronzy tail. Short straight black bill.
virrai2: Slender rail with rusty-orange underparts and breast, grey cheeks, brown-streaked olive-brown back, and black-and-white barred flanks. Long, slightly downcurved reddish bill, reddish-orange legs, short cocked tail.
virwar: Small grey warbler with a yellow rump and yellow patch on the breast, a bold white eye-ring, and a rufous crown patch. Underparts mostly whitish-grey, with yellow undertail coverts; thin pointed dark bill, dark legs.
viswar1: Plump warbler-like bird, olive-brown to greyish above, with a bright yellow throat and breast grading to olive flanks and rufous-tinged undertail. Distinctive white eye-ring and grey face; fine pointed dark bill.
vitpar1: Tiny long-tailed parrotbill, warm cinnamon-rufous crown and wings contrasting with greyish-brown back; pale buff-grey underparts with faint vinous wash on the throat and breast. Stubby pale conical bill, long graduated tail.
vitsyl1: Spectacular hummingbird; male has glittering green crown and throat, blue-green breast and dark back, with an extremely long, deeply forked iridescent violet-blue tail. Short straight black bill.
vivnil3: Stocky flycatcher; male deep blue above with a black face and lores, brilliant blue forehead and shoulders, and rich orange-rufous throat, breast and flanks fading to whitish belly. Short black bill, dark legs.
waicis3: Small streaky cisticola, warm brown above with heavy dark streaking on the crown and back, plain rufous-brown rump, and buffy-white unstreaked underparts. Fine pointed bill, pinkish legs, rounded tail tipped buff and black.
waldra1: Large glossy black ibis with iridescent green-and-purple sheen, a bare red face and crown, and a shaggy ruff of pointed neck plumes. Long decurved red bill and red legs.
walhae1: Small dark hawk-eagle, blackish-brown above, with a short erect crest; white underparts boldly streaked black on the breast and barred rufous on belly and thighs. Yellow legs, dark-banded tail.
walsta1: Slender glossy starling; male blackish with deep blue-green and purple iridescence and bright chestnut-rufous primary patch visible in the wing. Slim dark bill, dark eye, long graduated tail.
walsta2: Bizarre bird-of-paradise; male olive-brown with an iridescent emerald-green breast shield and violet crown, plus two pairs of long white wire-like 'standard' plumes arching from the shoulders. Pale greenish bill, yellow gape.
wanalb1: Huge albatross, mostly white with black wingtips and trailing edges; back whitens with age. Massive pale pink bill and pinkish legs; very long, narrow white wings with fine black flecking.
wantat1: Medium grey shorebird, uniform slate-grey above and on head and breast, with white belly and grey-barred flanks in breeding plumage; white supercilium and dark eyestripe. Straight dark bill, short yellow legs.
wardor1: Tiny tyrant flycatcher, olive-brown above with darker wings, and entirely bright lemon-yellow underparts. Faint pale lores, short dark bill, dark legs; plain face and rounded short tail.
wartro1: Trogon; male deep pinkish-red below with a slaty-pink to maroon back, dark grey head and breast, and finely vermiculated grey wing panel. Pale blue eye-ring and bill base; white-tipped undertail.
warvir: Plain grey-olive vireo, greyish above and whitish below with faint yellow wash on the flanks; pale whitish supercilium and dusky eyeline, no wing bars. Short stout grey bill, grey legs.
warwhe1: Small olive-green songbird with a bold white eye-ring, yellow-green crown and throat, and pale greyish-white belly. Olive flanks, yellow undertail coverts; fine pointed dark bill, pale legs.
watant1: Plump antpitta, warm brown above with a greyish face and a pale buff loral spot; whitish underparts boldly streaked and scaled dark on the breast and flanks. Stout pale-based bill, long pinkish legs, nearly tailless.
waterc1: Large dark rail; breeding male slate-grey to blackish overall, with a tall red frontal horn and red shield extending up the forehead. Yellowish-green bill, long greenish legs, short tail.
watgua1: Large dark guan, glossy blackish-brown overall with a slight greenish sheen, and a small bright yellow-orange wattle hanging from the throat. Bluish-grey bill, reddish legs, long broad tail.
watibi1: Dark ibis, blackish-brown overall with a glossy green-purple wing sheen, a conspicuous white shoulder/wing patch, and a small whitish throat wattle. Grey-streaked neck, decurved dark bill, dark legs.
watjac1: Striking jacana, deep chestnut-rufous body with a black head, neck and breast, bright yellow flight feathers, and a red frontal shield and wattles over a yellow bill. Greenish-grey legs with extremely long toes.
watkne1: Cryptic, large-eyed wader, sandy grey-brown finely vermiculated above and on the breast, with a whitish belly and a pale wing panel bordered by dark bars. Large yellow eyes, black-tipped yellow bill, greenish legs.
watlap1: Large lapwing, brown above with a black crown, white forehead and a long yellow facial wattle; whitish neck, brown breast, and white belly with a black band across the lower belly. Yellow legs, red-and-yellow bill.
watsta1: Pale grey starling with contrasting black wings and tail and a white rump; breeding male develops bare yellow facial skin and black hanging wattles on the head. Pale bill, dark legs.
wavwoo5: Rufous-and-buff woodpecker; warm cinnamon-rufous overall heavily barred black across the back, wings and underparts, with a bushy reddish-chestnut crest. Pale ivory bill, dark legs; males show a red malar stripe.
wawduc1: Upright long-necked whistling-duck, rich chestnut-brown overall with bold dark spotting on the buff flanks, a dark crown and hindneck, and a pale face and foreneck. Dark grey bill and legs.
wbbfly1: Small flycatcher; male deep indigo-blue above and on the throat and breast, with a contrasting clean white belly and undertail. Black lores, short black bill, pale legs; female duller brown above.
wbbwea1: Large heavy weaver, sooty-black overall with white-edged feathers giving a scaly look, and white flanks/underwing visible in flight. Massive whitish to pale conical bill, dark legs.
wbctyr1: Small chat-tyrant, grey-brown above with a bold white supercilium, dark grey face and breast, and a whitish to pale buff belly. Two pale wing bars, short black bill, dark legs.
wbgbir1: Large grey go-away-bird with a tall pointed grey crest, ashy-grey head, neck and upper breast, and a contrasting white belly. Long grey tail, stout greenish-yellow bill, dark legs.
wbgtyr1: Slim ground-tyrant, sandy grey-brown above and whitish below, with a clean white supercilium and dark lores. Blackish wings and tail with white edges; thin black bill, long black legs.
wbhori1: Black-and-yellow oriole; bright golden-yellow body with a black head, throat and upper breast, and black-and-yellow wings. Reddish to dull-pink stout bill, dark eye, blue-grey legs.
wbopar1: Dazzling small bird-of-paradise; male crimson back, yellow nape, green breast shield, and a bare turquoise-blue crown crossed by black lines, with two curled violet central tail wires. Pale bill, blue legs.
wbptyr1: Minute pygmy-tyrant, olive-green above with a rounded head and large staring pale eye-ring, and clean white underparts. Short blackish bill, pale legs; tiny stub of a tail.
wbrcha2: Robin-chat; bright orange-rufous underparts, rump and outer tail, slate-grey back and wings, and a black head with a bold white supercilium. Slim black bill, dark legs.
wbsbab1: Long-tailed scimitar-babbler, rufous-brown above with a grey crown, a bold white supercilium and a dark eyestripe, and white throat and breast. Long decurved yellowish bill, greyish legs.
wbseag1: Large eagle, pale grey above with a white head, neck and underparts; broad black flight feathers and a short wedge-shaped white tail with a dark base. Heavy grey hooked bill, pale grey legs.
wbswea1: Sparrow-like weaver, brown above with a bold white supercilium and a conspicuous white rump, and whitish underparts. Brown-streaked wings with pale wing bars; stout conical bill, pinkish legs.
wbtfan1: Black fantail with a contrasting white throat and breast and white spots on the wing coverts; broad fan-shaped black tail. Slim black bill, dark legs; rounded body.
wbtspi1: Small spinetail, brown above with a streaked rufous crown, a bold white supercilium, and a whitish throat with a small dark patch. Buffy-brown underparts, rufous wing patch, long pointed graduated brown tail.
wbtwar1: Tiny long-tailed warbler-like bird; male has a rufous-chestnut crown, bold white supercilium, lilac-pink throat and breast, and bluish-grey wings and tail. Olive-grey back, fine black bill, dark legs.
wbweye1: Small olive-green white-eye with a neat white eye-ring; greyish-white belly with a contrasting yellow throat and yellow undertail coverts. Olive upperparts, fine pointed dark bill, pale legs.
wbwwre1: Small short-tailed wren, rich chestnut-brown above with a bold black-and-white streaked face, a white throat and breast, and grey-brown flanks. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs, cocked tail.
wcfgle1: Foliage-gleaner, warm olive-brown above with a rufous-tinged tail, a pale buff throat and a white collar/breast contrasting with brownish flanks. Faint pale supercilium, straight pale-based bill, greyish legs.
wcptit1: Small tit-like bird with grey upperparts and pale whitish-grey underparts; whitish crown and forehead, black mask through the eye, and a chestnut-tinged back. Short conical grey bill and dark legs.
wcrcha1: Large robin-chat with a bright white crown, black face and sides of head, slaty-black back and wings, and rich orange underparts and rump. Long blackish tail, blackish bill and dark legs.
wcttyr1: Tiny tody-flycatcher with rufous-chestnut crown and nape, striking white cheeks and face, black throat and ear-surround, olive-green back and yellowish underparts. Fine dark bill, pale legs; very small with upright stance.
webhum1: Small green hummingbird with a short straight black bill, glittering green upperparts, white belly and flanks; male shows iridescent violet-blue throat patch and a green breast with white sides of neck. Short dark legs.
webwoo1: Small short-tailed woodcreeper, plain rich brown overall with rufous wings and tail and faint buff streaking on breast; short wedge-shaped pale-tipped bill. Pale buff throat, dark legs, climbing posture.
weclew1: Greenish leaf-warbler with grey-green upperparts, whitish underparts, a pale yellowish median crown-stripe bordered by dark lateral stripes, long whitish supercilium, and two pale wingbars. Dark thin bill, pinkish legs.
wecsun1: Sunbird with male showing metallic green crown, scarlet-red breast and back, olive-yellow belly and flanks, and dark wings and tail. Long decurved black bill, dark legs; female duller olive-yellow.
wectan1: Large dark tanager-like bird, slaty-grey above and on head with blackish face, white throat and central belly, and grey breast; rufous-tinged flanks. Stout black bill, dark legs, long tail.
weebil1: Tiny stub-billed bird, pale olive-brown above and creamy-buff below with faint streaking; short stubby pale bill with the diagnostic short pointed shape. Pale eye, brownish legs; smallest Australian bird.
wefgle1: Plump rufous-brown foliage-gleaner with warmer rufous wings, rump and tail, buffy throat and breast, and pale staring white eye. Dark olive-brown crown, stout pale-based bill, dark legs.
wehpit1: Stocky short-tailed pitta with glossy green back and wings, black head with chestnut crown and nape, black underparts, and a bright red lower belly and vent; pale blue wing-patch. Black bill, pinkish legs.
weiwoo1: Medium woodpecker with black-and-white barred back, pale buff-grey face and underparts, red crown and nape (red only on hindcrown in female), and a reddish belly patch. Chisel black bill, grey legs.
weka1: Large flightless brown rail, heavily streaked and mottled chestnut-brown and black above, greyer below, with strong reddish-brown legs and a stout pointed pale brown bill. Red eye, short rounded wings.
welswa1: Slim swallow with glossy blue-black upperparts, rusty-orange forehead, throat and face, and pale greyish-white underparts. Long forked tail, small dark bill, dark legs.
wemcha1: Large slender chachalaca, grey-brown above with a greyish head and neck, paler buff-brown underparts, and a long broad olive-brown tail tipped buff. Small dark bill, bare reddish facial skin, grey legs.
wepdov1: Plump grey-brown dove with pinkish-grey underparts, a black spot on the lower ear-coverts, bright blue bare skin around the orange eye, and white wing edges. Black bill, reddish legs.
werher: Slender dark heron, slaty-grey overall with a white throat-stripe (or all-white morph), long dark dagger bill with yellowish base, and dull yellow legs with yellow feet. Shaggy nape plumes when breeding.
wesant1: Antshrike with male slaty-grey overall, blackish crown, and white-tipped wing-coverts forming spots; female warm brown above, paler buffy below with rufous crown. Stout hooked black bill, grey legs.
wesbeg1: Large olive greenbul with greyish head, yellow throat and bushy whiskered chin, olive-green upperparts and yellowish-olive underparts. Heavy pale bill, grey legs; shaggy throat feathers.
