wesblu: Thrush-relative with male deep blue head, throat, wings and tail, rusty-orange breast and flanks extending onto the upper back, and a greyish belly. Small dark bill, dark legs; female grey-brown with blue tinge.
wesblu1: Plump finch with male glossy black overall and a brilliant scarlet-red face, throat, breast and rump; heavy two-toned bill (blue-grey upper, red lower). Red eye, dark legs; female blackish with red below.
wesbri1: Dull dark-brown bristlebird, finely scaled grey-brown on head and breast, with a long graduated brown tail and rufous wing-tinge. Long slender dark bill, reddish eye, brownish legs; skulking, ground-dwelling.
wesfie1: Antbird with male entirely glossy black with a fiery red eye; female rufous-brown above, cinnamon-buff below, also with a red eye. Stout black bill, grey legs; long-legged, slim.
wesfly: Small olive flycatcher, olive-green above, yellowish below, with a bold teardrop-shaped pale eye-ring (almond-shaped, pointed at rear), two whitish wingbars, and yellowish throat. Lower mandible orange, dark legs.
wesger1: Tiny grey-brown gerygone, grey-brown above and whitish below, with a white forehead spot, faint white supercilium, white outer-tail spots, and a thin dark bill. Red eye, dark legs; slender warbler-like build.
wesgre: Large slender black-and-white grebe with long thin neck, black crown extending below the red eye, white face and foreneck, blackish back, and a long dagger-like greenish-yellow bill. Lobed feet.
wesgul: Large heavy gull with white head and underparts, very dark slate-grey back and wings with white-tipped black wingtips, a stout yellow bill with red gonys spot, and pink legs. Pale eye.
weskin: Grey-headed kingbird with pale grey head and breast, lemon-yellow belly, brownish-grey back, and a blackish tail with white outer edges. Stout black bill, dark legs; upright tyrant posture.
wesmea: Stocky blackbird-relative, streaked brown above and on flanks, with a brilliant lemon-yellow throat and breast crossed by a bold black V on the chest. Pointed pale bill, pinkish legs, white outer tail.
wesowl1: Small eared owl, grey-brown finely streaked and vermiculated above and below, with prominent ear-tufts, a dark-bordered facial disc, and yellow eyes. Dark bill, feathered grey legs; cryptic bark-like pattern.
wesple1: Large grey go-away-bird-like turaco, mostly grey with fine white streaking on neck and breast, a long tail, and a bushy crest. Heavy yellow bill, dark legs; long-bodied with broad wings showing white patches.
wespuf1: Stocky puffbird, finely barred buff and brown above and on breast, with a buffy face and throat, dark crown, and a cinnamon-buff belly. Heavy black bill with reddish base, grey legs; large-headed.
wessan: Small shorebird, breeding birds rufous-tinged on crown, ear-coverts and scapulars, with dark arrow-shaped spots on white underparts and a slightly drooped black bill. Black legs; rufous wash on upperparts.
wesspi: Striped tanager with male black head boldly striped white above and below the eye, chestnut-orange nape and breast, olive-yellow back, and yellow rump; greyish belly. Small dark bill, dark legs.
wesspi1: Tiny honeyeater with male rufous-and-black head pattern, grey-brown back, white throat bordered by a black-edged rufous crescent, and a long fine decurved black bill. Reddish eye, dark legs; very small.
westan: Striking tanager with male bright yellow body, black back, wings and tail, two yellow-and-white wingbars, and a flame-red face and forehead. Pale stout bill, grey legs.
westho1: Tiny dull thornbill, plain grey-brown above and pale buff-grey below with faint fine streaking on forehead and throat, and a pale eye. Short fine dark bill, brownish legs; nondescript, small and rounded.
weswah1: Medium honeyeater, olive-brown above and paler grey-brown below, with a prominent bare yellow wattle of skin around and behind the eye. Slightly decurved dark bill, grey legs.
weswhi1: Olive-green and grey whipbird with grey head, a black throat bordered by white moustachial stripes, olive-green back and wings, and pale grey underparts. Short crest, dark bill, grey legs; long tail.
wetpig1: Plump green pigeon, male with green head and breast, an orange-buff or maroon wash on the mantle and breast, green belly, and a long wedge-shaped green tail. Pale bluish bill, reddish legs.
wetsab1: Large hummingbird, bronze-green above and pale grey below, with a long slightly decurved blackish bill, white postocular spot, and a long wedge-shaped tail with white outer corners. Grey-brown legs.
wetshe: Slender long-winged shearwater, uniformly dark sooty-brown overall (pale-morph birds whitish below), with a long wedge-shaped tail held closed. Slim dark grey bill, pinkish legs; graceful long wings.
wettyr1: Tiny tody-tyrant, olive-green above and pale yellowish below with faint olive breast streaking, a conspicuous whitish or pale eye, and pale wing edgings. Short flat dark bill, pale legs; upright stance.
wewpew: Drab pewee, dark grey-olive above and on the breast, with paler grey belly, two dull pale wingbars, and a slight peaked crown. Dark bill with dull orange base to lower mandible, dark legs; no eye-ring.
wfbeat1: Colourful bee-eater with green back and wings, a white forehead and broad white throat bordered by a black mask and a scarlet-red lower throat patch, buffy belly and red undertail. Long black bill, red eye.
wfqdov1: Plump terrestrial dove with a white face and throat, slaty-grey head and breast, rich rufous-brown back and wings, and a buffy belly. Reddish bill base, red legs; rounded body, short tail.
wfsowl2: Small rufous scops-owl with a white forehead and brows, no obvious ear-tufts, rich rufous-brown upperparts spotted with white and black, and buff underparts with fine dark markings. Pale bill, dark eyes, feathered legs.
wfwduc1: Long-legged whistling-duck with a clean white face and foreneck contrasting with a black hindcrown and lower neck, chestnut breast, finely barred flanks, and a black belly. Dark bill and long dark grey legs.
whbant1: Antbird with male grey above, white below, black face and throat, and white-spotted wing-coverts; female rufous-brown above, white below with rufous face. Long pinkish-grey legs, black bill; long-legged.
whbant2: Antshrike with male olive-brown above, a black-bordered white throat patch, broad black breast-band, and buffy underparts; rufous-buff crown. Female browner; stout hooked black bill, grey legs.
whbant3: Plump, almost tailless antpitta with rich olive-brown upperparts, a slaty-grey crown and nape, and clean white throat and belly contrasting with tawny-buff flanks and breast sides. Short stout dark bill; long pinkish legs; upright rounded posture.
whbant4: Small antbird with rufous-brown crown and upperparts, a black face and throat scaled or bordered with a white bib on the breast, and grey underparts. Dark bill, pale eye, grey legs; compact, short-tailed shape.
whbant5: Stocky antbird with bare blue skin around the eye, a pale greyish-white breast, rufous-brown back and wings, and a grey to brownish crown. Short dark bill, dark legs; rounded body with short tail.
whbant6: Male slaty-grey overall with a black face mask bordered above by a bold white supercilium and a black throat; female browner. Short stout dark bill, pale eye, grey legs; small rounded antbird.
whbant7: Round, short-tailed antpitta with olive-brown upperparts, a buffy-ochre face and broad pale supercilium, and creamy-white underparts with faint streaking on the breast sides. Dark bill, long pale legs; upright stance.
whbbab1: Babbler with warm rufous-brown upperparts, a grey head, and a clean white throat and central breast bordered by dark scaling, with rufous flanks and belly. Short dark bill, dark legs; rounded body and short tail.
whbbab3: Greyish-brown babbler with a dark crown, a long bold white supercilium and dark eyestripe, white throat, and pale buffy underparts. Long slightly decurved dark bill, dark legs; long graduated tail with white tips.
whbbla2: Small blackbird; breeding male glossy black with a vivid red throat and breast and a white supercilium over the eye. Female streaky brown with buff supercilium and pinkish wash on breast. Sharp pointed dark bill, dark legs.
whbbul2: Plain olive-brown bulbul with paler greyish-olive underparts, a whitish belly, and a short white supercilium with a dark loral stripe. Slim dark bill, dark legs; nondescript shape with slightly crested forehead.
whbbur4: Robin-like chat; male slaty-blue above with orange-rufous underparts and a bold white supercilium; female olive-brown with buffy underparts and a pale supercilium. Fine dark bill, pale pinkish legs; upright posture.
whbbus2: Medium bustard with a sandy-brown back finely vermiculated, a grey neck, and a white belly. Head boldly patterned black-and-white with a chestnut hindneck; bare yellow throat and red base to bill in male. Long greyish legs.
whbcan1: Small finch (serin) with heavily black-streaked olive-brown upperparts, two pale wingbars, a bright yellow rump, throat and breast grading to a white belly, and yellow face markings. Conical pale horn bill, pinkish legs.
whbcha1: Slim brown chachalaca with greyish-brown upperparts, a grey head and neck, and a contrasting whitish belly and vent. Bare reddish facial skin and throat; short dark bill, dark legs; long broad buff-tipped tail.
whbcha2: Black chat; male sooty-black with a white crown, white shoulder patch and white wing flashes; female blacker with a white throat and breast. Short dark bill, black legs; upright, robin-shaped.
whbcou2: Large coucal with a blackish head and neck showing a pale cream supercilium and pale-shafted streaks, rich rufous-chestnut wings and back, and dark underparts. Heavy black decurved bill, red eye, dark legs; long broad tail.
whbcra1: Small crake with olive-brown upperparts, slaty-grey underparts, and a striking face pattern of a broad white supercilium and white lower face separated by a dark eyestripe. Short greenish bill, yellowish-green legs; short cocked tail.
whbcus1: Pale grey cuckooshrike with a soft grey head and back, a whitish belly and vent, and dark wings; some races show a dark face mask. Stout black bill, dark legs; slim shrike-like body with long tail.
whbcus2: Cuckooshrike with grey upperparts and head, a clean white throat and breast, and a whitish belly; male shows dark grey lores. Black bill, dark legs; slender body with longish tail and pointed wings.
whbdro1: Glossy blue-black drongo with a contrasting white lower belly and vent and a deeply forked tail. Stout slightly hooked black bill, red eye, black legs; slim agile shape.
whbeme1: Small hummingbird; metallic bright green above and on the throat and breast, with a clean white lower belly and undertail. Straight blackish bill with pinkish lower base, dark legs; short slightly forked dark tail.
whbfan2: Active fantail, dark slaty-brown above with a bold white supercilium and white throat, a dark breast, and white underparts. Black bill, dark legs; broad fanned tail with white tips frequently spread.
whbfly1: Plain tyrant-flycatcher with greyish-brown upperparts, a whitish supercilium and pale face, a white throat, and pale grey-yellow underparts. Broad flat black bill, dark legs; upright Myiozetetes-like build.
whbfog1: Foliage-gleaner with warm rufous-brown upperparts and rufous wings and tail, a bold buffy-white supercilium and eye-ring on a darker face, and buffy-streaked breast paling to whitish belly. Slim straight bill, pale legs.
whbfrd1: Colourful fruit-dove; green above with a grey head and breast and a clean white breast patch and belly, a purple lower belly and yellow undertail (male). Short greenish-yellow bill, reddish legs; plump rounded body.
whbful1: Small fulvetta with a dark grey-brown crown, a long broad white supercilium and dark face, rufous-edged wings, a white throat and pale grey underparts. Fine dark bill, pale legs; rounded body, longish tail.
whbhaw2: Compact hawk, slaty-black above and on the head with a short white supercilium, and clean white underparts. Bare yellow-orange cere and legs; short dark bill; broad rounded wings and a black tail with a white band.
whbher1: Large hermit hummingbird, bronzy-green above with dull greyish underparts and a whitish belly, a dark cheek bordered by a pale buff supercilium and whitish throat stripe. Long decurved bill with yellow lower mandible; long white-tipped central tail feathers.
whbhum1: Small hummingbird, shining green above and on the sides, with extensively clean white throat, breast and belly. Straight blackish bill with pink base to lower mandible, dark legs; short slightly forked dark tail.
whblau1: Laughingthrush with warm olive-brown upperparts, a chestnut-brown crown, and a striking broad white face patch and supercilium contrasting with a dark eyestripe. Underparts buffy; dark bill, dark legs; longish rounded tail.
whbman1: Small stocky manakin; male black above with a white throat, collar and breast forming a bib, grey lower belly, and orange legs. Female plain olive-green. Short broad dark bill; very short tail; rounded body.
whbmes2: Ground-dwelling mesite with rich rufous-brown upperparts, a white throat and breast marked with bold dark crescent spots, and a pale supercilium. Short slightly decurved bill, strong dark legs; long broad tail, rail-like build.
whbmin3: Small minivet; male glossy black above with a white belly, an orange-red rump and reddish wing patch and tail sides; female greyer with paler underparts. Fine black bill, dark legs; slim with longish tail.
whbmoc1: Slim mockingbird, grey-brown above and pale buffy-white below, with bold white wing bands and a mostly white tail conspicuous in flight, plus a pale supercilium. Slender slightly decurved dark bill, dark legs.
whbmou1: Mousebird with soft grey-brown plumage, a whitish lower back and rump, and a long stiff tail. Crested head; stubby finch-like bill red above and bluish-white below; bright red legs and feet.
whbnot1: Small tinamou-like nothura with cryptic sandy-brown upperparts heavily barred and streaked black, a pale buffy throat and whitish belly, and dark-streaked breast and flanks. Short slender bill, yellowish legs; rounded, near-tailless body.
whbnut: Compact nuthatch with blue-grey upperparts, a black crown and nape, a clean white face and underparts, and rusty-buff undertail coverts. Straight pointed grey bill, grey legs; large head, short tail, climbing posture.
whbowl1: Small owl with rich brown upperparts spotted white, a streaked brown breast, and a distinctive face with bold white supercilia meeting over a dark-rimmed facial disc. Pale yellowish bill, dark eyes, feathered legs; rounded headed.
whbpar1: Stocky parrot with bright green wings and back, a clean white breast and belly, an orange-yellow crown and nape, and green cheeks. Pale horn bill, pinkish legs; short square tail, large-headed shape.
whbpic1: Tiny piculet with olive-brown upperparts faintly barred, a black crown spotted white (male with red forecrown spots), and whitish underparts barred with black. Short straight dark bill, grey legs; very short tail.
whbpic2: Tiny piculet, plain olive-brown above with a black cap dotted white (male red-fronted), and clean whitish underparts with minimal barring. Short pointed dark bill, grey legs; stubby tail.
whbpic3: Minute piculet, rufous-olive above with a short white supercilium behind the eye, rufous-buff underparts, and a yellowish-orange forecrown in the male. Very short pale bill, pinkish-orange legs; almost tailless.
whbpig1: Plump green pigeon, soft yellowish-green overall with a grey nape and crown, a white lower belly and vent, and chestnut undertail coverts; male shows a maroon wing patch. Pale bluish bill, reddish legs.
whbpur1: Small cotinga (purpletuft); pale grey above and whitish below with a bold white supercilium and forehead, dark wings, and concealed purple flank tufts. Short broad dark bill, dark legs; compact rounded body.
whbred1: Robin-like bird; male slaty blue-grey above and on the breast with a contrasting white belly and a rufous-and-black tail, plus a white supercilium; female browner. Fine dark bill, dark legs; upright posture.
whbros1: Rosefinch; male rosy-pink below and on the rump with brown-streaked upperparts and a broad white supercilium edged dark; female streaky brown with a pale supercilium and double wingbars. Stubby conical pale bill, dark legs.
whbscr1: Small scrubwren, olive-brown above and buffy-grey below, with a black face mask bordered by a short white supercilium and a white spot below the eye, and a pale eye. Fine dark bill, pale legs; short cocked tail.
whbsee1: Small seedeater; male pale grey above and whitish below with a white belly and a white wing speculum; female buffy-brown overall. Stubby conical pale bill, grey legs; compact finch-like shape.
whbsha1: Shama with glossy blackish upperparts and head, a bold white supercilium, rich orange-rufous underparts, and white in the wing and outer tail. Slender dark bill, pale legs; long graduated tail often cocked.
whbshb1: Shrike-babbler; male grey above with a black crown and broad white supercilium, chestnut and white wing markings, and pale grey underparts; female duller olive. Stout hooked dark bill, pale legs; large-headed, vireo-like.
whbsho1: Small chat-like bird, deep slaty-blue above and on the breast with a bold white supercilium and a white belly. Fine dark bill, dark legs; upright robin-like posture with a short tail.
whbsho10: Small, robin-like skulker; slate-blue upperparts and head with a short concealed white supercilium, contrasting white belly, blue-grey breast and flanks. Short tail, fine black bill, and pinkish legs.
whbsho3: Compact chat-like bird with deep slaty-blue head, breast and upperparts, a small white forehead/supercilium spot, and a clean white belly. Thin black bill, long pinkish legs.
whbsho4: Small dark robin-relative, uniform slaty-blue above and on breast, often with a hidden white supercilium and whitish belly. Short tail, slender black bill, pale pinkish legs.
whbsho6: Small rotund bird with olive-brown upperparts, rufous-tinged wings, grey breast and whitish belly; bold white eye-ring and pale supercilium on a grey head. Short tail, dark bill, pinkish legs.
whbspi1: Small rufous-brown furnariid with plain greyish-brown crown and back, rufous wings and tail, and a clean whitish throat and belly. Slender pointed dark bill, spiny tail tips, pale legs.
whbspi2: Tiny dark furnariid, rich rufous-brown above and on flanks with a short but distinct white eyebrow, whitish throat and central belly. Fine pointed bill, pale legs, short tail.
whbsun2: Small sunbird; male glossy metallic green head, throat and upperparts, sharply demarcated white belly and breast. Slender decurved black bill, dark legs; female plain grey-brown above, whitish below.
whbswa2: Glossy blue-black swallow with a broad white band across the breast and deeply forked tail. Short black bill, dark legs; clean contrast of white band on otherwise dark plumage.
whbtai1: Small warbler-like tailorbird with black crown and nape, white supercilium and face, rufous-tinged forehead, olive-green back and whitish underparts. Slender pointed bill, pinkish legs, cocked tail.
whbtan1: Grey-and-white tanager with grey crown and back, black mask through the eye, white throat and underparts, and black wings showing two white wing bars. Short conical bill, dark legs.
whbtap1: Small dark tapaculo, uniformly deep slaty-blue head, breast and upperparts with a contrasting clean white belly and lower flanks. Short black bill, pinkish legs, short cocked tail.
whbtap2: Small blackish-grey tapaculo with a prominent white supercilium contrasting the dark head and body, paler grey belly, and faintly barred flanks. Short black bill, brownish legs, short tail.
whbtit5: Small black-and-white tit; glossy black head, throat and upperparts, broad white cheek patch, white wing bars and white belly. Stubby black bill, grey legs.
whbtot1: Tiny flycatcher with olive-grey crown and upperparts, grey breast, white belly, and a pale eye-ring on a greyish face. Short broad blackish bill, two faint wing bars, pale legs.
whbtre1: Striking treepie with glossy black head, neck and breast, deep chestnut back and wings, white belly, rump and wing patch, and a long graduated black tail. Stout black bill, dark legs.
whbtyr1: Small olive flycatcher with grey-olive upperparts, bold white supercilium, two whitish wing bars on dark wings, pale grey breast and whitish belly. Thin dark bill, dark legs, longish tail.
whbwag1: Large pied wagtail with black head, breast and upperparts, a bold broad white supercilium, white belly, and a large white wing panel. Long black tail with white edges, slender black bill, dark legs.
whbwar2: Olive warbler with grey-olive upperparts, grey crown, broad white supercilium bordered by a dark eyestripe, and pale yellowish-white underparts. Slender pointed bill, pinkish legs.
whbwat1: Medium rail with slate-grey upperparts, crown and flanks, and a clean white face, throat and breast; rufous-chestnut undertail. Long greenish-yellow bill with reddish base, long yellowish legs.
whbwea1: Large stocky weaver with a white head and underparts, blackish-brown back, wings and tail, and bright orange-red rump and wing-coverts. Heavy blackish conical bill, dark legs.
whbwhe3: Small pale grey-green white-eye with olive-green upperparts, yellow throat and undertail, whitish or pale grey belly, and a narrow white eye-ring. Slender pointed bill, pale legs.
whbwhi2: Robust whistler; male grey above with a black head, white throat bordered by a black breast band, and white underparts. Heavy hooked black bill, grey legs; female plainer brownish-grey.
whbwoo2: Large black woodpecker with a clean white belly and lower back; male shows a bright red crest and red malar stripe. Long pale chisel bill, grey legs; bold black-and-white contrast.
whbwoo4: Smoky grey woodswallow with darker grey wings and head, a clean white breast and belly, and a black tail tipped white. Pale blue-grey bill with black tip, dark legs.
whbwoo5: Soft grey-headed woodswallow with a bold white supercilium, blue-grey upperparts, and rich chestnut breast and belly. Pale blue-grey bill with black tip, dark legs, white-tipped tail.
whbwoo6: Tiny hummingbird; male glittering green above with a violet-purple gorget, white breast band and belly, and rufous flanks. Long forked tail, short straight black bill.
whbwoo7: Green woodpecker with olive-green upperparts, a bold white supercilium and moustachial stripe framing the face, and barred greenish-and-buff underparts; male shows red crown and nape. Greyish bill, grey legs.
whbwre1: Small rufous-brown wren, warm brown above with a plain face showing a faint pale supercilium, clean white throat and belly, and buffy flanks. Slender slightly decurved bill, pale legs, cocked tail.
whbyuh1: Small olive-green babbler-like bird with greenish upperparts, a short bushy grey-and-olive crest, grey nape, and clean pale yellowish-white throat and belly. Stout pale bill, pinkish legs.
whcale1: Robin-like thrush with rich brown upperparts, slate-grey head and breast, and a contrasting clean white throat and chest patch. Slender black bill, long orange-pink legs.
whcant1: Plump antbird; male dark sooty-grey with a bold bare blue facial skin and a white cheek/throat patch. Female browner with white cheek; stout blackish bill, grey legs.
whcbab1: Warm brown babbler with rufous-brown upperparts and crown, plain face, and a clean white throat and breast contrasting buffy-brown flanks. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
whcbar1: Stocky green barbet, overall grass-green with brown-streaked head and breast and bold white streaking on the face and ear-coverts. Pale stout bill with bristles, greenish legs.
whcbla1: Sleek glossy black thrush with a broad clean white band across the upper breast/collar. Bright orange-yellow bill and eye-ring, dark legs; female duller brownish-black with paler collar.
whcbul2: Bulbul with a tall pointed black crest, black-and-white face pattern with a large white cheek patch, brown back, whitish underparts, and bright yellow vent. Short dark bill, dark legs.
whcela1: Small olive flycatcher with greyish-olive upperparts, two whitish wing bars, a partly concealed white crown patch, pale eye-ring, and whitish-grey underparts. Short dark bill, dark legs.
whcfor1: Elegant black-and-white chat with glossy black head, throat and back, a white forecrown, white belly and rump, and a very long deeply forked black-and-white tail. Slender black bill, pale pink legs.
whchon2: Black-and-white honeyeater with black head and upperparts, a large white cheek patch, white-streaked breast, and yellow wing panels. Slender slightly decurved black bill, dark legs.
whchor1: Slim blackish hornbill with white-tipped wings and a long white-tipped tail; head and droopy crest blackish with white. Pale yellowish casqued bill, dark legs.
whchor2: Large dark hornbill with a shaggy white bushy crest and white tail, black body and wings; male has a white neck and breast. Heavy blackish bill with low ridged casque, dark legs.
whcjac1: Slim glossy-green jacamar with metallic green upperparts and breast, rufous belly, and a white chin/throat patch. Very long thin straight black bill, short dark legs.
whcjay2: Deep violet-blue jay with a contrasting white throat and breast band and a blackish mask; richly coloured purplish-blue body. Stout black bill, dark legs.
whclau2: Boldly patterned laughingthrush with a fluffy erect white crest, white face, throat and breast, a broad black mask through the eye, and rufous-brown upperparts and belly. Stout dark bill, grey legs.
whcman1: Small stocky manakin; male with black crown and back, white face, throat and breast forming a beard-like collar, olive wings and rump. Short bill, bright orange legs; female olive-green.
whcman2: Small velvety-black manakin; male entirely glossy black with a bright snowy-white crown and nape. Short dark bill, dark legs; female plain olive-green with greyish head.
whcmon1: Striking monarch flycatcher with a white head and underparts contrasting glossy blue-black back, wings and tail. Stout pale blue bill, dark legs.
whcpar: Stocky green parrot with a white forehead and crown, dull bluish-grey throat and breast, scaly green body, and bright red undertail-coverts. Pale horn bill, grey legs.
whcpar1: Compact partridge with brown upperparts, grey breast, a black throat and a bold white cheek/face patch outlined in black, and chestnut-streaked flanks. Dark bill, reddish legs.
whcpig2: Large slate-grey pigeon with a gleaming white crown contrasting the dark grey body, and an iridescent green-and-bronze nape patch. Reddish bill with pale tip, dark red legs, pale eye.
whcpuf1: Plump puffbird with brown upperparts, rufous-brown breast and a white chest patch/collar, finely streaked underparts, and a whitish face. Heavy hooked blackish bill, greyish legs.
whcred1: Robust chat-sized redstart with glossy blue-black head, breast, back and wings, a clean white crown-cap, and a contrasting bright rufous-chestnut belly and rump. The broad chestnut tail has a black terminal band. Bill black; legs blackish.
whcsap1: Small hummingbird looking dark green overall; male has a violet-blue head, throat and breast, golden-green upperparts with a coppery rump, and a steel-blue tail. A small whitish chin patch shows in good light. Bill coral-red with a black tip.
whcsee1: Small finch-like seedeater; male black above with a black cap, white throat, broad black breast-band, white half-collar and buffy-cinnamon underparts, plus a white wing speculum. Stubby conical grey bill. Female plain buffy-brown.
whcsee2: Small seedeater; male has a black cap and face, white throat and partial collar, blackish upperparts and a contrasting cinnamon-buff rump and underparts, with a small white wing patch. Short stubby conical greyish bill. Female plain warm brown.
whcspa: Plump grey sparrow with a boldly black-and-white striped crown, plain grey face and unstreaked grey breast, and a brown, black-streaked back. Bill pink to yellowish, conical; legs pinkish. Long notched tail.
whcspa1: Tiny dumpy flycatcher with a huge broad flat bill, large head and very short tail. Olive-green above, yellowish-buff below, with a bold buffy-white crown stripe (often hidden), buff face and large dark eyes.
whcsta1: Medium grey-brown starling with a darker blackish head marked by white on the forehead and cheeks, a paler grey body, white vent and white tail corners. Bill orange with a dark tip; legs orange.
whcswi: Large robust swift, entirely glossy blackish, with a distinctive narrow white collar encircling the neck (broadest on the throat). Long scythe-shaped wings and a slightly forked tail; black bill and feet.
whctan1: Large velvety blue-black tanager with a contrasting shining white crown-patch and a vivid crimson-red throat and upper breast. Stout black bill; blackish legs. Strikingly large for a tanager.
whctap1: Small dark slaty-black tapaculo with a short cocked tail; shows a white spot or patch on the crown. Upperparts blackish, underparts dark grey, sometimes with faint barring on flanks. Bill and legs blackish.
whcter1: Slender pale grey tern with a clean white cheek bar separating the black cap from the grey body, dark grey upperwings and dark primary wedge. Long forked tail, dark-tipped reddish-black bill and reddish legs.
whcthi1: Small slender brown spinetail-like bird with a long graduated pointed tail. Dark brown upperparts, rufous-tinged wings, with a distinctive small white chin/throat patch and dusky-brown underparts. Thin pointed bill; dark legs.
whcthr1: Plain dark slate-grey thrush with paler grey underparts and a contrasting white chin and throat. Bill and eye-ring orange-yellow; legs dull. Dark reddish-brown wing tones may show in flight.
whctur1: Large glossy green turaco with a violet-blue crown and nape, a bold white cheek patch, and a red orbital ring. Crimson flight feathers flash in flight; tail dark glossy blue. Stout reddish bill.
whcwoo1: Plain dark brown woodcreeper with uniform sooty-brown plumage, slightly rufous wings and tail, and a contrasting small whitish chin/throat patch. Pale eye; straight blackish bill; dark legs.
wheant1: Small antwren-like bird; male grey overall with a black throat and breast bordered by white-tipped underparts and white scapular spots, and a prominent staring white eye. Slender dark bill. Female warmer brown below.
whebar1: Stocky dark blackish-brown barbet with whitish streaking on the breast, a bold white ear-patch and pale streaking on the throat. Heavy blackish bill surrounded by bristles; dark legs.
whebul1: Grey-brown bulbul with a black head and throat offset by a large white cheek/ear patch and a bright orange-yellow vent. Pale grey-brown underparts; black bill and legs. Crestless, neat-looking.
whebuz1: Medium greyish-brown hawk with a streaked breast, paler barred belly, a pale throat with dark central stripe, and a pale nape patch. Conspicuous staring whitish eye; yellow cere and legs; rufous-toned tail.
whecon1: Tiny warbler-like bird; male has a black crown, contrasting white ear-coverts, dark bluish-grey upperparts and pale grey underparts, with a white rump, white wing speculum and chestnut undertail-coverts. Fine pointed bill.
whedov1: Stocky brown pigeon with greyish head, an iridescent purple-pink neck patch, a bold white stripe below the eye (ear-streak), and warm brown upperparts. Reddish eye and orbital skin; reddish legs.
whegrs2: Dark ground-sparrow with an olive-green back, slaty-grey breast and head pattern of black face, bold white ear-patch and yellow patches at the bend of the wing and neck sides. Stout dark bill; pinkish legs.
whehon1: Medium olive-grey honeyeater with a grey head, an obvious white ear-tuft below the eye, and a yellowish wash on the wings. Dark bill, slightly curved; dark legs. Plain unstreaked body.
whehum: Small green hummingbird; male has a glittering green throat and breast, a violet-blue crown and chin, a black mask and a bold white stripe behind the eye. Upperparts bronze-green; bill red with a black tip.
wheimp2: Large pale grey-and-white imperial-pigeon with a whitish head and underparts, pale grey upperparts with a greenish gloss, and conspicuous bare red skin around the eye (spectacles). Reddish bill base; reddish legs.
whejac1: Chunky cinnamon-rufous jacamar with a heavy, swollen, pale pinkish-flesh bill, a small white ear-patch, and darker brownish wings and tail. Overall warm rufous-chestnut plumage; short legs.
whemon1: Small monarch flycatcher, blackish above with a white throat, breast and belly, a white ear-patch and white markings on the head. Blue-grey bill; dark legs. Slim, active build.
wheori1: Bright orange-and-black oriole with an orange-yellow head and nape, black throat, back, wings and tail, and a large conspicuous white patch in the wing secondaries plus white edges to the outer tail. Pointed blackish bill.
whepar2: Medium green parakeet, all green with scattered red flecking sometimes on the head and bend of the wing, a long pointed tail, and a bold bare white eye-ring around a pale eye. Pale horn-coloured bill.
whepuf1: Plump puffbird, brown above and buffy-white below, with a bold black-streaked crown, a broad black collar across the lower neck, a white ear-patch and white forehead. Stout coral-red bill; reddish legs.
whesib1: Slim long-tailed grey sibia with a dark grey crown, contrasting white ear-patch and throat, rufous-brown back and rufous-edged wings and tail. Whitish underparts; slender dark bill; grey-brown legs.
wheslf1: Plain slaty-grey flycatcher, uniformly grey above and paler grey below, with a conspicuous staring white eye. Slender dark bill; dark legs. Lacks wing bars or strong markings.
whesol1: Velvety blackish solitaire with a bold white ear-patch on the dark head, blacker upperparts and rufous-chestnut belly and vent. White patches show in the wing in flight. Dark bill; pale legs.
whevir: Small olive-green vireo with greyish-white underparts, yellow flanks, two whitish wing bars, and yellow 'spectacles' (eye-ring plus loral line). Distinctive staring white iris. Stout hooked bill; bluish-grey legs.
whfant2: Small antwren; male slaty-grey overall with a black throat and breast and conspicuous white flank tufts that flash from the sides, plus white-tipped wing coverts. Slender dark bill. Female warm buffy-brown below.
whfant3: Small antwren; male grey above with a black face and throat bordered by a broad white stripe down the side of the breast, white wing spots, and grey underparts. Slim pointed dark bill; pale legs.
whfher1: Slender pale blue-grey heron with a white face and forecrown, contrasting against the grey head and neck, and grey body. Long dark legs (yellowish toes) and a long dark grey dagger bill.
whfhon1: Grey-brown honeyeater with bold streaking, a white forehead and face, a black-and-white facial pattern, and a small yellow tuft behind the eye. Dark slightly curved bill; greyish legs.
whfibi: Glossy dark maroon-bronze ibis with iridescent green-and-purple wing sheen and a narrow band of white feathering bordering the bare red facial skin. Long downcurved grey bill; reddish legs and red eye.
whfman1: Small manakin; male glossy black with a shining white forehead extending back over the crown, a bright blue rump, a yellow central breast spot and yellow belly. Short bill; pale legs. Female plain olive-green.
whfnun1: Sleek slate-grey nunbird with a white forehead and chin, slightly darker wings, and an all-grey body. Conspicuous bright coral-red bill, slightly downcurved; dark legs. Slim, long-tailed build.
whfnun2: Dark slaty-grey nunbird with a white face and forehead, a chestnut-rufous belly and vent, and grey upperparts. Stout reddish-orange bill, slightly hooked; dark legs.
whfpar1: Stocky green amazon parrot with a white forehead and forecrown, bright red around and in front of the eye, blue on the crown, and red in the wing (males show more red). Pale horn bill; pale eye-ring.
whfred2: Small slaty-black whitestart (redstart) with a white forehead and forecrown patch, blackish upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and white outer tail feathers conspicuous in flight. Thin black bill; dark legs.
whfter1: Slender pale grey-and-white tern with a black cap separated from the bill by a narrow white forehead band, pale grey upperwings and white underparts. Long forked tail; black bill; reddish-black legs.
whfwoo1: Boldly patterned woodpecker, black above with white barring, a white face and underparts, a black mask, and a yellow-and-red crown patch (males). Black bill; greyish legs. Pale-fronted, contrasty look.
whgfly1: Small olive-brown flycatcher with a buffy face, a distinctive white throat (gorget) bordered by a dark necklace, and a tawny-buff breast. Rufous-tinged tail; small dark bill; pale legs.
whghon1: Plain olive-grey honeyeater, uniformly dull olive-grey overall with a slightly paler whitish gape and chin. Long, slightly downcurved dark bill; greyish legs. Drab and unmarked.
whgpig1: Plump green pigeon, bright yellowish-green overall with a greyish crown and nape, maroon-chestnut wing-coverts (males), and yellow edgings on the wing. Pale bluish-grey bill; reddish legs.
whhbab2: Large rufous-brown babbler with a contrasting whitish head and hood, rufous-brown back, wings and tail, and pale buffy underparts. Pale eye; stout pale yellowish bill; greyish legs. Long-tailed and bulky.
whhmun1: Small finch with white head and breast contrasting sharply with rich chestnut-brown back, wings, belly and tail; thick conical pale blue-grey bill. Dark legs. Compact rounded body typical of munias.
whhwoo: Black woodpecker with a strikingly white head and throat; body, back and underparts glossy black except white wing patches on the primaries showing in flight. Males have a small red nape patch. Bill and legs dark grey.
whhwoo1: Slender, long-tailed woodhoopoe with chestnut-rufous body, glossy greenish-black wings and long graduated dark tail, and a contrasting creamy-white head and breast. Bill and legs black; bill long and slightly decurved.
whhwre1: Large boldly patterned wren, white head, throat and underparts contrasting with dark chocolate-brown back, wings and tail; subtle dark eyestripe. Bill blackish, slightly decurved; legs greyish. Sturdy long-tailed body.
whiauk: Small dark auklet, slaty-grey overall, slightly paler below, with a forward-curling black crest on the forehead and conspicuous white plumes streaming back from the eye and base of the bill. Bill short and red; legs dark.
whibel2: Strikingly all-white pigeon-sized cotinga; male pure snow-white with a single long fleshy black wattle hanging from the base of the upper bill. Bill dark, legs dark. Stocky body.
whibro1: Plump emerald-green broadbill with a short broad bill; male has a black face mask and several black bars across the wing coverts. Tail short, legs dark, bill greenish. Rounded compact shape.
whicis1: Small warbler-like bird, plain warm brown above with faint streaking, buffy-white underparts and pale lores; rufous-tinged crown and wings. Tail brown, often dark-tipped. Thin pointed bill; pinkish legs.
whicoc1: Large all-white cockatoo with a broad backward-curving white crest; underwings and undertail washed pale lemon-yellow. Bill black, heavy and hooked; legs grey; bare eye-ring whitish.
whifly1: Tiny flycatcher, olive-brown above with a contrasting bright lemon-yellow rump, dull whitish throat and a small white tuft at the bill base; underparts pale buffy-yellow. Broad flat dark bill; pale legs.
whihaw1: Medium broad-winged raptor, body and head white with black-streaked crown; wings black above with a broad white panel, tail white with a single broad black subterminal band. Cere and legs yellow; bill black.
whihel1: Black-and-white shrike with grey crown forming a stiff white-tipped frontal crest, black mask, white underparts and white wing patches contrasting with black back, wings and tail. Striking yellow eye with a fleshy eye-wattle; bill black; legs orange-red.
whiher1: Elegant pale grey heron with creamy-buff head and neck, a black-bordered blue facial skin, and fine dark streaking; black-tipped wings. Bill pink with a black tip; legs short and dark. Slim graceful shape.
whiibi: All-white wading bird with black wingtips visible in flight; long down-curved reddish-pink bill, bare pink-red face and long reddish-pink legs. Bright blue eye. Slender long-necked body.
whikit1: Pale sandy-brown raptor with a whitish head and underparts, paler buff body and dark flight feathers; distinctive long, rounded, wedge-shaped tail. Bill dark with pale base; legs and cere dull grey-cream.
whimon1: Small, striking flycatcher, almost entirely pure white with contrasting black flight feathers and a black tail tip. Short black bill and dark legs. Plump upright body.
whiteh1: Small songbird with a pale greyish-white head and underparts and pale brown back, wings and tail; faint darker eye-line. Slender dark bill; dark legs. Compact, active warbler-like shape.
whiter3: Pure snow-white tern with large black eyes giving a wide-eyed look; slightly upturned slender black bill with a blue base; black legs with pale webs. Translucent wings, slightly forked tail.
whitre1: Slender long-winged treeswift, blue-grey above with a paler grey breast and white belly; long deeply forked tail. Males show a chestnut ear patch and white plumes streaking back from eye and bill base. Bill tiny; legs short.
whiwar2: Small green warbler, olive-green above and yellow below, with a grey crown bordered by dark lateral crown-stripes, a bold yellow supercilium and yellow eye-ring; usually one faint wing bar. Thin bill; pale legs.
whiwoo1: Mostly white woodpecker with white head, body and back; black flight feathers and black tail; small yellow patch around the eye and on the rump. Males show a red nape spot. Bill dark grey; legs greyish.
whiwre1: Rich rufous-brown wren above with a bold black-and-white streaked face, white throat and supercilium, white-bordered black malar (whisker) stripe, and warm buff-cinnamon underparts. Bill dark, slightly decurved; legs pinkish.
whiyuh1: Small crested babbler, grey-brown above with a pointed grey-brown crest, rufous ear-coverts, a white eye-ring and white throat; distinctive yellow neck collar. Underparts whitish with faint streaks. Thin dark bill; pale legs.
whlant1: Slaty-grey antbird with a black face and throat and a contrasting bold white crest/forehead stripe; underparts grey, paler on belly. Stout black bill; dark legs. Compact upright body.
whlant2: Plump, short-tailed antpitta, warm olive-brown above, with whitish lores and throat and a tawny-rufous breast and flanks fading to whitish belly. Stout pale-based bill; long pale pinkish-grey legs. Round, almost tailless shape.
whlgna2: Tiny slender gnatcatcher, blue-grey above and whitish below, with conspicuous white lores; breeding male has a black cap. Long black tail with white outer feathers held cocked. Thin black bill; black legs.
whlspi1: Plain rufous-brown spinetail, warm brown above with rufous wings and crown, whitish lores and throat, and pale greyish-buff underparts. Long graduated brown tail. Thin pointed bill; pale legs.
whltan1: Sexually dimorphic tanager; male entirely glossy black with a white shoulder patch (often hidden); female uniform rufous-brown, paler below. Conical bill, bluish-grey in male. Dark legs. Slim medium body.
whltyr1: Tiny tyrannulet, olive-green above and pale yellow below, with whitish lores, a faint pale eye-ring and two whitish wing bars; short tail. Very short stubby dark bill; dark legs. Compact rounded shape.
whlwar1: Olive warbler, olive-green above and bright yellow below, with a grey crown, broad white lores and a white spectacle/eye-ring giving a goggled look. Thin dark bill; pinkish legs.
whmbar1: Stocky barbet, glossy black above with a contrasting white mantle/back patch, white underparts and a red forehead and throat patch; black crown. Heavy pale bill; grey legs. Large head, short tail.
whmtyr1: Small marsh tyrant, male glossy black overall with a sharply contrasting pure white head. Stout black bill; black legs. Upright compact body; female browner with a grey-brown crown.
whncra1: Tall elegant grey crane with a white nape and hindneck stripe extending up the head, contrasting dark grey neck-front, and a bare red face patch around the eye. Body slate-grey, paler primaries. Bill greenish-grey; legs blackish-pink.
whncro1: Large all-black crow with a glossy purplish sheen and concealed white feather bases on the neck; striking red-orange eye. Heavy black bill; black legs. Robust corvid build.
whnhon2: Small olive-green honeyeater with a glossy black head, white throat and underparts, and a narrow white nape band across the back of the head; bare blue eye-skin. Olive back and wings. Thin black bill; dark legs.
whnhon3: Small honeyeater, olive-green above and white below, with a black head and a white crescent nape band; distinguished by a pale greenish-white (not blue) bare eye-skin patch. Thin dark bill; dark legs.
whnjac1: Striking hummingbird; male deep blue head and breast, white belly, glossy green back, and a broad white nape band; white tail with a dark central stripe. Bill straight black; legs dark.
whnjay1: Bold jay, dull blackish-brown above with a pale blue wash, creamy-white nape, underparts and tail tip, and a black face, throat and breast. Crown bristly; bill black; legs dark. Long-tailed.
whnlau1: Medium laughingthrush, warm olive-brown overall with a black face mask and throat, a white crescent on the neck/upper breast, and rufous-tinged wings and tail. Bill dark; legs pale. Rounded, long-tailed.
whnmon1: Monarch flycatcher, slate-grey above and on the breast with a black crown/cap and a contrasting white nape and white belly. Blue-grey bill with dark tip; bluish eye-ring; dark legs. Upright slim shape.
whnpar1: Small green parakeet, mostly bright green with a maroon-brown ear patch and forehead, a whitish breast patch, and red on the bend of the wing; tail green above, reddish below. Pale horn bill; grey legs.
whnpet: Medium gadfly petrel, grey above with a dark M across the upperwings, white forehead and underparts, and a distinctive whitish hindneck collar contrasting with a dark cap. Underwing white with dark margins. Black bill; pinkish legs.
whnpuf2: Large-headed puffbird, black above with a broad white collar across the nape, white forehead, throat and underparts, and a black breast band. Very heavy black bill, slightly hooked; dark legs. Stocky upright posture.
whnrav1: Very large glossy black raven with a broad white patch on the nape/hindneck; deep, strongly arched black bill. Black legs. Wedge-shaped tail; massive robust build.
whnrob1: Olive-brown thrush, warm brown above and paler below, with a contrasting white throat finely streaked black, buff-tinged flanks, and a bright orange-yellow eye-ring. Bill yellowish; legs pale pinkish.
whnsee1: Small seedeater, male blackish above with a white nape band, white wing patch and white underparts; black throat. Stubby conical pale bill; dark legs. Compact finch-like shape.
whnwoo1: Boldly marked woodpecker, golden-brown back, black-and-white spotted underparts, a black-and-white face pattern with a white stripe down the nape, and a red crest (male). Pale chisel bill; greyish legs.
whnxen1: Tiny pale flycatcher-like bird, greyish-brown above with a black crown and a white nape/hindcollar, whitish underparts and faint pale wing edgings. Short stout bill; dark legs. Small rounded body.
whocra: Very tall white crane with black wingtips visible in flight, a bare red crown and red-and-black face, and long dark legs. Heavy yellowish bill; long neck. Stately elegant build.
whpant1: Distinctive antbird, mostly slaty-black with a contrasting white head adorned by long, fan-like white plumes spraying from the crown and throat; rufous lower belly and undertail. Dark bill; reddish legs.
whphon1: Slim olive-yellow honeyeater with a brighter golden-olive head and a distinctive black-bordered white plume curving down the side of the neck. Underparts pale yellowish-grey; slightly downcurved dark bill, dark legs.
whqbus1: Small bustard; male sandy-brown above finely vermiculated, with black face, neck and underparts. In flight shows bold white wing patches (the white 'quills'). Reddish bill base, pinkish-grey legs; female buffier and duller.
whrbab2: Medium drab greyish-brown babbler with paler grey head and throat, contrasting whitish rump and lower belly. Plain wings and longish tail; pale yellowish eye, dark slightly decurved bill, sturdy legs.
whrfly: Stocky flycatcher with dark olive-black crown and face crossed by a bold white forehead-to-nape ring framing the head; olive-green back, bright lemon-yellow underparts. Broad black bill, dark legs.
whrhaw1: Small dark slaty-black hawk with blackish head and upperparts, a clean white rump band, and a black tail with a broad white band. Underparts blackish; rufous thighs, yellow cere and legs.
whrman1: Small velvety black manakin with a bushy white throat ruff and contrasting white lower throat/upper breast; rest of plumage glossy black. Short dark bill, dark legs; female plain olive-green.
whrmun: Small brown munia with dark chocolate-brown upperparts, head and breast, contrasting white belly and whitish rump; back finely streaked paler. Heavy bluish-grey conical bill, greyish legs.
whrsan: Small grey-brown sandpiper, scaly-streaked above with rufous-edged feathers; finely streaked breast and flanks, white belly. Diagnostic clean white rump band, fine straight dark bill with reddish base, dark greenish legs; long-winged.
whrsha: Slim thrush-like bird; male glossy blue-black head, breast and upperparts with bright orange-rufous belly and a white rump and very long graduated black-and-white tail. Black bill, pinkish legs.
whrshr1: Bold grey-brown shrike with white crown and forehead, broad black mask through the eye, white throat and underparts, and pale rump. Brown back and wings; heavy black hooked bill, dark legs.
whrsir1: Slim grey flycatcher-like bird with a slightly crested black crown, slate-grey face and back, and a conspicuous pale whitish rump. Black wings and tail with white feather edgings; black bill, dark legs.
whrsno1: Plump sandy-grey-brown snowfinch with pale greyish head, whitish underparts and a large white rump patch; blackish wings show white panels in flight. Short stout black bill, black legs.
whrswa1: Glossy steel-blue swallow with clean white underparts and a bright white rump; white supraloral line above the eye; blackish wings and slightly forked tail. Short black bill, dark legs.
whrtan1: Striking black-and-white tanager with black head, back and breast band, white throat and belly, and a bold white rump. Wings black with white markings; stout black bill, dark legs.
whsant1: Black antbird with a slaty-black body, deep black throat and underparts, and a bold white shoulder/scapular patch; bare blue facial skin around the eye. Black bill, greyish legs; female browner.
whsant2: Robust antshrike; male entirely sooty-black with a concealed white interscapular (shoulder) patch and pale eye. Heavy hooked black bill, dark grey legs; female warm rufous-brown overall.
whsant4: Small antvireo; male slaty-grey with blackish crown and face, white throat, and bold white streaking across the breast; white-spotted wing coverts. Pale eye, short hooked dark bill, greyish legs.
whsfai1: Tiny fairywren; breeding male glossy blue-black overall with a bright white patch on the shoulder/scapulars and white wing flashes. Fine black bill, blackish legs; female plainer brownish-black.
whsflo1: Small slaty-grey flowerpiercer, slightly paler below, with a bold white stripe down each side of the body. Sharply upturned hook-tipped black bill, dark legs; female duller olive-grey.
whsflu1: Small skulking rail; male rich chestnut head, neck and breast with the rest of the plumage blackish heavily spotted and barred with white. Greenish-yellow bill, reddish legs; female barred buff and black.
whsowl1: Small grey screech-owl, finely vermiculated grey-brown above with bold blackish streaking below and pale facial disc; yellow eyes and short ear tufts. Greenish-yellow bill, feathered grey legs.
whsrob1: Small robin with olive-green back, bright golden-yellow underparts and yellow-olive face marked by a tiny white spot before each eye (the 'star'); grey nape and crown. Black bill, dark legs.
whssta2: Pale grey starling with a whitish-grey head and underparts, soft pale grey back, and bold white wing patches and white-tipped tail; dark wings. Bluish-grey bill, pale legs; dark eye.
whstan1: Small tanager; male glossy black head, back, wings and tail with a clean white belly and a bold white shoulder patch. Stout blackish bill, grey legs; female olive-yellow with grey head.
whswar1: Small greenish leaf warbler with bold white eye-ring broken at front, greyish crown with faint pale median crownstripe, olive-green upperparts and yellowish underparts; two pale wing bars. Thin bill, pinkish legs.
whswar2: Dark warbler with slaty-grey upperparts and breast, a bold white supercilium and white throat, and a streaked appearance to the underparts. Olive-tinged flanks; thin dark bill, pinkish legs.
whswoo1: Slender woodcreeper with rufous-brown back and wings, a paler buff-and-whitish streaked head and underparts giving a frosted white-striped look, and rufous rump and tail. Slim slightly curved pale bill, grey legs.
whswoo2: Small woodpecker boldly barred black-and-white above and on the underparts (white-spotted), with a streaked face; male shows a red crown and nape. Whitish underparts spotted dark; dark bill, grey legs.
whtant1: Slaty-grey antbird with a gleaming white throat and white supercilium separated by dark lores, darker grey wings, and a black-and-white barred tail; bare blue skin around the eye. Black bill, grey legs.
whtant2: Plump rotund antpitta with olive-brown upperparts, rufous-tinged crown, a clean white throat, and rufous-brown breast and flanks; white belly. Very short tail, stout dark bill, long pinkish legs.
whtbul1: Olive-yellow bulbul with a slight crest, greyish-olive crown, bright white throat that puffs out, and yellowish underparts; olive-green back and wings. Dark bill, fleshy legs.
whtcac2: Large rufous-brown ovenbird-like cacholote with a shaggy crest, pale buffy face and a contrasting white throat patch; warm cinnamon-rufous body, brighter rufous wings and tail. Stout pale bill, greyish legs.
whtcan1: Streaky greyish-brown canary with heavily streaked upperparts, a plain white throat bordered by dark malar stripes, and pale streaked underparts; pale supercilium. Stout pale conical bill, dark legs.
whtcra1: Small crake with slaty-grey face and breast, a clean white throat, rich rufous nape and rufous-brown upperparts, and black-and-white barred flanks and belly. Greenish bill, reddish-pink legs.
whtdov: Plump ground dove, warm grey-brown above with a pinkish-grey breast and whitish forehead and throat; tail dark with bold white tips to outer feathers. Red orbital skin, dark bill, reddish-pink legs.
whtfan1: Slim dark fantail, sooty slate-grey overall with a white throat, a thin white supercilium, and a broad black-and-white fan tail with bold white tips. Fine black bill, dark legs.
whtfly1: Small olive-grey empidonax flycatcher with greyish-olive upperparts, a bold whitish eye-ring, a clean white throat, pale yellowish belly and two whitish wing bars. Short bill with pale orange lower mandible, dark legs.
whtfly2: Small flycatcher; male deep blue above and on the head with off-white belly and bold white panels in the outer tail feathers and white undertail; neat white throat. Dark bill, pale legs; female brown above.
whtger1: Tiny warbler-like bird with plain grey-brown upperparts, a bright lemon-yellow breast, white throat and white belly, and a faint pale eye-ring; red eye. Fine black bill, dark legs.
whtgol1: Slim hummingbird with shining golden-green upperparts and underparts, a white stripe behind the eye and white malar stripe, and white outer tail feathers tipped white. Straight red bill with black tip, dark legs.
whthaw: Large pale buteo, grey above with rufous shoulder patches and clean white underparts; broad rounded wings; white tail with a single black subterminal band. Yellow cere and legs, dark hooked bill.
whthaw1: Small compact buteo, dark brown above, with a clean white throat and white underparts variably streaked brown on the breast; dark-banded tail. Yellow cere and legs, dark hooked bill.
whthon1: Small honeyeater with glossy black head, white throat and a narrow white nape band, olive-green back and wings, and white underparts. Blue-grey crescent of bare skin above the eye; short dark bill, grey legs.
whthon2: Drab olive-brown sparrow-sized honeyeater with fine dark speckling on the breast and a small white tuft/patch on the cheek; paler below. Slightly downcurved dark bill, greyish legs.
whthor1: Large black-and-white hornbill, glossy black with a white belly, white thighs, and a white rump and tail tip; large casque-topped pale bill. Whitish lower legs (thighs); blackish bare facial and throat skin.
whthum2: Bright green hummingbird with glittering golden-green upperparts and head, a clean white throat and white central underparts, and white-tipped blackish outer tail feathers. Straight black bill, dark legs.
whtior1: Small iora; male olive-green above with a black crown, yellow underparts, two white wing bars, and conspicuous white outer tail feathers. Greyish hooked bill, blue-grey legs; female greener.
whtjac1: Small dark jacamar, glossy bronzy-green above and on the breast band, with a clean white throat and white belly; short tail. Long straight black bill, dark legs.
whtjay1: Striking small jay with deep blue body, a white throat and white crescent below the eye, and a black mask and breast band; whitish forecrown. Black bill, blackish legs.
whtjay2: Boldly patterned jay with deep blue upperparts and breast, creamy-white face, throat and belly, a black mask and white moustachial stripe, and a long blue tail tipped white. Black bill, dark legs.
whtkin1: Slim flycatcher with grey crown and back, blackish mask through eye, and bright lemon-yellow underparts including throat; the chin shows white. Tail dark and notched; bill short and black; legs dark.
whtkit: Pale, elegant raptor with clean white head, underparts and tail; pale grey back and upperwings marked by bold black shoulder patches. Dark eye-patch, ruby-red eyes, hooked black bill, yellow legs; long pointed falcon-like wings.
whtlap1: Sandy-brown plover with pale grey face and breast, white belly, and very long bright yellow legs. In flight striking black, white and brown wing pattern with white tail; slender black bill, dark eye.
whtlau1: Brown laughingthrush with rufous-tinged upperparts, a clean white throat and breast contrasting a dark band, and chestnut-brown belly. Greyish-brown crown, pale face; blackish bill, sturdy build, longish tail.
whtman1: Small compact manakin; male glossy blue-black overall with a sharply defined white throat patch. Short black bill, dark legs, stubby tail; rounded body typical of manakins.
whtnee: Large, heavy-bodied swift with stout cigar-shaped body, dark glossy greenish-brown plumage, pale throat and a broad white horseshoe of white from undertail across the flanks. Short stiff spine-tipped tail; scythe-shaped wings.
whtnig1: Small, pale grey-brown nightjar mottled and vermiculated; male shows prominent white outer tail feathers and white wing flashes. Cryptic camouflage plumage, large dark eyes, tiny bill, short legs.
whtnig3: Large dark nightjar with heavily mottled grey-brown and blackish camouflaged plumage; shows a small white throat patch and small white spots near the wing-bend. Big dark eyes, tiny bill; long broad wings.
whtnut1: Small nuthatch with blue-grey upperparts, black eyestripe, white cheeks and throat, and warm buff-orange underparts. White spots in the dark tail corners; straight pointed grey bill, short tail, strong feet.
whtoxy1: Warm rufous-brown babbler-like bird with olive-tinged upperparts and a bright rufous-orange crown and face; throat and breast white grading to buff belly. Slim dark bill, longish tail, pale legs.
whtpla1: Finch-like bird with thick conical bill; male grey above with reddish forehead and rufous-chestnut underparts, and a white wing-bar/white tail tips. Streaked back, dark wings, reddish eye; stout greyish bill.
whtpta1: Stocky grouse; breeding male mottled grey-brown and barred above with white belly, and entirely white wings and tail year-round. Feathered white legs and feet; small black bill, red eye-comb.
whtque1: Brilliant iridescent green head, back and breast with a metallic golden-green sheen, contrasting crimson-red belly. Tail green above with white outer-tail tips below; yellow bill, dark eye; plump trogon shape.
whtrai1: Rich chestnut-brown rail with greyish face and breast and a contrasting white throat; flanks and belly barred black and white. Long slightly down-curved reddish bill, reddish legs; rounded body, short tail.
whtrob2: Slaty blue-black robin (male) with darker wings and tail, white patches at the base of the outer tail, and small white flank/belly patches. Slim black bill, dark legs, upright thrush-like posture.
whtsco1: Small dark screech-owl with rich brown, heavily streaked and vermiculated plumage and a contrasting clean white throat. Dark eyes, short ear-tufts, greyish bill, feathered legs; rounded compact form.
whtsee1: Small finch-like seedeater; male black above and on the head with a bold white throat and white underparts, and white wing patches. Thick pale grey conical bill, dark legs; compact rounded body.
whtsic1: Hermit hummingbird with extraordinary strongly down-curved sickle-shaped black bill. Dark olive-green upperparts; underparts buff-grey heavily streaked dark, with white-tipped tail. Reddish legs; medium-large hummingbird build.
whtspa: Plump sparrow with brown streaked back, grey breast, bold black-and-white striped crown, yellow lores, and a sharply defined white throat. Greyish bill, pinkish legs; long-tailed, stocky.
whtspa1: Tiny flat-headed flycatcher, olive-brown above and buff below, with a large rounded head and concealed yellow crown patch; broad face shows pale eye-ring and whitish throat. Very short, broad flat bill; short tail.
whtswa1: Small dark swallow, glossy blue-black above and on the breast with a white lower belly and white thigh tufts. Slightly notched tail; tiny black bill, dark legs; slender aerial build.
whtswi: Slim black-and-white swift with blackish upperparts, white throat extending down the centre of the breast, and white flank patches. Long narrow scythe-shaped wings, forked tail; tiny bill.
whtswi1: Small dark swift, blackish-brown overall with a paler whitish throat and white-tipped tail feathers giving a scaly tail-tip look. Long pointed wings, slightly forked tail; tiny black bill.
whttap1: Skulking ground bird, dark grey-brown above with a bold white throat and breast, and rufous-brown flanks barred darker. Short cocked tail, strong pinkish legs, slender dark bill; rounded body.
whtthr1: Olive-brown thrush with greyer head, a contrasting white throat finely streaked dark, and pale grey-buff underparts. Bold orange eye-ring, yellow-orange bill, yellowish legs; typical upright thrush shape.
whttin1: Plump, ground-dwelling tinamou; rich brown above finely spotted and vermiculated with buff, paler grey-brown below, and a white throat. Small head, slender greyish down-curved bill, grey legs; short tail.
whttit1: Tiny long-tailed tit, soft grey above with a white crown and white throat, rufous-buff flanks and breast-band. Very small black bill, dark legs; fluffy round body with a long graduated tail.
whttou1: Huge black toucan with a white throat and breast bordered below by a red band, and red undertail coverts. Massive bill mostly black with a yellowish-orange ridge and base; blue facial skin around eye.
whttow1: Plain grey-brown towhee with greyer head, brownish back, and pale underparts; throat white bordered by a fine necklace of dark streaks. Conical greyish bill, pinkish legs; long tail, stocky build.
whttre2: Nuthatch-like bird with rich rufous-brown back and rump, dark wings and tail, and bold white throat and breast streaked dusky on the belly. Slender slightly upturned blackish bill; clings to trunks, stiff tail.
whttre3: Mottled grey-brown treecreeper with paler greyish underparts and a buff-white throat; pale buff wing-bar shows in flight. Slender, slightly down-curved blackish bill, short legs, stiff pointed tail for trunk-climbing.
whttro: Mostly white seabird with a long pair of slender white tail-streamers; bold black diagonal bars across the upperwing and a black mask through the eye. Yellow-orange bill, dark webbed feet; slim graceful form.
whttro1: Trogon with iridescent green head, back and breast, sharply bordered yellow belly, and a fully white-barred undertail. Pale blue eye-ring, yellow bill; female greyer; compact upright posture, broad tail.
whttyr1: Small olive-grey tyrannulet with two whitish wing-bars, a white supercilium, dark wings, and a contrasting white tail with dark central feathers. Pale yellowish belly; short dark bill, dark legs.
whttyr2: Olive-brown tyrannulet with a bold white supercilium, dusky crown, two pale wing-bars, and pale grey throat grading to yellowish belly. Short black bill, dark legs; small slim flycatcher build.
whtwhe1: Glossy black wheatear with a clean white crown (in adult male), white rump and white tail with a black inverted-T pattern, and white undertail coverts. Black bill and legs; upright slim posture.
whtwhi1: Plump whistler; male greyish-olive above with a yellowish wash, a contrasting white throat, and yellow-olive underparts. Stout dark bill, greyish legs; rounded head, short tail.
whtwoo1: Large robust woodcreeper, warm brown overall with a contrasting white throat and fine pale streaking on the head and breast; rufous wings and tail. Long, heavy pale slightly down-curved bill; strong feet, stiff tail.
whtwoo2: Green woodpecker with olive-green back, barred underparts, a white throat, and a red crown and nape (male). Yellowish face markings; dark bill, greyish legs; medium-sized, stiff tail for climbing.
whveup1: Tiny stubby euphonia; male glossy blue-black above and on the throat and breast with a contrasting yellow forecrown, and a yellow belly with white undertail/vent. Short thick dark bill, dark legs.
whvmyn: Dark grey myna, blackish on the head, with a short erect frontal crest, white wing-patch and white undertail coverts. Yellow bill, yellow legs; pale eye; upright stocky starling build.
whvmyn1: Large dark grey myna with blackish head and prominent erect frontal tuft, bold white wing-patches and white vent. Orange-yellow bill, yellow legs, dark eye; sturdy upright posture.
whvplu1: Robust hummingbird; male glossy green overall with a contrasting white vent/undertail coverts. Straight blackish bill; dark slightly forked tail; pinkish feet; relatively large, heavy-bodied.
whvsha1: All-black shama with a glossy blue-black sheen and a contrasting white vent/undertail coverts; long black tail often cocked. Slender black bill, dark legs; slim upright thrush-like build.
whvwhi1: Plump whistler; male grey-brown above with a white throat and a grey breast-band separating it from the buff-white belly and whitish vent. Stout dark bill, greyish legs; rounded head, short tail.
whwbec1: Compact becard; male glossy black above with bold white wing edgings and white-tipped tail, and dark grey underparts. Stout hooked black bill, dark legs; large-headed, short-tailed.
whwblt1: Slim tyrant; male glossy black overall with a striking white wing-patch obvious in flight. Pale bluish-grey base to the black bill, dark legs; upright posture, longish tail.
whwblt3: Black-and-white tit; glossy black head, throat and upperparts with a white nape patch, white wing-bars and white belly. Short black bill, grey legs; small active build.
whwcho1: Large all-black crow-like bird with a glossy sheen and bold white wing-patches that flash in flight; striking red eyes. Long down-curved blackish bill, black legs; slim, long-tailed.
whwdov: Plump grey-brown dove with a bold white wing-stripe along the folded wing edge, blue bare skin around an orange-red eye, and a small black ear-mark. Black bill, pinkish-red legs; squarish tail with white corners.
whwfai1: Tiny wren with a long, cocked tail. Breeding male is rich cobalt-blue overall with bright white wings and a black tail; females and non-breeders are plain pale brownish-grey with a bluish tail. Short black bill, dark legs.
whwgro1: Large, heavy-billed finch. Male has a blackish head, throat and breast grading to greyish back, with yellow undertail coverts and rump; wings black with a white wing patch and yellow-green wing edgings. Stout greyish bill, dark legs.
whwgua1: Large, dark, slim chachalaca-like guan with glossy brownish-black plumage, a bushy black crest, bare bluish-grey facial skin and a red dewlap. Conspicuous white patch in the primaries shows in flight. Greyish bill, reddish legs.
whwher1: Large hermit hummingbird, dark green above and dingy greyish below, with a black mask bordered above and below by bold white stripes (whisker and supercilium). Long decurved black bill with reddish base; squarish dark tail lacking long central streamers.
whwlau1: Medium laughingthrush, warm rufous-brown overall with a contrasting grey crown and nape, a blackish face and white whisker streak. Greyish wings and tail with rufous tones, pale underparts. Dark bill, pinkish-grey legs.
whwpar: Small green parakeet with a long pointed tail. Bright yellow-green overall with a large yellow-and-white wing patch (yellow lesser coverts, white secondaries) very conspicuous in flight. Horn-coloured bill, pinkish legs.
whwpic1: Tiny piculet, olive-brown above and white below heavily marked with dark scaling and white scalloping. Male has a red-spotted black crown speckled white; female crown all dark with white spots. Short dark bill, grey legs.
whwpot1: Medium-large nightjar-like potoo, cryptically mottled greyish-brown with darker streaking, giving bark-like camouflage. Shows a distinctive white patch on the wing coverts. Large dark eyes, short stout bill with wide gape, short legs.
whwpuf1: Plump, big-headed puffbird, rufous-brown above and buffy-white below with dark streaking, a prominent white whisker stripe behind the bill and a buffy collar. Heavy black hooked bill, dark eyes, greyish legs.
whwrob2: Small, plump robin, sooty-black overall with a contrasting white belly and a bold white wing patch and white outer-tail tips. Short black bill, dark legs; compact upright posture.
whwspi1: Small spinetail, grey-brown above with a rufous crown and rufous wing patch, greyish underparts, a black throat patch and bold white malar/whisker stripes. Slim dark bill, pale legs; longish graduated tail.
whwswa1: Glossy green-and-blue swallow above, pure white below, with a clean white rump and a large white patch on the inner wing (white-tipped tertials). Short black bill, dark legs; slightly forked tail.
whwtan1: Small tanager. Male is brilliant scarlet-red with black wings marked by two bold white wing bars and a black tail; female olive-yellow with similar white wing bars. Pale greyish bill, grey legs.
whwtit2: Boldly patterned tit, glossy black head, throat and upperparts with a clean white nape patch, white cheeks and white belly; black wings with a white wing patch. Short black bill, dark legs.
whwtri1: Small slim cuckooshrike. Male is pied: glossy black crown, back and tail with a broad white supercilium, white underparts and a large white wing patch; female greyer-brown above with fine barring. Black bill, dark legs.
whwwoo1: Medium pied woodpecker, black-and-white barred above with large white wing patches, whitish underparts faintly streaked, and a pinkish vent. Male has a red crown patch. Greyish bill, grey legs.
wibant1: Plump, short-tailed terrestrial antbird, rich rufous-brown above with two white wing bars; male has a black throat and breast scaled white, bordered by grey; greyish flanks. Stout dark bill, pinkish legs.
wibhor1: Rufous-and-buff ovenbird, warm rufous-brown above with a paler whitish supercilium and throat, buffy-white underparts, and a faint pale wing band. Slender slightly decurved dark bill, pinkish legs.
wibpip1: Small, plump, big-eyed bird, bright olive-green above with a grey crown, a yellow eye-ring and lores, yellow throat and breast, and two yellow-buff wing bars on dark wings. Short stubby bill, pale legs.
wibwre1: Small dark wren, deep brown above and below with a contrasting bold white band across the wing and faint pale scaling. Very short cocked tail, slender dark bill, pinkish legs.
wilant1: Slim antbird. Male is dark slaty-grey overall with a small white interscapular patch and white-tipped wing coverts forming spots; female browner-grey with buffy tones below. Slender dark bill, greyish legs.
wilfly: Small drab Empidonax flycatcher, greyish-olive above with an indistinct whitish eye-ring, two pale wing bars, a whitish throat and pale yellow-washed belly. Dark upper bill, orange lower bill; dark legs.
willet1: Large, stocky grey shorebird, plain brownish-grey above and pale below (mottled and barred in breeding plumage), with a striking bold black-and-white wing band visible in flight. Straight heavy grey bill, long bluish-grey legs.
wilpha: Slim, elegant phalarope. Breeding female has pale grey crown and back, a bold black-and-chestnut stripe down the neck side, white throat and reddish wash on the neck; male duller. Very fine needle-like black bill, yellowish legs.
wilplo: Stocky small plover, sandy grey-brown above and white below with a single broad breast band (black in male, brown in female) and a white forehead. Notably long, heavy black bill; pinkish-grey legs.
wilsap: Distinctive sapsucker, male glossy black above with a white wing patch, two white head stripes, a red throat and yellow belly; female very different, finely barred brown with a brown head and yellow belly. Black bill, grey legs.
wilsni1: Cryptic snipe, intricately striped brown, buff and black above with bold buff back stripes, dark crown stripes, barred flanks and a white belly. Very long straight dark bill, greenish legs.
wiltur: Very large dark fowl, iridescent bronze-and-green barred body plumage, a bare bluish-and-red wattled head, and a fan-shaped barred tail. Male has a chest tuft (beard) and spurred legs; stout pale legs.
wilwag1: Slim black-and-white fantail, glossy black head, upperparts and breast with a clean white belly and a bold white eyebrow; long black tail often fanned. Fine black bill, dark legs.
wincis3: Small streaky warbler-like cisticola, rufous-brown crown, heavily black-streaked tawny-brown back, plain buffy underparts, and a rufous-edged tail with dark subterminal spots and white tips. Fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
wincis4: Small cisticola with a plain rufous crown, greyish-brown streaked back, whitish underparts washed buff on the flanks, and a longish tail with pale tips. Fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
winwre3: Tiny dark wren, deep rufous-brown overall finely barred darker on wings, belly and flanks, with a faint pale eyebrow and a very short cocked tail. Thin dark bill, pinkish-brown legs.
wiscis1: Very small short-tailed cisticola, rufous crown, heavily black-streaked tawny back, plain buffy-white underparts. Breeding male blackish-faced. Tiny dark-tipped tail, fine bill, pinkish legs.
withum1: Tiny hummingbird. Male is bronzy-green above with a brilliant iridescent magenta-pink elongated gorget flaring at the sides, whitish below; female green above, pale buff below with green-spotted flanks. Short straight black bill, dark legs.
witman1: Small drab manakin, dull olive-green above and paler yellowish-grey below with a faint yellow forecrown spot and pale eye-ring. Short black bill, pale orange legs.
witman2: Small manakin. Male has a black body, a vivid yellow belly and lower back, a brilliant scarlet crown, white eyes, and two long curved wire-like tail filaments; female green above, yellow below with shorter wires. Dark bill, pale legs.
witswa1: Sleek swallow, glossy steel-blue above with a chestnut crown patch, pure white underparts, and two extremely long thin wire-like outer tail streamers. Short black bill, dark legs.
wlswar: Small bright warbler, olive-green above and yellow below; male has a glossy black cap on a yellow face. Slim dark bill, pale legs; active and round-headed.
wnbfin1: Medium brushfinch, olive-green above and bright yellow below, with a black head marked by a white central crown stripe (or rufous crown in some forms) and a yellow throat. Stout dark bill, greyish legs.
woewar1: Plain warbler, warm olive-brown above and buffy below, with a buff-and-black striped head (bold black crown stripes over buff). Relatively long, pointed pale pinkish bill; pinkish legs.
wofdov1: Large, striking fruit-dove, green back and wings with yellow wing spotting, a pale grey head, a deep purple-maroon throat and breast, and a yellow belly. Orange-red bill, reddish legs.
wonpig1: Large, plump grey pigeon, slaty blue-grey above and on the breast, with a white forehead, bold black-and-white spotting on the flanks and belly, and a white throat. Dark reddish bill, pinkish-red legs.
wookin1: Striking kingfisher, pale grey head and underparts, a bright turquoise-blue back, rump and tail, and black-and-blue wings. Bicoloured bill, red upper and black lower mandible; dark reddish legs.
wooscj2: Crestless jay, dull azure-blue head, wings and tail, a greyish-brown back patch, a whitish throat finely streaked and bordered by a faint blue necklace, and pale grey underparts. Black bill, dark legs.
woosto: Very large white wading bird with black flight feathers, a bare blackish scaly head and neck, and a heavy down-curved dusky bill. Long dark legs with pinkish feet; broad white body.
woothr: Plump thrush, warm rufous-brown above brightest on the head and nape, fading to olive-brown on the tail, with a white eye-ring and bold large round black spots on white underparts. Pale-based dark bill, pinkish legs.
worspa: Small plain sparrow, grey head with a rufous crown, a pale eye-ring, grey unmarked underparts and a streaked brown back. Notably pinkish bill, pinkish legs.
wrbfin1: Medium brushfinch, dark olive-green above and yellow below, with a blackish head, a yellow throat, and a distinctive thin white eye-ring (white-rimmed eye). Stout dark bill, greyish legs.
wrbhor2: Slim hornbill, greyish-brown above with white-spotted wing coverts, whitish underparts and a streaked greyish head, with bare pinkish facial skin. Long slender curved red bill; dark legs.
wrehor1: Large hornbill, glossy black body with a white tail; male has a creamy-buff face and neck with a rufous crown, female all-black neck; both have a bluish throat pouch with a dark bar. Pale yellowish bill with a low ridged casque; dark legs.
wrenth1: Plump, almost tailless wren-like bird with dull olive-bronze upperparts and slate-gray underparts and face; bright orange-rufous central crown stripe bordered by black. Short thin dark bill; longish pinkish legs.
wrenti: Small, very long-tailed grayish-brown bird, plain warm brown above and buffy below with faint dusky streaking on breast; rounded head, short bill, and pale cream or whitish eye. Pinkish-brown legs.
wrihor1: Large black-and-white hornbill; black body with white tail and a huge yellow-and-red casque-topped bill. Male has yellow throat and face skin, blue eye-ring; female has blue throat and black face. Blackish legs.
